providers.description: A genuinely inclusive day and overnight camp on Melwood's 108-acre recreation center in rural Charles County (about an hour south of DC), co-run with Children's National Hospital so campers with disabilities and chronic medical conditions — and their siblings and typical peers — camp together with real medical support on site. Activities include swimming, climbing wall, ropes courses, archery, canoeing, horseback riding, and campfires. Melwood subsidizes more than half the true cost of every camper.
providers.ages: Day camp 5-18; overnight 8-18; two-week teen program
providers.locations: Melwood Recreation Center, 9035 Ironsides Rd, Nanjemoy MD 20662 (Charles County, ~1 hr south of DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates on campaccomplish.org/experience-rates (not captured in indexed copies); every camper's fee is already subsidized >50% below true cost; no extra fees for campers needing more support, including 1:1 assistance
providers.financial_aid: Need-based scholarships including full scholarships — request on the initial application; in 2024 they met 100% of aid requests
providers.hours: Day camp 8am-5pm Monday-Friday; overnight is residential weekly sessions
providers.extended_care: Ask camp (day campers travel from a wide radius)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly day and overnight sessions, summer 2026; campers can combine any weeks; an open-enrollment inclusive day-camp week ran June 22-26, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; contact camp about remaining late-July/August day or overnight openings

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration typically opens Jan-Feb; no verified date)
reg_2027.mechanism: application (register online; scholarship request on initial application)
reg_2027.notes: One of the only overnight options in the region for kids with chronic health conditions (Children's National medical staffing). Counselor ratios run 1:1 to 1:8 by need. Indicate scholarship interest on the first application — do not wait.

providers.fit_notes: The go-to for a child with medical complexity or a disability who wants a real camp experience (including sleepaway) with clinical backup, and for siblings who want to attend together. Located an hour+ from DC — day camp commute is significant.
providers.verification_note: Site fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed campaccomplish.org, Children's National, and ACA pages: Melwood + Children's National partnership, day 5-18 / overnight 8-18, 8am-5pm day hours, >50% subsidy and full scholarships (100% of 2024 requests met), 1:1-1:8 ratios, Nanjemoy address, phone 301-870-3226, email confirmed, 2026 sessions active. Dollar rates not extractable from indexed copies, so confidence stays medium.

providers.name: Camp Arena Stage
providers.description: A multi-arts day camp from DC's storied Arena Stage where campers ages 8-15 build their own daily schedule from classes in theatre, dance, music, visual art, filmmaking, and writing — more arts-buffet than single-show camp, held on the leafy Georgetown Visitation Prep campus. Beloved for letting kids sample widely and go deep in what they love, capped by sharing performances.
providers.locations: Georgetown Visitation Preparatory School campus, 1524 35th St NW, Washington DC (camp operated by Arena Stage, SW DC)
providers.price_detail: Per official logistics page (search-verified): $300 non-refundable deposit; full tuition listed at $1,400 with balance due June 5, 2026 (confirm which session length this covers); 20% off for military/teacher/first-responder families; returning-camper discounts ($50-$100) by Feb 14
providers.financial_aid: Need-based scholarships by application (dedicated scholarships page)
providers.hours: Full day Mon-Fri (exact hours on logistics page)
providers.extended_care: Extended day historically offered; verify with camp office
providers.sessions_2026: 3-week session July 6-24; 2-week session July 27-Aug 7
providers.status_2026: 3-week session in progress (ends July 24); 2-week session starts July 27 — call 202-600-4064 to ask about remaining spots

reg_2027.opens: December-January (registration live well before the Feb 14 returning-camper deadline; Arena also hosts a DC-area performing-arts camp fair in mid-January)
reg_2027.notes: Returning campers get an early-registration discount through mid-February — a signal that loyal families rebook fast. Book by midwinter for choice of session.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for the multi-passionate kid who can't pick one art form; the choose-your-own-schedule model is unique in the area. Less ideal for a kid who wants to rehearse and star in one big show.

providers.name: Camp Fantastic
providers.org: Special Love, Inc. (with NIH/NCI Pediatric Oncology Branch)
providers.description: A one-of-a-kind week of overnight camp for about 100 children with cancer (ages 7-17, within 5 years of treatment), held each August at the Northern Virginia 4-H Center in Front Royal with NIH National Cancer Institute physicians and nurses providing full on-site medical care since 1983. Campers do ropes course, canoeing, swimming, and cooking like any camp — chemo schedules, ports, and counts are handled quietly in the background; teens get exclusive lodging and an LIT track for 16-17s.
providers.ages: 7-17 with cancer (within 5 yrs of treatment, or 7 yrs post-BMT)
providers.locations: Northern Virginia 4-H Educational Center, Front Royal VA; run by Special Love (Winchester VA), serving the DC/MD/VA region
providers.price_detail: Special Love fundraises so cost is never a barrier; families pay little or nothing — confirm current policy with Special Love
providers.financial_aid: Core to the model — no family turned away for inability to pay
providers.sessions_2026: August 2-8, 2026 (one session, ~100 campers)
providers.status_2026: Application window closed for Aug 2026 session; contact Special Love about waitlist

reg_2027.opens: winter/spring 2027 via Special Love
reg_2027.mechanism: application with medical eligibility review (treatment-timeline criteria)
reg_2027.notes: Not competitive in the ordinary sense — eligibility and medical clearance drive admission; Special Love also runs family weekends and sibling programs year-round. Verified via official Special Love and NCI pages.

providers.fit_notes: For families navigating pediatric cancer this is the gold-standard week — full NIH medical team on site. Eligibility is strictly diagnosis-based.

providers.name: Camp Happyland / Rappahannock Camp & Retreat Center
providers.org: The Salvation Army (National Capital Area Command)
providers.description: The Salvation Army's ACA-accredited overnight camp in Richardsville, VA (about 70 miles south of DC) serves kids 8-17 from the communities its DC-area corps serve — swimming, hiking, outdoor skills, and crafts at an extraordinary price: about $75 per week plus a one-time $15 registration fee. Registration goes through your neighborhood Salvation Army unit rather than a website checkout.
providers.ages: 6-17 (one-week specialty camps for children and teens)
providers.price_detail: $75/week + $15 one-time registration fee (2026, per Salvation Army division camp pages); field trips extra; confirm exact fee with your local NCA corps as fees can vary by area command
providers.locations: 21457 Happyland Drive, Richardsville, VA 22736 (Culpeper County, ~70 mi south of DC); registration via Salvation Army corps in DC, MD, VA
providers.hours: Week-long residential sessions (5-6 days)
providers.financial_aid: Fees are already donor-subsidized; corps can waive or reduce further for families in need — ask your local unit

reg_2027.opens: Spring, through local Salvation Army corps community centers
reg_2027.notes: There is no central online signup — call or visit the Salvation Army location nearest you (Sherman Ave NW, Solomon G. Brown SE, etc.) to register and arrange fees. Sessions fill through early summer; start in April.

providers.sessions_2026: One-week themed overnight sessions June-August 2026 (MusiCamp, Victory All Sports, STEM, ART, Great Outdoors, Leadership Trek), ages 6-17
providers.status_2026: 2026 season running June-August; late-session space possible via local corps
providers.fit_notes: Probably the cheapest real sleepaway camp available to DC-area kids. Faith-based but serves all families. The corps-based registration is a hurdle for newcomers — expect phone calls, not web forms.
providers.phone: 540-399-1197 (camp facility); register through your local corps
providers.verification_note: Site fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed official Salvation Army pages (salvationarmynca.org, southernusa.salvationarmy.org NCA, Potomac division): camp operates 2026 at Richardsville VA serving DC/MD/VA/WV youth via local corps, specialty week themes, $75/wk + $15 registration for 2026, facility phone 540-399-1197. Corrected ages from 8-17 to 6-17 per official pages. Fee figure appears on division-level pages; NCA-specific fee not separately verified, so confidence stays medium.

providers.name: Camp Horizons (Virginia)
providers.description: A large coed overnight camp in the Shenandoah Valley near Harrisonburg, VA (~2.25 hours from DC) with 40+ years of history and unusually broad programming across seven departments — aquatics, equestrian, performing arts, outdoors, science/tech, sports, visual arts — plus specialty tracks (Equestrian, Adventure, Leadership, and the performing-arts intensive 'Camp Up With People'). One- and two-week sessions for ages 6-17, with a big international camper and staff contingent that gives it a broader-than-Beltway feel.
providers.ages: 6-16 (Journey 6-11, Explorer 12-16; Leadership program for teens; Camp UpBeat 11-14)
providers.locations: 3586 Horizons Way, Harrisonburg VA 22802 (Shenandoah Valley, ~2.25 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: ~$1,975/week (e.g., $3,950 for 2-week Camp UpBeat session, 2026); Leadership $2,750/week; Equestrian $4,000/week; $375 non-refundable registration fee per camper per session; monthly payment plans through May 29, 2026; sibling discount $300/2-week ($150/1-week) session
providers.financial_aid: None advertised; sibling and early-enrollment discounts only — ask camp about aid
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: One- and two-week sessions June 21 - August 15, 2026 (e.g., Jun 21-Jul 4, Jul 5-18, Jul 19-Aug 1, Aug 2-15); registration closes 10 days before each session
providers.status_2026: Season underway with rolling registration until 10 days before each session start — late-July and August sessions may still be bookable via the dates/rates page

reg_2027.opens: rolling (register any time; monthly payment plans through late May)
reg_2027.notes: Registration for each session closes 10 days before its start date, so genuinely late registration is possible. $375/session registration fee. Specialty tracks (Equestrian, at $4,000/wk) cap early. Note: camp is in the Shenandoah Valley (Harrisonburg), not NoVA. Verified via camphorizons.com/dates-rates indexed copies and ACA profile.

providers.fit_notes: Good fit for a kid who wants to sample everything or a family that decided on sleepaway camp late in the year. Horse-focused kids should compare the Equestrian track's steep price against Camp Rim Rock.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via search-indexed copies of camphorizons.com/dates-rates plus ACA and 2026 listings: address, phone, ages 6-16, $375 registration fee, 10-day registration cutoff, payment plans through May 29 2026, 2026 session dates, and per-program pricing ($3,950/2wk UpBeat, $2,750/wk Leadership, $4,000/wk Equestrian).

providers.name: Camp Levine
providers.description: The DC region's flagship music-and-arts day camp, run by the nonprofit Levine Music for nearly 1,000 campers per summer across four campuses. Kids of any musical background (no experience needed) rotate through singing, instrument play, musical theatre, dance, visual art, and games in two 3-week sessions; a competitive Teen Apprentice program lets rising 7th-12th graders assist classrooms and earn volunteer hours. Great for younger kids who like the arts broadly rather than one instrument intensively.
providers.ages: 4-12 (Pre-K through rising 6th grade; Pre-K campers must be 3.5 by Jan 15) for campers; rising 7th-12th grade for Teen Apprentices
providers.locations: DC NW campus (2801 Upton St NW), DC SE campus at THEARC (1901 Mississippi Ave SE, Session 1 only), MD campus at Music Center at Strathmore (5301 Tuckerman Ln, North Bethesda), VA campus at West Falls (Falls Church)
providers.price_detail: 2026: Session 1 (3 weeks) $1,512 full day (~$504/wk); Session 2 $1,599 (~$533/wk); half day Pre-K/K at Strathmore $1,056/$1,092. Before care (8-9:30am) and after care (3:45-6pm) extra.
providers.hours: 9:30am-3:45pm full day; 9:30am-1pm half day (Pre-K/K Strathmore only); before care from 8am, aftercare to 6pm
providers.extended_care: Yes - before care 8-9:30am and aftercare 3:45-6pm for additional fee
providers.financial_aid: Yes - formal tuition assistance program; the 2026-season application opened Nov 21, 2025, and families are encouraged to apply when enrolling
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: June 22-July 10 (no camp July 3); Session 2: July 13-31
providers.status_2026: Session 1 complete; Session 2 begins July 13 (tomorrow as of snapshot). Camp fills quickly and sessions sell out - join the waitlist if a class is full; contact 202-686-8000 x1080 for last-minute openings.

reg_2027.opens: Early winter - tuition assistance applications open in late November (Nov 21 for the 2026 season) with enrollment following in winter (Dec-Jan); exact 2027 open date not yet posted
reg_2027.notes: First-come online enrollment per campus; Levine's own site warns camp 'fills quickly and classes/sessions will sell out' - waitlists open by spring, so register early. Teen Apprentice is a separate competitive application (form + references + interview). Apply for tuition assistance at the same time you enroll (application opens late November).

providers.fit_notes: Best for arts-curious kids 4-12 who want variety, not conservatory rigor - genuinely beginner-friendly. Not for teens seeking instrument intensives (see Summer at Levine) or kids who want a single-discipline deep dive.

providers.name: Camp Live Out Loud (Camp LOL) - Ivymount Outreach
providers.description: A Saturday-morning summer program run by Ivymount, the region's nationally known special-education school in Rockville, serving youth and young adults ages 6-30 of all ability levels. Ivymount-trained educators lead activities in music, movement, sports, games, socialization, and the arts. Think of it as expert-led social recreation and skill-building from autism-education specialists rather than a full-day camp.
providers.ages: 6-30 (youth and young adults of all ability levels)
providers.locations: Ivymount School, 11614 Seven Locks Rd, Rockville MD 20854
providers.price_detail: Fees not published in search-indexed sources; application fee waived for Recreational Programs — contact Ivymount Outreach
providers.financial_aid: None found; ask Outreach office
providers.hours: Saturday mornings during summer (number of weeks varies by year; confirm with Outreach)
providers.sessions_2026: Saturday-morning summer sessions presumed but not verified for 2026
providers.status_2026: Not confirmed for summer 2026 — the program page remains live and Ivymount Outreach is operating in 2025-26, but no 2026 Camp LOL schedule is search-indexed; email outreach@ivymount.org

reg_2027.opens: spring (contact Outreach office; no public opening date announced)
reg_2027.notes: New applicants are invited to a 'meet and greet' before placement — Ivymount Outreach matches participants to programs by conversation, not open registration. Ivymount Outreach is active (2025 Resource Guide and Resource Fair published on ivymount.org), but the Camp LOL page's indexed content dates from 2018-2019; confirm current-year dates and fees at outreach@ivymount.org.

providers.fit_notes: For autistic kids/teens/young adults (including adults up to 30, rare in this market) whose families want expert-led social skill-building. Saturday-only — does not solve weekday childcare. Ivymount's weekday extended-school-year programs are generally limited to enrolled students.
providers.verification_note: Program page on ivymountoutreach.org confirmed live and indexed (ages 6-30, Saturday mornings, meet-and-greet intake); Ivymount org confirmed active via ivymount.org 2025 Outreach Resource Guide/Fair; could not confirm a 2026 Camp LOL session schedule or fees — indexed program content is 2018-2019 vintage. Dropped unverifiable phone extension x157.

providers.name: Camp Rim Rock
providers.description: A classic all-girls sleepaway camp on 750 acres in Yellow Spring, WV (about 2 hours from DC) with a famous horseback riding program plus swimming, arts, sports, and campfire tradition — a multi-generation favorite of Washington families. Core sessions are two weeks (combinable to 4 or 6), with one-week Riding Camps and a one-week Mini-Camp in June for first-time rising 1st-3rd graders. Girls choose their own activity schedule.
providers.ages: 6-16 (Mini-Camp for rising 1st-3rd grade first-timers, one year only; General Camp through age 16)
providers.locations: 343 Camp Rim Rock Rd, Yellow Spring, WV 26865 (Hampshire County, ~2 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026: 2-week General session $4,300; 4-week $8,000 (multisession discount included); 1-week Riding Specialty $2,150; 1-week Mini-Camp (Jun 21-27) $2,625. $1,000 deposit due Feb 1, balance due Apr 1. WV sales tax extra; tuition includes room/board, instruction, laundry, camp t-shirt
providers.financial_aid: $200 discount for combining a Riding + General session; other referral/sibling discounts have been offered — need-based aid not advertised, ask camp
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: 2-week General sessions (combinable into 4-week, $8,000), two 1-week Riding Specialty sessions, 1-week Mini-Camp Jun 21-27, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; popular riding weeks fill early — contact camp (347-746-7625) about late-session openings

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (rolling; returning families re-enroll first); $1,000 deposit due Feb 1, balance Apr 1
reg_2027.notes: Riding sessions are the first to sell out — horse-focused campers should book early; combining Riding + General sessions earns a $200 discount. Verified against camprimrock.com/dates-and-rates indexed copies with 2026 pricing.

providers.fit_notes: For girls who want horses and single-gender tradition within an easy drive. Mini-Camp is a purpose-built first sleepaway. Not for kids seeking coed or one-week general sessions (minimum general session is two weeks).
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via search-indexed copies of camprimrock.com/dates-and-rates and home page: 2026 tuition ($4,300/2-wk, $8,000/4-wk, $2,150 Riding, $2,625 Mini-Camp Jun 21-27), $1,000 deposit due Feb 1/balance Apr 1, address, phone 347-RIM-ROCK, email, ages, and the $200 Riding+General combo discount.

providers.name: Camp Riverview (DPR Free Overnight Camp)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: DC's best-kept secret: a completely FREE week-long camp on the Potomac River in Scotland, Maryland (southern MD, near where the Potomac meets the Chesapeake), exclusively for DC-resident kids 8-13, offered as both overnight and day camp with boating, fishing and crabbing, ATV trail rides, environmental science, campfires and outdoor adventure. For many DC kids it is their first night away from home, at zero cost - an extraordinary deal run by the parks department.
providers.locations: Camp Riverview, Scotland, MD (Potomac River waterfront); open to DC residents of all wards
providers.price_detail: Free for DC residents, including overnight lodging, meals, and activities (ATV trails, fishing, crabbing, pedal boating)
providers.hours: Week-long sessions; full-service overnight camp with a day-camp option
providers.financial_aid: Free to all DC-resident participants
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long overnight/day sessions June 22 - August 7, 2026 (season ends Aug 7, earlier than the DPR day-camp calendar)
providers.status_2026: 2026 lottery closed May 4 (offers confirmed by May 17); season runs only through Aug 7 — call DPR at (202) 673-7647 about any unfilled late-July/early-August spots

reg_2027.opens: late April (2026 lottery ran noon Apr 27 - May 4; enrollment offers confirmed by May 17)
reg_2027.notes: Separate from DPR's February day-camp lottery — a second, later lottery many families miss; all entries get equal odds regardless of entry time. DC residency required and verified. In 2025 DPR opened in-person registration for unfilled weeks mid-season (url_more press release) — if you miss the 2027 lottery, watch for a similar announcement.

providers.fit_notes: For adventurous DC kids ready for a week away (a day-camp option exists for kids not ready to sleep over); not for kids who need daily parent contact. Free overnight camp is nearly unique in the region. Dates verified against the splash page and DPR press release via search-indexed copies.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via campriverview.splashthat.com indexed copy and dpr.dc.gov: free for DC residents, ages 8-13, Scotland MD, 2026 season Jun 22-Aug 7, lottery noon Apr 27-May 4 2026 with May 17 offer-confirmation deadline. Corrected reg note: the in-person-registration press release (url_more) was for 2025, not announced for 2026.

providers.name: Camp Shakespeare
providers.description: The Tony-winning Shakespeare Theatre Company's two-week summer camps where kids 6-11 and teens 12-18 spend their days on voice, movement, acting, text analysis, and real stage-combat training, building toward performing scenes from a featured play each session. Returning teens can audition for a three-week Intensive. Sessions run in Penn Quarter (downtown DC) and McLean, VA.
providers.ages: 6-18 (youth 6-11; teens 12-18; returning teens audition for 3-week Intensive)
providers.locations: STC facilities in Penn Quarter, Washington DC, and a McLean, VA site — location varies by session, check when registering
providers.price_detail: 2026 summer session tuition not verified on accessible pages (spring-break camp was $465/week as a reference point); non-refundable deposit $250 per registration ($125 for one-week camps). Sibling, multi-week, and military discounts
providers.financial_aid: Limited scholarship funds by application (Camp Shakespeare Scholarship Application on STC site)
providers.hours: Shorter than a standard camp day: Youth (6-11) 9:00am-3:00pm; Teens roughly 10:00am-4:00pm (10:00am-2:30pm in some sessions). Mon-Fri; no camp July 3
providers.extended_care: Not verified; ask CampSTC@ShakespeareTheatre.org — plan for the short day
providers.sessions_2026: Youth: Session I June 21-July 1 (A Midsummer Night's Dream), Session II July 5-15 (As You Like It), Session III July 18-29 (Macbeth); parallel teen sessions plus 3-week teen Intensive. Detailed logistics emailed to registered families by May 22, 2026
providers.status_2026: Session III (from July 18) upcoming as of mid-July — contact STC for remaining space; earlier sessions complete

reg_2027.opens: January (typical; not verified)
reg_2027.notes: Teen Intensive requires an audition and prior Camp Shakespeare experience. Scholarship application is separate and earlier. Double-check which sessions are DC vs McLean before booking, and note the shorter-than-9-to-4 camp day when planning work coverage.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for word-loving kids — text analysis and stage combat give it real substance, and no prior Shakespeare is expected. Kids who mainly want to sing and dance will be happier at a musical-theatre camp. Note the day ends at 3pm (youth) — a logistics gotcha vs 9-4 camps.

providers.name: Camp Sitar (Sitar Arts Center)
providers.description: Adams Morgan's renowned community arts center runs a multi-week, full-day summer arts camp where elementary and middle schoolers build their own schedule from studio arts, acting, music, dance, darkroom photography, and digital arts, plus field trips and performances. Tuition is on a sliding scale with 80% of seats reserved for families below 60% of area median income — the most affordable serious arts camp in DC.
providers.ages: elementary and middle school (roughly 5-14); broader Sitar programs serve 0-24
providers.locations: 1700 Kalorama Rd NW, Washington DC (Adams Morgan)
providers.price_detail: Sliding fee scale; Sitar's stated policy is that no one is turned away for inability to pay. 80% of enrollment reserved for families under 60% AMI
providers.hours: Full-day program
providers.financial_aid: Core to the model: sliding scale for all, deep subsidy for low/moderate-income families
providers.sessions_2026: Six-week full-day summer program (multiple sessions), per the Camp Sitar 2026 brochure
providers.status_2026: FULL — Camp Sitar 2026 is at capacity; a waitlist form is open and families are contacted as space frees up

reg_2027.opens: March (historic brochures say 'reserve your space in March'; spring registration announced via Sitar's channels)
reg_2027.notes: Fills to a waitlist well before summer — 2026 was full by early March. Watch sitarartscenter.org and their Instagram in February, and have income documentation ready for sliding-scale pricing.

providers.fit_notes: A genuine multi-arts immersion at a price almost any family can manage — extraordinary value and a warm, diverse community. Because affordability is the mission, middle/high-income families should expect limited seats and register the moment it opens.

providers.name: Camp St. Charles
providers.description: Maryland's flagship Catholic overnight camp, on the Wicomico River in Southern Maryland (Newburg, ~90 minutes from DC), running 1- and 2-week coed sessions since 1952 with swimming, horseback riding, archery, riflery, ropes course, boating, tubing, and daily faith life woven in. This is the traditional sleepaway-camp experience DC Catholic families have sent kids to for generations.
providers.locations: Newburg MD (Southern Maryland, Wicomico River) — draws heavily from the DC region
providers.price_detail: Overnight-camp pricing per 1- or 2-week session; $100 early-bird discount if paid in full by Jan 31; multi-session and sibling discounts
providers.financial_aid: Campership tuition-assistance program for qualifying families
providers.sessions_2026: 1- and 2-week overnight sessions June-August
providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-summer session availability possible — check dates-rates page

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (registration for the following summer opens in autumn; early-bird pricing runs through Jan 31)
reg_2027.notes: Popular sessions and girls' weeks can fill by spring; registering by the January early-bird deadline is the norm for returning families. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting a faith-centered classic sleepaway camp at 1-week starter length for kids as young as 6. Explicitly Catholic — daily prayer and Mass are part of camp life.

providers.name: Camp Tall Timbers
providers.description: A coed traditional overnight camp in High View, WV — right at the 2-hour mark from DC and long marketed specifically to Washington families, with round-trip transportation from Dulles included in the registration fee. Sessions run 2, 3, 5, or 7 weeks with 40+ activities including horseback riding, sports, waterfront, and arts. It occupies the 'full-service private sleepaway, but closer and less clubby than the New England camps' niche.
providers.ages: 7-16 (coed)
providers.locations: High View, WV (~2 hr from DC); round-trip transport from Dulles airport included
providers.price_detail: Exact tuition not published in indexed copies (expect private-sleepaway range, roughly $1,500+/wk); deposits at enrollment: $250 (2-week) / $500 (3, 5, 7-week). Fee includes linens, towels, Dulles round-trip transport, camp t-shirt; tuition covers riding, laundry, counselor gratuities
providers.financial_aid: Sibling discounts $200 (2-week) / $300 (3-week session); refer-a-friend $100 off for both families; need-based aid not advertised
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: 2, 3, 5, and 7-week coed sessions, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; contact camp (301-874-0111) about second-half (late July-August) openings

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (rolling enrollment via CampInTouch; returning campers re-enroll first)
reg_2027.notes: First-come rolling enrollment with deposit ($250 for 2-week, $500 for longer); 2-week minimum makes it a bigger commitment than day camps but gentler than 3-4-week-minimum sleepaways. Included Dulles-area round-trip transportation is unusual and genuinely convenient for DC families. Verified via camptalltimbers.com indexed pages; exact tuition unverified.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting a polished coed private sleepaway close to DC with horseback included in tuition. No one-week option; not a budget choice.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via search-indexed copies of camptalltimbers.com/sessions and ACA profile: coed ages 7-16, High View WV, 2/3/5/7-week 2026 sessions, deposits $250/$500, sibling ($200-$300) and refer-a-friend ($100) discounts, Dulles transport included, phone 301-874-0111. Exact tuition is behind CampInTouch and not indexed, so price band remains estimated — medium confidence.

providers.name: Camp Washington: Summer Day Camp at Mount Vernon
providers.org: George Washington's Mount Vernon (Mount Vernon Ladies' Association)
providers.description: A week of colonial life on George Washington's actual estate: archaeology, 18th-century music and games, horticulture, costumed interpreters, and the estate's heritage-breed animals, capped by a Friday family open house. Lunch, snacks, backpack, and t-shirt are included - rare for the price point.
providers.ages: Rising 4th-6th graders (roughly 9-12)
providers.locations: George Washington's Mount Vernon estate, 3200 Mount Vernon Memorial Hwy, Mt Vernon VA (~30 min south of DC, car required)
providers.price_detail: $400/session members, $460/session non-members (non-member rate just over the $450 band line); includes lunches, snacks, backpack, and t-shirt
providers.hours: 9 am-3 pm (drop-off from 8:45 am, pick-up 3 pm)
providers.sessions_2026: Two one-week July sessions historically; indexed pages show 'July 8-12' and 'July 22-26', which align to 2024 (not 2026) weekdays - confirm exact 2026 dates with Mount Vernon
providers.financial_aid: Two full-week scholarships awarded per summer via a scholarship application (complete form plus requested materials)

reg_2027.opens: Typically spring via mountvernon.org events calendar
reg_2027.notes: Only two small sessions, member discount ($60), and no bus access - registration is straightforward but capacity is tight. Members should watch the calendar from March. A two-scholarship program exists; apply early.

providers.status_2026: Event page remains live but its listed session dates (July 8-12, July 22-26) match the 2024 calendar, not 2026 weekdays - the indexed copy is stale. Whether camp is running summer 2026 is unconfirmed; call or email before planning.
providers.fit_notes: Great for history-loving kids and newcomers wanting the full 'colonial Virginia' experience; requires a significant drive from DC/MoCo with no aftercare, so hard for two-commuter families.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); verified via multiple searches of mountvernon.org indexed copies: name, URL, ages, $400/$460 pricing, 9am-3pm hours, 8:45 drop-off, and 2-scholarship program all confirmed, but listed dates match the 2024 calendar so 2026 operation is unverified - medium.

providers.name: Camp William B. Snyder (Cub Scout Day & Resident Camp)
providers.org: Scouting America, National Capital Area Council
providers.description: NCAC's 350-acre Cub Scout camp in Haymarket, VA with a water-slide pool, fishing/boating wetland, BB and archery ranges, and an air-conditioned dining hall — the 'starter' scout camp for younger kids. Offers short 3-4 night resident sessions (including a Bear-rank-specific week) and day camp weeks, sized for first-time-away-from-home campers.
providers.ages: Cub Scout ages: day camp for rising Tigers (completed K) through rising Arrow of Light (completed 4th grade); resident weekends for Cub Scouts with adult chaperones; Bear Camp for rising Bears
providers.locations: Camp William B. Snyder, 6100 Antioch Rd, Haymarket, VA 20169 (~45 min from DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026 fees behind scoutingevent.com 'View Pricing' pages (not indexed); sessions priced per 3-4 night block or day-camp week; lunch included in day camp for Scouts and chaperones; 1-2+ adult leaders free per unit depending on scouts registered; comparable NCAC Cub resident sessions run ~$325/scout
providers.hours: Day camp 9 am-4 pm (lunch included); resident weekends Thursday evening check-in through Sunday morning check-out; Bear Camp Monday morning through Thursday mid-day
providers.financial_aid: NCAC camperships - financial support application for NCAC youth members at 247scouting.com/forms/082-FinancialSupport2026

reg_2027.opens: winter (2026 registration was live by early year; first 200 Cubs registered got a free Camp Snyder drawstring bag)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via scoutingevent.com (082-SnyderResident); registration is by unit (pack) and must meet Scouting America chaperone rules
reg_2027.notes: Must be a registered Cub Scout. Short-session format is the easiest first overnight in the region; registration for each session closes ~10-14 days before it starts (2026: Weekend 1 closed 6/7, Weekend 3 closed 6/28). Popular June weekends fill first.

providers.sessions_2026: Resident Weekends June 18-21, June 26-28, July 9-12, plus Bear Camp June 29-July 2, 2026 (all with BBs, archery, aquatics, handicrafts, field games); day camp weeks (9am-4pm) per portal; Scouting America merit badge weeks also offered
providers.status_2026: All 2026 resident sessions concluded as of July 12 (final Resident Weekend was July 9-12; its registration closed June 28); contact camp for any remaining summer programming
providers.fit_notes: Gentlest overnight on this list (3 nights, AC dining hall, pool). Scouting membership required; sessions are early-summer only, so plan for June.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via indexed gotosnyder.org and scoutingevent.com 082-SnyderResident26/082-BearCamp26 pages: 2026 session dates, day-camp ages/hours, address, phone, email, unit-based first-come registration, free-leader policy, and NCAC financial-support form - only exact dollar fees remain unverified, so medium.

providers.name: Camp Woodend (Nature Forward)
providers.description: The region's classic naturalist day camp, run on Nature Forward's 40-acre Woodend Sanctuary in Chevy Chase, with weekly themed camps for pre-K through high school built around creek stomping, habitat hikes, and real environmental-science content. Counselors are experienced environmental educators (8:1 child-teacher ratio) and the vibe is gentle, screen-free, and genuinely educational rather than sporty. This is the go-to for a kid who loves bugs, birds, and mud more than ball games.
providers.ages: Pre-K through grade 12 (K-8 core camps; grades 9-12 via Teen Naturalist Training)
providers.locations: Woodend Sanctuary, 8940 Jones Mill Rd, Chevy Chase, MD 20815
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates in the 2026 catalog PDF (natureforward.org); member discount applies. Comparable published rates: 4-day spring camp $420 member/$485 nonmember; extended day $140/week. Exact summer weekly rates not directly verified via indexed copies.
providers.hours: 8:45am-3:45pm (drop-off window 8:45-9:15am, pick-up 3:45-4:10pm); extended camp to 5:00pm for grades 1-6
providers.extended_care: Aftercare until 5:00pm for grades 1-6 (approx. $140/week per catalog); NOT offered for kindergarten camps; no before-care
providers.financial_aid: Scholarships available — contact the Camp Director; membership lowers fees
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 22 - August 21, 2026, grouped into blocks (6/22-7/10, 7/13-7/31, 8/3-8/21)
providers.status_2026: Season underway. Popular weeks fill and carry free waitlists (48-hour claim window when a spot opens; staff say most camps see at least one cancellation) — contact the registrar at 301-652-1851 for current August openings.

reg_2027.opens: Late January (2026 registration opened January 22); catalog posts in December
reg_2027.notes: First-come online at a published date; members get discounted rates and the most popular K-2 weeks fill within days. Join Nature Forward before registration day and be online at opening time. Free waitlists are worth joining.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for curious, outdoorsy kids who don't need high-adrenaline programming; not the pick for a kid who wants sports, pools, or big-group energy. Woodend has no swimming, and kindergarten camps have no extended-day option.

providers.name: Camps Airy & Louise
providers.org: Camps Airy & Louise (Aaron and Lillie Straus Foundation)
providers.description: Historic (founded 1922) brother-sister Jewish overnight camps in the Catoctin Mountains barely an hour-plus from DC — Camp Airy for boys in Thurmont, Camp Louise for girls in Cascade, with joint activities between them. Grades 2-12 with both long sessions and short starter sessions for rising 2nd-5th graders, plus generous new-camper incentives (up to $900 off, first-come). The single-gender structure and century of tradition give it a distinct old-school warmth.
providers.ages: 7-17 (entering grades 2-11/12; short sessions for rising 2nd-5th; new 2026 3rd short session for new rising 2nd-3rd graders)
providers.locations: Camp Airy (boys): Thurmont MD; Camp Louise (girls): Cascade MD (~1.25 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: Tuition figures not published in verifiable indexed copies (expect Jewish-overnight-camp range, roughly $1,000+/wk); fee includes laundry, excess medical insurance, canteen, arts & crafts, AiryLou activities, bunk photo, camp t-shirt; full tuition due by Feb 1 for that summer; 2.4% credit-card fee; new campers up to $900 off (first-come, not need-based)
providers.financial_aid: Camperships (need-based scholarships, offered since 1922) via airylouise.org/dates-registration/financial-support-camperships/ with forms/documentation/deadlines, plus One Happy Camper eligibility for first-timers
providers.hours: Residential (overnight)
providers.sessions_2026: Long sessions (grades 2-11) plus 1st/2nd short sessions (rising 2nd-5th) and a NEW one-time 3rd short session for new rising 2nd-3rd graders
providers.status_2026: Season underway; 2026 enrollment closed (full tuition was due Feb 1, 2026); Camper-In-Training Day for prospective 2027 families held July 12, 2026

reg_2027.opens: Summer 2027 sign-ups begin around Camper-In-Training Day (held July 12, 2026 at both camps) and continue through fall 2026
reg_2027.notes: Register in summer/fall for best session choice and to catch new-camper incentives (up to $900, first-come). Cancellation fees escalate monthly from Jan 1 ($250) to May 1 ($2,000); full tuition due Feb 1. Short sessions are a gentle sleepaway on-ramp for 7-9 year olds. Prospective-family virtual pizza parties run Jan-Mar.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting single-gender Jewish overnight camp with deep tradition and a shorter drive than most sleepaways. Baltimore-DC blended community. Kids wanting coed camp should look at Capital Camps instead.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via indexed airylouise.org pages: org, URLs, phone, email, campership program, Feb 1 2026 tuition deadline, escalating cancellation-fee schedule, new-camper $900 incentive, new 2026 3rd short session, and July 12 2026 Camper-In-Training Day - dollar tuition still unverified, so medium.

providers.name: Capital Camps
providers.description: THE Jewish overnight camp of the Washington community — a 425-camper pluralistic camp in Waynesboro, PA (about 1.5 hours from DC) where a large share of DC/MoCo Jewish kids spend their summers together. Sessions run 2 to 7 weeks for grades 3-12 with classic camp program plus strong Jewish community life. Expect to see your kid's school friends there; the social continuity is a big part of the draw.
providers.ages: 8-17 (entering grades 3-12; 2-week starter option for rising grades 3-5)
providers.locations: 12750 Buchanan Trail East, Waynesboro PA (~1.5 hr from DC); DC-area bus transport
providers.price_detail: Full summer (7 weeks, Jun 21-Aug 9, 2026) $8,000; shorter sessions priced proportionally (~$1,100+/wk). Application fee plus deposit at registration.
providers.financial_aid: Need-based aid via Clarity platform (apply within 14 days of enrollment; early deadline Nov 16 for fall registrants); One Happy Camper grants of $500-$1,000 for first-time campers in 2+ week sessions
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: June 21 - Aug 9, 2026; Session 1 (4 wks, with 2-wk option for rising 3rd-5th), Session 2 (3 wks), full summer 7 wks
providers.status_2026: Season underway (ends Aug 9); popular sessions filled long ago — 2026 essentially closed

reg_2027.opens: early fall 2026 (returning campers first, then new; financial-aid deadlines start early November)
reg_2027.notes: Register in the fall with deposit, then file financial aid within 14 days — aid is reviewed only for deposited campers. Popular grade cohorts fill by winter. Cancellation fees escalate from January. Verified via capitalcamps.org indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: The default choice for DC-area Jewish families wanting their kids' school/synagogue community at camp. Pluralistic (all levels of observance). First-time-camper grants make the 2-week starter session a lower-risk entry. Not for families seeking a secular setting.

providers.name: Capital Fencing Academy Summer Camps
providers.description: A competitive fencing academy on Nebel Street in North Bethesda whose summer camps continue skill development after regular classes end in June, with games and drills for club fencers and pre-season intensive elite sabre camps for tournament-track athletes. More performance-oriented than DC Fencers Club — this is where committed young sabre fencers train.
providers.ages: Youth ages ~7 and up through teens, all levels (club trains ages 7+ recreational to internationally competitive); sabre-focused
providers.locations: 12180 Nebel St, North Bethesda/Rockville MD 20852
providers.price_detail: $450 per week per fencer (top of the $$ band); $100 daily rate if not attending the full week; free early drop-off (8:30 am) and late pick-up (3:30 pm)
providers.hours: Full-day camp weeks; free early drop-off from 8:30 am and late pick-up until 3:30 pm (confirm core hours with club)
providers.extended_care: Free early drop-off 8:30 am / late pick-up 3:30 pm
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Six consecutive weekly Summer Sabre Camp sessions beginning week of July 13, 2026; daily drills, games, conditioning, and sports-psychology work
providers.status_2026: 2026 Summer Sabre Camps run six consecutive weeks beginning the week of July 13, 2026 - just starting as of July 12; contact club for space

reg_2027.opens: Spring (with the club's summer schedule; 2026 camps published on capfencing.com/camps by spring)
reg_2027.notes: First-come registration through the club (questions to info@capfencing.com); club families and current students book early. Sabre-only academy - epee/foil fencers should look elsewhere. Daily-rate option ($100/day) adds flexibility.

providers.fit_notes: Best for kids already fencing or ready to commit to sabre competitively; total beginners will find DC Fencers Club's camps a gentler entry.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via indexed capfencing.com/camps and related pages: URL, phone, email, Nebel St address, ages 7+, $450/wk + $100/day pricing, free 8:30/3:30 extended care, and 2026 six-week run starting week of 7/13/26 - key facts confirmed from current official content, so high.

providers.name: Capitol City Circus School Summer Camp
providers.description: DC's only real circus school, in Navy Yard, where campers spend a week on flying trapeze, aerial arts, juggling, and trampoline, then pick a specialty and build a routine for a Friday performance. A two-week intermediate camp serves returning campers, and an advanced camp offers focused training for dedicated young acrobats. The rare camp where a total beginner genuinely flies on a trapeze by week's end — capacity is small, and registration opens February 1.
providers.ages: 6 and up for all-levels/beginner camp weeks (most campers roughly 6-14); intermediate (two-week) and advanced camps for returning or experienced campers; other youth programs span 12 months-16
providers.locations: 10 I St SE, Washington, DC 20003 (Navy Yard, near South Capitol & I St SE)
providers.hours: Week-long day camp ending in a Friday afternoon performance; exact daily hours not verified - see site
providers.price_detail: Pricing not published in verifiable indexed copies - contact the school
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly all-levels sessions (sample flying trapeze, aerial arts, juggling, trampoline, then build a routine for the end-of-week show), plus a two-week intermediate camp for returning campers and an advanced camp for experienced young acrobats
providers.status_2026: Page live and titled SUMMER CAMP 2026, but current availability not verified as of mid-July 2026; small program that historically fills after its February opening - call for cancellations

reg_2027.opens: Early February (2025 registration opened February 1 at 10 pm; intermediate/advanced applications due ~January 15)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online for all-levels weeks; application (due mid-January) for intermediate/advanced camps
reg_2027.notes: The niche appeal (flying trapeze in the city) plus tiny capacity means popular weeks go quickly after the early-February opening - set a calendar reminder. The camp page URL still uses the 'summer-camp-2025' slug even though the page is titled SUMMER CAMP 2026 - it is the current page.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via indexed capcitycircus.com pages: org active, URL (2025 slug, 2026 title) current, Navy Yard address, camp tiers, Feb-1-at-10pm registration pattern, and mid-January intermediate application deadline - pricing, hours, and 2026 availability unverified, so medium.

providers.name: Capitol Debate Summer Camp at American University
providers.description: A nationally known two-week debate and public-speaking intensive on the American University campus, with day (commuter) and residential (overnight) options for middle and high schoolers. Veteran coaches (many with 15-30 years experience) run practice debates with lengthy individual feedback — built for kids who want real confidence and argumentation skills, not just a taster.
providers.ages: Ages 10-17 (middle and high school)
providers.locations: American University, 4400 Massachusetts Ave NW, Washington DC (residential campers stay in air-conditioned Letts Hall double rooms; dining at Mary Graydon Center); also virtual programs
providers.price_detail: 2026: commuter $3,299 for the two-week session (weekdays plus Saturday, 9am-5pm; tuition only), residential $4,099 (tuition, Letts Hall room, and board); additional $100 safety & compliance fee; optional coaching 'Boost Package' add-ons ~$499-$799
providers.financial_aid: Not verified; they market early-bird and package discounts
providers.sessions_2026: July 12-23, 2026 (two weeks) at American University; multiple virtual sessions also offered
providers.status_2026: The AU session started July 12, 2026 - effectively closed for 2026; virtual sessions may still have space

reg_2027.opens: Fall of the prior year (rolling)
reg_2027.notes: Straight online enrollment (no application or audition) with early-bird pricing tiers; residential spots are more limited than day spots. Book by winter for the cheapest rate and guaranteed residential housing.

providers.fit_notes: For kids genuinely interested in speaking/argument — two weeks is a big commitment. Newcomers should compare with the free WUDL institute before paying four figures; Capitol Debate offers more polish and a residential option.
providers.hours: 9 am-5 pm weekdays plus Saturday (commuter); residential is overnight
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via indexed capitoldebate.com pages: 2026 AU dates July 12-23, ages 10-17 (corrected from 11-18), commuter $3,299 / residential $4,099 (residential price now confirmed), Letts Hall/Mary Graydon details, $100 safety fee, and Boost Packages $499-$799 (corrected from $499-$899) - key 2026 facts confirmed from official content, so high.

providers.name: Capitol Hill Arts Workshop (CHAW) Summer Camp
providers.description: Capitol Hill's beloved neighborhood arts center runs multi-arts summer camps (visual art, theater, music, movement) for pre-K through high school, with every week ending in a Friday demonstration for families. Full days run 9-5 with before/after care, all materials included — one of the few true full-coverage arts camps in DC, which is why Hill families race for spots.
providers.ages: Pre-K through high school (banded by age; half-day options for youngest)
providers.locations: 545 7th Street SE, Washington DC 20003
providers.price_detail: 2026 tuition not verified from official copy; tuition assistance and payment plans available
providers.hours: 9am-5pm full day; before/after care extends the day
providers.financial_aid: Tuition assistance and payment plans; CHAW's ethos is that no child is turned away for inability to pay
providers.sessions_2026: June 22-26, July 6-10, July 13-17, July 20-24, July 27-31, Aug 3-7 (no camp week of June 29)
providers.status_2026: Registration opened Feb 10 at noon and CHAW camps historically fill by spring; scattered openings possible — not verified as of July 12

reg_2027.opens: mid-February (Feb 10 at 12:00 noon in 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Set a calendar alarm for the noon opening — this is one of the Hill's fastest-selling camps. Email brian@chaw.org about aid before registration so you're ready to enroll the day it opens.

providers.fit_notes: The classic choice for Capitol Hill families wanting an artsy, community-rooted camp with real 9-5 coverage. Multi-arts sampler format — kids wanting deep single-discipline training (serious ceramics, filmmaking) will outgrow it.

providers.name: Capitol Hill Cooperative Nursery School & The Hill Preschool Summer Camps
providers.org: Capitol Hill co-op preschool community (CHCNS; The Hill Preschool)
providers.description: Capitol Hill's co-op preschools run small neighborhood summer sessions: CHCNS (60+ years old, ages 2.5+) does classic Hill preschooling with walking field trips to Eastern Market, Yards Park splash pools, and the Botanic Garden, while The Hill Preschool offers summer camp for children up to age six. These are intimate, parent-involved programs — the opposite of a big commercial camp — and among the only true under-4 options on the Hill.
providers.ages: 2.5-6 (CHCNS from 2.5; The Hill Preschool 3.5-6, fully potty trained)
providers.locations: Capitol Hill, Washington DC (CHCNS at 421 Seward Square SE; The Hill Preschool campus nearby)
providers.price_detail: Session-based weekly tuition; CHCNS charged ~$385/wk in recent summers with early-bird, alumni, sibling and multi-week discounts (2026 early-bird discount advertised); The Hill Preschool charges per weekly session plus a $50 non-refundable application fee, payment in full to hold a spot — confirm current rates with each school
providers.financial_aid: Co-op participation keeps costs down; no formal aid program found — ask each school
providers.sessions_2026: The Hill Preschool 2026: drop-in June 15-17, then themed weeks June 22-26, June 29-July 2, July 13-17, July 20-24, Aug 10-14 (space/moon, dinosaurs, STEAM, performance art, geology, with Smithsonian field trips); CHCNS runs its own June-July week blocks
providers.status_2026: Mid-July: The Hill Preschool 2026 weeks of Jul 13-17, Jul 20-24 and Aug 10-14 are on the calendar — space limited, waitlist via the school (contact Nina Mack); CHCNS mid/late-summer availability not posted — contact directly

reg_2027.opens: late winter (roughly February-March), enrolled and alumni families first
reg_2027.notes: Both schools fill largely through their school communities and word of mouth; newcomers should email both schools in January-February. CHCNS advertises an early-bird summer-camp discount; The Hill Preschool requires a $50 application fee and full payment to secure a spot. Exact 2027 dates not yet posted.

providers.fit_notes: For Hill families wanting a walkable, familiar-feeling first camp for a toddler/preschooler. For ages 3-10 with full-day needs on the Hill, see Adventures on the Hill (summercampdc.com) instead.
providers.hours: Preschool camp day roughly 8:30am drop-off to 3:00pm pick-up (CHCNS pattern); confirm per school
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed copies of chcns.us/summer-camp-overview and thehillpreschool.org/summer-camp-current (2026 camp live with session dates, ages 3.5-6, $50 fee) — both schools operating 2026 camps; current weekly prices not indexed (CHCNS ~$385/wk historic), so medium.

providers.name: Casey Trees TreeWise Camps & High School Summer Crew
providers.description: DC's urban-forestry nonprofit runs two youth tracks: TreeWise spring-break and summer nature camps for kids (STEAM-themed weeks of science experiments, crafts, neighborhood hikes, and a field trip focused on trees, pollinators, and watershed care) and a paid High School Summer Crew that employs teens to water, weed, mulch, and monitor Casey Trees plantings across the city. Both are rooted in real conservation work rather than generic camp programming.
providers.ages: TreeWise summer camp 2026 (Branching Out in Brookland): rising 4th-5th graders who live in or attend school in DC; spring-break camps serve elementary ages; Summer Crew: high school
providers.locations: Casey Trees HQ, 3030 12th St NE (Brookland), with field trips (US National Arboretum, Franciscan Monastery tree planting); crew works sites across DC
providers.price_detail: Camp was $150 for the full week in 2025; 2026 sessions register via Eventbrite (similar community-priced range). High School Summer Crew is a PAID summer job
providers.hours: Camp day 8:30am-3:30pm (drop-off 8:30-9:00, pick-up 3:00-3:30); snacks provided, campers bring lunch (lunch assistance available)
providers.financial_aid: Camp scholarships available — email education@caseytrees.org; Summer Crew pays teens
providers.sessions_2026: Summer camp 'Branching Out in Brookland': Aug 3-7 and Aug 10-14, 2026 (plus spring-break TreeWise camps earlier in the year); crew runs the summer work season
providers.status_2026: 2026 camps confirmed: Aug 10-14 week SOLD OUT (waitlist open, tickets released as they free up); Aug 3-7 week still listed on Eventbrite — check availability. Crew hiring for 2026 is closed.

reg_2027.opens: January (2026 spring-break + summer registration opened together in early January)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via Eventbrite; all registrations final
reg_2027.notes: Camps: first-come on Eventbrite with small capacity (2026 Aug 10-14 week sold out with a waitlist); complete the detailed registration/waiver form linked in the confirmation email. Crew: application in late winter — teens can also route via DC's SYEP window. Email education@caseytrees.org for announcements and scholarships.

providers.fit_notes: TreeWise suits science-y city kids who like purposeful outdoor time; the crew is one of the few paid outdoor summer jobs for DC teens. Not a full-summer childcare plan.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed copies of caseytrees.org/2026-nature-camps-for-kids and live 2026 Eventbrite listings — ages narrowed to rising 4th-5th graders (was wrongly 'roughly 5-12'), hours, both Aug 2026 sessions, sell-out/waitlist status, scholarships, and $150/wk 2025 price confirmed; Summer Crew existence confirmed via official copy.

providers.name: Catholic University Pre-College Summer Programs
providers.description: A slate of career-taste pre-college programs on the Brookland campus: Engineering New Frontiers and the mentored Da Vinci Scholars research experience, Experiences in Architecture (3 weeks), a Summer Nursing Institute, writing workshops, and the residential High School Drama Institute (July 13-31, 2026; $4,750 all-inclusive). Smaller and more personal than the GW/Georgetown giants, on a Metro-accessible Red Line campus.
providers.ages: High school students (Engineering New Frontiers targets rising juniors and seniors; other programs vary)
providers.locations: Catholic University campus, 620 Michigan Ave NE (Brookland-CUA Metro); residential and commuter options by program
providers.hours: Varies by program; residential (Drama, Engineering New Frontiers) and commuter options depending on program
providers.price_detail: High School Drama Institute: $4,750 for 3 weeks (July 13-31, 2026) including all housing, meals, workshops, and theatre tickets; engineering/architecture/nursing fees posted per program page and not verified this pass
providers.financial_aid: Not verified — ask each program office
providers.status_2026: 2026 programs running per individual calendars — Drama Institute July 13-31, 2026 confirmed; most application windows closed by late spring; 2026 Summer Session registration was open

reg_2027.opens: winter/early spring, varies by program
reg_2027.notes: Each program (Engineering New Frontiers, Computational Biosciences, Experiences in Architecture, Conway Nursing camp, Drama Institute) has its own application and deadline — check the specific program page in January. Smaller cohorts than GW/Georgetown mean earlier caps but better mentor access.

providers.sessions_2026: Program-specific: 1-week (Engineering New Frontiers, nursing) to 3-week (Experiences in Architecture, Drama Institute July 13-31) sessions
providers.fit_notes: Great for teens testing a specific career (nursing and architecture programs are rare finds locally). Catholic identity is present but programs draw students of all backgrounds.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed copies of summer.catholic.edu (Special Programs + admission pages), drama.catholic.edu and architecture.catholic.edu — program lineup, high-school audience, rising jr/sr target for engineering, and Drama Institute $4,750 / July 13-31 2026 all confirmed from official 2026 pages.

providers.name: CCACC Academy Summer Camp (Chinese immersion & enrichment)
providers.org: Chinese Culture and Community Service Center (CCACC)
providers.description: Rockville's Chinese community center runs a large 9-week summer camp (71 courses across eight learning areas in 2026) for ages 5-13, anchored by Mandarin immersion and Chinese culture alongside academics, arts, and enrichment electives. The most substantial Mandarin summer option in Montgomery County, at community-center prices.
providers.locations: CCACC Academy, 9400 Key West Ave, Rockville MD 20850
providers.price_detail: Course-based enrollment via their shop site: 2026 summer course listings around $679-$739 tuition plus ~$30 fees (course lengths vary); 10% early-bird discount for online registration by March 31, 2026, and an automatic 15% discount when total registered weeks reach 10+
providers.financial_aid: None found (early-bird and 10+ week volume discounts only)
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - August 21, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season running June 22-Aug 21, 2026; course-based enrollment means later-summer blocks often still open — check shop.ccaccacademy.org 2026 Summer Camp category

reg_2027.opens: Early in the year, with early-bird discount deadline March 31
reg_2027.notes: Online first-come via shop.ccaccacademy.org; register before 3/31 for the 10% early-bird discount, and 10+ registered weeks trigger an automatic 15% discount. The course-based model takes some assembling — call to build a full-day Mandarin-heavy schedule.

providers.fit_notes: Great for heritage speakers and beginners alike, and one of the few options running to late August. The course-menu structure suits families who want to customize; less turnkey than a standard camp.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed copies of ccaccacademy.org/summer-camp, shop.ccaccacademy.org 2026 category, and CCACC's official announcement (ccacc-dc.org) — ages 5-13, June 22-Aug 21 2026 dates, 3/31 early-bird, phone/email/address, and 2026 course pricing (~$679-$739 + fees) all confirmed from official sources.

providers.name: CentroNía Summer Camp
providers.description: Columbia Heights' bilingual (Spanish/English) early-education institution runs summer camp/care for preschool and young school-age children at its Columbia Road campus, extending its dual-language, multicultural model through the summer. CentroNía primarily serves working immigrant families and accepts DC childcare subsidy, making it one of few truly affordable bilingual summer options for the youngest kids.
providers.ages: Roughly 3-10 (organization serves ages 0-10; core strength is bilingual early childhood, with school-age out-of-school-time programming)
providers.price_detail: Fees offset by DC child care subsidy vouchers for eligible families; market-rate for others — call for current rates
providers.locations: 1420 Columbia Rd NW, Washington DC 20009 (Columbia Heights); also operates other DC early-learning sites (e.g., Stevens Early Learning Center)
providers.hours: Full-day child-care hours (designed for working parents); Admissions office Mon-Fri 8:30am-5:30pm
providers.financial_aid: Accepts DC subsidy (via OSSE child care voucher); serves predominantly low-income families — apply through CentroNía's Admissions office

reg_2027.opens: Spring for summer; year-round enrollment/waitlist for childcare seats
reg_2027.notes: Enrollment works like childcare admissions, not camp registration — call 202-332-4200 ext. 8100 or email admissions@centronia.org, ask about summer/out-of-school-time (Studio R.O.C.K.S. runs all-day during summer break), and start OSSE subsidy paperwork early since voucher approval takes weeks.

providers.sessions_2026: Full-day bilingual summer programming (early childhood care plus Studio R.O.C.K.S. school-age all-day summer-break program) at Columbia Heights campus, 2026
providers.status_2026: Enrollment-based; out-of-school-time and Studio R.O.C.K.S. summer programming operating in 2026 — mid-summer starts sometimes possible if seats open, call admissions
providers.fit_notes: Best for families with preschoolers/young elementary kids who need full-day bilingual summer care, especially subsidy-eligible families. Details were only verifiable via search-indexed copies, not the live site — confirm everything by phone.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed centronia.org pages — org active, address/phone confirmed, admissions email (admissions@centronia.org, ext 8100) added, and official copy confirms summer out-of-school-time + Studio R.O.C.K.S. all-day summer-break programming; no distinct 2026 'summer camp' page indexed, so upgraded low→medium not higher.

providers.name: Children's Chorus of Washington (year-round + planned summer camp)
providers.description: DC's premier treble choral program: six auditioned ensembles plus two open-entry, no-audition classes for K-3 (Prep and Training Ensemble), performing at major venues around the city. It does not run a 2026 summer camp, but has announced plans for a Northeast DC site and a summer camp beginning in FY2027 - one to watch for 2027 planners; meanwhile fall enrollment/auditions happen in spring and late summer.
providers.ages: K-grade 12 (K-2 register without audition; grades 3-12 by audition)
providers.locations: Two rehearsal campuses: NW and SW Washington DC; NE DC site planned for FY27 (expansion into all four quadrants underway)
providers.price_detail: 2025-26 season tuition from $1,290 (grades K-1) to $1,380 (grades 2-3) plus $50 non-refundable enrollment fee, varying by ensemble (spring-only 2026 session $800); means-tested aid via Federal Financial Assistance Calculator; SING DC Choral Scholars Program gives select 4th-5th graders full tuition, transportation, and uniforms for three consecutive seasons
providers.hours: Weekly rehearsals during school year (not a day camp)
providers.financial_aid: Yes - means-tested aid (needs-based by income/household size) plus full-ride SING DC Choral Scholars Program
providers.sessions_2026: No summer 2026 camp; 2026-27 season enrollment via spring/summer auditions
providers.status_2026: No summer camp exists in 2026. 2025-26 season auditions (grades 3-12, Sept start) and K-2 registration ran per enrollment page; mid-season auditions typically January.

reg_2027.opens: Watch childrenschorusdc.org - first-ever summer camp planned for FY2027 alongside the new NE DC site (per CCW's official Expand Our Reach plan); season auditions run spring/late summer
reg_2027.mechanism: audition (grades 3+); open enrollment for K-2 classes
reg_2027.notes: K-2 classes are open-entry (no audition) - an easy on-ramp. The FY27 summer camp and NE-site launch are stated on CCW's own Expand Our Reach page; details/dates not yet published — confirm directly before counting on it.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who love to sing: K-2 entry is genuinely no-audition, and serious singers grade 3+ get top-tier choral training. Not a summer childcare solution in 2026 - included mainly for 2027 planning.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed childrenschorusdc.org pages (enrollment-info, auditions, expand-our-reach) — grades/audition mechanics, 2025-26 tuition ($1,290-$1,380 + $50 fee, $800 spring), means-tested aid, SING DC Choral Scholars details, two campuses (NW+SW, corrected from NW-only), and FY27 NE-site + first summer camp all confirmed from official site.

providers.name: Children's Science Center Lab Camps
providers.description: Weekly themed STEM camps (Cosmic Creations, Epic Engineering, Superhero Species, Museum Makers) inside Northern Virginia's interactive children's science museum at Fair Oaks Mall, with experienced STEM educators and small groups. A NoVA option worth the drive for elementary kids who love the museum format.
providers.ages: Grades 1-5
providers.locations: Children's Science Center Lab, 11948 Fair Oaks Mall, Fairfax VA
providers.hours: Full-day weekly camps (confirm exact hours with camps@childsci.org)
providers.price_detail: Not verified on official page
providers.financial_aid: Nonprofit with access programs; camp-specific aid not verified
providers.status_2026: 2026 season running (June 22 - Aug 21 themed weeks); July/August weeks were listed — email for current openings

reg_2027.opens: typically late winter
reg_2027.notes: First-come; small group sizes mean favorite weeks cap early. Members often get early or discounted access — worth joining if you plan to register.

providers.sessions_2026: Themed weeks June-August 2026 (e.g., Cosmic Creations June 22-26, Epic Engineering July 6-10, Museum Makers Aug 17-21)
providers.fit_notes: Sweet spot is grades 1-5 museum-lovers. DC/MoCo families should weigh the Fairfax commute against KID Museum, which serves a similar kid closer in.

providers.name: Circle Yoga Summer Camp
providers.description: The DC area's dedicated kids-yoga-and-mindfulness studio (founded 2003 as Budding Yogis, now a cooperative) a block from Chevy Chase Circle, running full-day camp weeks for ages 6-12 that mix yoga, mindfulness, crafts, and outdoor play — explicitly free from competition and pressure. The standout non-competitive movement option in the region: ideal for sensitive, anxious, or simply gentle kids for whom sports camps and performance camps both miss.
providers.ages: 6-12 (full-day camp); kids' yoga classes for younger children during the school year
providers.locations: 3838 Northampton St NW, Washington, DC 20015 (1 block south of Chevy Chase Circle); campers go outside multiple times daily and eat lunch at a nearby park
providers.price_detail: $495/week; single mixed-age group capped at 16 campers per week with lead teacher, camp assistant/coordinator, and teen assistant
providers.financial_aid: Partial scholarships available from a limited budget — contact the studio (stated on official camp page)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions through summer 2026 (specific week list on official page; not fully search-indexed)
providers.status_2026: Operating summer 2026 — camp page live and updated in 2026; third-party 2026 listings report only a handful of spaces left. Call the studio for current-week openings.

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; 2026 enrollment was open by spring
reg_2027.notes: Tiny program (16 kids/week, one group), so favorite weeks fill early; plan to sign up in late winter/spring rather than waiting. 2027 dates/pricing not yet published.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); verified via search-indexed copies of circleyoga.com camp page (dated April 2026), circleyoga.com contact page, and Yelp (updated May 2026): ages 6-12, $495/wk, 9am-3pm, 16-camper cap, partial scholarships, phone/email confirmed. Removed unverified 4200 Davenport St claim; added scholarship info and contacts.

providers.name: City Blossoms Summer Saturdays & Open Times
providers.description: Free, kid-driven creative garden programming at City Blossoms' community green spaces — including the Girard Children's Community Garden (Columbia Heights), the Studio garden (Brightwood Park), and the Farm at Kelly Miller (NE) — with garden art, recipe-making, and open exploration. Summer Saturdays run weekly mornings in July-August alongside multi-day-a-week Open Times April-October. It's neighborhood gardening-as-play rather than a camp.
providers.ages: All ages with caregiver; kid-driven garden programming aimed at young children through elementary
providers.locations: Girard Children's Community Garden (Columbia Heights NW); The Studio garden, 516 Kennedy St NW (Brightwood Park); The Farm at Kelly Miller Youth Garden (NE DC); Marion St Intergenerational Garden
providers.price_detail: Free; drop-in, no RSVP needed for groups of five or fewer
providers.hours: Open Times April-October: Girard Wed + Fri 4-6pm; Farm at Kelly Miller Wed + Fri 3-5pm; Summer Saturdays (when offered) ~10am-12pm at the Studio garden — check calendar
providers.financial_aid: Free program
providers.sessions_2026: Ongoing April-October drop-in Open Times (Girard Wed/Fri 4-6pm, Kelly Miller Wed/Fri 3-5pm); Summer Saturdays series unconfirmed for 2026
providers.status_2026: Organization active with 2026 calendar posted; garden Open Times running now (April-October drop-in). Summer Saturdays series at the Studio garden could not be confirmed for 2026 — indexed event pages are from prior years; check cityblossoms.org/calendar.

reg_2027.opens: No registration — drop-in
reg_2027.notes: Nothing to plan; just show up during posted Open Times (no RSVP for 5 or fewer people). Useful as a free supplement between camp weeks. Groups larger than five should contact the org first.

providers.fit_notes: Not childcare — a caregiver stays. Best for younger kids and families who want a recurring free neighborhood ritual; exact 2026 Summer Saturdays dates were only partially verifiable.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via search-indexed cityblossoms.org pages: org active, Open Times schedules (Girard Wed/Fri 4-6pm, Kelly Miller Wed/Fri 3-5pm, April-Oct, no-RSVP drop-in), Studio garden address 516 Kennedy St NW, phone/email. Summer Saturdays 2026 series not confirmed (only 2022/2023 event pages indexed) — medium confidence.

providers.name: City Kids Wilderness Project (Ranger Program + Broken Arrow Ranch summer)
providers.description: A completely free seven-year outdoor-adventure pathway for DC youth: kids join as 6th graders and progress as a cohort through backpacking, whitewater kayaking, climbing, and summers at the organization's Broken Arrow Ranch in Jackson Hole, Wyoming. Once accepted, families never pay for anything, including travel. One of the most remarkable free opportunities in the city — and correspondingly selective and commitment-heavy.
providers.ages: Entry in 6th grade; cohort continues through high school (roughly 11-18)
providers.price_detail: Free including gear, food, and travel to Wyoming
providers.locations: DC base at 2437 15th St NW (Columbia Heights); summer programming at Broken Arrow Ranch, Jackson, WY
providers.hours: Summer season June 1 - Aug 17, 2026 (multi-week overnight trips by cohort); year-round DC weekend/afterschool activities
providers.financial_aid: N/A — program is 100% free for accepted DC youth; recruitment focuses on under-resourced communities

reg_2027.opens: Applications for the next Ranger class (2026-27) open in fall/winter; join the newsletter to be notified
reg_2027.notes: Apply the year your child is in 5th/6th grade — this is the only entry point. ~130 youth total across all seven years, so seats per class are limited. Email recruitment@citykidsdc.org about school visits and eligibility.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer season June 1 - August 17, 2026 in Jackson, WY (enrolled cohorts only)
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer restricted to already-enrolled Rangers; new-family action item is the fall application for the next class
providers.fit_notes: For 5th/6th grade families ready to commit to a multi-year program and comfortable with a child traveling to Wyoming. Not a sign-up-for-a-week camp; you cannot join mid-pathway.

providers.name: City of Gaithersburg Summer Camps (Camp Discovery, Imagination, Endeavor & specialty camps)
providers.org: City of Gaithersburg Parks, Recreation & Culture
providers.description: Gaithersburg runs a full municipal camp system out of the Activity Center at Bohrer Park: traditional day camps by grade (Camp Imagination and Camp Discovery for younger kids, Camp Endeavor for ages 11-14) plus Summertime Tots/Kindergarten camp and specialty weeks in sports, dance, visual/performing arts, STEM and skateboarding. The signature perk: campers swim at the Water Park at Bohrer Park twice a week, and Camp Endeavor sessions include field trips or SSL projects.
providers.ages: 2-5 (tots/pre-K) through grades 9-12 (teen programs); core day camps roughly K-8, Camp Endeavor 11-14
providers.locations: Activity Center at Bohrer Park, 506 S. Frederick Ave, Gaithersburg, plus other city facilities; swimming at the Water Park at Bohrer Park
providers.hours: One-week sessions starting the fourth week of June; typical day-camp hours with before/after care options (verify per camp)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care offered for core camps — verify per listing on RecXpress
providers.price_detail: Per-week resident/nonresident pricing on RecXpress; 2026 prices not independently verified. Payment plans available for select programs before May 15.
providers.financial_aid: City residents who qualify may apply for financial assistance before registering; payment plans available

reg_2027.opens: mid-February (2026: residents Wed Feb 11, open registration Wed Feb 18)
reg_2027.notes: One-week resident head start matters for the water-park camps and skateboarding weeks. Apply for financial assistance BEFORE registration opens — it must be in place at signup. Teens can also apply for paid junior-counselor and volunteer roles with the camps.

providers.status_2026: Season underway (began fourth week of June); week-by-week availability on RecXpress (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Excellent value for upcounty families — the twice-weekly water-park swimming is the standout. Resident vs nonresident pricing gap is meaningful.

providers.name: City of Takoma Park Camps (Camp Takoma & specialty camps)
providers.org: City of Takoma Park Recreation Department
providers.description: Takoma Park's small, community-flavored recreation camps: flagship Camp Takoma (ages 6-13, 9am-4pm, weekly themes like 'Camp Around the World') plus New Ave Adventure Camp and rotating specialty weeks in basketball, dance, theater and arts. Tiny capacity and a fiercely loyal local following mean it books out almost immediately each February.
providers.ages: 6-13 (Camp Takoma); other camps vary
providers.locations: Takoma Park Community Center (7500 Maple Ave) and city recreation facilities
providers.hours: 9am-4pm Mon-Fri, weekly sessions (Camp Takoma)
providers.extended_care: Historically offered for a fee — verify per listing on the city's ActiveNet
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing on Takoma Park ActiveNet; 2026 prices not independently verified (city camps are typically well below private rates)
providers.financial_aid: Recreation Department scholarships for Takoma Park residents; scholarship awardees got a dedicated early registration window (noon, Feb 11, 2026)

reg_2027.opens: February (2026: scholarship awardees at noon Wed Feb 11, general registration following)
reg_2027.notes: Traditional camp 'fills up fast' per the city itself — treat the February opening like a ticket drop. Residents should apply for scholarships in advance to use the early window. Very small capacity per week.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; at least one Camp Takoma session (July 27-31 'Camp Around the World') is marked FULL. Check ActiveNet for any remaining weeks (verified via search-indexed listing, July 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Beloved, granola, neighborhood-scale camp — ideal for Takoma Park families who want walkable and affordable. Outsiders face both late registration and long odds.

providers.name: CityDance Summer Dance Camps & Intensives
providers.description: The region's biggest nonprofit dance organization runs themed week-long summer dance camps for kids at its Strathmore (North Bethesda) and Rockville studios, plus a Friendship Heights DC option, with age bands starting at 5-7 and 8-10 and running up through the teens, mixing ballet, jazz, hip hop, tap, and crafts. A separate audition-based Conservatory track offers serious pre-professional summer intensives at Strathmore for ages 8-20. Camps suit total beginners; the intensives are for trained dancers.
providers.ages: 5-16 for studio day camps (bands roughly 5-7, 8-10, 11-13/11-17 plus teen weeks); Conservatory summer intensives ages 9-19 by audition (video auditions accepted year-round)
providers.locations: CityDance School & Conservatory at Strathmore, 5301 Tuckerman Ln, North Bethesda (301-581-5204); Friendship Heights DC studio, 5207 Wisconsin Ave NW (202-545-7207); Rockville studio, 11711 Parklawn Dr; Strathmore Square studio, 10511 Strathmore Hall St, North Bethesda; Falls Church studio, 255 West Falls Station Blvd (conservatory intensives)
providers.hours: Camps Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:00pm; Conservatory intensives full-day (schedule varies by program)
providers.price_detail: 2026 camp weeks roughly $395-$450: e.g., POP! Dance Makers Camp ages 8-10 $395, POP! Dance Makers Intensive ages 11-17 $450, Back to Dance camps $415 (per indexed official pages); aftercare extra
