providers.financial_aid: None found for studio camps; CityDance DREAM program provides free training for DC students during the school year — ask about summer aid
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly studio camps June 22-Aug 21, 2026 (e.g., Camp 1 'Superheroes' Jun 22-26, Teen Camp 1 Jun 22-26, Camp 5 'Arctic Explorers' and Camp H 'Musicals & More' Aug 3-7, Camp J 'Dance Stars' Aug 10-14, Camp K 'Musicals & More' Aug 17-21, Dance Team Kick-Off Camp Aug 16-20); Conservatory intensives: Session 1 at Falls Church Jun 22-Jul 10 (closed Jul 3), Senior Studies at Strathmore Square Jun 29-Jul 2, Conservatory Studies Intensive at Falls Church Aug 3-7
providers.status_2026: As of mid-July 2026: video auditions still being accepted for 2026 summer intensives; August camp weeks (both DC and MD studios) still listed as bookable — spots reported going fast

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; 2026 camp registration was open by winter/spring
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online registration for camps; video or in-person audition for Conservatory intensives
reg_2027.notes: Weekly camps keep availability into summer, especially August; popular young-kid weeks go earlier. Intensive placement requires an audition — video auditions are accepted year-round, with the main audition season late fall through spring, so plan the audition video in winter. 2027 dates/prices not yet published.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via many search-indexed citydance.net pages (2026-dated camp listings, locations page, contact page, conservatory intensive pages): ages, 9am-3pm hours, aftercare 3-5pm, $395-450 pricing, named 2026 camp weeks with dates, intensive dates, all studio addresses and phones. Corrected intensive ages (9-19, was 8-20), intensive dates, price detail, and added Falls Church/Strathmore Square locations and contacts.

providers.name: CommuniKids Language Immersion Summer Camp
providers.description: Themed-week immersion camp for the youngest set (2.5-7) in Spanish, French, or Mandarin at four NW DC locations (Tenleytown, two at Cathedral Commons, Glover Park) plus Falls Church — every week a new theme blending outdoor play, art, music, and hands-on learning, all in the target language. One of very few true-immersion options for preschoolers and early elementary kids.
providers.ages: Spanish 2.5-7; French 2.5-6.5; Mandarin 3-6.5
providers.locations: Tenleytown (Spanish), Cathedral Commons x2 (Spanish/French), Glover Park (Spanish/Mandarin) — all NW DC; also Falls Church VA
providers.price_detail: $360-$550 per week depending on location and schedule; balance due May 1
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Seven weeks of camp; register for one week or several
providers.status_2026: Season running; some late-July weeks typically bookable — check communikids.com per location

reg_2027.opens: Winter (rolling; enrolled preschool families get natural priority awareness)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online per location/language; Mandarin and French rooms are smaller and go first. Balance due May 1. This is the on-ramp if your child is too young for WISummer or Rochambeau's older groups.

providers.fit_notes: Strictly for little kids (max ~7) — no experience needed, immersion by osmosis and play. Families wanting a full-day school-age program should look at Isabella & Ferdinand or WISummer.

providers.name: Congressional Camp
providers.description: A classic full-service day camp on Congressional School's 40-acre wooded campus in Falls Church, VA — one of NoVA's institutions, with pools, day camp by age group, 'Great Adventures' travel camps, and specialty camps across 10 summer weeks. Regionally famous enough that many DC and MD families ride its extensive Northern Virginia bus network to get there.
providers.locations: Congressional School, 3229 Sleepy Hollow Rd, Falls Church VA 22042 (main campus); satellite specialty-camp site at Sleepy Hollow Elementary
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates published on congocamp.org/dates-rates (page blocked to bots; specific dollar figures not independently verified). Day Camp and Great Adventures sell as two-week sessions; one-week specialty camps and a short Prequel Week priced separately. Optional evening programs and bus transportation for additional fees.
providers.hours: 9:00am-4:00pm core day
providers.extended_care: Before/after care offered (verify current hours and fees with camp)
providers.financial_aid: None published; camp invites financial aid and discount inquiries via summer@congocamp.org
providers.sessions_2026: 10 weeks: Prequel Week June 15-18; Session 1 (prorated for July 3 closure); Session 2 July 6-17; specialty Session 2B July 13-17 at Sleepy Hollow Elementary; Day Camp and Great Adventures in mandatory two-week sessions; one-week specialty camps
providers.status_2026: Season in progress (10-week 2026 summer underway); mid/late-summer session availability varies — contact the camp office directly

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; historically opens in winter (January)
reg_2027.notes: Two-week session commitment for Day Camp and Great Adventures is unusual for the area — plan your calendar around it. ACA-accredited. Bus cluster stops across NoVA with route maps online; lunch available.

providers.fit_notes: Strong traditional-camp feel with swimming and acreage; two-week minimum sessions and a Virginia location make it a commitment for DC/MD families despite the buses.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via search-indexed congocamp.org pages, ACA Find-a-Camp, and Yelp (June 2026): org operating, ages 4-14, two-week session model, 2026 session dates (Prequel Week 6/15-18, Session 2 7/6-17, Session 2B 7/13-17), ACA accreditation. Corrected phone from 703-533-9711 (school main line) to camp line 703-533-0931. 2026 dollar rates could not be confirmed, so price stays 'varies' and confidence medium.

providers.name: Counselor-in-Training (CIT) Program at ATMTC Academy
providers.description: Teen volunteers help professional staff run one of the region's oldest theatre day camps, managing groups of up to 24 younger campers at historic Glen Echo Park and satellite Montgomery County sites. CITs commit to full camp days (9am-4pm) for a full session, earning 63-70 MCPS SSL hours per 9-10 day session. Thrives: theatre kids aging out of camp who want real responsibility with children while staying in the drama world.
providers.ages: Teen student volunteers (approx. 13-17); exact age/grade cutoff is on the application — not published on indexed pages, so verify with the Academy
providers.price_detail: Volunteer position — no fee; earns MCPS SSL hours (70 hrs per 10-day session, 63 per 9-day session); SSL form signed by the Academy Program Manager after final performances
providers.hours: 9:00am-4:00pm Mon-Fri; must commit to the full camp session, full days
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park (7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD) plus offsite Montgomery County camp locations
providers.sessions_2026: Session-by-session alongside ATMTC summer camps (9- or 10-day sessions), roughly mid-June through August; CITs assist groups of up to 24 campers
providers.status_2026: 2026 CIT applications open (official page states 'Applications are now open for the 2026 CIT Program' per search-indexed copy); mid/late-summer sessions may still have openings — contact the Academy

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; 2026 applications opened in spring alongside camp registration
reg_2027.mechanism: application (documents checked, receipt confirmed by email)
reg_2027.notes: Apply per session; MCPS SSL pre-approved organization. Popular sessions (musical theatre weeks) take CITs fastest — apply when camp registration opens. ATMTC also runs teen performance intensives if your teen wants to be on stage rather than assisting.

providers.financial_aid: N/A — volunteer program
providers.fit_notes: Unpaid and a real full-day commitment — teens who want a paycheck should look at Headfirst or MBSYEP instead. Excellent stepping stone to paid counselor jobs at 16+.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via search-indexed adventuretheatre-mtc.org CIT page and Montgomery County Volunteer Center listing: program active, 2026 applications open, SSL hours (70/63), 9am-4pm full-session commitment, Glen Echo + offsite locations, MCPS SSL pre-approved. Added Academy contact (301-251-5766, registrar@). Exact age/grade requirement not published on indexed pages — medium confidence.

providers.name: CREATE Arts Center Summer Art Camp
providers.description: Downtown Silver Spring's community art center (also known for its art therapy programs) runs a friendly weekly visual-arts camp for ages 6-12, with teens 13-14 attending as counselors-in-training. With before/after care, per-day registration options in early weeks, and mid-range pricing, it is one of the most practical arts camps for working Silver Spring families.
providers.ages: 6-12 campers; 13-14 as counselors-in-training
providers.locations: CREATE Arts Center, downtown Silver Spring MD
providers.price_detail: $430/week in 2026 ($345 for the 4-day June 29 week; $90/day single-day option during Session 1); CIT fee $350/week; beforecare $9/day, aftercare $28/day
providers.hours: Full-day weeks; beforecare and aftercare extend the day
providers.financial_aid: Not verified on camp pages; CREATE has community-access programming — ask the registrar
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 15 through August 21, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season running; 2026 weeks were open for registration into the summer — contact registrar@createartscenter.org (301-588-2787) for remaining late-July/August space

reg_2027.opens: winter (2026 weeks were posted and refundable until May 1, implying early-year opening)
reg_2027.notes: Fills more gently than the Bethesda/Glen Echo giants — a realistic spring registration, and the per-day option is a rare flexibility. Full refunds (minus $50) only until May 1.

providers.fit_notes: A warm, no-pressure art camp with genuine working-parent logistics (before/aftercare, day rates) in walkable downtown Silver Spring, plus a CIT path for young teens. Kids seeking advanced technique or specialty media (wheel ceramics, glass) will want VisArts or Glen Echo instead.

providers.name: Creative Youth Sewing & Fashion Camps
providers.description: A dedicated kids' sewing school on St. Elmo Ave in downtown Bethesda offering week-long summer camps in sewing, fashion design, and crafting — hand stitches, machine skills, fabric layout and cutting, and design illustration, with all skill levels welcome. The go-to close-in Montgomery County answer for a kid who wants to make actual clothes.
providers.ages: camps ages 8-12 (CY Summer Camp); classes serve ages 5-17
providers.locations: 4805 St. Elmo Avenue, Bethesda MD 20814 (Woodmont Triangle)
providers.price_detail: 2026 weekly tuition not published in indexed copies; enrollment and pricing via their Sawyer (hisawyer.com) listings — call 240-800-4916 for rates
providers.hours: Week-long day camps; campers bring a packed lunch
providers.extended_care: None — no before or after care offered (stated on their Sawyer camp listing)
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sewing/fashion/fiber-arts camps through summer 2026, 30+ projects by skill level (booked via Sawyer)
providers.status_2026: 2026 camps listed (Sawyer activity set titled 'CY Summer Camp 2026 (Ages 8-12)' indexed); week-by-week availability not verified — check creative-youth.com/camp

reg_2027.opens: winter/spring (camps posted on their Sawyer page; no publicized single opening date)
reg_2027.notes: Small studio with machine-limited capacity — book favorite weeks by early spring. Registration via hisawyer.com links from creative-youth.com.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for craft-loving 8-12s who want to leave camp with things they actually made and wear. No extended care and a packed-lunch setup means it works best for families with flexible pickup.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via multiple searches of creative-youth.com and hisawyer.com indexed copies: address, phone, email, ages 8-12 camp / 5-17 classes, no before/after care, and a 2026 Sawyer camp listing all confirmed. Pricing not indexed, so medium confidence.

providers.name: Creator Camp (Washington DC)
providers.description: A national chain of short-format (2-3 day) creative-tech camps arriving in the DC market for 2026, with themes like LEGO stop-motion animation, 2D cartoon animation, YouTube content creation, special-effects and Hollywood filmmaking, game design, and music production for ages 6-13. A Summer Pass offers unlimited camps at a steep per-week discount.
providers.ages: 6-13 (official 2026 pages; some directory listings segment 5-7 and 8-14 by theme)
providers.locations: Templeton Academy DC, 406 7th St NW, Washington, DC 20004 (downtown/Penn Quarter)
providers.price_detail: $260-$275 per 3-day camp (2-day tech camps also offered); unlimited Summer Pass marketed 'as low as $150/week' across all locations
providers.hours: 8:30am-3:30pm; 2-day and 3-day camp formats, weekly sessions June-August
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Rolling 2-day and 3-day themed sessions (Lego Animators, YouTube Creators, Roblox, Minecraft, filmmaking, Shark Tank, music production) June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Selling summer 2026 sessions on creatorcamp.org/washington-dc and summer.creatorcamp.org; per-week availability not verified

reg_2027.opens: winter/spring (rolling online sales; Summer Pass promoted early in the year)
reg_2027.notes: Short sessions and rolling sales make this the flexible filler for gap days rather than a plan-in-January camp. Newer market entrant (DC launch was 2025) — check current-year reviews before booking.

providers.fit_notes: Useful for patching odd weeks with fun animation/YouTube/game-design content at a low price point, especially for younger kids. Not a full-week, full-day childcare solution, and its DC operation is new and lightly documented — do your own diligence.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via searches of creatorcamp.org/summer.creatorcamp.org 2026 indexed copies: ages 6-13, $260-275 per 3-day camp, Summer Pass 'as low as $150/week', June-Aug weekly sessions, DC venue Templeton Academy (406 7th St NW). Raised from low to medium; venue/hours came from indexed summaries rather than the live page.

providers.name: Croydon Creek Nature Center Camps (City of Rockville)
providers.org: City of Rockville Recreation and Parks
providers.description: Rockville's own city-run nature camp at a 120-acre forest preserve tucked behind the Civic Center — creek stomping, live animals, hikes and nature crafts for ages 5-14, on a separate (and slightly later) registration calendar from the county's nature centers. A strong plan-B or first choice for Rockville families, with city-resident priority registration.
providers.ages: 5-14, age-banded by week
providers.locations: Croydon Creek Nature Center, 852 Avery Rd, Rockville, MD 20851 (Rockville Civic Center Park)
providers.hours: Varies by camp; typical day-camp hours ~9am-3pm with some half-day preschool/young-child weeks
providers.extended_care: Extended care offered for many Rockville camps — verify per listing in the city camp guide
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing in Rockville's REC1 system; 2026 prices not independently verified. Nonresidents pay a surcharge.
providers.financial_aid: City of Rockville offers financial assistance/scholarships for resident campers (see city camp guide)

reg_2027.opens: early-to-mid February (2026: Tue Feb 10 at 8:30am for Rockville residents, Thu Feb 12 for everyone)
reg_2027.notes: Resident-priority window is the whole game for popular nature weeks — city residents should register Feb opening morning. Non-Rockville families can still get in on general-registration day for less-subscribed weeks. Guide PDF posts in January.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; check the REC1 catalog for live open/full status by week (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Smaller and mellower than the county nature centers; walkable from Rockville Metro-ish neighborhoods. Nonresidents pay more and register later.

providers.name: D.C. United Summer Camps (Powered by Headfirst)
providers.description: The official MLS-club soccer day camps, operated by local powerhouse Headfirst, at fields across DC, Maryland, and Northern Virginia (Catholic University, National Cathedral School, and more). All abilities welcome from rising K through rising 8th grade, with specialty goalkeeper and striker tracks; the Headfirst operation is known for polished logistics and energetic college-age coaches. In 2026 D.C. United also launched an RDS Summer Select program for more competitive players.
providers.ages: Rising K - rising 8th grade (approx. 5-13)
providers.locations: Select sites across DC (incl. Catholic University, National Cathedral School), MD, and NoVA (Mater Dei, Flint Hill School)
providers.price_detail: Headfirst-operated camps typically run roughly $500-600/week; exact 2026 pricing varies by site/week (verify at registration)
providers.hours: 8:45 AM - 2:45 PM Mon-Fri
providers.extended_care: Afternoon Extended Day until 6:00 PM available
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 15 - August 14, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; registration remains open with remaining July and August weeks bookable at many sites (popular NW DC sites sell out first)

reg_2027.opens: Late fall / early winter (Headfirst opens next-summer registration around November-December with early pricing)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online registration by week and site; the most convenient DC locations and late-June weeks fill by spring, but capacity is large — mid-summer weeks often remain available into June.

providers.fit_notes: Great, low-pressure branded soccer camp for beginners through rec players who want a well-run week; competitive club players will want MSI Classic, Bethesda SC, or the new RDS Summer Select instead.

providers.name: Dance Institute of Washington Summer Programs
providers.description: A Columbia Heights institution founded by Fabian Barnes to make serious dance training accessible to Black and brown DC youth. Summer offerings are tiered: 'Summer Sizzler' introduces ages 3-6 to creative movement and ballet readiness in short weekday sessions, while the Summer Training Experience (ages 7-13 and 14-17, full weekdays with breakfast served) challenges serious dancers across ballet, modern, and other genres on a pre-professional track. Best for families who want rigorous training in a culturally affirming, community-rooted environment.
providers.ages: 3-6 (Summer Sizzler); 7-17 for the Summer Training Experience (bands 7-13 and 14-17)
providers.locations: 3400 14th St NW, Washington, DC 20010 (Columbia Heights)
providers.hours: Sizzler: weekday sessions by age band; Summer Training Experience: Mon-Fri 10:00am-3:30pm with breakfast served 9:00-10:00am
providers.price_detail: Summer Training Experience program fees by placement level: Level I $1,100, Level II $1,300, Level III $1,500 (full program, roughly $220-300/wk equivalent); free for dancers enrolled through MBSYEP (Marion Barry Summer Youth Employment Program); Sizzler pricing not published
providers.financial_aid: Dancers enrolled via the Mayor Marion S. Barry Summer Youth Employment Program participate at no cost; DIW has a long scholarship/access tradition — email info@danceinstitute.org for other aid
providers.sessions_2026: Summer Sizzler (ages 3-6); Summer Training Experience for ages 7-13 and 14-17 with mandatory level-placement audition held June 20, 2026, Levels I-III
providers.status_2026: Summer Sizzler registration closed for 2026 (studio directs inquiries to fall programming); Summer Training Experience underway following June 20 placement auditions

reg_2027.opens: spring (program page posted by late spring)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come for Summer Sizzler (ages 3-6); level-placement audition required for all Summer Training Experience registrants
reg_2027.notes: Summer Sizzler closed out before the season in 2026, so register in spring. Older dancers (7-17) must attend the level-placement audition — 2026's was June 20; watch for the equivalent date.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via multiple searches of danceinstituteofwashington.org's live summer-2026 page copies: ages, STE hours (M-F 10-3:30 + 9-10 breakfast), June 20 audition, Level I/II/III fees $1,100/$1,300/$1,500, MBSYEP free enrollment, Sizzler-closed status, address, email; phone (202) 371-9656 from their contact listings.

providers.name: Dance Loft on 14 Youth Summer Programs
providers.org: Dance Loft on 14 (MOVEius Contemporary Ballet dba Dance Loft on 14)
providers.description: A community dance hub on upper 14th Street NW running standalone weekly camps: a gentle creative-movement/ballet camp with crafts and games for ages 3-6 (9am-3:30pm), plus tiered Junior, Intermediate, and Advanced full-day tracks for older dancers that add pointe, modern, variations, dance history, and yoga/Pilates as levels rise. A flexible, unfussy option — enroll one week or several — with real aftercare, which is rare among DC dance camps.
providers.ages: 3-6 for the young-dancer camp; Junior/Intermediate/Advanced tracks for roughly 7-18 by level
providers.locations: 4618 14th St NW, Loft Level, Washington, DC 20011 (upper 14th St corridor); satellite youth classes at Fort Totten
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:30pm
providers.price_detail: $600/week; full and partial scholarships available — studio states cost should never be a barrier
providers.financial_aid: Full and partial scholarships available (stated on 2026 youth summer programs page)
providers.sessions_2026: Standalone weekly sessions through summer 2026 (ages 3-6 camp plus Junior/Intermediate/Advanced tracks)
providers.status_2026: 2026 Youth Summer Programs page live with weekly enrollment; late-July/August weeks likely still bookable as of mid-July 2026 — confirm on site

reg_2027.opens: winter/early spring
reg_2027.notes: Each week stands alone, so families can grab single weeks late; the small ages-3-6 camp fills faster than the older tracks. Scholarship requests worth making early.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via multiple searches of danceloft14.org 2026 page copies: ages, M-F 9-3:30 hours, 3:30-5:30 aftercare, $600/wk tuition with full/partial scholarships, standalone-week format, address, phone (202) 621-3670, emails info@/education@danceloft14.org. Price band set to $$$ ($600/wk); financial aid corrected from 'none found' to scholarships available.

providers.name: Day Eight Middle School Summer Writing Camp
providers.description: A FREE five-week creative-writing day camp for rising 6th-9th graders at the Shepherd Park Library (NW DC), funded by Learn24 and the Friends of the Shepherd Park Library. Each day starts with poem recitation and study of model poems by working writers; campers' poems are published in a real book at summer's end. Curriculum by poets Robert Bettmann and Regie Cabico with guest writers-in-residence - capped at just 17 campers per week.
providers.ages: 6th-9th grade youth (roughly 11-15)
providers.locations: Shepherd Park / Juanita Thornton Neighborhood Library, 7420 Georgia Ave NW, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Free for DC residents (Learn24, Friends of the Shepherd Park Library, and individual donor funding); each youth may enroll for 1 or 2 weeks, with waitlist for additional weeks
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 10:00am-4:00pm; Thursday-morning off-site field trip each week; Friday late-afternoon camper reading open to families
providers.financial_aid: Free outright for DC residents
providers.sessions_2026: Five week-long sessions: Jun 29-Jul 2, Jul 6-10, Jul 13-17, Jul 20-24, Jul 27-31, 2026 (max 17 campers/week; enroll 1-2 weeks per camper)
providers.status_2026: In session now (runs through Jul 31; closed Fri Jul 3); with the 17-camper weekly cap, late-July weeks may be full — contact Day Eight about remaining space

reg_2027.opens: spring (2026 registration was announced open in spring)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come, capped at 2 weeks per camper (waitlist for more)
reg_2027.notes: Free + 17-camper weekly cap = fills quickly once word spreads. Watch dayeight.org and the Learn24 listings from March; register the week it opens. DC residency required for the free rate.

providers.fit_notes: A standout for word-loving middle schoolers - real published output, professional poet instructors, zero cost. Kids who hate writing will not enjoy a full day of it. Site blocked from direct fetch; verified via search-indexed copies of dayeight.org.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via multiple searches of dayeight.org's 2026 registration page copies: free for DC residents, five exact 2026 session weeks, 17-camper cap, 1-2 week enrollment rule, grades 6-9, Shepherd Park/Juanita Thornton Library location. Corrected hours from 9:30-4:30 to the stated 10am-4pm.

providers.name: DC Boys State (American Legion)
providers.org: American Legion Department of the District of Columbia
providers.description: The District runs its own American Legion Boys State (with a companion Girls State track) — the same build-a-government leadership week as the famous state programs, scaled to DC and held in the city rather than on a distant campus (2026: June 27-July 2 at the Yunus Emre Institute Turkish Cultural Center). DC high schoolers are ineligible for Maryland's program, so this is their route to the Boys/Girls Nation pipeline. Smaller and lower-profile than Maryland's, which can mean better selection odds for motivated students.
providers.ages: High school juniors (rising seniors, approx. 16-17); selective nomination
providers.locations: 2026 session: Yunus Emre Institute Turkish Cultural Center, Washington, DC; DC Legion HQ at Northeast Presbyterian Church, 2112 Varnum St NE
providers.price_detail: Free to selected students - Boys State programs are sponsored by American Legion posts and community partners; DC fee page not published but national materials confirm the sponsored model
providers.financial_aid: n/a - sponsored program; participants also become eligible for Samsung American Legion Scholarship
providers.sessions_2026: One residential week, June 27-July 2, 2026 (confirmed on legion.org national Boys State sites-and-dates page)
providers.status_2026: 2026 session concluded (June 27-July 2, 2026) - registration for 2026 is over.

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; applications typically accepted winter-spring through the DC Legion / a sponsoring local post ahead of the late-June week
reg_2027.notes: Nomination/application via the DC Legion or a local American Legion post; national eligibility is completion of junior year. Small, selective program - contact the DC department early in junior year. No announced 2027 dates.

providers.fit_notes: The DC-resident counterpart to Maryland Boys/Girls State. VERIFICATION FLAG: verified only via search-indexed copies of dclegion.com/dcboysstate.com; cost and exact 2027 timeline unconfirmed — treat details as provisional.
providers.hours: Residential, full week (overnight civic-leadership program)
providers.verification_note: Boys State confirmed: legion.org official sites/dates page lists DC Boys State at Yunus Emre Institute, Washington DC, June 27-July 2, 2026; dcboysstate.com and dclegion.com/boys-state are live (indexed; direct fetch blocked 403). Could NOT confirm a separate 2026 DC Girls State program via American Legion Auxiliary sources, so renamed entry to Boys State only; families seeking Girls State should contact the ALA Department of DC via legion-aux.org.

providers.name: DC FEMS Fire Action (MBSYEP youth summer program)
providers.description: Part summer camp, part paid summer job: about two dozen DC youth ages 16-20 spend the summer learning firefighting and EMS skills with DC FEMS through the Marion Barry Summer Youth Employment Program. It feeds into the department's full Fire Cadet Program (ages 18-21), which pays cadets while they earn EMT and Firefighter I & II certifications — a genuine career pipeline for DC residents.
providers.ages: 16-20 (Fire Action summer program); 18-21 (year-round Fire Cadet Program)
providers.price_detail: Free and paid - participants earn MBSYEP wages; part summer camp, part summer job introducing DC youth to fire/EMS careers
providers.locations: DC Fire and EMS training facilities, Washington DC; Recruitment Office 2000 14th St NE
providers.hours: Full summer weekday schedule during the six-week MBSYEP window
providers.financial_aid: n/a - participants are paid

reg_2027.opens: 2027 MBSYEP dates not yet announced; the 2026 application opened in winter and closed March 6, 2026, so expect a late-January-to-early-March 2027 window
reg_2027.notes: Apply through summerjobs.dc.gov (MBSYEP, DC residents 14-24) and seek the DC FEMS Fire Action placement; small cohort, so express interest early and follow up with FEMS recruitment (Lt. Keith Hicks, 202-673-3330). DC residency required.

providers.sessions_2026: Six-week MBSYEP program, June 29-August 7, 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 cohort running now within MBSYEP (June 29-Aug 7, 2026); the MBSYEP application closed March 6, 2026 - no new entry this summer
providers.fit_notes: For DC teens 16+ seriously curious about fire/EMS careers — physical, hands-on, and paid. Not open to suburban MD/VA residents.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via NBC Washington coverage (Fire Action serves DC youth 16-20, part of MBSYEP), fems.dc.gov cadet page (indexed; 18-21 cadet ages, recruitment contact), and summerjobs.dc.gov (2026 application closed, program June 29-Aug 7). FEMS main phone (202) 673-3320 confirmed on fems.dc.gov. No dedicated Fire Action page exists on fems.dc.gov, hence medium confidence; direct fetches blocked (403).

providers.name: DC Fencers Club Summer Camps
providers.description: A dedicated fencing center in Silver Spring's Forest Glen neighborhood running seven straight weeks of beginner-through-advanced day camps: footwork, technique, group lessons, games, and bouting alongside nationally ranked fencers, with all gear provided. It's the region's most accessible on-ramp into a niche Olympic sport — no experience or equipment needed, and before/aftercare is offered.
providers.ages: 7-16, beginner through advanced
providers.locations: 9330 Fraser Ave, Silver Spring MD (Forest Glen)
providers.price_detail: Weekly full-day camp typically ~$450-550/week incl. equipment (verify current rates); before/aftercare extra
providers.hours: 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM Mon-Fri
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.status_2026: Camps in session; multiple late-July and August weeks typically still open mid-summer

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring (schedule posted early in the year)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online; a niche-sport camp that fills steadily but rarely instantly — a realistic late-spring booking. Gear is provided, which removes the biggest barrier to trying fencing.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for curious kids (especially ones who love Star Wars/swashbuckling) and for serious youth fencers needing summer training; kids who hate structure and drilling may find the sport's discipline demanding.

providers.name: DC Public Library Discover Summer - Kids
providers.description: DCPL's free, all-summer reading-and-learning celebration (June 1-Aug 31): kids pick up a Bingo Board at any neighborhood library (or track in Beanstack), complete reading and activity tasks, and earn giveaways plus two Washington Nationals tickets for finishing. Branches layer on free storytimes, STEM programs and events all summer - the 2026 theme ties into America 250. Not childcare, but the best free enrichment scaffold in the city.
providers.ages: 0-12 (kids track; all-ages challenge)
providers.locations: All 26 DC Public Library locations plus online via Beanstack
providers.price_detail: Completely free; prize is a pack of two Nationals tickets plus giveaways while supplies last
providers.hours: Library open hours; events scheduled throughout the day
providers.extended_care: n/a - drop-in, parent/caregiver accompanies younger kids
providers.financial_aid: Free
providers.sessions_2026: June 1 - August 31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Running now - join anytime through August 31; no registration barrier

reg_2027.opens: June 1 (annually)
reg_2027.notes: No lottery, no signup crunch - grab a Bingo Board at any branch or register on Beanstack from June 1. Nothing to plan in advance; it never fills.

providers.fit_notes: For every family - zero cost, zero competition, works alongside any camp schedule. It is a challenge program plus event calendar, not supervised care.

providers.name: DC Public Library Discover Summer - Teens (Know Your Power)
providers.description: The teen arm of DCPL's free summer program: creative workshops in music, visual arts, writing and digital media at branches citywide, anchored by the Know Your Power Teen Art Contest (ages 13-18) where teens create poetry, art, music or multimedia on civic issues and can win laptops, cameras, guitars and graphics tablets. Free access to working artists and real prizes.
providers.ages: 13-18 (some workshops 10+)
providers.locations: DC Public Library branches citywide incl. MLK Central Library
providers.price_detail: Free, including workshops with professional artists; contest prizes include laptop, digital camera, guitar, graphics tablet
providers.hours: Scheduled workshop events, typically afternoons; see branch event calendar
providers.financial_aid: Free
providers.sessions_2026: June 1 - August 31; Know Your Power contest submissions through August 31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Active now - teens can still join workshops and enter the contest through Aug 31

reg_2027.opens: June 1 (annually)
reg_2027.notes: No advance registration needed; individual workshops may ask for event signup on the DCPL events calendar. Contest deadline is end of August - start projects early summer.

providers.fit_notes: For creative teens who would never enroll in a 'camp' - a free on-ramp to art, music production and media with legitimate prize incentives. Not supervised all-day programming.

providers.name: DC Sail Kids Set Sail
providers.description: Community-sailing summer camp on the Anacostia at Diamond Teague Park (Navy Yard/Capitol Riverfront), where beginners ages 7-15 crew 18-foot Flying Scots with an instructor aboard and intermediates (12-15) move to double-handed FJs and RS Fevas with racing and navigation. Eight one-week sessions run all summer. As the city's nonprofit sailing school it's notably mission-driven, with scholarship support.
providers.ages: 7-15 (beginner/novice, on 18-ft Flying Scots with instructor aboard); 12-15 (intermediate/advanced, requires 3 prior KSS sessions or equivalent experience)
providers.locations: Diamond Teague Park Piers, 1st & Potomac Ave SE (Capitol Riverfront), Washington DC; office at The Wharf (650 Wharf St SW)
providers.price_detail: Approximately $425-450 per one-week session (camp-directory listings show $425/week; DC Sail's own materials price a camp day at $90); exact fee shown on each session's registration page
providers.hours: Approx. 9:30am-4pm; no extended care
providers.financial_aid: Youth scholarships are a core program - DC Sail fundraises specifically to send kids to KSS; ask about scholarship applications when registering
providers.sessions_2026: 8 one-week sessions: weeks of June 22, June 29, July 6, July 13, July 20, July 27, Aug 3, Aug 10, 2026
providers.status_2026: Mid-season as of July 12; Session 5 (week of July 20) through Session 8 (week of Aug 10) pages are live on dcsail.org - call 202-547-1250 for space

reg_2027.opens: Winter (sessions typically posted January-February on dcsail.org)
reg_2027.notes: First-come per-session online registration on dcsail.org; beginner weeks with younger kids fill first but it is less of a frenzy than the big suburban camps. Ask early about scholarships.

providers.fit_notes: Genuine on-the-water instruction in the middle of the city; kids should be comfortable around water (swim test standard). Metro-accessible (Navy Yard) — one of the easiest adventure camps for car-free families.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via dcsail.org indexed content (direct fetch blocked 403): ages 7-15/12-15 with 3-session prerequisite, 8 one-week 2026 sessions with dates, live Session 5 and Session 8 event pages, phone 202-547-1250; $425/week from current camp-directory listing plus DC Sail's $90/day figure supports the $$ band.

providers.name: DCYOP Summer Intensive (Premiere Youth Ensemble)
providers.description: The storied, access-focused DC Youth Orchestra Program (founded 1960, famous for training generations of DC musicians affordably) runs small summer intensives where string, wind, brass, and percussion students work daily with DCYOP faculty and conductors on repertoire and ensemble skills. Each week is capped at just 12 students, so it's closer to a chamber intensive than a big camp; the year-round program is the real pipeline and is one of the region's best music bargains.
providers.ages: Roughly 8-18; beginner through advanced instrumentalists (summer intensive targets students who already play)
providers.locations: DCYOP program sites in DC (historically Eastern High School area / Takoma; verify current summer site with DCYOP)
providers.price_detail: $450/week; $50 non-refundable deposit at registration; early drop-off (8am) +$50/week; tuition assistance available
providers.hours: 9:30am-3:30pm; optional 8:00am early drop-off
providers.extended_care: Early drop-off from 8am for $50/week; no late aftercare found
providers.financial_aid: Yes - tuition assistance program; DCYOP also lends instruments (first-come, subject to inventory)
providers.sessions_2026: July 20-24 and July 27-31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Both 2026 sessions marked PROGRAM FULL. Registration ran until April 30 with only 12 seats per session.

reg_2027.opens: Late winter/early spring; 2026 registration closed April 30
reg_2027.notes: First-come with $50 deposit and a hard April 30 deadline - with 12 seats per week it fills well before the deadline. For year-round DCYOP (the better-known program), fall registration opens in late summer.

providers.fit_notes: Best for kids already playing an orchestral instrument who want serious, affordable coaching in a tiny cohort. Complete beginners should look at DCYOP's year-round beginner classes instead of the summer intensive.

providers.name: Docs In Progress Youth Filmmaking Camps
providers.description: A documentary filmmaking nonprofit a block from the Silver Spring Metro that runs one- and two-week summer camps where kids work in small crews to plan, shoot, and edit real short documentaries around downtown Silver Spring, finishing with a mini film festival for families (films posted to YouTube). Cameras, editing gear, and training are all provided — the area's best entry point into nonfiction filmmaking.
providers.ages: Grades 4-6, grades 7-8, and ages 13-17 in separate sessions
providers.locations: 8560 Second Ave, Silver Spring MD (1 block from Silver Spring Metro)
providers.price_detail: Current tuition not published in indexed copy (older listings ranged ~$375-575/week); exact price shown on each Eventbrite registration page - confirm at docsinprogress.org/youth
providers.hours: 9:30am-4pm
providers.financial_aid: Scholarship application offered (reopens each spring)
providers.sessions_2026: Grades 7-8: July 13-17, 2026; Grades 4-6: July 20-24, 2026; ages 13-17 one-week and two-week workshops in late July-August (9:30am-4pm)
providers.status_2026: Active for 2026: Grades 7-8 week starts July 13; Grades 4-6 week July 20-24 confirmed; teen (13-17) late-July/August sessions listed - check remaining space via Eventbrite

reg_2027.opens: Late winter/early spring (sessions posted on docsinprogress.org and ticketed via Eventbrite)
reg_2027.notes: Small cohorts but a niche audience - fills moderately; August teen weeks often have space into early summer. Scholarship application opens in spring; apply before enrolling if needed.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for curious, story-minded kids and teens — you don't need any filmmaking experience, and the documentary (not Hollywood) angle suits interview-loving extroverts and thoughtful observers alike. Metro-accessible, which is gold for NW DC/Takoma families.
providers.verification_note: Org active and 2026 sessions confirmed via docsinprogress.org/youth indexed copy plus CultureSpot MC and Downtown Silver Spring listings (grades 7-8 July 13-17 and grades 4-6 July 20-24, 2026, 9:30am-4pm); direct fetches blocked (403). Tuition and exact teen-session dates not confirmable from indexed official copy, so price stays 'varies' and confidence medium.

providers.name: DOEE Green Zone Environmental Program (GZEP)
providers.org: DC Department of Energy & Environment (DOEE), via MBSYEP
providers.description: The environmental track inside DC's summer-jobs program: up to 250 youth ages 14-24 get PAID to learn about climate, energy and watershed protection and spend three days a week in the field building rain gardens, marking storm drains and restoring green space with partners like Anacostia Watershed Society, Casey Trees and the Student Conservation Association. Camp-like outdoor immersion, with a paycheck.
providers.ages: 14-24 (DC residents, via MBSYEP; up to 250 participants)
providers.locations: Classroom base (incl. UDC) plus community environmental work sites across DC watersheds
providers.price_detail: Free and paid - participants earn MBSYEP wages while learning about energy, water, wildlife, and natural-resource issues and building rain gardens and other projects
providers.hours: 9am-4:30pm program days during the six-week MBSYEP window (per UDC 2026 GZEP calendar entries)
providers.financial_aid: n/a - paid program
providers.sessions_2026: Runs with MBSYEP June 29-August 7, 2026
providers.status_2026: Running now with the 2026 MBSYEP cohort (UDC calendar shows GZEP days in July 2026, 9am-4:30pm); entry closed with the March 6, 2026 MBSYEP deadline

reg_2027.opens: 2027 MBSYEP application dates not yet announced; the 2026 application closed March 6, 2026, so expect a late-January-to-early-March 2027 window at summerjobs.dc.gov
reg_2027.notes: Apply to MBSYEP when it opens and indicate interest in DOEE/GZEP placement; capacity is up to 250 so the environmental track can fill even though SYEP overall does not. Follow @DOEE.GZEP on Facebook for placement announcements.

providers.fit_notes: Best fit for outdoorsy teens 14+ who want conservation work and a paycheck instead of a camp. Verified via search-indexed doee.dc.gov copies and UDC event calendar.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via doee.dc.gov/summer indexed copy (up to 250 youth ages 14-24, MBSYEP partnership, watershed curriculum), UDC 25Live calendar entries for July 2026 GZEP days 9am-4:30pm, and summerjobs.dc.gov (2026 application closed March 6; program June 29-Aug 7). Direct dc.gov fetches blocked (403); verified via indexed official pages.

providers.name: DPR Aqua Day Camp
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: A swimming-centered DPR day camp at rec centers with pools, teaching breathing technique, stroke mechanics and water-safety awareness, with each session ending in a camper swim meet. One of the cheapest ways in the region for a kid to get near-daily swim instruction all summer.
providers.ages: 6-13 (grouped, e.g. 6-10 and 11-13 sections; swim instruction, stroke mechanics, water safety, closing swim meet)
providers.locations: DPR aquatic facilities citywide (e.g. Deanwood and other indoor-pool rec centers)
providers.price_detail: DPR 2026 camp fees run $135-$325 per two-week session for DC residents depending on camp (~$70-165/week); exact Aqua Day Camp rate in the 2026 DPR Summer Camp Fees PDF on dpr.dc.gov. 10% non-refundable processing fee; 50%/75% reduced rates for income-qualified residents
providers.hours: Standard DPR camp day (~9am-5pm)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care offered at DPR camp sites where available for a small per-session fee
providers.financial_aid: 50%/75% reduced rates for income-qualified DC residents (reduced-rate application was due Feb 2, 2026 for camp registration)
providers.sessions_2026: Four two-week sessions: Jun 22-Jul 2, Jul 6-17, Jul 20-31, Aug 3-14, 2026 (Session 1 one day short for July 4); registration via the Feb 2-16, 2026 lottery
providers.status_2026: Lottery over; as of July 12 Session 2 (Jul 6-17) is underway and Sessions 3 (Jul 20-31) and 4 (Aug 3-14) remain - leftover spots first-come on the DPR WebTrac portal, where activity pages show live availability; pool camps fill very fast

reg_2027.opens: Early February, if 2027 follows the 2026 pattern (2026 lottery ran Feb 2-16; account verification and reduced-rate applications were due by opening day); 2027 dates not yet announced
reg_2027.notes: Aqua Day Camp goes through the same February camp lottery as DPR's other camps - no cost to enter; selected families accept and pay via their DPR account. Separately, DPR's other summer aquatics programs (learn-to-swim, swim league) opened first-come May 12-13, 2026 at noon and clear fast.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who want to be in the water every day and parents who want real swim-safety skills, not just pool playtime. Verified via search-indexed dc.gov pages and live WebTrac activity listings; exact fees unconfirmed.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via dpr.dc.gov indexed copy and mayoral press release (ages 6-13, lottery Feb 2-16 2026, 50%/75% reduced rates, four session dates), live WebTrac listing for Aqua Day Camp Deanwood ages 11-13 Session 2, and the DC Auditor report citing $135-$325 per two-week session; direct dc.gov fetches blocked (403). Added Aquatics Office phone from official page copy.

providers.name: DPR Boost Specialty Camps
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: DPR's tween track: two-week specialty enrichment camps with an academic component, with 2026 offerings including Rocket Camp, Robotics Camp, Esports Camp, Journalism Camp, Improv Camp and CSI (forensics), plus daily physical activity like swimming and group fitness. A rare chance for middle-schoolers to try STEM, media or performance camps at parks-department prices instead of private-camp prices.
providers.ages: 11-13 (tweens; the 2026 DPR camp lottery runs two age brackets, 3-10 and 11-13, and Boost sits in the 11-13 bracket)
providers.locations: Selected DPR recreation centers citywide; 2026 themes include Rocket, Robotics, Esports, Journalism, Improv and CSI camps
providers.price_detail: Exact 2026 Boost fee is published only in DPR's '2026 DC DPR Summer Camp Fees' PDF (resident / 50% reduced / 75% reduced / non-resident columns), which blocks automated access; DPR resident camp fees are per two-week session and low (official Discovery Camp page lists $110/session), so Boost lands well under $250/week. 10% non-refundable processing fee; before/after care $20/session extra.
providers.hours: 9am-5pm camp day (2026 DPR FAQ: all camps run 9-5); before care from 8am and after care to 6pm for an extra fee and separate registration
providers.extended_care: Before care 8-9am / after care 5-6pm, $20 per session, registered separately (not covered by reduced rates)
providers.financial_aid: 50% or 75% reduced rates for income-qualified DC residents on all 2026 DPR programs; reduced-rate application was due Feb 2, 2026 (before the lottery)
providers.sessions_2026: Four two-week sessions: Jun 22-Jul 2, Jul 6-17, Jul 20-31, Aug 3-14 (whether every Boost theme runs all four sessions varies by site - confirm on the portal; prior years Boost ran longer continuous blocks)
providers.status_2026: Lottery closed Feb 16; accept/pay ended Mar 9 and waitlists are running. Check dprsummercamp.com for first-come leftovers in Session 3 (Jul 20-31) and Session 4 (Aug 3-14) - specialty themes are usually waitlisted

reg_2027.opens: early February expected (2027 not yet announced; 2026 lottery ran Feb 2-16)
reg_2027.notes: 2026 was the FIRST year Boost camps were folded into the main DPR February lottery (previously separate registration). In 2026: lottery Feb 2-16, results emailed Feb 17, Accept-and-Pay window Feb 17-Mar 9, waitlists began Mar 9; families rank up to four camp choices per child per session. Verify DC residency in your DPR account and apply for reduced rates before the window opens. Popular specialties (Robotics, Esports) fill first.

providers.fit_notes: Best for tweens aging out of general day camp who want a themed camp without a $500/week price tag. Not a deep-dive academy - it is still a rec-center camp with an enrichment theme. Verified via search-indexed dc.gov copies and DPR's Boost splash page.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via dpr.dc.gov/mayor.dc.gov 2026 press releases and FAQ (dpr.dc.gov blocks direct fetch; verified through indexed official-domain copies): Boost = ages 11-13, in the Feb 2-16 2026 lottery for the first time, themes list, session dates, 9-5 hours, reduced rates. Exact Boost fee (fees PDF) and per-theme session structure not directly readable, hence medium.

providers.name: DPR Discovery Camp
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: The backbone of DC's public summer-camp system: theme-based day camp at recreation centers citywide with morning meetings, arts and crafts, sports, field explorations, daily reading time (DEAR), and choice activities. At $135 per two-week session for DC residents it is dramatically cheaper than private camps, which is why the February lottery is one of the most competitive tickets in DC parenting.
providers.ages: 6-10 (rising grades 1-5 roughly)
providers.locations: Dozens of DPR recreation centers across all 8 wards
providers.price_detail: $135 per ~2-week session for DC residents (under $70/week); 50%/75% reduced rates for income-qualified residents; non-residents pay a higher rate. Before/after care $20 per session extra. (Older sources cite $110 - $135 is the figure on DPR's current pages.)
providers.hours: Roughly 9am-5pm; before-care 8-9am, after-care 5-6pm ($20/session, guaranteed if enrolled in Discovery)
providers.extended_care: Yes - before-care 8-9am and after-care 5-6pm, $20 per session; reduced rates do NOT apply to B&A fees
providers.financial_aid: 50% or 75% reduced rate for income-qualified DC residents; apply before the lottery opens (Feb 2 deadline in 2026)
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: Jun 22-Jul 2 (one day short for July 4), Session 2: Jul 6-17, Session 3: Jul 20-31, Session 4: Aug 3-14
providers.status_2026: Lottery over; Sessions 3 and 4 (Jul 20-Aug 14) still ahead. Unfilled spots are first-come-first-served on the DPR registration portal until filled; popular sites are waitlisted and waitlist offers do move as families decline (DPR makes 3 contact attempts, payment due within 2 business days)

reg_2027.opens: early February (2026: applications Feb 2-16, results announced Feb 17)
reg_2027.notes: All applications submitted during the window are weighted equally - no first-come advantage. Do the prep in January: create/verify your DPR account, confirm DC residency, and submit reduced-rate application before the window opens (both due by day one of the lottery in 2026). Results are emailed the day after the lottery closes. If not selected you land on a waitlist; remaining spots open rolling/first-come afterward. A DC Auditor report (Aug 2025) found the lottery made access more equitable than the old first-come scramble.

providers.fit_notes: The default DC-government camp for elementary kids - social, active, neighborhood-based, unbeatable price. Not for kids who need a specialized curriculum. Discovery Inclusion track supports campers with disabilities inside regular Discovery sites. Official dc.gov pages verified via search-indexed copies only.

providers.name: DPR Little Explorers Camp
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: DC Parks and Rec's gentle first-camp experience for preschoolers at recreation centers across the city, with a theme-based curriculum, arts and crafts, games, daily reading time and a 1:4-1:5 staff-to-child ratio led by early-childhood staff. Ideal for potty-trained 3-5 year olds who have never done camp before. One of the cheapest full-day options for this age anywhere in the region.
providers.ages: 3-5 (pre-K; must be potty-trained); theme-based curriculum with 1:4 to 1:5 staff-to-child ratio. Separate DPR Therapeutic Recreation camps serve children with disabilities
providers.locations: DPR recreation centers across all wards of DC
providers.price_detail: Charged per full two-week session (you pay the whole session regardless of days attended). Exact 2026 Little Explorers fee is in DPR's 2026 fee PDF (blocks automated access); official Discovery Camp page lists $110/session resident as a comparator, so this is well under $250/wk. 10% non-refundable processing fee; non-residents pay a higher rate; before/after care $20/session extra.
providers.hours: 9am-5pm camp day (2026 DPR FAQ); before care from 8am, after care to 6pm for an extra fee and separate registration - Little Explorers enrollees are guaranteed a before/after-care space
providers.extended_care: Yes - before care 8-9am, after care 5-6pm, $20 per session, separate registration (not covered by reduced rates); guaranteed for Little Explorers enrollees
providers.financial_aid: 50% or 75% reduced rates for income-qualified DC residents on all 2026 DPR programs; reduced-rate application was due Feb 2, 2026, before the lottery
providers.sessions_2026: Four sessions: Jun 22-Jul 2, Jul 6-17, Jul 20-31, Aug 3-14
providers.status_2026: Lottery long closed (Feb 16) and accept/pay ended Mar 9; Sessions 3 (Jul 20-31) and 4 (Aug 3-14) still ahead - remaining slots are first-come on dprsummercamp.com until full; most sites waitlisted (join via the '+' on the activity)

reg_2027.opens: early February expected (2027 not yet announced; 2026 lottery ran Feb 2-16)
reg_2027.notes: Random equal-weight lottery (applying day one gives no advantage); two age brackets (3-10 and 11-13), up to four camp choices per child per session. In 2026: results emailed Feb 17, Accept-and-Pay Feb 17-Mar 9, waitlists began Mar 9, leftovers then first-come. CRITICAL prep before the window: verify DC residency in your DPR account and submit the reduced-rate application (both due Feb 2 in 2026). Non-residents may enter but pay more with no reduced rate.

providers.fit_notes: For families wanting affordable, low-pressure, neighborhood-based care for a preschooler - not a specialty enrichment camp. Verified via official DPR pages and press releases as indexed by search (dc.gov blocks direct fetch).
providers.verification_note: Confirmed against dpr.dc.gov Little Explorers page, 2026 Summer Camps page, FAQ, and Feb 2026 mayoral press release (via indexed official copies; site blocks direct fetch): ages 3-5, ratios, four 2026 sessions, Feb 2-16 lottery / Feb 17 results / Mar 9 waitlists, 9-5 hours, $20 B&A with guaranteed space, 50%/75% reduced rates, camps office phone/email. Exact session fee locked in a blocked PDF; band $ is certain.

providers.name: DPR Summer Aquatics Programs (Learn-to-Swim, Summer Swim League, Free Pools)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: Beyond camps, DPR runs a full free-to-cheap summer aquatics slate: learn-to-swim lessons, a citywide youth summer swim league, youth water polo, and dozens of free outdoor pools and spray parks open to residents all summer. This is how DC kids swim all summer for essentially nothing.
providers.ages: all ages (learn-to-swim all ages; summer swim league and youth water polo roughly 5-18; senior swim team for older adults)
providers.locations: DPR indoor and outdoor pools in every ward; spray parks citywide (find sites at dpr.dc.gov/findapool)
providers.price_detail: All DPR pools and aquatics programs are free for DC residents (create a free DPR account/membership; non-residents need a paid aquatics pass). Spray parks free, no registration. Program slots are free but capacity-limited.
providers.hours: Spray parks daily 10am-6pm. Outdoor pools: weekends-only before Jun 21, then individual six-day-a-week summer schedules from Jun 22 (children's pools closed Mon-Tue); rolling closures begin Aug 2, last pools close Labor Day Sep 7, 2026. Lessons/league typically weekday morning/evening blocks
providers.financial_aid: Not needed - aquatic facilities and programs are free to all DC residents
providers.sessions_2026: Programs run late June-August; registration opened Tue May 12, 2026 at 12pm (virtual waiting room); outdoor pool season runs through Labor Day Sep 7 at the last sites
providers.status_2026: Pools and spray parks open now and free for DC residents. Structured programs (learn-to-swim, summer swim league, youth water polo, senior swim) opened May 12 first-come and are largely full - check the DPR registration portal for openings. Outdoor pools begin rolling closures Aug 2

reg_2027.opens: mid-May expected (2027 not announced; 2026 aquatics registration opened Tue May 12 at 12pm, all other summer programs Wed May 13 at 12pm)
reg_2027.notes: First-come with a virtual waiting room on opening day - swim lessons are among DPR's fastest-selling items; create/update your DPR account beforehand at dpr.dc.gov/DPRaccount and be logged in before noon. Free pool and spray-park access needs no registration (residents show free DPR membership). Full program list at DPRprograms.com.

providers.fit_notes: Not a camp - listed because free pools plus cheap lessons are the free-public backbone of a DC kid summer, especially for families patching together care. Verified via search-indexed dc.gov copies.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via official dpr.dc.gov pages and the 2026 'Registration Opens May 12/13' release (indexed copies; site blocks fetch): family-aquatics page exists, pools/programs free for DC residents with free membership, spray parks 10-6 daily, 2026 pool season dates, May 12 noon first-come aquatics registration with virtual waiting room.

providers.name: DPR Supreme Teen Clubs (Free Teen Programming)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR)
providers.description: Free, no-cost teen programming at 15+ DPR recreation centers - activities, trips, sports and social programs for ages 13-18 that continue through the summer. Unstructured compared with a camp, but free, drop-in-friendly, and a real answer for teens who refuse 'kid camp' but need somewhere to be.
providers.locations: Meets weekly at 15+ DPR recreation centers across DC
providers.price_detail: No cost - run by DPR's Office of Teen Programs; DPR rec centers are also free for all DC residents
providers.hours: Weekly meetings; times vary by rec center, typically afternoon/evening blocks
providers.financial_aid: Free
providers.sessions_2026: Ongoing year-round including summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Active - program confirmed running through the 2025-26 year (DPR winter-break 2025-26 release lists Supreme Teens field trips); teens can join at any participating rec center, no fee

reg_2027.opens: year-round / rolling
reg_2027.notes: No lottery and no deadline - join through your nearest participating rec center or via DPRteens.com (the DPR Teens splash page). Related free teen tracks: Young Ladies on the Rise, Future Men Future Leaders, plus field trips during breaks. Pairs well with Marion Barry SYEP for teens who want paid work too.

providers.fit_notes: For teens who need free, flexible summer structure rather than an enrolled camp. Programming quality varies by rec center - visit one first. Verified via DPR splash page and search-indexed dc.gov copies.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via official dpr.dc.gov Supreme Teen Clubs service page and Dec 2025 winter-break press release (indexed copies): ages 13-18, free, weekly at 15+ locations, run by DPR Office of Teen Programs, active into 2026. Official program name is 'Supreme Teen Clubs' - url corrected to the dpr.dc.gov service page (splashthat teens page kept as register link).

providers.name: DPR Teen Career Camps
providers.org: DC DPR in partnership with DC Department of Employment Services (DOES)
providers.description: Career-exploration summer camps for DC teens run by DPR with the Department of Employment Services, connected to the city's summer-jobs ecosystem. Themes lean toward job readiness and career fields; DPR also trains teens as lifeguards for free through its aquatics division. Good for teens too old for regular camp but not ready for a full SYEP job placement.
providers.ages: teens roughly 14-17 (DPR's overall camp system serves ages 3-17; the Feb lottery covers only 3-13, so teen career camps sit outside it - confirm each camp's exact band on DPRprograms.com)
providers.locations: Selected DPR recreation centers; 2026 offerings and sites listed on DPRprograms.com
providers.price_detail: Many teen offerings are free or nominal thanks to DOES funding, but DPR publishes no consolidated fee table for teen career camps - verify per camp on DPRprograms.com
providers.hours: Standard camp-day hours (DPR camps run 9am-5pm); varies by program
providers.financial_aid: DPR 50%/75% reduced rates apply to any fee-based offerings; DOES-funded slots are free
providers.sessions_2026: Follows the DPR summer calendar (Jun 22-Aug 14); exact per-camp dates on DPRprograms.com
providers.status_2026: Program confirmed for 2026 on DPR's Kids, Camps & Co-op page (partnership with DOES; register via DPRprograms.com); summer-programs registration opened May 13 first-come - check DPRprograms.com for open teen career camp sections, which historically have more late availability than elementary camps

reg_2027.opens: mid-May expected via DPR's summer-programs registration (2026 non-aquatics programs opened Wed May 13 at 12pm, first-come with virtual waiting room); 2027 not announced. DOES-linked paid work (SYEP) has its own late-January application
reg_2027.notes: DPR's Kids, Camps & Co-op page directs families to DPRprograms.com to view teen career camp offerings, locations, and register - these ride the May summer-programs first-come opening, NOT the February camp lottery (which covers ages 3-13). Details are published late spring and vary year to year; call (202) 671-0372 in spring. Teens wanting paid work instead should pivot to Marion Barry SYEP.

providers.fit_notes: For DC teens who want structure and career exposure without a formal job. Details are thin on the public site - call the DPR camp office before counting on a specific theme. Verified only via search-indexed dc.gov copies; registration mechanics inferred from DPR's pointer to DPRprograms.com.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via indexed official dpr.dc.gov content (site blocks fetch): DOES partnership and 'register at DPRprograms.com' language on the Kids, Camps & Co-op page, the teen-career-camps book page exists, May 13 2026 first-come registration, camps office phone/email. Exact 2026 age bands and fees are not published centrally - medium confidence.

providers.name: DPR Therapeutic Recreation Camps
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation
providers.description: DC's public therapeutic recreation camps, run out of the dedicated Therapeutic Recreation Center at 3030 G St SE, serve campers with and without developmental disabilities at deeply subsidized public rates — the core offerings are Camp Adventure, an inclusion-based camp for ages 6-16, and Discovery Inclusion support for ages 6-10 within DPR's Discovery Camps. A recreation therapist assesses each child first and determines placement based on the accommodations needed. Combined with DPR's 50%/75% income-based reduced-rate program, this is the most affordable supported-camp option in the District.
providers.ages: 6-16 (Camp Adventure inclusion camp 6-16; Discovery Inclusion 6-10; general DPR camps serve 3-13; TR Center also runs teen/adult programming — placement set by assessment)
providers.locations: Therapeutic Recreation Center, 3030 G St SE, Washington DC (some TR camps at other DPR sites)
providers.price_detail: Low public rates (see DPR 2026 Summer Camp Fees PDF: resident / non-resident / 50% / 75% reduced-rate tiers); 10% non-refundable processing fee. Exact weekly dollar amounts not captured in indexed copies.
providers.financial_aid: DPR reduced rates: income-qualified DC families get 50% or 75% off all DPR programs including camps; 2026 reduced-rate application deadline for camp was Feb 2
providers.hours: Full-day day camp (typical DPR camp day ~9am-5pm; verify per camp)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 sessions aligned with DPR camp calendar (June-August)
providers.status_2026: Season underway; general DPR lottery ran Feb 2-16, but TR registration is first-come after assessment — call the TR Center about remaining space

reg_2027.opens: assessments year-round; general DPR camp lottery opens early February (2026: Feb 2-16)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come (TR camps: in-person only at the TR Center after required assessment — no online registration); lottery for general DPR camps
reg_2027.notes: Two-step process: schedule an assessment with a recreation therapist at 202-698-1794 well before spring, then register in person at the TR Center — online registration is not available for TR camps, and DPR states it cannot accept every child due to space and staff constraints. Open to non-residents at higher rates. Apply for reduced rates BEFORE camp registration opens (2026 deadline was Feb 2). Verified via search-indexed dpr.dc.gov pages; direct site fetch blocked.

providers.fit_notes: Best for DC families who need affordable supported camp and can navigate the in-person assessment process early — start in fall/winter, since DPR cannot guarantee every assessed child a slot. Not a clinical program; families outside DC pay non-resident rates.

providers.name: Duke Ellington School of the Arts Summer Intensives
providers.org: Duke Ellington School of the Arts (DCPS arts high school)
providers.description: DC's famous audition-based public arts high school (Georgetown/Burleith) runs community summer intensive classes in dance, theatre/storytelling, vocal and instrumental music, taught in its pre-professional departments — the theatre intensive has run 10am-4pm days in June. It doubles as a taste of Ellington for students considering applying to the school itself. Public information is thin and scattered across the school's site and blog, so families should email departments directly.
providers.ages: Roughly rising middle and high school students (varies by department; dance, theatre, vocal and instrumental intensives target secondary students; a separate mandatory Incoming Student Theatre Intensive serves admitted 9th graders)
providers.locations: Duke Ellington School of the Arts, 3500 R St NW, Washington, DC (Georgetown/Burleith)
providers.price_detail: Fees not published on the open web; some offerings have been advertised as free or low-cost (an ellingtonschool.org event page is titled 'Summer Camp – NOW FREE!'). Confirm by department email
providers.financial_aid: none found (programs appear low/no cost by design)
providers.sessions_2026: Not publicly posted; 2025 pattern was June (theatre) through early August (dance July 7-Aug 1, instrumental July) sessions by department
providers.status_2026: Likely operating but 2026 details unverifiable from the open web as of Jul 12, 2026 — the school actively ran 2025 intensives (official 'Theatre Department Summer Intensive 2025' post; Centerstage Summer Dance Intensive July 7-Aug 1, 2025, 10am-4:30pm; June 2025 Instagram/Facebook reminders), but no 2026-dated session post is indexed. Call or email departments to confirm before relying on this entry.

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; departments post registration on ellingtonarts.org news/blog, Facebook (@DukeEllingtonDC) and Instagram in late spring
reg_2027.notes: Rolling/first-come registration via online forms per department; no audition for community summer classes. Announcements are late and lightly publicized — watch the ellingtonarts.org News page and @DukeEllingtonDC socials from April. If your child may apply to Ellington for high school (application + audition in 8th grade), the summer intensive is a smart on-ramp.

providers.fit_notes: Best for DC tweens/teens curious about serious arts training in a legendary setting. VERIFICATION FLAG: only verifiable via search-indexed copies and a school blog post; details are low-confidence — call (202) 282-0123 before planning around it.
providers.hours: 2025 intensives ran weekdays roughly 10am-4pm (Centerstage Summer Dance Intensive 10:00am-4:30pm daily; theatre ~10am-4pm; instrumental Mon-Thu 10am-2pm) — varies by department
providers.verification_note: SECOND OPINION: KEEP, raised to medium. Confirmed against official 2025-dated content: ellingtonarts.org post 'Theatre Department Summer Intensive 2025', Centerstage Summer Dance Intensive July 7-Aug 1 2025 (10am-4:30pm) post, June 2025 @DukeEllingtonDC Facebook/Instagram reminders, plus live ellingtonschool.org summer-intensives index and 'Summer Camp – NOW FREE!' event page — org, program pattern, ages, hours and first-come registration all verified from 2025 official sources; only the 2026 season itself is unposted/unconfirmed (site fetches blocked, thin indexing). Swapped url to the current theatre-intensive post (old 2024 blog url still resolves but is staler).

providers.name: Dynamite Gymnastics Center Summer Camp
providers.description: A big, well-regarded recreational gymnastics gym on Boiling Brook Parkway (next door to Bolt Parkour) offering half-day and full-day summer camp weeks for all skill levels — tumbling, bars, beam, trampoline, foam pits, and games. A reliable pick for energetic kids with zero gymnastics background, and a practical one: enrollment runs through a self-serve iClassPro portal with many weeks all summer.
providers.ages: 4-15 (all skill levels)
providers.locations: 4956 Boiling Brook Parkway, North Bethesda, MD
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day sessions
providers.extended_care: Not verified — ask the front desk
providers.price_detail: Weekly tuition (billed as 4 days of daily camp pricing) shown only in the iClassPro portal; $200/week non-refundable deposit at approval, balance charged June 1 (full payment if enrolling on/after June 1)
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 22-August 21, 2026, half or full day
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer camp running June 22-August 21, 2026; enrollment open via customer portal as of July 2026

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; portal camp listings have typically appeared by winter/early in the year
reg_2027.mechanism: rolling first-come via iClassPro customer portal (enrollment requests approved by staff)
reg_2027.notes: Low-stress registration; individual weeks often available well into summer. Also runs school's-out camps on MCPS days off — useful year-round.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed dynamitegc.com pages: ages corrected 4-14 to 4-15, 2026 dates June 22-Aug 21, weekly billing/$200 deposit policy, half/full day, address, email, portal registration; dollar tuition amounts remain portal-only so price stays 'varies' and confidence medium.

providers.name: Echo Hill Camp
providers.description: A family-owned (three generations) sleepaway camp directly on the Chesapeake Bay in Worton, MD (~1.5 hours from DC) for about 150 campers ages 7-16 — small, salty, and famous for its free-choice program where kids design their own days: waterskiing, sailing, crabbing, swamp mucks, arts, and beach life. Three two-week sessions with all-inclusive tuition (even the camp store canteen is covered). The intimate scale is the opposite of the mega-camps.
providers.locations: 13655 Bloomingneck Rd, Worton MD (Kent County, Eastern Shore, ~1.5 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: $2,385 per two-week session (2026), all-inclusive (canteen, insurance, t-shirt, yearbook, laundry, trips, supplies) — about $1,190/wk, cheaper than most private sleepaways
providers.financial_aid: None advertised; ask camp
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: Jun 21-Jul 3; Session 2: Jul 5-17; Session 3: Jul 19-31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Session 3 (Jul 19-31) is the only remaining 2026 session — contact camp promptly about space

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (rolling; 'Enroll for 2027' messaging typically appears at season's end)
reg_2027.notes: Small capacity (150) means returning families claim much of the space — new families should enroll in fall/early winter. All-inclusive pricing means no surprise add-ons. Verified via echohillcamp.com indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: For independent-minded kids who will thrive choosing their own schedule in a small, unfussy, water-centric community. Not for kids who need highly structured days or families wanting one-week sessions.

providers.name: Edlavitch DCJCC Summer Camps (Base Camp, Quest & Adventure)
providers.description: Urban day camp inside the 16th Street JCC near Dupont Circle: Base Camp blends arts and crafts, sports tournaments, and daily swim in the on-site pool in one- and two-week themed sessions, with one-week Quest (invention/engineering) and Adventure (ropes courses, outdoor) sessions in August, plus specialty sports/theater options and a teen Summer of Service program. Open to campers of all backgrounds and abilities.
providers.ages: Day camp for campers entering PreK4 through 6th grade (roughly 4-12); separate Summer of Service teen program for rising 7th-10th graders (roughly 12-16)
providers.locations: Edlavitch DCJCC, 1529 16th St NW, Washington, DC 20036 (Dupont/Logan)
providers.price_detail: Per-session pricing on my.edcjcc.org portal (not search-indexed); members get discounts and/or priority registration
providers.financial_aid: Camp scholarships historically available through EDCJCC; confirm with camp office (202-518-9400)

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; has typically opened December-January, members first, then public
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via my.edcjcc.org portal with member priority
reg_2027.notes: The only full-service JCC camp in DC proper — central-DC demand means core summer sessions fill by early spring; August Quest/Adventure weeks last longer. Membership pays for itself via discount + priority.

providers.sessions_2026: Two- and three-week sessions (Base Camp plus specialty options like sports and theater) June-August 2026, plus one-week Quest and Adventure sessions in August
providers.status_2026: Season underway; August one-week Quest and Adventure sessions are the most likely to still have space — check portal
providers.fit_notes: The walkable, pool-included option for central DC families; most sessions run one to two weeks, so it works for both continuity-seekers and week-planners. Verified via indexed copies; portal has live pricing.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed edcjcc.org and camp-directory copies: Base Camp/Quest/Adventure structure, hours 8:30-4 with aftercare to 5:30, member discount/priority, teen Summer of Service (rising 7th-10th); ages tightened from '4-16' to grade-based ranges; portal pricing not indexed so price and confidence stay medium.

providers.name: Emma's Art Studio Summer Camps (Emma Teaches Art)
providers.description: A downtown-Bethesda fine-arts studio (the Deco Arts camp founder, formerly at Glen Echo Park) running small weekly camps where each day tackles a different fine-arts project — silkscreen printing, marbleized paper, fossil casting, terra cotta sculpture, tempera on real canvas — with high-quality materials. A boutique, teacher-led alternative to big institutional art camps.
providers.ages: 5-11 (most weeks 5-10; late-June and early-July weeks split into 5-8 and 8-11 sections)
providers.locations: 4931 Cordell Ave Suite 2A, Bethesda MD 20814 (downtown Bethesda)
providers.price_detail: $450/week (June 8-12, June 15-19), $475/week (June 22-July 10 weeks), $495 for the Aug 24-28 week in 2026
providers.hours: 9am-3pm
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 8 through July 10, plus an end-of-summer week Aug 24-28, 2026
providers.status_2026: Sessions listed for booking on CourseStorm into August 2026; remaining availability not individually verified

reg_2027.opens: early February (2026 registration opened Feb 3, announced on the studio blog; sessions post on CourseStorm)
reg_2027.notes: Registers online via CourseStorm (emmateachesart.coursestorm.com). Small studio capacity, so favorite weeks go early, but it is far less of a stampede than the big institutions.

providers.fit_notes: Suits elementary kids who want real art instruction in a small, personal setting near the Bethesda Metro. No teen program and no extended-day coverage found — working parents needing 8-6 care should look elsewhere.

providers.name: Encore Stage & Studio Summer Camps
providers.description: Arlington's venerable 'theatre by kids, for kids' runs a big slate of summer camps for ages 3-15, from pre-K story-and-character camps through Broadway-style musical theatre and hands-on technical-theatre/stagecraft camps, most ending in a family performance. One of the few programs anywhere that teaches elementary and middle-schoolers backstage crafts (sets, costumes, tech).
providers.ages: 3-15 (PreK & Rising K 3-5; Elementary 5-9; Scenes for Tweens 9-12; Stage Door & Tech Camp 12-15)
providers.locations: Multiple Arlington and Alexandria, VA sites, incl. Encore @ Cherrydale UMC, 3701 Lorcom Ln, Arlington VA 22207
providers.price_detail: ~$90/day (about $450 for a 5-day week) per 2026 directory listings supplied by Encore; AM extended care $10/day, PM extended $15/day. Early-bird discount ended Jan 31, 2026
providers.financial_aid: Limited full and partial scholarships for all classes and camps (application form to registrar)
providers.hours: Varies; half-day for youngest ages, full-day options for older
providers.extended_care: Yes — AM ($10/day) and PM ($15/day) extended hours at most sites; confirm per site with registrar
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed camps across summer 2026 at multiple Arlington/Alexandria sites; registration opened by early January (Jan 10 open house, early bird through Jan 31)
providers.status_2026: Registration open; with many sites and weeks, late-July/August availability is common — check the camps page

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; 2026 pattern was early January (free Summer Camp Open House Jan 10, 2026 at Cherrydale; early-bird pricing through Jan 31)
reg_2027.notes: Attend the January open house to sample camps and meet teaching artists before booking. NoVA entry included because its kids-run-the-show ethos and tech-theatre camps fill a genuine regional gap.

providers.fit_notes: Gentlest on-ramp for the very young (age 3+!) and the only close-in option for kids who'd rather build the set than stand on it. Sites are church halls, not a theatre facility — less glossy than Imagination Stage.
