providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed encorestage.org copy plus Encore-supplied 2026 listings (KidsOutAndAbout, KidFriendly DC): ages 3-15 with groupings, phone, sites, Jan 10 2026 open house, Jan 31 early-bird deadline, ~$90/day + $10/$15 extended care — price now banded $$; medium because pricing came from directory listings rather than the official domain.

providers.name: Engineering For Kids of Northern Virginia
providers.description: Hands-on engineering camps (aerospace, mechanical, LEGO-based, junior robotics) run by an educator-led franchise at churches and community centers around Northern Virginia, with half-day and full-day weeks. A solid, lower-key alternative to the big-brand STEM camps for kids ages 4-14 who like to build.
providers.locations: Multiple community sites across Northern Virginia serving Bristow, Centreville, Fairfax and surrounding communities (sites vary by season); no verified DC or Montgomery County sites
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day options; exact hours vary by site
providers.price_detail: Not published in search-indexed official pages; call or check the camps page for per-week pricing
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer camps running (listed in 2026 NoVA camp guides and directories; official camps page live); rolling registration, later weeks typically available — call to confirm

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; schedule has typically been posted by the franchise in late winter/early spring
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come by site and week; smaller sites cap quickly but the program as a whole rarely sells out far ahead.

providers.fit_notes: Good for younger builders (the 4-7 'junior' range is a real strength — most STEM camps start at 6+). DC/MoCo families face a Virginia drive.
providers.sessions_2026: Half-day and full-day weekly camps across summer 2026 at multiple NoVA sites
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed engineeringforkids.com/northern-virginia pages and 2026 directory listings (summercamps.com, Fairfax Moms 2026 guide): franchise active for summer 2026, ages 4-14, phone 703-997-1919, half/full-day formats, Bristow/Centreville/Fairfax service area; pricing and exact site list not indexed, so medium.

providers.name: ESF Summer Camps at The Academy of the Holy Cross
providers.description: The Mid-Atlantic camp chain ESF runs its full model — Mini/Day Camp, Senior Camp with leadership focus, and Multi-Sports Camp with 60+ activities — on the 28-acre Academy of the Holy Cross campus in Kensington, near Rockville Pike. Professional, systems-driven camping similar in spirit to Headfirst, serving the Kensington/Bethesda/Rockville corridor.
providers.ages: Roughly 3-15 (rising Preschool through 10th grade; Mini Camp for first-time campers through 4-week Senior Camp leadership program)
providers.locations: The Academy of the Holy Cross, 4920 Strathmore Ave, Kensington MD 20895
providers.price_detail: 2026 tuition table on esfcamps.com/bethesda/rates (amounts not search-indexed); tuition includes all activities, snacks, camp T-shirt and Multi-Sports bus transport; 5% off 6+ weeks, 3% sibling discount, reduced rate for June 29-July 3, 2026 week; optional Camp Protection Plan $60/camper/week; optional weekly lunch program from May 2026; payment plans ran through May 15, 2026
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm standard
providers.extended_care: Yes — Extended Day 'Club OT' before and after core hours
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions across summer 2026 (Traditional Day Camps, Mini Camp, Multi-Sports, specialty camps; Senior Camp as a 4-week program)
providers.status_2026: Operating summer 2026 at Holy Cross (2026 rates page live, June 29-July 3 2026 week discounted, lunch program launched May 2026); first-come enrollment continued into season — check rates page for open weeks

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; ESF has run a fall 'Sign Up & Gear Up' early-access period with full enrollment opening in winter
reg_2027.notes: Sign up for early access in the fall for first crack and early pricing; deposit holds spots and monthly payment plans run to mid-May. No lottery.

providers.fit_notes: A polished chain-camp alternative to Headfirst for families east of Bethesda (Kensington, Wheaton, Silver Spring, Rockville); less distinctive personality than the single-campus legacy camps.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed esfcamps.com/bethesda and academyoftheholycross.org pages: 2026 season active at Holy Cross Kensington, hours 9-3 with Club OT extended day, ages rising Preschool-10th grade (widened from 3-14), discount/payment-plan/protection-plan mechanics for 2026; dollar tuition not indexed so price_band stays 'varies' and confidence medium.

providers.name: F. Scott Fitzgerald Theatre Performing Arts Camps (City of Rockville)
providers.org: City of Rockville Recreation and Parks
providers.description: Theater day camps run by the city at its 446-seat F. Scott Fitzgerald Theatre in Rockville Civic Center Park: campers are cast in a play, rehearse with accomplished DC-area teaching artists, and put on a final performance for family and friends on a real proscenium stage. A rare chance to do genuine staged theater at municipal-camp prices, without auditions.
providers.ages: Approx. 8-12 for the theatre arts sessions at the Fitzgerald (per search-indexed 2026 Camp Guide); other Rockville drama/arts camps cover additional grade bands
providers.locations: F. Scott Fitzgerald Theatre, Rockville Civic Center Park, 603 Edmonston Dr, Rockville, MD 20851
providers.hours: Day-camp hours per session; see 2026 Camp Guide (city camps generally offer full- and half-day formats)
providers.extended_care: Extended care available for many Rockville camps — verify per listing in REC1
providers.price_detail: Per-session pricing in Rockville's REC1 catalog; 2026 session prices not independently verified. Nonresident surcharge applies.
providers.financial_aid: City of Rockville financial assistance available for resident campers

reg_2027.opens: Early-to-mid February 2027 expected, with all Rockville camps (2026 confirmed: Feb 10 residents / Feb 12 general, 8:30 a.m., at rockvillemd.gov/registration)
reg_2027.notes: No audition — casting happens inside the camp — which makes these popular with first-time performers. Register during the resident window if eligible; popular performance weeks fill first. 2027 dates not yet announced; pattern based on the city's official 2026 announcement.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; theatre arts camps confirmed in the city's official 2026 Camp Guide and city news release. Session-level availability lives in the REC1 catalog (not individually verified as of July 12, 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Great confidence-builder for stage-curious kids; families seeking conservatory-level training should compare Imagination Stage or Adventure Theatre (private, pricier).
providers.sessions_2026: Theatre arts camp sessions at the F. Scott Fitzgerald Theatre, summer 2026, per the city's 2026 Camp Guide; Rockville's camp season runs late June through mid-August
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); verified via search-indexed rockvillemd.gov pages: theatre page resolves, 2026 Camp Guide PDF lists theatre arts programs (ages 8-12) at 603 Edmonston Dr, and city news release confirms Feb 10/12, 2026 8:30 a.m. registration via REC1. Ages narrowed from 'elementary through teen' to guide-indexed 8-12; prices still unverified.

providers.name: First Tee — Greater Washington DC Summer Camps & Life Skills Classes
providers.description: The national youth-golf-plus-character-education nonprofit's DC chapter teaches at two storied public courses: Langston Golf Course (NE DC, historically significant as a Black golf landmark) and East Potomac/Hains Point (SW DC). Week-long summer camps and multi-week Life Skills Experience classes braid golf instruction with values curriculum and even STEM tie-ins, at accessible nonprofit prices with aid available. One of the best-value 'real sport' introductions in the city.
providers.ages: 7-18 per First Tee DC's official FAQ (grouped by age; teen pathways available)
providers.locations: Langston Golf Course, 2600 Benning Rd NE; East Potomac Golf Links, 972 Ohio Dr SW (Hains Point)
providers.price_detail: Exact 2026 camp fees not search-verifiable; chapter states no child is turned away for inability to pay, with scholarships for military families and free/reduced-lunch students. For comparison, playDCgolf's commercial junior camps at the same courses run roughly $249-399/week.
providers.hours: Typically half-day sessions; playDCgolf's Players Camp option at the same courses runs 9 a.m.-3 p.m.
providers.financial_aid: Yes — official policy: never turns a child away for inability to pay; scholarships for active-duty military families and free/reduced-lunch qualifiers; reduced fees by need
providers.sessions_2026: Summer session July 13 - August 16, 2026; week-long camps and Life Skills Experience classes at Langston and East Potomac
providers.status_2026: Summer session starting July 13; registration open with classes still being added to the schedule as of early July 2026

reg_2027.opens: Rolling, roughly 30 days before each session per the chapter's official FAQ (summer registration around June; summer session runs early July to end of August)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online registration; classes are posted to the schedule page as they open, so check back — demand is steady but not frantic. Separately, playDCgolf (the courses' operator) sells first-come commercial junior camps at Langston and East Potomac (Pee Wee ages 5-8, Junior Fundamentals and Players Camp ages 9-15) — a good fallback if First Tee weeks fill.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for families wanting an affordable, character-forward intro to golf at genuinely historic public courses; kids wanting all-day camp coverage will need aftercare arranged elsewhere since sessions are half-day.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); verified via search-indexed firstteedc.org content: summer session Jul 13-Aug 16 2026, ages corrected to 7-18, registration ~30 days pre-session, no-turn-away financial aid policy, phone. Exact camp prices unconfirmed, so price band stays an informed estimate.

providers.name: FLOC Summer Programs (tutoring + Outdoor Education Center camps)
providers.description: FLOC provides free educational services to low-income DC students: in summer that means free intervention tutoring in math and reading (2nd-12th grade, offered online) plus free residential outdoor camps at its 350-acre Outdoor Education Center in Harpers Ferry, WV — ropes courses, climbing, canoeing, and hiking with a teamwork curriculum. A rare free combination of academics and overnight outdoor adventure.
providers.ages: Elementary through high school (one-on-one reading/math tutoring plus middle school, high school, and postsecondary readiness coaching); school-age/teen cohorts for OEC residential camps
providers.price_detail: Free to students from low-income DC families
providers.locations: DC office at 1301 Pennsylvania Ave SE, Third Floor, Washington, DC 20003 (Capitol Hill); camps at the 350-acre Outdoor Education Center near Harpers Ferry, WV
providers.hours: Tutoring in scheduled sessions (up to four hours weekly per student); OEC camps are multi-day residential
providers.financial_aid: Services are free for low-income DC families — that is the program design

reg_2027.opens: Spring for summer academies and OEC camp sign-ups; students in FLOC's school-year Neighborhood Tutoring Program get priority
reg_2027.notes: Register by calling (202) 462-8686 or through floc.org. FLOC runs two summer academies alongside its nine school-year programs; the residential OEC camps invite students from FLOC's DC programs and West Virginia partners — join the tutoring program during the school year to be in the pipeline.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer academies (tutoring plus field trips) and residential OEC camp sessions, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Organization active (official site and third-party listings current through 2026); summer tutoring/academies continue over the summer to fight learning loss, and OEC hosts DC and WV youth for summer camps. 2026 session-level rosters not independently verified.
providers.fit_notes: For families who want free academic catch-up with a genuine camp experience attached. The overnight WV camps are a big deal for kids who have never left the city; requires engaging with FLOC's programs rather than one-click registration.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); verified via search-indexed floc.org, Catalogue for Philanthropy, and current directory listings: org active in 2026, address (1301 Pennsylvania Ave SE, 3rd Fl), phone (202) 462-8686, free-to-low-income model, OEC in Harpers Ferry with summer camps, and 'two summer academies'. 2026-specific dates unconfirmed.

providers.name: Folger Shakespeare Library (no summer camp — free family programs and school-year teen fellowship)
providers.description: The world's largest Shakespeare collection, on Capitol Hill, does NOT run a summer camp — stop searching for one. Instead it offers free drop-in summer family programming (Family Takeovers every other Saturday, an all-ages Youth Poetry Workshop) and, for teens, the prestigious Lily McKee High School Fellowship, a free five-month humanities deep-dive that runs January-May with applications in the fall. Families wanting an actual Shakespeare day camp should look at Shakespeare Theatre Company's Camp Shakespeare downtown.
providers.ages: Family workshops designed for ages 5-7 and 8-11 with the adults who join them; Lily McKee Fellowship for high school sophomores-seniors
providers.locations: Folger Shakespeare Library, 201 East Capitol St SE, Washington, DC
providers.price_detail: Folger Family Takeover workshops and youth programming are free; Lily McKee Fellowship is free to selected fellows
providers.financial_aid: n/a — offerings are free
providers.sessions_2026: Family workshops on select Saturdays — paused June-July 2026, resuming August; free summer events per the Folger's What's On calendar
providers.status_2026: Confirmed: no summer camp exists. Free family Saturday workshops are on hiatus for June and July 2026 and resume in August; other free summer events (e.g., Reading Rhythms reading party in the Great Hall) run meanwhile. Lily McKee applications for the Jan-May 2027 cohort expected in fall 2026.

reg_2027.opens: n/a for camps (none exists); Lily McKee Fellowship applications open in the fall for the January-May cohort
reg_2027.notes: For the Fellowship: competitive application, about two dozen DC-area sophomores, juniors, and seniors per five-month cohort. For families: free Saturday workshops, no camp to book — note they are on hiatus in June and July 2026 and resume in August. The Secondary School Shakespeare Festival (March) is the other famous Folger youth entry point, organized through schools.

providers.fit_notes: Great free enrichment stop for literary families and a standout school-year credential for humanities teens; not a childcare or camp solution. Verified via search-indexed copies of folger.edu pages.
providers.hours: Select Saturdays for family workshops; Fellowship meets in ~15 in-person sessions January-May
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); verified against search-indexed 2026 folger.edu content: student-programs page live, family workshops free for ages 5-7/8-11 on select Saturdays but on hiatus June-July 2026 (corrected from 'every other Saturday all summer'), Lily McKee is a five-month Jan-May program of ~24 fellows via competitive application. All key facts from official-source copy.

providers.name: Fort Dupont Ice Arena Summer Skating Camps (DPR & DC-ICE)
providers.org: DC Department of Parks and Recreation (arena operator since Dec 2025); DC-ICE (Inner City Excellence)
providers.description: Fort Dupont Ice Arena in Ward 7 — the rink that trained generations of Black skaters east of the river — runs the Kids On Ice program providing free or heavily subsidized figure skating, hockey, and speed skating instruction, plus summer skating camps (about four one-week sessions serving 1,100+ kids). The affiliated DC-ICE camp offers a full-day skating-based day camp for ages 6-12 with 3+ hours of ice time daily. Note: the arena is transitioning under DC DPR management with programming grants, so program names and registration may shift.
providers.ages: DPR summer skating camp: 9-13 and 14-18 age groups (max 20/session, all skill levels, skates and helmets provided); DC-ICE full-day camp: 6-12
providers.price_detail: DPR camps use low DC-resident rates (confirm current fees when registering); DC-ICE camp $300/wk full day, $150/wk AM-only, $375/wk extended day, sibling discounts
providers.locations: Fort Dupont Ice Arena, 3779 Ely Pl SE, Washington DC 20019 (Ward 7); DC-ICE also uses Canal Park Ice Rink and Anacostia Park Skating Pavilion
providers.hours: DPR camp full day; DC-ICE offers full, half (AM), and extended-day options
providers.financial_aid: DPR pricing is subsidized for DC residents; DC-ICE is a mission-driven low-cost program — ask each provider. The Friends group's free Kids On Ice lessons are no longer the arena's program; DPR is funding replacement programming via grants

reg_2027.opens: DPR summer-camp registration historically opens late winter/early spring on dpr.dc.gov; DC-ICE 2026 registration opened April 20 — expect similar spring timing for 2027
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online (DPR registration system for DPR camps; innercityexcellence.org for DC-ICE)
reg_2027.notes: Major change: Friends of Fort Dupont Ice Arena withdrew in fall 2025 and DPR now runs the renovated arena (reopened Dec 6, 2025 after a $39M renovation) — fdia.org content is stale; use dpr.dc.gov. Kids On Ice free-lesson slots as previously known no longer exist in that form.

providers.sessions_2026: DPR summer skating camp June 22-Aug 13, 2026 (weekly sessions, max 20 participants); DC-ICE weekly full-day camp sessions through the summer
providers.status_2026: Arena open and running summer 2026 programming under DPR management; DPR summer skating camp runs June 22-Aug 13, 2026 and DC-ICE camp is operating (registration opened April 20). Friends of FDIA no longer operates the arena.
providers.extended_care: DC-ICE extended-day option $375/wk; ask DPR about its camp's care hours
providers.fit_notes: A genuinely unique east-of-the-river institution — where else does a kid skate for free in July? Best for families near Fort Dupont/Benning. Program structure is in flux with the DPR transition, so verify details each season.
providers.verification_note: Corrected for the late-2025 management transition: HillRag (Nov 2025), DPR/DGS releases and dpr.dc.gov confirm Friends of FDIA withdrew and DPR reopened the arena Dec 6, 2025; url repointed from stale fdia.org to dpr.dc.gov. DPR 2026 camp dates/ages and DC-ICE prices/ages/April-20 registration confirmed via search-indexed DPR and innercityexcellence.org pages (direct fetch blocked). Phone (202) 204-2290 confirmed. Medium confidence because exact DPR camp fees could not be confirmed.

providers.name: Geneva Summer Camp for Young Children
providers.description: An award-winning preschool summer camp at a well-regarded Potomac early-childhood school, with nine weekly themes (dinosaurs, circus, rockets) delivered through science experiments, arts, gardening, water play, creek expeditions, and visiting performers and petting zoos. One of the very few close-in camps that genuinely takes 2-year-olds, and you can pick individual weeks.
providers.locations: 11931 Seven Locks Rd, Potomac MD 20854
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day schedules available
providers.price_detail: Priced per week by half/full-day schedule; contact school for current rate sheet
providers.financial_aid: None advertised for summer
providers.sessions_2026: Nine themed weeks, roughly mid-June through mid-August
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; later-summer weeks may still have space for the youngest age groups — call to confirm

reg_2027.opens: mid-winter (enrollment for the coming summer typically posted by January-February)
reg_2027.notes: Current Geneva families enroll first in practice; the 2s and young-3s classrooms are the scarce commodity regionally, so newcomers with toddlers should contact the school in January. Verified from official site.

providers.extended_care: Ask school; full-day option covers most working-parent needs
providers.fit_notes: Best-in-class option for ages 2-4 in the Potomac/Rockville corridor. Older kids (5-6.5) may prefer a bigger camp with more range.

providers.name: Georgetown Hoyas Basketball & Soccer Camps
providers.description: Georgetown's varsity programs run summer camps on the Hilltop: the Men's Basketball Elite Camp (boys 8-18, all skill levels despite the name) at McDonough Gymnasium and the Thompson Athletic Center with commuter and overnight options, plus men's soccer camps aimed at developing and showcase-level players from one of the nation's top college soccer programs. Kids train in the actual varsity facilities with college staff and players.
providers.ages: Basketball Elite Camp: boys 8-18, all skill levels; soccer camps skew to competitive youth/HS players (rising freshmen through seniors for ID camps)
providers.locations: Georgetown University campus (McDonough Gymnasium / Thompson Athletic Center), 37th & O St NW, Washington, DC
providers.price_detail: Basketball Elite Camp: $585 commuter / $685 overnight for the five-day session (incl. lunch, dinner, t-shirt), per third-party listing; soccer camp fees vary by session via Ryzer
providers.hours: Five-day sessions for basketball with overnight option; soccer camps run 2-3 day sessions
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Basketball Elite Camp June 23-27, 2026 (McDonough/Thompson); men's soccer prospect camp July 12-14, 2026 and Early ID Clinic mid-July 2026, registration via Ryzer
providers.status_2026: June basketball Elite Camp has passed; men's soccer still had summer 2026 sessions in progress or upcoming as of July 12 — a showcase/prospect camp July 12-14 and an Early ID Clinic with registration open through July 16, 2026

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring (soccer staff has posted registrations as early as December for the following summer; other staffs post January-April)
reg_2027.notes: Each sport's coaching staff runs registration separately (a common college-camp quirk) — search '[sport] camp guhoyas' rather than expecting a central portal; soccer registers via Ryzer (register.ryzer.com). First-come; elite/ID camps for older players effectively function as recruiting showcases and require a current physical.

providers.fit_notes: Best for sports-mad kids who'll be thrilled by big-time college facilities, and for HS players eyeing recruiting exposure; not a general day camp and there's no aftercare.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); verified via search: guhoyas.com/feature/MSOC_Camp resolves as the official men's soccer camp page, 2026 soccer prospect camp Jul 12-14 and Early ID Clinic (reg through Jul 16) confirmed via Ryzer/aggregator listings, basketball Elite Camp details ($585/$685, boys 8-18, Jun 23-27) confirmed only via third-party basketballexposurecamps listing — hence medium.

providers.name: Georgetown Prep Sports Camps
providers.description: Sport-specific weekly camps (basketball, baseball, football, golf, lacrosse, soccer) led by Georgetown Prep's own varsity coaches on the school's 90-acre North Bethesda campus, alongside hosted programs like Jr. Wizards basketball and Nike Golf Camps. High-level instruction and facilities in a straightforward 9-3 day.
providers.ages: Roughly 6-16, varying by sport and hosted camp (Jr. Wizards hosted weeks welcome all ages/skill levels)
providers.locations: Georgetown Preparatory School, 10900 Rockville Pike, North Bethesda, MD
providers.price_detail: Per-camp pricing on gprep.org/camps and hosted-camp sites, not centrally published; hosted Jr. Wizards week at Prep is $375/week (incl. reversible jersey, t-shirt, two Wizards home-game tickets)
providers.hours: Full-day camps 9:00 a.m.-3:00 p.m.; half-day camps 9:00 a.m.-12:00 p.m.; evening football camp 5:00-8:00 p.m. Lunch purchasable from the Hoya Cafe or bring your own.
providers.extended_care: Not standard; Jr. Wizards drop-off 8:30-9:00 a.m., pick-up promptly at 3:00 p.m. — verify per camp
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026 across sports; hosted Jr. Wizards week at Prep confirmed Aug 3-7, 2026, 9 a.m.-3 p.m.
providers.status_2026: In season; GP Sports Camps site live with basketball, baseball, football, golf, lacrosse, and soccer pages, and the hosted Jr. Wizards week Aug 3-7, 2026 was still being promoted as of July — reasonable late-summer availability

reg_2027.opens: Winter/spring, rolling by sport; hosted camps (Jr. Wizards via Monumental Sports, Nike) open on their own national schedules
reg_2027.notes: Each sport registers separately on gprep.org/camps; no single opening day. Coach-led camps in marquee Prep sports (basketball with varsity head coach Howard Blue, lacrosse) fill first.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who want real coaching in one sport rather than a general camp; Metro-accessible (Grosvenor). Summer camps are open to boys and girls despite Prep being a boys' school — confirm per camp.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); verified via search-indexed gprep.org/camps pages (live, listing basketball/baseball/football/golf/lacrosse/soccer, 9-3 full-day and 9-12 half-day hours, Hoya Cafe lunch) plus Monumental Sports/DCFamily listings confirming Jr. Wizards at Prep Aug 3-7 2026 at $375/wk. Raised from low to medium; Prep-run camp prices still unpublished centrally.

providers.name: Georgetown University Summer Academies (Pre-College)
providers.org: Georgetown University School of Continuing Studies
providers.description: One-, two-, and three-week intensive academies for high schoolers on Georgetown's campus — international relations, medicine, forensic science, business, law and more — taught through lectures, simulations, and site visits, with commuter and residential options. One application covers all academies; once approved, you register for whichever has space. Thrives: academically ambitious teens (and college-list builders) who want a taste of university life in DC; commuter option makes it practical for local families.
providers.ages: High school students (~rising 9th-12th, varies by academy)
providers.price_detail: From $3,095 for one week (commuter); residential and multi-week options cost more
providers.locations: Georgetown University main campus, 37th & O St NW
providers.sessions_2026: Rolling 1-3 week sessions across June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-July/August academy sessions sometimes retain space — registration is complete only when tuition is paid, so check the academy finder for open sections.

reg_2027.opens: roughly late fall/winter (application portal opens well before summer; academies fill on a rolling basis)
reg_2027.notes: Application (with counselor/teacher reference) unlocks registration; actual seats are first-come, first-served after approval, and marquee academies (medicine, IR) fill by early spring. Comparable local options: GW Pre-College Summer Immersion (2-week, dates TBA); American University's Discover the World of Communication is on hiatus for 2026 with a redesign expected for 2027.

providers.financial_aid: Limited; inquire with SCS — do not assume aid is available
providers.fit_notes: Expensive and academically flavored rather than academically rigorous in the credit-bearing sense (academies are non-credit enrichment). For local teens, commuter enrollment cuts cost substantially versus residential.

providers.name: Girl Scouts Nation's Capital Day & Twilight Camps
providers.description: Dozens of 100% volunteer-run day and evening camps across the region each summer — outdoor skills, crafts, songs, s'mores, hands-on science — including long-running camps in Montgomery County (one in Boyds has run 40+ years) and evening 'twilight' camps like Camp Firefly where the campfire is the finale. Any girl can join Girl Scouts to attend; these are among the cheapest organized camps anywhere in the DMV.
providers.ages: Youth completing grades K-11 (per GSCNC: 'camp is open to all youth completing K-11'); teen program-aide roles for older Girl Scouts; non-members may attend after joining at mygs.girlscouts.org
providers.locations: Dozens of volunteer-run sites across DC, Montgomery County, Northern Virginia and beyond; specific sites vary yearly — see the annual program guide
providers.price_detail: Camps are '100% volunteer run' (per gscnc.org), so fees are low and set per camp — 2026 per-camp fees are listed in the Doubleknot program guide and were not individually verified. Full tuition is due at registration; the Early Camper Placement process instead takes a $25 deposit with the balance due by May 1, 2026.
providers.hours: Varies: day camps roughly 9am-4pm; twilight/evening camps run late afternoon-evening; some camps include an overnight
providers.financial_aid: Up to 100% camp scholarships; day-camp assistance applications opened Jan 2, 2026 — GSCNC says apply for a Camp Scholarship BEFORE registering, since full tuition is due at registration

reg_2027.opens: Early January (2026 cycle: scholarship/assistance apps opened Jan 2; Doubleknot registration and Early Camper Placement followed; balance deadline May 1)
reg_2027.mechanism: Varies by camp: all day/evening camp registration runs through gscnc.doubleknot.com — some camps are first-come, others use a lottery; placed Early Camper Placement families secure spots with a $25 deposit via an emailed link
reg_2027.notes: Camper must be a registered Girl Scout (GSUSA dues rise to $65/yr for the 2027 membership year, from $45 in 2026) — join anytime, no troop required. Check your target camp's mechanism (first-come vs lottery) in the annual program guide before January; popular neighborhood camps fill within days.

providers.sessions_2026: One-week day and evening sessions June-August 2026 at volunteer-run sites region-wide
providers.status_2026: Season underway (July 2026); registration was conducted via Doubleknot — check the 2026 landing page for any remaining late-summer openings
providers.fit_notes: Unbeatable price for outdoorsy, screen-free weeks; volunteer-run means shorter hours and no aftercare — hard for two-full-time-job families without backup. Girls only. Membership cost rising to $65/yr materially changes the all-in price for one-week campers.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed gscnc.org and gscnc.doubleknot.com copies (direct fetch blocked): url/landing page live, grades K-11, camp@gscnc.org, Jan 2 2026 scholarship opening, Doubleknot first-come/lottery split, $25 ECP deposit with May 1 2026 balance, '100% volunteer run'. Exact 2026 per-camp fees sit behind Doubleknot listings and were not verified, so confidence stays medium.

providers.name: Girl Scouts Nation's Capital Sleep-Away Camps (Potomac Woods, May Flather, Winona)
providers.description: GSCNC's three resident camps: Camp Potomac Woods (Leesburg, VA — closest to DC), Camp May Flather (George Washington National Forest, VA — high adventure, fully unplugged), and Camp Winona (Hughesville, MD — traditional waterfront camp), offering 2-night mini-camps up to full weeks with themed specialties (horses, water, arts, adventure). Entry is by an equal-chance lottery, not speed — a rarity among DC camps — and scholarships can cover up to 100%.
providers.ages: grades 2-12 (mini-camps for younger; CIT/counselor tracks for teens)
providers.locations: Camp Potomac Woods (Leesburg, VA), Camp May Flather (Mount Solon, VA), Camp Winona (Hughesville, MD)
providers.price_detail: Fees vary by program length/theme (nonprofit pricing, generally far below private sleepaway); $25 deposit secures placement, balance due May 1
providers.hours: Residential (2-night mini-camps through 1-week sessions)
providers.financial_aid: Up to 100% scholarships for all campers; request link arrives in confirmation email after deposit

reg_2027.opens: lottery expected mid-December 2026 - early January 2027 (2026 cycle: Dec 15-Jan 5, placements Jan 12, general registration Jan 20)
reg_2027.mechanism: lottery via Jotform with buddy/sibling matching, then placement email + $25 deposit; leftover spots go first-come at general registration
reg_2027.notes: Explicitly NOT first-come — all lottery entries have equal odds, so there is no midnight refresh race. Enter the lottery in December, and use Jan 20 general registration as the backup for unfilled programs. Must be a registered Girl Scout.

providers.sessions_2026: Themed sessions of 2 nights to 1 week across summer 2026 at all three camps
providers.status_2026: Season underway; general registration remained open after Jan 20 for unfilled programs — late-summer openings possible via Doubleknot
providers.fit_notes: The gentlest and cheapest on-ramp to sleepaway camp in the region, with mini-camps as a trial run. Lottery means you plan in December, not panic in January. Girls only.

providers.name: Girls Rock! DC Summer Camp
providers.description: A volunteer-powered, social-justice-rooted rock camp where campers form a band, learn an instrument from scratch, write an original song in one week, and perform it at a real DC venue (showcases at the Black Cat). Centers girls, trans, non-binary and gender-expansive youth - especially Black, brown, Indigenous, and native-Washingtonian young people - with radically accessible pay-what-you-can pricing from $0.
providers.locations: Camp site varies year to year (DC school/community buildings); showcase at Black Cat on 14th St NW. Open to DC, MD, VA youth.
providers.price_detail: $0-600 per week, pay-what-you-can sliding scale - no one turned away for inability to pay
providers.hours: Weekday day camp; week ends with Saturday showcase
providers.financial_aid: Built-in: entire tuition is a $0-600 sliding scale
providers.sessions_2026: July 13-18 and August 3-8, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration for 2026 summer camps is CLOSED (filled in spring). Adult volunteer applications ran through April 24.

reg_2027.opens: Spring (roughly February-April); announced via email list
reg_2027.notes: Application, not first-come checkout - priority given to the communities the org centers; demand exceeds spots, so apply as soon as the form opens and join the mailing list in winter. Volunteer applications open around the same time.

providers.fit_notes: For girls and gender-expansive kids who want to make music and find community - zero experience needed and cost is never a barrier. Not for boys (cis boys are outside the program's scope) or families needing guaranteed enrollment on a fixed schedule.

providers.name: Girls Who Code — Pathways Summer Program
providers.description: Girls Who Code's free virtual summer program for high schoolers (girls and nonbinary students): a flexible, mostly self-paced computer-science experience with live components, replacing the famous Summer Immersion Program, which ended after summer 2025. Because it is online, it works from any DC/MoCo bedroom and pairs easily with a part-time job or volunteer gig. Thrives: girls with zero-to-some coding experience who want a structured CS credential and a national peer community without cost.
providers.ages: High school students (grades 9-12)
providers.price_detail: Completely free
providers.locations: Virtual
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 Pathways cohort (multi-week, virtual)
providers.status_2026: Closed — 2026 application ran Jan 8-Apr 10 (early decision Feb 25); program in session. Note: the old in-person Summer Immersion Program is discontinued.

reg_2027.opens: early January (2026 cycle opened Jan 8)
reg_2027.notes: Apply by the early-decision deadline (late February) for best odds; final deadline early April. Low barrier — no experience required — but spots are finite.

providers.financial_aid: Free program
providers.fit_notes: Virtual-only, so no in-person social component locally — combine with an in-person activity. Teens wanting in-person DC coding should look at university pre-college CS tracks instead.

providers.name: Girls Who Code Pathways (free, virtual)
providers.description: Girls Who Code retired its famous Summer Immersion Program after 2025 and replaced it with Pathways: a completely FREE 6-7 week virtual summer program (June 29 - Aug 14, 2026) with self-paced tracks in game design, data science, AI, cybersecurity, and web development plus a Discord community. Zero coding experience required — one of the best free STEM opportunities anywhere for girls and nonbinary students.
providers.ages: Girls and nonbinary students in grades 8-12 during the current school year (rising 9th through graduating seniors)
providers.locations: Fully virtual, self-paced with live community
providers.hours: Self-paced across the 6-7 week window
providers.price_detail: Completely free; laptop/hotspot support available for US students
providers.financial_aid: Free program; device and hotspot access support for students who need it
providers.status_2026: 2026 application window closed (opened Jan 8, closed April 10); program running June 29 - Aug 14, 2026

reg_2027.opens: early January (2026 cycle opened Jan 8)
reg_2027.notes: Short application (two short responses, written or video); early decision deadline ~late Feb, final deadline ~mid-April. Not competitive-selective like the old SIP, but apply early. Note: the in-person Summer Immersion Program no longer exists — ignore outdated listings.

providers.sessions_2026: June 29 - Aug 14, 2026
providers.fit_notes: Best zero-cost on-ramp to coding for teen girls/nonbinary students, and self-paced enough to stack with other camps. Virtual format requires some self-motivation.

providers.name: Glen Echo Park Aquarium Camps
providers.description: Marine-science day camps run by the small teaching aquarium inside Glen Echo Park, with themed weeks (e.g., Chesapeake Critters) built around live animal encounters, ecology, and hands-on science. A gentler, animal-centered alternative to sports or arts camps for younger kids who love creatures.
providers.ages: 3-12 (single-day family camps for 3-5 with a caregiver; most weeklong camps ages 6-8; two camps for 8-12. Campers must be 6 by June 1 to register for weeklong camps)
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park Aquarium, Glen Echo Park, Glen Echo MD
providers.price_detail: $450/week for weeklong camps in 2026; July 4 week single-day mini-camps $75/day; ages 3-5 family camp runs as single-day sessions (~$40/day)
providers.hours: 9am-3pm daily for weeklong camps; family camp is a morning session
providers.extended_care: Park aftercare 3-6pm available for an added weekly fee
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Themed weeks mid-June through mid-August 2026, plus single-day mini-camps the week of July 4 and single-day family camps for ages 3-5
providers.status_2026: Registration opened Feb 4, 2026 at 10am via Glen Echo's system; check ProClass for remaining July/August weeks

reg_2027.opens: early February (Feb 4 at 10am in 2026; camps listed in the January Glen Echo catalog)
reg_2027.notes: Registers through Glen Echo Park's ProClass system. Young-age weeks (6-8) are small-capacity; under-6s cannot enroll in weeklong camps — use the family day camps instead. Contact 301-955-6256 / info@gepaquarium.org with questions.

providers.fit_notes: Great for animal-obsessed 6-10s, with single-day family camps to ease preschoolers in (caregiver attends). Skews young — the oldest band is 8-12, so nothing for teens.

providers.name: Glen Echo Park Camps: Puppetry & Children's Theater (The Puppet Co.)
providers.org: Glen Echo Park Partnership for Arts and Culture / The Puppet Co.
providers.description: Glen Echo Park — the arts-filled historic amusement park that also houses Adventure Theatre — offers a summer camp catalog that includes puppetry and children's-theater offerings connected to The Puppet Co., the only year-round dedicated puppet theatre between New York and Atlanta. Offerings vary year to year (workshops for ages ~8-13 are typical); check the park's camps catalog rather than the Puppet Co. box office.
providers.ages: Park camps serve ages 5-17 overall; puppetry/children's-theater offerings vary by session (puppetry workshops historically ~8-13)
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo, MD (Arcade Building classrooms; Puppet Co. Playhouse)
providers.price_detail: Priced per camp in the Glen Echo Park Spring-Summer 2026 catalog; 2026 puppetry-camp prices not verified — browse the catalog or call the park
providers.financial_aid: Not verified; ask the Glen Echo Park Partnership (info@glenechopark.org)
providers.hours: Most Glen Echo Park camps run 9am-3pm in one-week sessions
providers.sessions_2026: One-week day camps all summer 2026, mostly 9am-3pm, ages 5-17, including theater & puppetry offerings; a dedicated Puppet Co.-branded weekly camp could not be individually verified
providers.status_2026: Registration for Spring & Summer 2026 classes & camps is open; day camps run all summer with theater & puppetry among the camp categories — call 301-634-2222 for current openings

reg_2027.opens: Late winter, when the park's Spring-Summer catalog posts (2026 registration was open by spring)
reg_2027.notes: Registration runs through the Glen Echo Park Partnership's online registration system, not The Puppet Co. itself (The Puppet Co.'s classes page routes to the park). Verify current puppetry camp offerings each year — a dedicated Puppet Co. weekly camp is not guaranteed annually.

providers.phone: 301-634-2222 (park classes/camps); The Puppet Co. box office 301-634-5380
providers.fit_notes: Include on your list only if your kid is specifically puppet-obsessed or you want the Glen Echo Park setting; offerings are workshop-scale, not a full camp brand. Could not verify a 2026 summer puppetry camp on any accessible page — treat as a lead, not a booking.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed glenechopark.org and thepuppetco.org copies (direct fetch blocked): both orgs active, /camp page live, Summer 2026 registration open, camps 9am-3pm ages 5-17 with theater & puppetry category, park phone/email and Puppet Co. box office 301-634-5380. Specific puppetry-camp prices/ages for 2026 not verified.

providers.name: Glen Echo Park Summer Camps
providers.org: Glen Echo Park Partnership for Arts and Culture
providers.description: A historic former amusement park in a national park setting that is now the region's densest cluster of working art studios, offering weeklong camps in ceramics, glassblowing, painting, drawing, dance, theater, music, magic, and more. Kids take camp inside real artist studios (kilns, glass furnace, darkroom) rather than a rec-center classroom, and the ProClass catalog lets you mix different studios week by week. One of the signature arts-camp experiences of the DC area for newcomer families on the MD side.
providers.ages: 4-17 (official range is ages 5-17 with a few camps starting at 4; most core art camps are elementary-age, teen camps to 17)
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD (studios throughout the park)
providers.price_detail: Most weeklong day camps roughly $450-600 depending on studio (example: $495 fine-arts weeks; Aquarium weeks $450); aftercare about $150/week where offered. Verify per camp in the catalog.
providers.hours: Most camps 9am-3pm
providers.extended_care: After Camp extends the day until 6pm for many camps (~$150/week)
providers.financial_aid: None found on the camps pages; ask the registrar (registrar@glenechopark.org, 301-634-2255)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions mid-June through the Aug 24-28 end-of-summer week; catalog posted January, registration opened Feb 4, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open and ongoing per the park's site; many popular early-summer camps full but assorted mid/late-July and August sessions still listed in ProClass, including end-of-summer weeks

reg_2027.opens: early February (Feb 4 in 2026; digital catalog posts in January)
reg_2027.notes: Online-only via the ProClass system; you can build a wish list from the January catalog before registration opens. Glassblowing, ceramics, and the best-known art camps fill within days to weeks; late-summer weeks linger longer.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for kids who want real art-making in real studios and families who like a la carte weekly scheduling. Not a single all-summer camp community — each week can be a different program — and the park's partner-run structure means quality/format varies by studio.

providers.name: Goldfish Swim School Jump Start Clinics (Rockville)
providers.description: A learn-to-swim intensive rather than a camp: daily 30-minute lessons Monday-Thursday (or similar consecutive-day blocks) in a warm 90-degree indoor pool, designed to compress months of weekly-lesson progress into a week or two. Small ratios (4:1) and a famously gentle, confidence-first method make it the practical answer for the newcomer family whose child can't yet swim — a safety prerequisite many DC-area camps quietly assume.
providers.ages: 4 months-12 years (Goldfish's full lesson range; Jump Start clinics serve children across that range by level)
providers.locations: Goldfish Swim School Rockville, 1701 Rockville Pike, Rockville, MD 20852 (serves Bethesda/Potomac families); other DMV franchises exist (e.g., Gaithersburg)
providers.hours: 4 or 5 consecutive days of 30-minute lessons; families pick a daily time slot
providers.extended_care: Not applicable — lesson intensive, not childcare
providers.price_detail: Paid weekly per clinic block (4-5 consecutive days of 30-min lessons); Rockville pricing not published online — other Goldfish locations run roughly $82-130/week plus a $25 annual membership for non-members; call for Rockville rates
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 Jump Start Clinic blocks (4-5 consecutive days, 30 min/day) running through the summer
providers.status_2026: Rockville is 'now booking Summer 2026 Jump Start Clinics' per its official page; weekly consecutive-day blocks run through the summer

reg_2027.opens: Spring (booking typically opens by April-May)
reg_2027.notes: Preferred time slots (early evening, late morning) go first; existing members book before the public. If the goal is 'swim-safe before camp season,' do a clinic block in late spring rather than mid-summer.

providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed goldfishswimschool.com/rockville pages (direct fetch blocked): dedicated Rockville Jump Start Clinics page exists and is booking Summer 2026, format 4-5 consecutive 30-min days, ages 4mo-12yr, address 1701 Rockville Pike, phone 301-640-5592. Rockville-specific pricing unpublished; price band inferred from other Goldfish locations (~$82-130/wk).

providers.name: Goshen Scout Reservation
providers.org: Scouting America, National Capital Area Council
providers.description: NCAC's massive 4,000-acre summer reservation on Lake Merriweather near Goshen, VA (~3.5 hrs from DC): three Scouts BSA camps (Olmsted, Marriott, Bowman), two Cub/Webelos camps (Ross, PMI), and the Lenhok'sin High Adventure backcountry program. The definitive DC-area scouting summer — merit badges, sailing and aquatics on the lake, ranges, and week-long troop camping; individual scouts without a troop trip can attend via the Provisional Camp.
providers.ages: Cub Scouts through Scouts BSA/Venturers (~7-17; Lenhok'sin 13+)
providers.locations: Goshen Scout Reservation, 340 Little India Rd, Goshen, VA 24439 (Rockbridge County, ~3.5 hrs from DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026: youth $580/wk early bird (by Apr 15), $610 after; adults $310-350; $200 non-refundable unit registration fee per troop; all program fees included
providers.hours: Residential, week-long sessions
providers.financial_aid: Council camperships available (Feb deadline)

reg_2027.opens: units reserve sites starting fall 2026; individual fee milestones spring 2027 (2026 pattern: merit badge sign-ups Mar 31, early-bird ends Apr 15)
reg_2027.mechanism: unit (troop/pack) registration via gotogoshen.org; individuals without a unit trip use Provisional Camp
reg_2027.notes: Registration flows through your troop, not parents — ask the troop's Goshen coordinator in the fall. Early-bird pricing ends mid-April. Newcomers: join a troop first; provisional weeks are the solo-scout backdoor.

providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions late June through early August 2026 across all camps; 2026 Leaders Guide published Feb 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; unit slots were set months ago — provisional openings possible, contact council
providers.fit_notes: For scouting families this is the summer anchor; for non-scouts it requires joining. Rustic, phone-free, merit-badge driven — thrives on kids who like structure plus wilderness.

providers.name: Green Acres Camp
providers.description: A progressive, woodsy day camp on Green Acres School's 15-acre North Bethesda campus with its own outdoor pool, mixing swimming, sports, arts, STEM, and performing arts in weekly sessions for ages 4 through rising 8th grade. Mid-market pricing, real financial aid through SSS, and generous multi-week discounts make it one of the more accessible school camps.
providers.ages: 4-14 (age 4 through rising grade 8)
providers.locations: Green Acres School, 11701 Danville Drive, North Bethesda MD 20852
providers.price_detail: Averages ~$460/week (2026); 5% off for 4-7 weeks, 10% off full summer; $65 application fee + $250 deposit per camper
providers.hours: Core day with care from 7:30am to 6:00pm
providers.extended_care: Yes — before care from 7:30am and after care until 6pm
providers.financial_aid: Need-based tuition grants via School and Student Service (SSS) — submit Parents Financial Statement
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June 22-Aug 14, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season runs to Aug 14; weekly-enrollment model means August weeks often have space — check dates-and-rates page

reg_2027.opens: Mid-January for the public (2026: current school and returning camp families first, all others from Jan 15)
reg_2027.notes: Application-then-approval flow (not instant checkout) with priority for returning families; on-site pool weeks and youngest groups are tightest. Aid applicants should start SSS paperwork before January.

providers.fit_notes: A strong value pick with actual financial aid and a pool on campus; smaller and homier than Landon/Bullis, without their sports depth.

providers.name: GW Campus Sports Camps (Nike Tennis at GW + Craig Jones Soccer)
providers.org: George Washington University coaching staffs / US Sports Camps
providers.description: GW's Foxhall-area athletic facilities host two notable summer options: the Nike Tennis Camp at the GW Tennis Center (2100 Foxhall Rd NW) with 16 weekly coed sessions for ages 6-17 at all levels, and Craig Jones/Matt Watts soccer camps run by GW's soccer coaching staffs on the Mount Vernon campus. Convenient for Palisades/Foxhall/Georgetown families wanting college-facility camps without leaving NW.
providers.ages: Tennis: 6-17 (beginner-advanced); soccer camps by age group
providers.locations: GW Tennis Center, 2100 Foxhall Rd NW; GW Mount Vernon campus fields
providers.price_detail: Nike Tennis: $499-789 per 5-day week (full/half day); soccer camps priced separately
providers.hours: Full-day and half-day weekly sessions
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Nike Tennis: 16 sessions June 29 - August 21, 2026; soccer camp dates per program sites
providers.status_2026: Registration open; with 16 tennis sessions through Aug 21, late-summer weeks remain bookable in July

reg_2027.opens: Late fall/winter (US Sports Camps opens next-summer Nike camps around November-January)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via ussportscamps.com for tennis; coach-run websites for soccer. Big session count means this is one of the easier quality camps to get into late — a useful backup when everything else is full.

providers.fit_notes: Reliable, professional, somewhat corporate camp experience; kids get strong instruction but less neighborhood-camp charm. Priciest weeks are the full-day options.

providers.name: GW Pre-College Summer Immersion (incl. Corcoran arts tracks)
providers.description: Two-week, full-day, non-credit intensives for rising 10th-12th graders in downtown DC — international affairs, public policy, biomedical engineering, museum studies, and arts tracks drawing on GW's Corcoran School — using the city itself as a classroom. Its Corcoran-affiliated ArtReach GW Summer Intensive is a separate free arts opportunity for eligible students.
providers.ages: Rising high school sophomores, juniors, and seniors
providers.locations: GW Foggy Bottom campus, Washington DC
providers.hours: Full weekdays for each two-week session (commuter program; no housing in Summer Immersion)
providers.price_detail: Multi-thousand-dollar per-session tuition typical of DC university pre-college programs; exact 2026 fee not verified — confirm on the program page
providers.financial_aid: Not verified for Summer Immersion; ArtReach GW Summer Intensive is free for participants
providers.status_2026: Rolling admission as space allowed; final deadlines were May 31 (Session I), June 14 (Session II), June 28 (Session III) — Session III just closed, program is now underway/complete for 2026

reg_2027.opens: applications typically open in late fall/winter; priority deadline around May 1
reg_2027.notes: Rolling admission — earlier applicants get first pick of topics; apply by the priority deadline (May 1 in 2026) since popular tracks cap. Three session windows let you fit it around other plans.

providers.sessions_2026: Three 2-week sessions across summer 2026
providers.fit_notes: Best for teens who want a resume-credible academic deep-dive plus downtown-DC field experiences. Non-credit; families wanting transcript credit should compare Georgetown Summer College or UMD Terp Young Scholars.

providers.name: Headfirst Summer Camps
providers.description: The DC area's biggest homegrown day-camp operator, running polished multi-activity, STEM ('Innovators, Inc.'), sports, and day camps on rented private-school campuses (St. Albans/National Cathedral School and St. Patrick's in NW DC, Mater Dei and Norwood in Bethesda, Flint Hill in Oakton VA). Known for heavy counselor training, slick logistics, and week-by-week flexibility — a safe default pick for newcomer families with elementary-age kids who want a high-quality general camp without knowing the local landscape.
providers.ages: 3-13 (PreK through rising 7th-8th grade, varies by camp type)
providers.locations: Six DC-metro sites: St. Albans School & National Cathedral School (Cathedral Heights NW DC), St. Patrick's Episcopal Day School (Palisades NW DC), Mater Dei School and Norwood School (Bethesda MD), Flint Hill School (Oakton VA)
providers.price_detail: $675/week verified for Bethesda (Mater Dei) K-7 multi-activity weeks in July 2026; varies by camp and site. Extended day extra.
providers.hours: Core day roughly 8:45am-2:45pm or 9:00am-3:00pm depending on program/site (e.g. Mater Dei K-7 runs 8:45-2:45, PreK 9:00-3:00); optional before/aftercare extends the day
providers.extended_care: Yes — before and aftercare sold by the week at extra cost
providers.financial_aid: None found on official site
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June through August at all sites (Mater Dei PreK weeks run June 8-Aug 10)
providers.status_2026: Enrollment open in-season and site says camps are 'filling quickly'; individual late-July and August weeks may still have space at some sites — check the Camp Finder by week

reg_2027.opens: Not published; join their 'insider list' to be notified when registration opens (historically opens well before spring — exact month could not be verified on the official site)
reg_2027.notes: Week-by-week online enrollment, no lottery. Popular sites (Cathedral Heights, Palisades) and early-summer weeks fill first. No busing — parents drive, so pick the site near you.

providers.fit_notes: Great for K-5 kids who want variety and polish; not for families needing bus transportation, financial aid, or teen programming. One of the pricier general camps.

providers.name: Higher Achievement Summer Academy
providers.description: A no-cost six-week summer academy that is half summer school, half summer camp for academically motivated middle schoolers from under-resourced communities. Scholars take core classes aligned to the year ahead, pick electives like theater and dance, take field trips, and end the summer with day and overnight college visits. It is a multi-year commitment program (afterschool plus summer), not a drop-in camp — kids who want a serious academic boost with mentors thrive here.
providers.ages: Middle school 'scholars' (rising 5th-8th graders)
providers.price_detail: No-cost six-week program — part summer school, part summer camp: core academics, electives (theater, fitness, dance), field trips, and closing college visits, all free
providers.locations: 14 Achievement Centers across the DC Metro region (DC wards plus Alexandria, VA and Prince George's County, MD); org also operates in Baltimore and Richmond. DC HQ: 317 8th Street NE, Washington, DC 20002
providers.hours: Full weekday program during the six-week academy; exact daily hours vary by Achievement Center
providers.financial_aid: Program is entirely free; targeted at students in under-resourced communities

reg_2027.opens: Rolling recruitment, heaviest late winter-spring for the following school year and summer
reg_2027.notes: Families apply to the multi-year scholar program (not just the summer); enrollment goes through the local Achievement Center. Contact the center in your ward or info@higherachievement.org / 202-861-7753. Verify via higherachievement.org in early 2027.

providers.sessions_2026: Six-week Summer Academy, summer 2026 (typically late June-early August); exact dates set per Achievement Center and not published on indexed pages
providers.status_2026: Summer Academy is for enrolled scholars; mid-summer walk-in enrollment is not offered — families should inquire now for the 2026-27 school year cohort
providers.extended_care: Not a wraparound-care camp; hours are program-set
providers.fit_notes: For families who want a free, rigorous academic + enrichment summer and can commit to the year-round program. Not for families seeking flexible week-by-week childcare or a single-week camp.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed higherachievement.org copies (direct fetch blocked): org active, summer-of-opportunities page live and describes the no-cost six-week Summer Academy (academics + electives + trips + college visits) for middle schoolers, 14 DC Metro Achievement Centers incl. Alexandria and Prince George's, HQ address/phone/email. Exact 2026 session dates not published, hence approximate wording.

providers.name: Hirshhorn Teen Studio (free Saturday art workshops)
providers.description: Free art-making workshops for teens on Saturday afternoons at the Hirshhorn: each session mixes gallery time with hands-on experimentation (painting, printmaking, fiber art, sculpture) in the museum's Art School. Weekly during the school year and roughly one Saturday a month in summer - not a day camp, but a reliable free creative outlet year-round.
providers.ages: 13-19 (summer session listings say 13-18)
providers.locations: Hirshhorn Art School, museum lower level, National Mall
providers.price_detail: Free; register once and the registration covers all sessions in the season
providers.hours: Saturdays 2:30-4 pm; weekly during the school year, about one Saturday per month in summer
providers.sessions_2026: Summer Teen Studio runs roughly one Saturday a month through summer 2026 (e.g. 'Show Your Colors!', 'Keep It Tight(-Knit)'); weekly sessions resume with the school year - check the Hirshhorn event calendar for dates
providers.financial_aid: Free program

reg_2027.opens: Rolling - register once per season (online form during the school year; email HMSGEducation@si.edu for summer sessions), then attend any Saturday
reg_2027.notes: No selection, no deadline; join one session or all of them. Good on-ramp for teens who miss the Emerging Artists application window.

providers.status_2026: Active; monthly Summer Teen Studio Saturdays are running through summer 2026 - verify specific dates on the museum event calendar.
providers.fit_notes: All skill levels explicitly welcome, 'novice notebook doodler' up. Useful for newcomer teens to meet peers; not childcare and not full-day, and summer sessions are only about monthly.

providers.name: Hirshhorn Teens: Emerging Artists Program
providers.description: A free, selective 8-week summer intensive for high-school artists at the Smithsonian's modern art museum: two days a week of studio work, gallery time, and behind-the-scenes access with museum professionals, ending in a public showcase of the teens' work. One of the best free arts opportunities in the city for a committed teen.
providers.ages: High school students ages 15-19 in the DC metro area
providers.locations: Hirshhorn Museum, Independence Ave & 7th St SW, National Mall
providers.price_detail: Free; must commit to all 16 sessions plus showcase
providers.hours: Mondays and Tuesdays, 11 am-3 pm
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - August 11, 2026 (Mon/Tue), showcase Saturday August 15
providers.financial_aid: Program is free

reg_2027.opens: Applications open late winter; 2026 applications were due Monday April 6 with decisions by April 24
reg_2027.notes: Open to any DC-metro high schooler 15-19; request application materials by email via the program page. Requires full attendance commitment - treat the early-April deadline as firm.

providers.status_2026: 2026 cohort selected and program in session (June 22-Aug 11); applications closed.
providers.fit_notes: For teens serious about making art and meeting other teen artists - not a drop-in craft camp. Two days/week means it pairs well with a job or another program.

providers.name: Holton-Arms Creative Summer
providers.description: A beloved arts-forward day camp on the Holton-Arms campus on River Road, where campers rotate through visual arts, theater, dance, swimming, and creative play in three-week sessions. One of the area's most demanded camps — core programs were full with closed waitlists by early June 2026 — so it rewards families who plan early.
providers.ages: 4-13 (Creative Fours; Creative Fives/rising K; Junior Campers 6-7; Creative Campers 7-13)
providers.locations: Holton-Arms School, 7303 River Road, Bethesda MD 20817
providers.price_detail: 2026: $1,655 per 3-week day-camp session (~$550/week), $150 deposit; refundable only until May 1
providers.hours: Full camp day; extended day available
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: June 22-July 10; Session 2: July 13-31, 2026
providers.status_2026: FULL — Creative Campers, Junior Campers, and Creative Fives full with waitlists closed as of June 5, 2026; do not count on 2026 space

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January); CampBrain
reg_2027.notes: Among the fastest-filling camps in the guide — 2026 was fully waitlisted before the season started. Register the day it opens; note the three-week session commitment.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for arts-loving elementary kids who thrive in a warm, tradition-rich camp; wrong fit if you need week-by-week flexibility or waited past January.

providers.name: HoopEd Summer Camps
providers.description: A long-running local operator of deliberately small basketball camps (plus multi-sport 'Day Camp' options) at school gyms across DC and close-in Montgomery County, including Grace Episcopal Day School in Kensington. Camps are intentionally capped so every kid gets coaching attention — a good pick for kids who'd get lost at a giant camp. Full-day schedule with cheap, flexible before/aftercare makes it working-parent-friendly.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-13
providers.locations: School-gym sites in NW/NE DC and Montgomery County (e.g., Grace Episcopal Day School, Kensington)
providers.price_detail: $365-375/week; aftercare $15/day (until 5:30 PM); before-care 7:30-9:00 AM available
providers.hours: 9:00 AM - 3:30 PM; before-care from 7:30 AM, aftercare to 5:30 PM
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions mid-June through August 2026 across sites (e.g., Kensington weeks June 29-July 3 and July 20-24)
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; because camps are small, popular weeks fill, but multiple late-July/August weeks across sites usually still open in July

reg_2027.opens: Winter (schedule typically posted January-February)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online per site/week. Small caps mean specific convenient sites fill by spring even though the overall operation is large — book your home-site weeks early.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for kids who want real basketball reps with individual attention; not a spectacle-scale camp, and multi-sport day camp options exist for siblings who don't love hoops.

providers.name: Horizons Greater Washington Summer Academy
providers.description: A completely tuition-free multi-week (roughly five-to-six-week) summer academic-and-enrichment program for K-8 students from underserved DC and Maryland families, hosted on three independent-school campuses: Maret School and St. Patrick's Episcopal Day School in NW DC and Norwood School in Bethesda. Mornings blend literacy and STEAM academics with swimming — every child learns to swim — plus arts, field trips, and confidence-building, at least six hours a day, with meals and daily transportation provided free. Students typically enter after kindergarten via teacher recommendation at partner schools and return every summer through 8th grade.
providers.ages: 5-14 (K-8; students enter after kindergarten and re-enroll each summer through 8th grade)
providers.locations: Maret School (NW DC), St. Patrick's Episcopal Day School (NW DC), Norwood School (Bethesda)
providers.price_detail: Tuition-free (privately funded); breakfast, hot lunch, snacks, supplies, and daily transportation included
providers.financial_aid: Program is free — that is its purpose; eligibility requires free/reduced-price lunch qualification at admission
providers.hours: At least 6 hours/day for 25+ program days
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026: June 26 - July 31 (Maret site, 140 K-8 students; St. Patrick's and Norwood sites run similar calendars)
providers.status_2026: Enrollment closed for 2026 (new-student enrollment event was March 21); program in session now

reg_2027.opens: winter-early spring (2026 new-student event was Saturday March 21); Salesforce application portal
reg_2027.notes: Not open-enrollment: students must qualify for free/reduced-price lunch and are recommended by teachers at partner schools (Bancroft ES, HD Cooke, LAMB PCS, Simon ES, Bishop Walker, Rock Creek Forest ES, Rolling Terrace ES), typically for the summer after kindergarten; siblings of enrolled students get preference. Once in, children keep their seat every summer through 8th grade. Verified via horizonsgreaterwashington.org indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: Life-changing free option — with meals and transportation included — for eligible families at partner schools, but only for them. Newcomers not at a recommending school cannot simply sign up; check whether your assigned school is on the list.

providers.name: iD Tech Camps at American University
providers.org: iD Tech (national provider; DC host campus: American University)
providers.description: The biggest national tech-camp brand, hosted on AU's leafy NW DC campus: weeklong courses in Python, Roblox/Minecraft game dev, AI, 3D printing, and robotics with small groups (roughly 8 students per instructor) of exact-age peers. Best for kids who want a real tech curriculum and a taste of a college campus; note the premium price.
providers.ages: 7-17 (grouped by age; separate teen tracks)
providers.locations: American University, 4400 Massachusetts Ave NW, Washington DC. NOTE: the Georgetown University site is NOT running in summer 2026 — AU is the only DC host campus.
providers.hours: Day camps roughly 9 am - 5 pm Mon-Fri; day and overnight options advertised
providers.price_detail: Day camps from $1,149/week at the AU campus (2026 published starting price; iD Tech's cheapest campuses start at $949 but AU is not one of them); overnight room-and-board upgrade additional (approx. $679/week, not re-verified); payment plans from $375 down
providers.extended_care: Not verified; iD Tech typically offers extended-day add-ons — confirm at checkout
providers.financial_aid: None found on location page
providers.status_2026: Season runs June 23 - Aug 8, 2026 at AU; registration open, late-season weeks may still have seats. Georgetown campus confirmed not operating in 2026 (iD Tech's own page says so).

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (iD Tech opens next-summer registration in Oct-Nov with early-bird pricing)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come by course/week; popular courses (Roblox, Minecraft, AI) at popular weeks sell out by spring but the season rarely sells out entirely. Book by winter for best course choice and early-bird discount.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, June 23 - Aug 8, 2026
providers.fit_notes: Great for kids already excited about games/coding who want structured skill-building. Expensive relative to local options; beginners are welcome but a motivated kid gets the most value.

providers.name: Identity Youth Summer Programming
providers.description: Montgomery County's main organization for Latino and other historically underserved youth runs free summer activities out of its Youth Opportunity Centers (Gaithersburg, Wheaton area) and school-based sites: soccer and outdoor sports nights, skill-building and academic-gap workshops, family events, and wraparound services — all bilingual and trauma-informed. Aimed squarely at teens who fall through the cracks of paid camp culture.
providers.ages: Ages 7-25 (per Identity's official description); summer activities skew middle school through young adult
providers.price_detail: Free
providers.locations: Main office: 620 E Diamond Ave Suite K, Gaithersburg, MD. Youth Opportunity Centers: 415 E Diamond Ave, Gaithersburg (240-702-6127) and 7676 New Hampshire Ave #411, Takoma Park (240-670-8122). Wellness Centers at Gaithersburg, Kennedy, Northwood, Seneca Valley, Watkins Mill, and Wheaton high schools
providers.hours: Varies by site; evening and daytime summer activities
providers.financial_aid: All programming free

reg_2027.opens: Rolling; summer activity schedules posted late spring
reg_2027.notes: No formal camp registration — youth connect through the two Youth Opportunity Centers (Gaithersburg and Takoma Park), school Wellness Centers, or by calling. Spanish-language intake available. Just show up or call in June.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 sports, therapeutic recreation, and skill-building programming across Montgomery County sites via YOCs and Wellness Centers
providers.status_2026: Open and rolling through summer 2026 (drop-in/ongoing services model; specific 2026 summer activity calendar not published on indexed pages)
providers.fit_notes: The right door for Latino/immigrant teens in mid-Montgomery County who need free, welcoming summer structure — not a childcare-hours day camp for younger kids, and not activity-registration in the suburban-camp sense.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed identity-youth.org copies (direct fetch blocked): org active serving ages 7-25, free services, main office 620 E Diamond Ave Ste K / 301-963-5900 / info@identity-youth.org, two YOCs (Gaithersburg + Takoma Park, added), six high-school Wellness Centers as listed. Specific 2026 summer schedule not verifiable online, hence medium.

providers.name: Imagination Stage Summer Camps
providers.description: The region's flagship children's theatre-arts organization, with a purpose-built Bethesda facility and satellite camp sites in Rockville and NW DC. Offers 1- to 4-week camps in acting, musical theatre, dance, and filmmaking for ages 4-18, from playful early-childhood drama to production-level teen programs. The default first choice for many MoCo families; polished, well-staffed, and strong on inclusion supports for kids with disabilities.
providers.ages: 4-18 (pre-K through rising grade 12; programs banded by age)
providers.locations: Main facility 4908 Auburn Ave, Bethesda; satellite camp sites in Rockville and Northwest DC (check each camp's listed site)
providers.price_detail: 2026 per-week tuition not verified on an open page; ranges widely by length (1-4 weeks) and half vs full day. Payment plans available (set up by Apr 1, paid in full by May 1). Late pickup fee $10 + $1/min after 15 min.
providers.financial_aid: Tuition assistance awarded on financial need, rolling basis (contact Pam Freedy via imaginationstage.org); payment plans for all camps
providers.hours: Varies by camp; half-day options for youngest ages, full-day for most
providers.extended_care: Before and after care offered at main sites (details/prices on registration pages)
providers.sessions_2026: 1-, 2-, 3-, and 4-week sessions across the summer at Bethesda, Rockville, and NW DC sites; summer preview grid published Dec 10, 2025
providers.status_2026: Registration open as of mid-July; a multi-site weekly structure means later-summer sessions often still have seats, but popular musical-theatre weeks fill by spring

reg_2027.opens: December (2027 preview grid typically posts ~Dec 10; registration opens mid-to-late December)
reg_2027.notes: Popular sessions and best-known productions fill by early spring; register in December-January for full choice. Payment-plan setup closes April 1. Tuition assistance is rolling — apply early. Flag accessibility needs at registration; their Access team supports inclusion.

providers.fit_notes: Best all-around starter theatre camp — genuinely beginner-friendly at every age, with real craft for kids who get serious. Not the pick for a teen wanting a gritty conservatory vibe; teen programs skew polished/musical. Official site blocked our direct fetch; details verified via search-indexed copies of official pages.

providers.name: International School of Music Summer Music Camps
providers.description: Bethesda/Potomac's large private music academy runs summer camps and group classes - keyboard exploration, singing, creative music/improvisation, and 'Instrument Explorers' (first touch of wind, string, and percussion instruments) - alongside its core private-lesson program. A polished, lesson-school environment suited to families who want summer to feed into year-round study.
providers.ages: Group classes/camps mainly young children (~3-12: keyboard, vocal, and Instrument Explorers classes); private lessons from age 5 through adult
providers.locations: ISM campuses in Bethesda (4701 Sangamore Rd, Suite LL-03, Bethesda, MD 20816) and Potomac, MD
providers.price_detail: Pricing not published on indexed pages; contact 301-365-5888 (free trial offered)
providers.hours: Varies by program; largely half-day/class-length blocks
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Summer camps/classes scheduled July 12-August 8, 2026 per the ISM 2025/2026 calendar
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer camp window begins around July 12 per the school calendar; enrollment handled directly — contact the school for openings

reg_2027.opens: Spring; rolling
reg_2027.notes: Rolling enrollment by phone/online; free trial offered. Rarely a high-pressure deadline — a low-stress option for late planners in Bethesda/Potomac.

providers.fit_notes: Best for preschool-elementary kids starting music in a structured lesson-school setting, or ISM students keeping momentum in summer. Not a full-day camp solution for working parents; verify schedule blocks.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed ismw.org copies (direct fetch blocked): summer-music-camps page live, two campuses (Bethesda Sangamore Rd + Potomac), enrollment phone 301-365-5888 with free trial, info@ismw.org, 2025/26 calendar showing summer programs July 12-Aug 8. Camp pricing and exact per-camp ages not published, so medium.

providers.name: International Spy Museum — Spy Camp Teen Volunteer Program
providers.description: High-energy teens (15-18) staff the Spy Museum's popular Spy Camp for kids: preparing 'mission' materials, running carpool check-in/out, and supporting counselors for both camp weeks, earning about 80 volunteer hours plus behind-the-scenes museum experience. Requires an evening training session and full availability 8am-4pm for both weeks. Thrives: reliable, outgoing teens who want a short, intense, resume-worthy volunteer gig with a cool-factor museum.
providers.price_detail: Volunteer position; earns ~80 service hours
providers.hours: 8:00am-4:00pm daily for both camp weeks, plus one 4-hour evening training
providers.locations: International Spy Museum, 700 L'Enfant Plaza SW
providers.sessions_2026: Camp weeks July 13-17 and July 20-24, 2026; training July 9
providers.status_2026: Full — 2026 recruitment is complete; camp weeks start July 13.

reg_2027.opens: January 2027 (museum states summer 2027 applications post in January)
reg_2027.notes: Small corps, fills well before summer — apply in January. Must commit to BOTH weeks plus training. Listed with the Montgomery County Volunteer Center, so hours generally count for MCPS SSL (confirm with your school).

providers.financial_aid: N/A — volunteer program
providers.fit_notes: Only a two-week commitment — great as one building block of a patchwork teen summer, useless as full-summer coverage.

providers.name: Isabella & Ferdinand Outdoor Spanish Summer Camp
providers.description: A 100% Spanish-immersion outdoor day camp for ages 5-13 across partner sites and outdoor venues in DC — sports (2026 theme: World Cup), art, adventures, and outdoor learning, entirely in Spanish, with a flexible pick-your-weeks model across nine weeks. The strongest full-day Spanish immersion option for elementary and middle-school kids in the District proper.
providers.locations: Partner sites and outdoor venues in Washington DC (varies by session)
providers.price_detail: $560/week; before-care $80/wk (7:30-8:30am); aftercare $120/wk (3:00-5:30pm)
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.hours: Core day to 3:00pm; before-care from 7:30am, aftercare to 5:30pm
providers.sessions_2026: Nine weeks, June 22 - August 21, 2026 (Weeks 1-9); pick any combination
providers.status_2026: Season running through Aug 21 — remaining July weeks (Jul 13, Jul 20, Jul 27) and all August weeks were bookable per the 2026 camp page; one of the better late-summer options

reg_2027.opens: Around December-January (rolling)
reg_2027.notes: First-come pick-your-weeks online; runs deep into August (rare!), which makes its late-summer weeks valuable — but also means early weeks fill first. Groups are leveled, so true beginners are placed appropriately.
