providers.fit_notes: Best for active kids — it's genuinely outdoor and physical, in Spanish all day. Full-coverage hours with before/aftercare suit working parents. Covers the awkward mid-August gap most camps ignore.

providers.name: JAM Camp (Jazz Academy of Music)
providers.description: A two-week afternoon jazz camp (1-5pm) led by the Jazz Academy of Music (saxophonist Paul Carr's long-running Montgomery County jazz-education nonprofit) at North Chevy Chase Christian Church. Instrumentalists and vocalists ages 8-18 focus squarely on jazz improvisation - learning to solo - with combos, theory, and a final performance; the half-day format pairs well with a morning camp.
providers.ages: 8-18, instrumental and vocal
providers.locations: North Chevy Chase Christian Church, Chevy Chase MD (Jazz Academy also runs a Southern Maryland camp)
providers.price_detail: $995 for the full two-week camp incl. music and t-shirt (~$500/week but half-day); $200 deposit starts a payment plan; full payment due June 1
providers.hours: 1:00-5:00pm, Monday-Friday, two weeks
providers.financial_aid: Payment plans offered; scholarships not confirmed
providers.sessions_2026: June 29-July 10, 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 session has ended (concluded July 10). Season over for this provider.

reg_2027.opens: Winter/early spring; payment plans begin April 1
reg_2027.notes: Rolling registration with payment due June 1; doesn't have the frenzy of the big day camps, but jazz-serious families register by spring. Some playing experience helps a lot.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who play an instrument (or sing) and specifically want to learn to improvise - it's the most improvisation-focused option in Montgomery County. Afternoon-only schedule is a plus for camp-stackers, a minus for full-day childcare.

providers.name: Johns Hopkins CTY Day Program at The Woods Academy (Bethesda)
providers.org: Johns Hopkins University Center for Talented Youth (CTY)
providers.description: New for 2026: the storied Johns Hopkins gifted-education program opened a Bethesda day site at The Woods Academy, running three one-week-style courses (June 28 - July 17) like The Physics of Engineering, Numbers: Zero to Infinity, and Shaping Our World: Architecture for advanced learners in grades 2-6. Admission requires joining CTY and qualifying test scores — this is a plan-ahead program, not a sign-up camp.
providers.ages: Grades 2-6, advanced learners with CTY-qualifying test scores
providers.locations: The Woods Academy, 6801 Greentree Rd, Bethesda MD (also Gilman School and JHU Homewood sites in Baltimore)
providers.hours: 9:00 am - 4:00 pm Mon-Fri
providers.price_detail: Tuition not verified for the Woods Academy site; CTY day programs are premium-priced and CTY offers need-based financial aid
providers.financial_aid: CTY has an established need-based financial aid program — apply alongside enrollment
providers.status_2026: Running June 28 - July 17, 2026; enrollment opened January and courses were 'filling fast' by March per local coverage

reg_2027.opens: January (2026 enrollment opened in January; first-come after eligibility established)
reg_2027.notes: Two-step: (1) join CTY and submit/sit for qualifying test scores — do this in fall; (2) enroll first-come when courses open in January. New MoCo site means local demand is high; do not wait on testing.

providers.sessions_2026: Three weeks, June 28 - July 17, 2026 (weekly courses)
providers.fit_notes: For genuinely advanced, school-loving kids who want intellectual peers — the CTY credential also unlocks their famous online and residential programs later. Testing requirement makes it impossible to book last-minute.

providers.name: Jones-Haywood Dance School Summer Program
providers.description: Founded in 1941 by Doris Jones and Claire Haywood to train Black ballet dancers when other schools would not, Jones-Haywood is a DC cultural landmark (alumni include Chita Rivera and Sylvester Campbell) still training dancers in NW DC. Its summer program offers all-day training in ballet, tap, jazz, and modern plus fitness, creative writing/poetry, and a student-choreography movement lab; select advanced students access travel intensives (Cannes, Martha's Vineyard).
providers.ages: Roughly 4-18 (grouped by level; conservatory track for serious students)
providers.price_detail: Tuition not published online; historically kept affordable with scholarships as part of the school's access mission - call for current rates
providers.locations: 1200 Delafield Pl NW, Washington DC 20011 (Petworth/16th Street corridor)
providers.hours: All-day summer programming (ballet, tap, jazz, modern, conditioning, creative writing, choreography lab, dance careers course)
providers.financial_aid: Scholarship support consistent with the school's founding mission of serving students of all socio-economic backgrounds; inquire directly

reg_2027.opens: Spring; enrollment direct through the school
reg_2027.notes: Register via joneshaywooddanceschool.com or by phone (202-882-4039); placement may determine level. Advanced travel intensives (Cannes, France and Oak Bluffs, Martha's Vineyard) are by selection. Not a mass-market camp - classes are intimate and can fill.

providers.sessions_2026: All-day summer training program, summer 2026 (specific dates unpublished)
providers.status_2026: School is active (site live, listings updated 2026); summer 2026 session dates and openings not published online - verify directly with the school office
providers.extended_care: All-day format; no separate aftercare published
providers.fit_notes: A storied, nurturing place for young Black dancers (all welcome) to get serious classical training without Kennedy-Center-conservatory prices. Families wanting casual one-week dance sampling should look at studio camps instead. Pricing unverified online — call.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via indexed copies of joneshaywooddanceschool.com/programs and /contact plus third-party listings updated Feb 2026: org active, URL correct, summer all-day program and travel intensives confirmed, phone/email/address added; 2026 dates and tuition remain unpublished.

providers.name: Joy of Motion Dance Center Summer Camps
providers.description: DC's big everybody-dances nonprofit studio runs weekly themed camps (ballet, hip hop, musical theatre, choreography, world dance, plus specialty 'Triple Threat' and 'Little Pop Star' weeks) at its home inside the Atlas Performing Arts Center on H Street NE. Explicitly welcoming to brand-new dancers alongside experienced ones, with a joyful recreational vibe rather than a conservatory one. One of the strongest financial-aid programs of any DC dance studio.
providers.ages: 5-14, split into Lower Level (5-9) and Upper Level (10-14) camps; classes from age 2
providers.locations: Atlas Performing Arts Center, 1333 H Street NE, Washington, DC 20002 (Atlas district); check site for additional studio locations
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-4:00pm
providers.price_detail: $425/week ($340 for the shortened July 4th week) per search-indexed official page; before care $15/week add-on, aftercare $25; supervised lunch included for full-day campers
providers.financial_aid: Yes - entirely need-based aid awarded at 100%, 75%, or 50% of tuition (HUD income thresholds for the Washington/Arlington metro area); submit the financial-aid application before enrolling; reviewed in 3-5 business days
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly camps late June through mid-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open (registerjomdc.org live) with weekly camps running into mid-August; official page copy shows camps late June through mid-August

reg_2027.opens: winter/early spring (rolling)
reg_2027.notes: Rarely a panic sign-up: individual weeks stay available later than most DC camps. Financial-aid families should apply and receive a decision before enrolling, so start that paperwork in late winter. Full camp day is 9-4 with optional before care and aftercare stretching coverage to 8am-6pm.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via indexed copies of joyofmotion.org summer-camps and tuition-financial-aid pages: ages 5-14, $425/wk ($340 July 4th week), 9-4 hours, 8-9am/4-6pm care, Atlas location, phone, email, tiered 100/75/50% need-based aid all confirmed; exact 2026 week dates in indexed copy may be stale, so medium.

providers.name: Jr. Wizards & Mystics Summer Basketball Camps
providers.description: The official Wizards/Mystics youth camps: week-long coed Jr. Wizards sessions plus girls-only Mystics camps at school gyms around the DMV, including DC sites (620 Michigan Ave NE) and suburban locations. Tuition includes a reversible jersey, t-shirt, and two tickets to a Wizards/Mystics home game, which makes it a favorite for NBA/WNBA-fan kids. Instruction is fundamentals-focused and welcoming to all levels ages 6-14.
providers.locations: Multiple DMV sites incl. Washington DC (620 Michigan Ave NE, sessions July 6-24), Bowie MD, Episcopal HS Alexandria VA
providers.price_detail: $375/week standard; Black Friday rate $275/week if registered by Dec 15; $100 of tuition is a non-refundable registration fee (refundable balance through April 1)
providers.hours: 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM Mon-Fri
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Mid-June through early August 2026; DC (Michigan Ave NE) weeks July 6-10, 13-17, 20-24; Bowie July 27-31 & Aug 3-7; Alexandria Aug 3-7
providers.status_2026: Season underway; late-July and early-August sessions (Bowie, Episcopal HS) typically still bookable in July

reg_2027.opens: Fall (registration open by November, with Black Friday discount pricing through Dec 15)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via LeagueApps (dcfamily.com). Insider tip: register before Dec 15 and save $100/week. Refund-friendly through April 1 (minus $100 fee), so early booking is low-risk.

providers.fit_notes: Fun, brand-name basketball week for fans and rec players; girls who want an all-girls environment should pick the Mystics-branded sessions. Serious AAU-level players may find it too fundamentals-oriented.

providers.name: JTCC Future Champs Summer Camp
providers.description: JTCC in College Park is one of the top junior tennis training centers in the United States — a nonprofit campus that has produced tour pros — and its Future Champs summer camp brings that coaching to everyday kids ages 4-18, grouped tightly by age and level. Full-day and half-day options run all summer, and strong players can be spotted for JTCC's champions pathway. Included despite sitting just over the DC line in Prince George's County because it is the region's flagship tennis facility.
providers.ages: 4-18, grouped by age and skill
providers.locations: JTCC, 5200 Campus Dr, College Park MD (just outside NE DC); satellite programs incl. Chantilly VA
providers.price_detail: Full-day weekly rates typically in the $500-700 range, half-day less (verify at jtccregister.org); credits available if cancelled 2+ weeks out
providers.hours: Mon-Fri, full-day and half-day options
providers.financial_aid: JTCC is a nonprofit with need-based aid across its programs; ask about camp scholarships
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly Monday-Friday sessions all summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration open (opened winter 2026); mid/late-summer weeks generally still bookable in July

reg_2027.opens: January-February (2026 registration was announced open in winter)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online; capacity is large so panic-booking isn't needed, but preferred weeks/full-day slots go by late spring. No refunds within 14 days of your camp week; account credit between May 1 and 2 weeks out.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for any kid serious about tennis, and still friendly to beginners; families wanting a cheaper neighborhood option should compare Rock Creek Tennis Center or Montgomery TennisPlex.

providers.name: KEEN Greater DC (Kids Enjoy Exercise Now)
providers.description: Completely free adaptive sports and recreation for young people with developmental or physical disabilities, built on a 1:1 pairing of each athlete with a trained volunteer. Sessions (sports, swim, music, bowling, Zumba, fitness) run year-round on weekends — including through the summer — at sites in DC, Montgomery County, and Northern Virginia. It is not a weekday camp, but it is one of very few zero-cost, no-child-turned-away recreation options for kids with significant disabilities.
providers.locations: Rotating sites in DC (e.g., Deanwood Aquatic Center), Montgomery County, and NoVA (Vienna, Fairfax)
providers.price_detail: All programs 100% free to athletes and families
providers.financial_aid: Not applicable — everything is free
providers.hours: Weekend sessions, typically 1.5-3 hours (e.g., Sat/Sun mornings and afternoons)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 weekend sessions confirmed (e.g., June 21: MD Kids Sports, VA Sports, Zumba, bowling)
providers.status_2026: Running through summer 2026; new athletes accepted on a rolling basis — most programs currently have room, some have waitlists

reg_2027.opens: rolling year-round
reg_2027.notes: Submit the New Athlete Registration Form any time; athletes are added to programs continuously and waitlisted where full. Submit early even if waitlisted. Verified via keengreaterdc.org indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: For kids with significant disabilities who need true 1:1 support and zero cost — a supplement to (not substitute for) weekday camp coverage, since sessions are short and on weekends. Not for families needing full-day summer childcare.

providers.name: Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens free summer camps & Junior Ranger programs
providers.org: National Park Service (with Friends of Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens)
providers.description: DC's only national park devoted to water gardens — acres of lotus and water lilies on the Anacostia in Ward 7 — runs free week-long summer (and spring break) day camps specifically for Ward 7 students in grades 2-8, exploring wetland plants and wildlife in their own backyard. Year-round free ranger walks, a Junior Ranger badge booklet, and the huge free Lotus & Water Lily Festival (July 18-19, 2026) round out one of the city's best zero-cost nature offerings.
providers.ages: Camps: 2nd-8th grade Ward 7 students; ranger programs and festival: all ages
providers.price_detail: Everything free — entry, camps, ranger programs, festival
providers.locations: Kenilworth Park & Aquatic Gardens, 1550 Anacostia Ave NE, Washington DC
providers.hours: Camps run week-long day sessions; park open daily
providers.financial_aid: N/A — free

reg_2027.opens: Spring announcements via nps.gov/keaq and Friends of Kenilworth Aquatic Gardens
reg_2027.notes: The free Ward 7 camps have small capacity and are announced through the park and Friends group — call (202) 692-6080 or watch kenaqgardens.org in March-April 2027. Junior Ranger and festival need no registration.

providers.sessions_2026: Week-long free camps for Ward 7 2nd-8th graders; Lotus & Water Lily Festival July 18-19, 2026, 10am-8pm
providers.status_2026: Festival upcoming next weekend (July 18-19, 2026); drop-in ranger programs running all summer
providers.fit_notes: A hidden-gem free nature camp if you live in Ward 7, and a spectacular free family outing for everyone else in mid-July when the lotuses bloom. Camp eligibility is geographically restricted; drop-in programs are not.

providers.name: Kennedy Center Dance Lab (ON HIATUS for summer 2026)
providers.description: A selective summer intensive for about 30 high school dancers (entering grades 9-12) featuring daily ballet and modern technique, choreography, dance-health, and career/leadership training at the Kennedy Center. The official application page states the program is on hiatus for summer 2026 due to a scheduling conflict, with summer 2027 information promised in fall 2026. Worth calendaring for 2027 if you have a serious pre-professional dancer.
providers.ages: Entering grades 9-12 (approx. 14-18), pre-professional dancers
providers.locations: Kennedy Center, 2700 F St NW (Foggy Bottom)
providers.price_detail: Tuition for future summers not published; could not verify past cost from official sources
providers.financial_aid: none found (unverified - check when 2027 details post)
providers.sessions_2026: None - on hiatus
providers.status_2026: Not running summer 2026 - official page confirms hiatus due to a scheduling conflict and directs applicants to check back in fall for summer 2027. Kennedy Center's school-year Ballet Class Series and Contemporary Dance Series master classes continue as alternatives.

reg_2027.opens: Official page says check back fall 2026 for summer 2027 program information; past applications ran winter/spring via smapply portal
reg_2027.notes: Selective application (dance experience/essays, video component in past cycles); only ~30 spots. Check kennedy-center.org pre-professional training page starting September 2026.

providers.fit_notes: For ambitious teen dancers seeking a leadership-oriented intensive rather than a technique-only summer program. Verified via search-indexed copies of official Kennedy Center/smapply pages (direct fetch blocked).
providers.hours: Two-week intensive (daily schedule posted per cycle)
providers.verification_note: Verified via current indexed copy of the official kennedy-center.org Dance Lab page (2026): hiatus for summer 2026 confirmed verbatim ('check back this fall for information about the summer 2027 program'), grades 9-12 and two-week format confirmed; tuition genuinely unpublished.

providers.name: Key Bridge Boathouse Youth Paddling Camp
providers.org: Boating in DC / Adventures Unbound (NPS concessioner)
providers.description: A kayak/canoe/paddleboard day camp launching from the Georgetown waterfront under Key Bridge, capped at 21 kids per week with an 8:1 staff ratio and themed days (river-detective paddles, balance challenges, water games). Kids build real paddling confidence on the Potomac in the middle of the city. Run by the same concessioner operating DC's public boathouses.
providers.ages: 9-14 (per official camp pages)
providers.locations: Key Bridge Boathouse, 3500 Water St NW, Georgetown, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: $572/week base (2026); stackable discounts to max 20% off ($457.60); early-bird discount for registering early; week of June 29-July 2 is 20% off (no camp July 3)
providers.hours: Standard camp day (June-August camp line: 703-223-2451)
providers.financial_aid: None found; early-bird and stacking discounts only
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, June 22 - August 14, 2026 (8 weeks, alternating Theme A/B; no camp July 3)
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; 2026 camps confirmed running weekly June 22 - August 14 with alternating A/B theme weeks - check remaining late-July/August weeks directly for availability

reg_2027.opens: January (registration opens in January per official camp page; early-bird discount for early registrants)
reg_2027.notes: First-come; the January early-bird window is the signal to book. Small weekly capacity means prime mid-summer weeks go early even though the brand is low-hype.

providers.fit_notes: Urban-adventure sweet spot for water-loving kids in DC proper — walkable/bikeable from Georgetown. Swimmers only; no aftercare.
providers.verification_note: Verified via current indexed copies of boatingindc.com/camps and capital.adventuresunbound.com/summer-camps (2026): ages corrected to official 9-14, $572/week base, max 20% stacked discount ($457.60), June 22-Aug 14 weekly schedule, camp phone 703-223-2451, and January registration opening all confirmed on official pages.

providers.name: KID Museum Summer Camp
providers.description: The region's flagship maker-learning museum runs summer camps led by professional maker educators, blending woodworking, electronics, robotics, fabrication, and craft into hands-on project weeks that end in a Friday 3:30pm family showcase. It is the definitive 'maker' camp in close-in Montgomery County, at Bethesda Metro Center and the Davis Library site.
providers.ages: approximately 6-14 (strictly grade-banded - KID Museum enrolls by grade level, not age; verify bands per week on the site)
providers.locations: 3 Bethesda Metro Center, Suite 140, Bethesda MD 20814 (primary); some weeks historically at Davis Library, 6400 Democracy Blvd, Bethesda - verify location per week
providers.price_detail: 2026 weekly price not verified from official copy; a 10%-off promo (code SC2026) ran through July 5, 2026 (now expired)
providers.hours: Full-day weeks with Friday 3:30pm camp showcase (exact hours vary by camp)
providers.financial_aid: Camps available free or at reduced cost for families who need assistance via the 'Access for Every Maker' initiative; educator and military discounts on family programming
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed camps across summer 2026 (cardboard construction, music production, maker skills, space, wizarding, and more) in Bethesda
providers.status_2026: Still open - registration stays open until camps sell out; the 10% SC2026 promo ended July 5, 2026. Good bet for late availability

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration open by early in the year; runs until camps sell out)
reg_2027.notes: Registers via their iClassPro portal. Fills steadily rather than instantly - a realistic option even for spring planners, though specific popular weeks/grade bands sell out. Enrollment is by grade, and age restrictions are enforced.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for kids who like building, tinkering, and tools more than drawing — real saws, soldering, and sewing machines with trained educators. Less suited to kids wanting traditional fine art or an outdoor-heavy summer.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via current indexed copies of kid-museum.org summer-camp pages (2026): registration-until-sellout, SC2026 promo through July 5, Friday 3:30pm showcase, Bethesda Metro Center Suite 140 address, phone, and email confirmed; weekly price and exact hours not visible in indexed copy, and Davis Library location for 2026 unverified.

providers.name: Kids After Hours (KAH) Summer Camps
providers.description: A long-running MoCo before/after-school operator that converts elementary schools into summer day camps — KAH Day Camp for elementary kids, Explorers (grades 3-4, $510/session), Adventure Camp (grade 4+, $575/session with big field trips), Travelin' Teens (grades 4-9), and Skate Camp (7-12). Extended care is baked into the price, which makes it one of the more working-parent-friendly private camps in the county.
providers.ages: Entering K through 9th grade depending on program: KAH Day Camp K-5th, Skate Camp ages 7-12, Explorers rising 3rd-4th, Adventure Camp rising 5th-9th
providers.locations: Barnsley & Farmland ES (Rockville), Flora Singer & Glen Haven ES (Silver Spring), Garrett Park ES (Kensington), William Tyler Page ES (Burtonsville)
providers.hours: Full day with before/after care included; final week (Aug 10-14) runs adjusted hours 8:30am-4:30pm
providers.price_detail: KAH Day Camp $390/session, Skate Camp $425/session, Explorers $510/session, Adventure Camp $575/session (trip spending money extra); before/after care included
providers.financial_aid: Not advertised on official pages — ask office (301-933-6888)
providers.sessions_2026: 8 weeks, June 22-Aug 14, 2026; final week (Aug 10-14) is a field-trip week
providers.status_2026: In session; 2026 summer confirmed running 8 weeks June 22-Aug 14 across all six MoCo school sites — call about remaining space (site blocks automated fetch; verified via official kidsafterhours.com pages in the search index)

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; KAH historically posts summer details and opens registration in winter (roughly December-February)
reg_2027.mechanism: rolling first-come by session, registered online via kidsafterhours.com
reg_2027.notes: Less frenzied than MoCo Rec's registration day; many families register at their school-year KAH site. 2027 dates unannounced as of 2026-07-12 — pattern-based, not an announced fact.

providers.fit_notes: Practical, unfussy full-coverage camp at neighborhood schools; not a specialty or nature experience. Good default for two-working-parent households near a KAH site.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed 2026 dates (Jun 22-Aug 14), all program tiers and prices ($390/$425/$510/$575), all six school locations incl. William Tyler Page/Burtonsville, phone, and final-week hours via multiple search-indexed copies of official kidsafterhours.com pages (summer-camp-overview, explorers, adventure-camp, kah-day-camp, locations). 2027 registration opening is pattern-based, not announced.

providers.name: Knock On Wood Tap Studio Summer Session
providers.description: A beloved rhythm-tap institution (est. 1994) two blocks from Takoma Metro, notable for its multigenerational, diverse community and roots in Black percussive-dance tradition. Summer is a class session rather than a day camp: kids and teens can join weekly tap classes June-August, with drop-in options and special workshops (percussive dance samplers, improv jams). Best as a supplement — an inexpensive way for a kid to try tap — not a full-day childcare solution.
providers.ages: Age 4 through teen, grouped by age/level; adult classes too
providers.locations: 6925 Willow Street NW, Suite 101, Washington, DC 20012 (Takoma, 2 blocks from Takoma Metro, across from CVS)
providers.hours: Class-schedule based, not camp hours; summer session June 22-Aug 9, 2026 (no classes July 2-5)
providers.extended_care: Not applicable — class session, not a camp
providers.price_detail: Drop-in $23/class; class cards $22/class in 5- or 10-packs (cards valid 4 months)
providers.financial_aid: None advertised
providers.sessions_2026: Summer session June 22-August 9, 2026 (closed July 2-5); workshops incl. North American Percussive Dance Sampler with Becky Hill July 14-Aug 4 (4 sessions) and improv workshop/jam July 19
providers.status_2026: Open — summer 2026 schedule posted; drop-in and rolling registration mid-session is normal here

reg_2027.opens: Spring (summer schedule typically posted around May)
reg_2027.mechanism: rolling/first-come via MindBody account; drop-in always welcome
reg_2027.notes: No sign-up race. Kids can drop in with tap shoes mid-summer, which makes this a great fill-in around other camps. Registration/class cards run through MindBody.

providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed 2026 session dates (Jun 22-Aug 9, closed Jul 2-5), address, prices ($23 drop-in/$22 class cards), age-4 minimum, MindBody registration, workshops, and phone 202-722-1625 via search-indexed copies of official knockonwood.org pages (summer-session-2026, class-faqs, register-pay) plus Main Street Takoma listing.

providers.name: Kode With Klossy (free coding camp, DC site)
providers.description: Karlie Kloss's foundation runs FREE two-week intensive coding camps (web dev, mobile apps, machine learning, data science) for young women and gender-expansive teens 13-18, with Washington DC among its in-person host cities for 2026 alongside a virtual option. No experience needed; the cohort community and instructors get rave reviews.
providers.ages: 13-18, young women and gender-expansive youth
providers.locations: Washington DC in-person site (exact host venue announced to admitted students; varies by year); virtual camps also offered
providers.hours: Full weekdays for the two-week session
providers.price_detail: Completely free
providers.financial_aid: Free program
providers.status_2026: 2026 applications closed March 31; camps running June-August 2026 in DC and other cities

reg_2027.opens: typically winter (applications open by early in the year; 2026 deadline was March 31)
reg_2027.notes: Application with two short-response questions (written or video). Free and popular, so treat the deadline seriously — set a January reminder to apply.

providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions in June, July, and August 2026
providers.fit_notes: Excellent free alternative to $1,000+/week commercial tech camps for eligible teens. Boys are not eligible; families needing all-summer coverage should note it is a single two-week session.

providers.name: Labyrinth Games & Puzzles Summer Camps
providers.description: Capitol Hill's beloved board-game shop runs screen-free Board Game Camp (ages 8+) and Game Design Camp (ages 7+): mornings-to-afternoons of curated modern board games, strategy analysis, and — in design camp — building and playtesting your own game. It's a cult favorite with tiny capacity, run by people who take games seriously as a craft.
providers.ages: 7+ (Game Design) and 8+ (Board Game Camp)
providers.locations: 645 Pennsylvania Ave SE, Washington DC
providers.hours: Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:00pm
providers.price_detail: $450/week including $30 non-refundable deposit
providers.financial_aid: None advertised
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long camps plus a Summer Game Club through the summer
providers.status_2026: 2026 weeks largely sold out (registration opened Jan 10); occasional openings posted on the shop blog

reg_2027.opens: early January — 2026 registration opened Saturday Jan 10 at 12:00pm sharp
reg_2027.notes: One of the fastest sellouts on Capitol Hill — spots go within days; be online at the posted noon opening. Verified from official site.

providers.extended_care: After-care offered per the camps page — confirm hours
providers.fit_notes: Perfect for strategy-minded, screen-averse kids; a full week of tabletop is heaven for the right kid and torture for one who needs to run around.

providers.name: Landon Summer
providers.description: One of the largest school-run summer operations in the region: a 75-acre Bethesda campus with dozens of day, sports, specialty, and academic camps for boys and girls, plus an 8-week bus network across the area. Its mix-and-match weekly structure and sheer variety make it a one-stop shop for building an entire summer.
providers.ages: 4-16 (varies by camp)
providers.locations: Landon School, 6101 Wilson Lane, Bethesda MD 20817
providers.price_detail: Per-camp pricing in CampBrain; sessions run one to six weeks
providers.hours: Core camp day; extended day and bus options
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: 9 weeks of programs; buses run 8 weeks, June 15-Aug 7, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration stays open until noon the Wednesday before each session if space remains — real chance of finding late-July/early-August openings in the big catalog

reg_2027.opens: Mid-January (2026: early registration Jan 15 at 1pm for returning/current families, general Jan 16 at 11am)
reg_2027.notes: Two-tier opening gives returning families a one-day head start; popular sports weeks go fast. CampBrain, card/ACH only. Bus seats are separate and also sell out.

providers.fit_notes: Coed in summer despite being a boys' school; the go-to for sporty kids and families needing busing. Huge scale means less intimate than smaller camps.

providers.name: LAYC Summer Youth Programs
providers.description: The region's anchor bilingual youth-development organization runs free summer opportunities out of its Columbia Heights headquarters and Maryland centers: outdoor trips, learn-to-row programs, college-life visits, leadership development, arts, and its free DC Teen Center drop-in space. Programming is bilingual (English/Spanish) and built for immigrant and first-generation families, with wraparound services (case management, health, workforce) attached.
providers.ages: Mostly middle and high school; DC Teen Center serves ages 12-18; org overall serves youth 0-26 across 50+ programs
providers.price_detail: Core youth programs and Teen Center drop-in free to DC youth; some Maryland programs also free
providers.locations: HQ 1419 Columbia Rd NW, Washington DC 20009 (Columbia Heights); LAYC Maryland Multicultural Youth Centers serve Montgomery and Prince George's counties
providers.hours: Varies by program; Teen Center operates drop-in hours, with a daily youth employment program and recreational hours during summer
providers.financial_aid: Programs are free; no aid application needed

reg_2027.opens: Rolling; summer-specific opportunities (outdoor trips, learn-to-row, college immersion, leadership) posted on the Youth Corner page in spring
reg_2027.notes: Enrollment is low-barrier — walk in, call (202) 319-2225, or sign up via the Youth Corner page. Spanish-speaking staff throughout. Capacity varies per activity, so ask in April-May for summer trips.

providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 Youth Corner slate includes outdoor exploration, learn-to-row, college-life immersion, and leadership programs; community-service/internship hours available
providers.status_2026: Operating; Teen Center (ages 12-18) hosts a daily summer youth employment program and recreational hours, and most summer programs accept youth on a rolling basis through the summer
providers.fit_notes: Best free option for Latino/immigrant middle- and high-schoolers in Columbia Heights and inner Montgomery County who want mentorship and activities without cost or paperwork barriers. Younger elementary kids are not the audience.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed org active, HQ address, phone (202) 319-2225, Teen Center ages 12-18 drop-in model with summer employment/rec programming, and Youth Corner summer offerings (outdoors, learn-to-row, college life, leadership) via search-indexed official layc-dc.org pages (home, programs, dc-teen-center, youth-corner, locations). Free-of-charge model consistent with official drop-in-center descriptions.

providers.name: LEAF Youth Program at Common Good City Farm
providers.description: A free, drop-in-friendly farm education program (Learning for the Environment, Agriculture and Food) on a working urban farm in LeDroit Park, where elementary-age kids garden, harvest, cook, and eat together on weekday afternoons. It runs in spring/summer/fall sessions rather than as an all-day camp. A rare zero-cost way for city kids to get real dirt-under-the-fingernails farm time.
providers.ages: Elementary-age focus (roughly 5-12); younger and older welcome
providers.locations: Common Good City Farm, V St NW between 2nd & 4th, LeDroit Park, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Free; parents fill out a registration form in advance or at first visit
providers.hours: Tue/Thu/Sun 4:30-6:00pm; Wed 3:30-6:30pm (not a full-day program)
providers.financial_aid: Free program
providers.sessions_2026: Spring, summer, and fall sessions; summer session running now
providers.status_2026: Open now — drop-ins welcome as capacity allows; sign up for one or more days per session.

reg_2027.opens: Rolling; session signups post on the farm's site each season
reg_2027.notes: No race for spots — commit to a weekly day or drop in. Also note their paid teen youth-employment program for older kids.

providers.fit_notes: An after-camp/afternoon supplement, not childcare or a full-day camp; perfect for families near LeDroit Park/Shaw wanting free, recurring farm time.

providers.name: Learn24 / MOST-DC Free Summer Programs & OST Youth Scholarships
providers.org: DC Office of Out of School Time Grants and Youth Outcomes (with Greater Washington Community Foundation)
providers.description: DC's umbrella for free and subsidized summer programming: the MOST-DC portal (most.dc.gov) is the one-stop search-and-register hub for thousands of K-12 out-of-school-time programs, over 145 of them grant-funded to be free in 2026. Separately, the Learn24 OST Youth Scholarship (administered by the Greater Washington Community Foundation) pays $1,000-$3,000 toward one fee-based summer program per student — up to $5,000 for summer programs that solely serve youth with disabilities (year-round programs qualify for up to $5,000, or $10,000 disability-specific). This is the first place a DC family with a tight budget should look.
providers.locations: Programs citywide across all 8 wards; portal-based
providers.price_detail: Funded programs are free; scholarships $1,000-$3,000 for summer programs (up to $5,000 for programs solely serving youth with disabilities); year-round programs up to $5,000 ($10,000 disability-specific)
providers.financial_aid: This IS the aid: OST Youth Scholarship applications reviewed on a rolling basis at thecommunityfoundation.org/learn24-ost; awards based on individual need and fund availability (minimum award $1,000)
providers.hours: Varies by program
providers.sessions_2026: FY2026: ~$27M invested across 145+ organizations serving 18,000+ DC youth; DCPS free summer enrichment (e.g., CTE Robotics, Mini Medics for rising 7th-9th)
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 programs running; scholarship applications reviewed rolling while funds last — some free programs accept late enrollment

reg_2027.opens: summer scholarship application opens early March (2026: March 2); school-year/year-round application opens late October (2025: Oct 20); portal listings and Summer Strong grants announced late winter-spring
reg_2027.mechanism: rolling (varies by program; scholarship applications reviewed on a rolling basis while funds last)
reg_2027.notes: Mark the early-March opening for the summer scholarship and apply immediately before funds deplete; the disability supplement (up to $5,000 for summer, $10,000 year-round) is one of the region's least-known aid sources. DC residents only. Verified via learn24.dc.gov and Community Foundation indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: Essential starting point for any DC-resident family priced out of private camps, and for families of kids with disabilities seeking the enhanced scholarship. Quality varies program-to-program — vet individual providers on the portal.

providers.name: LearnServe International (Fellows, Incubator & Summer Abroad)
providers.description: A DC-based social-entrepreneurship pipeline: teens learn to design and pitch their own venture tackling a social problem (Fellows/Incubator), and graduates can join two-week summer service-learning trips to Zambia, Costa Rica, or Paraguay, developing a community action project with local partners. Free for DCPS and DC public charter students; everyone else pays a sliding scale from $50 to $1,250. Thrives: idealistic self-starters who want substance — and a rare genuinely affordable international summer experience.
providers.ages: Middle and high school students (drawn from 40+ DMV public, charter, and independent schools); travel programs are for high schoolers (~14-18)
providers.price_detail: Fellows/Incubator free for DCPS and DC public charter students; sliding scale $50-$1,250 for all others based on ability to pay; travel costs separate with substantial aid — no participant turned away for financial reasons
providers.locations: Programs in-person and virtual across the DC region; program locations/travel in Zambia, Costa Rica, and Paraguay (2025-26 Incubator tracks: US/Zambia English, US/Costa Rica Spanish)
providers.sessions_2026: Travel programs ~3 weeks, late June-early July 2026 (Zambia/Costa Rica tracks); Fellows/Incubator run Oct-April during the school year
providers.status_2026: Active; 2026 travel cohorts were set during the 2025-26 program year (trips run ~3 weeks late June-early July, so underway or just completed); enrollment for the next Fellows/Incubator year opens spring and stays open into fall

reg_2027.opens: Enrollment for the Fellows/Incubator year that feeds summer-2027 travel opens in spring (typically April-May) and remains open into fall
reg_2027.mechanism: application — free, via an Arly account linked from the LearnServe site
reg_2027.notes: Plan a year ahead: travel is tied to the school-year Fellows/Incubator (program year Oct-April; 2025-26 Incubator ran US/Zambia and US/Costa Rica tracks). For a newcomer family, applying in spring-fall 2026 is the path to summer 2027 travel. Note learnserve.org and learn-serve.org are both official.

providers.financial_aid: Core model: free for DC public/charter students, sliding scale $50-$1,250 for all others; no one turned away due to family finances
providers.fit_notes: The most economically accessible 'summer abroad' in the region, but not an on-demand purchase — it rewards families who plan a full year out.
providers.hours: Fellows/Incubator meet during the Oct-April program year; travel programs ~3 weeks, late June to early July, with mandatory meetings Feb-Oct
providers.extended_care: N/A — leadership/travel program, not a day camp
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via search-indexed official learn-serve.org and learnserve.org pages: free for DCPS/DC-charter students, $50-$1,250 sliding scale, Oct-April program year, spring (Apr-May) enrollment opening via free Arly application, and 3-week late-June travel programs (corrected from 'two-week') with 2025-26 US/Zambia and US/Costa Rica Incubator tracks. Cross-checked with Globalize DC and Catalogue for Philanthropy listings.

providers.name: Lessans Camp JCC (Inclusion & Kochavim programs)
providers.description: The flagship Jewish day camp of close-in Montgomery County, and the region's best-known fully inclusive mainstream camp: its nationally recognized Inclusion program provides individualized 1:1 support so campers with disabilities join typical bunks, while the award-winning Kochavim bunk serves teens and young adults ages 12-21 with 1:1 or 2:1 counselor support, keeping them alongside neurotypical campers while building socialization and independent-living skills (an Avodah track adds supported work experience). Activities span art, theater, cooking, STEM, music, dance, nature, swimming, and a rock wall on the Rockville campus; open to all faiths and a strong first choice for a family that wants one camp for siblings with and without disabilities.
providers.ages: 4-21 (Pre-K through entering 12th grade; Kochavim ages 12-21)
providers.locations: Bender JCC campus, 6125 Montrose Rd, Rockville MD
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates not published in verifiable indexed copies; $200 non-refundable deposit per camper, installment plan (2026 installments charged Jan 21, Feb 18, Apr 22). Member discounts apply.
providers.financial_aid: Camperships/financial assistance available through the JCC and Jewish Federation channels (One Happy Camper-style grants for Jewish overnight camps do not apply here); contact camp office for aid details — specifics not verified online.
providers.hours: Full-day camp; extended care historically offered (verify with camp)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care typically available at Bender JCC camps — confirm with camp office
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - Aug 7, 2026 in 3 sessions (Jun 22-Jul 2, Jul 6-17, Jul 20-Aug 7) plus pre/post-camp weeks for Pre-K-entering 6th
providers.status_2026: Season underway (runs through Aug 7 plus post-camp weeks); inclusion slots are capacity-limited — contact camp office about any remaining Session 3/post-camp space

reg_2027.opens: early December (Summer 2026 applications opened to the public Wed Dec 3 at noon; expect a similar early-December date for 2027)
reg_2027.notes: Registration opens 'to the public' in early December — JCC members typically get earlier or priority access, so confirm member timing if you plan to join. Inclusion program slots are limited by support-staff capacity and fill much earlier than general enrollment — contact the inclusion coordinator in the fall to discuss your child's needs before registering. Verified via search-indexed copies of benderjccgw.org.

providers.fit_notes: Best for families who want real inclusion in a big, high-energy mainstream camp with Jewish content (all faiths welcome). Kochavim gives teens/young adults 12-21 a supported bunk of their own while still participating in camp-wide activities. Not a therapy program — kids who need clinical-level behavioral support may be better served by Camp Accomplish or public therapeutic rec camps.

providers.name: Life Pieces to Masterpieces Summer Program
providers.description: A nationally recognized arts-based development program for Black boys and young men from Wards 7 and 8, built around collaborative painting ('Life Pieces') plus academics and character curriculum. The six-week summer camp runs 8am-4pm and, like everything at Life Pieces, is completely free for families. Boys can join as young as 3 and many stay through young adulthood.
providers.ages: Boys and young men ages 3-14 for the summer program (org serves ages 3-25 year-round)
providers.price_detail: Free for youth and families, including the six-week summer program ('LPTM Connecting Communities Across the Globe')
providers.locations: 5600 Eads St NE, Washington, DC 20019 (Deanwood, Ward 7); expanding programming to THEARC (Ward 8)
providers.hours: 8:00am-4:00pm daily, six weeks, June-August
providers.financial_aid: Entirely free; mission-focused on Black boys and young men from Wards 7 & 8

reg_2027.opens: Enrollment inquiries year-round; summer intake arranged in spring directly with the organization
reg_2027.notes: No public registration portal - families contact Life Pieces (programs contact: Loretta Curry, Director of Programs and Education) to enroll; apprentices join the year-round community with summer included. Capacity limited; start by early spring 2027.

providers.sessions_2026: Six-week summer program, 8am-4pm daily, June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Operating; summer program serves enrolled apprentices - contact the organization directly about openings
providers.extended_care: 8-4 day already long; no separate aftercare published
providers.fit_notes: One of the best free full-day summers in Wards 7/8 for Black boys — art-centered, deeply relational, year-round community. Only for boys/young men; girls should look at Washington School for Girls or THEARC partners.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed lifepieces.org pages (direct fetch bot-blocked): summer program page exists, ages 3-14, six weeks 8:00-4:00, free, June-August; address/phone confirmed via lifepieces.org/contact search results (5600 Eads St NE, 202-399-7703); enrollment-by-contact mechanism confirmed; program email is Director of Programs per site.

providers.name: Lions Camp Merrick
providers.description: A Lions Clubs-supported overnight camp in Nanjemoy (Charles County, ~1 hr from DC) with specialized week-long programs: a diabetes camp for ages 6-16 (with ADA support and a session for families of newly diagnosed kids, historically 'Camp Glyndon'), and a Deaf/Hard-of-Hearing/KODA program hosting ~90 youth per week. Lions fundraising keeps fees far below typical overnight-camp rates.
providers.ages: Diabetes solo camp ages 6-16; family diabetes week ages 5-16 plus up to 3 family members; Deaf/HOH/KODA youth weeks; adult blind and PTSD programs
providers.locations: 3650 Rick Hamilton Pl (PO Box 56), Nanjemoy, MD 20662 (Southern Maryland; draws from the whole DC region)
providers.price_detail: $50 non-refundable registration fee; camper participation fees around $1,000/week (sibling rate $600 in the sibling session; family session historically ~$200/family member), offset by Lions Club and ADA subsidies and Sponsor-a-Camper camperships
providers.financial_aid: Substantial - Lions Club sponsorships and a Sponsor-a-Camper campership program; ADA supports the diabetes weeks
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long summer sessions: diabetes solo, diabetes+sibling, and family diabetes weeks (~80 campers each); Deaf/HOH/KODA weeks (~90 campers each); adult blind week
providers.status_2026: Operating in 2026; website registration active, but 2026 session dates were not visible in indexed copies - call 301-870-5858 about remaining space

reg_2027.opens: Winter (applications historically available by January-February); 2027 dates not yet announced
reg_2027.mechanism: application with medical/audiology forms per program, via website registration
reg_2027.notes: Register through lionscampmerrick.org; diabetes weeks coordinate with endocrinology clinics - ask your care team about referral timing. 2027 specifics not announced as of July 2026.

providers.fit_notes: For T1D kids and Deaf/HOH kids (plus hearing kids of Deaf adults) who deserve a normal sleepaway week with staff who natively handle their needs; rustic riverside camp, not a resort.
providers.hours: Overnight/residential (week-long sessions)
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed lionscampmerrick.org pages (direct fetch bot-blocked): org active, programs (deafness/blindness/diabetes/PTSD), diabetes ages 6-16 solo and 5-16 family, address, phone, $50 reg fee and ~$1,000/wk participation fee with $600 sibling rate, Sponsor-a-Camper page; 2026 session dates and exact current subsidized rates could not be confirmed, so medium.

providers.name: Locust Grove Nature Center Summer Camps
providers.description: The close-in Bethesda option among the county's five nature-center camps, tucked into Cabin John Regional Park — naturalist-led weeks of hikes, live animals, crafts and forest play for preschoolers through elementary kids. Its location makes it the most convenient public nature camp for Bethesda/Chevy Chase/Potomac families, and it fills accordingly.
providers.ages: Preschool through elementary, age-banded by week (half-day preschool camps plus full-day camps such as Junior Naturalist, Creek Explorers, LOST! Wilderness Survival)
providers.locations: Locust Grove Nature Center, 7777 Democracy Blvd, Bethesda, MD 20817 (Cabin John Regional Park)
providers.hours: Full-day camps typically 8:30am-4:00pm; preschool half-day camps 8:30am-12:30pm
providers.extended_care: Aftercare for select camps - check individual ActiveMONTGOMERY listing
providers.price_detail: Per-week pricing on ActiveMONTGOMERY (comparable Parks nature weeks ~$350 in 2026; half-day preschool weeks less); $25 early-bird discount (code EarlyBird) by April 3, 2026
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship (need-based, Montgomery Parks camps); 2026 fund reported fully awarded

reg_2027.opens: Late January (2026 registration opened Tuesday, January 20 at 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY - expect the same pattern)
reg_2027.notes: Bethesda-side nature camp weeks are among the first Montgomery Parks camps to sell out - treat opening morning as mandatory. Early-bird $25 discount by early April; Dream Camp scholarship applications open around the same time.

providers.status_2026: Season underway; camps largely full with scattered cancellation seats findable via ActiveMONTGOMERY's availability filter (individual openings not verified as of July 12, 2026)
providers.fit_notes: Perfect for younger nature-lovers in the Bethesda/Potomac area; small and nurturing rather than adventure-intensive.
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly camps through summer 2026 per the Montgomery Parks 2026 Summer Camps guide (Locust Grove section, p.32)
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via montgomeryparks.org search-indexed pages and 2026 camp-guide PDF listing (direct fetch/PDF download blocked): Locust Grove is in the official 2026 guide, registration opened Jan 20, 2026 6:30am on ActiveMONTGOMERY, EarlyBird $25 by Apr 3, phone 301-765-8660; corrected hours to 8:30-4 full-day / 8:30-12:30 preschool per official counselor posting; per-week prices not individually confirmed, so medium.

providers.name: Lowell Summer
providers.description: A nurturing, play-forward camp on Lowell School's 8 wooded acres bordering Rock Creek Park in NW DC, serving an unusually wide range from age 2¾ through 15 in two-week sessions. Its progressive, whole-child ethos and forest-edge campus give it a gentler feel than the big sports-driven school camps.
providers.locations: Lowell School, 1640 Kalmia Road NW, Washington DC 20012 (borders Rock Creek Park)
providers.price_detail: 2026: from $765 half-day / $1,035 full-day per two-week session (~$380-520/week); 25% non-refundable deposit
providers.hours: Half- and full-day; before/after camp care offered
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions across the summer of 2026
providers.status_2026: First-come enrollment ran from January; unfilled spots released to waitlist after May 1 payment deadline — call about remaining late sessions

reg_2027.opens: Early January (2026: Lowell families Jan 5 at 9am, public Jan 12 at 9am)
reg_2027.notes: One-week head start for school families; youngest (preschool) groups fill fastest. Two-week session structure — plan in fortnights.

providers.fit_notes: One of very few quality options for kids under 3.5, and a haven for gentle, nature-loving kids; upper Rock Creek location is easiest for 16th Street/Shepherd Park/Takoma families.

providers.name: Mad Science of Washington DC Summer Camps
providers.org: Mad Science of Washington DC (Silver Spring-based franchise)
providers.description: A DC-area institution for decades: classic wow-factor science camps (rockets, chemistry, robots, dinosaurs, secret-agent lab, the NASA-designed space academy week) at neighborhood sites across DC and close-in Maryland. The go-to affordable science option for elementary kids — more demonstrations, slime, and launches than screens.
providers.ages: Grades 1-5 per most 2026 listings (some partner sites list 1-6); roughly ages 6-11
providers.locations: Multiple sites each summer including Tenleytown (NW DC), Chevy Chase, Eastern Market/Hill Center (Capitol Hill), and Silver Spring (HQ: 12065 Tech Rd)
providers.hours: Full day 9:00 am - 4:00 pm; half day 9-12 or 1-4 at some sites
providers.price_detail: $315-$390/week (2026); $30/week multi-week discount with code multicamp2026; Red Hot Robots adds $15 materials fee
providers.extended_care: Before care 8:00-9:00 am and after care 4:00-6:00 pm available at all camps (confirmed for 2026; e.g. Hill Center listing runs 8 am - 6 pm)
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.status_2026: Season running now (8 themed weeks: Crazy Chemworks, NASA Academy, Red Hot Robots, Eureka!, Secret Agent Lab, Dinosaurs, Flight Academy, Science in Motion). Core season June 22 - July 31 at most sites, with extra August weeks at Tenleytown (Dinosaurs Aug 10-14, NASA Aug 17-21); some mid/late-July and August weeks still bookable online.

reg_2027.opens: typically January-February
reg_2027.notes: First-come online. NASA and robots weeks at the DC sites fill first (by late spring); Silver Spring HQ site usually holds space longest. One of the better values in the sector — book popular weeks by March.

providers.sessions_2026: 8 themed weeks; June 22 - July 31 at most sites plus August weeks at Tenleytown
providers.fit_notes: Perfect for curious elementary kids who want explosions and rockets, not rigor. Ends around grade 5 — older kids should look at iD Tech, KID Museum, or Montgomery College.

providers.name: Maret Summer Programs
providers.description: Week-long enrichment camps and sports camps taught largely by Maret faculty on the school's leafy Woodley Park campus — Minecraft/tech, academics, arts, basketball, football, and more for ages 6-16. Smaller and simpler than the mega school camps, with some of the most reasonable price points among DC private-school summers.
providers.ages: 6-16 (varies by camp; e.g. volleyball rising grades 6-10, boys basketball ages 8-16, Spanish immersion rising grades 1-5, Outdoor Explorers rising grades 3-5)
providers.locations: Maret School, 3000 Cathedral Ave NW, Washington DC (Woodley Park)
providers.price_detail: 2026 confirmed: full-day camps $450/week, half-day $350/week; volleyball, boys basketball, and Outdoor Explorers $425; Spanish Immersion $500 - notably below the $600+ area norm
providers.hours: Typically 9:00am-3:00pm; short weeks around Juneteenth and July 4
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions from June 15 into late July 2026: Week 1 June 15-18 (Juneteenth), Week 2 June 22-26, Week 3 June 29-July 2 (July 4 observed), Week 5 July 13-17, continuing per camp
providers.status_2026: Season in progress into late July; individual camps may still have space - the catalog shows per-camp availability

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January-February); not formally announced
reg_2027.notes: Straightforward first-come online signup, open to non-Maret families; smaller program means less frenzy than Landon/Bullis but popular individual camps (Minecraft) still fill. Contact: Associate Director of Summer Programs Murugi Njoroge.

providers.fit_notes: Great value and a walkable option for Woodley Park/Cleveland Park families; smaller menu and shorter season than the big Bethesda operations.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed maret.org summer-programs pages (direct fetch bot-blocked): 2026 week dates (June 15 start, Juneteenth/July 4 short weeks), full-day $450 / half-day $350, specialty camp prices ($425-500), ages 6-16, open to non-Maret participants, online first-come registration.

providers.name: Mariner Sailing School Youth Camps (Belle Haven Marina)
providers.description: Half-day youth sailing camps in 14-foot Sunfish out of Belle Haven Marina, just south of Old Town Alexandria beside the Dyke Marsh wildlife preserve — on light-wind days kids paddle canoes/kayaks through the marsh or swim. Basic and advanced weeks run continuously from June into the first week of September, later than almost anyone else. Morning (10-1) and afternoon (1:30-4:30) sessions.
providers.ages: Roughly 8-16 (youth basic and advanced tracks; exact age band not published in indexed copies - confirm by phone)
providers.locations: Belle Haven Marina, George Washington Memorial Pkwy, Alexandria, VA
providers.price_detail: Half-day week format in 14-foot Sunfish sailboats; current per-week rates not published in indexed copies - call or check saildc.com
providers.hours: Morning 10:00am-1:00pm or afternoon 1:30-4:30pm
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions (morning 10-1 or afternoon 1:30-4:30) through the first week of September
providers.status_2026: Running weekly through the first week of September 2026 with rolling enrollment - among the best remaining 2026 options for a late-summer week

reg_2027.opens: Spring; rolling all season
reg_2027.notes: Low-stress rolling enrollment - this is the camp you can still book in June. Half-days pair well with a morning or afternoon at home.

providers.fit_notes: Small, old-school, and flexible; half-day only, so not a childcare solution. The Dyke Marsh setting adds real nature to the sailing.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed saildc.com pages (direct fetch bot-blocked): youth basic and advanced camp pages live, weekly sessions through first week of September, morning 10-1 / afternoon 1:30-4:30, Sunfish boats, phone 703-768-0018; 2026 prices and exact age band not confirmed, so medium.

providers.name: Mary's Center Teen Program (summer)
providers.description: The community health center that anchors DC's Latino immigrant community runs a free year-round Teen Program — a safe after-school and summer space with computer classes, career fairs, college campus trips, health education, and mentoring, integrated with Mary's Center's medical and social services. Summer programming continues the drop-in/enrollment model at its DC and Silver Spring sites.
providers.ages: Teens ages 14-17
providers.price_detail: Free
providers.locations: Mary's Center sites in DC (Adams Morgan/Petworth area) and Silver Spring, MD
providers.hours: Program hours vary by site and season
providers.financial_aid: Free program

reg_2027.opens: Rolling year-round
reg_2027.notes: Teens enroll by calling 202-851-3968 or via any Mary's Center site; bilingual staff, no immigration-status barriers. Year-round program (computer classes, career fairs, college trips) rather than a capacity-constrained camp.

providers.sessions_2026: Ongoing teen programming through summer 2026 (not session-based)
providers.status_2026: Open, rolling enrollment; year-round teen programming continues through summer 2026
providers.fit_notes: A trusted, no-cost landing place for immigrant teens — especially newcomer families already using Mary's Center for healthcare. It is enrichment and mentorship, not a structured 9-to-4 camp.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed maryscenter.org teen-program page (direct fetch bot-blocked): program active and free, ages corrected from 12-19 to the official 14-17, enrollment phone 202-851-3968 added, activities (computer classes, career fairs, college trips) confirmed; summer-specific schedule not separately published.

providers.name: Maryland Boys State & Maryland Girls State
providers.org: American Legion Department of Maryland / American Legion Auxiliary
providers.description: A week-long residential immersion in state government for rising seniors — participants build mock cities, run for office, draft legislation, and elect a governor, with alumni networks full of governors and senators. Boys State runs at McDaniel College (Westminster) and Girls State at Salisbury University; local American Legion posts sponsor participants, so the week is typically free to families. Standouts can be selected for Boys/Girls Nation in DC. Montgomery County students apply through the state program online.
providers.ages: Rising high school seniors (approx. 17)
providers.locations: Boys State: McDaniel College, Westminster, MD; Girls State: Salisbury University, Salisbury, MD (residential; serves students statewide incl. Montgomery County)
providers.price_detail: Sponsored by local American Legion posts — typically no cost to families
providers.financial_aid: n/a — post-sponsored
providers.sessions_2026: Boys State: June 21-27, 2026; Girls State: June 21-26, 2026
providers.status_2026: Closed — applications ran Nov 15, 2025-May 1, 2026; the 2026 sessions concluded in late June.

reg_2027.opens: Around November 15, 2026; applications due May 1, 2027
reg_2027.notes: Apply at mdboysgirlsstate.org; a local Legion post sponsors you (the program helps match). Not a lottery and rarely 'full' — but strong applications and early submission matter, and it is a genuine college-application differentiator.

providers.fit_notes: Best for civically engaged rising seniors comfortable with a structured, somewhat old-school residential week; only one summer of eligibility, so juniors must plan ahead. Verified via official mdlegion.org and mdboysgirlsstate.org (search-indexed).

providers.name: Maryland Writing Project Summer Writers Camps
providers.org: Maryland Writing Project at Towson University (National Writing Project site)
providers.description: Teacher-led creative-writing camps run by the National Writing Project's Maryland site: one-week half-day camps on the Towson University campus for kids who love to write, plus a genuinely unusual self-paced virtual/asynchronous camp that runs all summer for grades 4-12 at a reduced cost. The online option is one of the region's best-value writing programs and works from anywhere.
providers.ages: Grades 3-12 (in-person: grades 3-7 and 8-12 weeks; async: grades 4-12)
providers.locations: In-person at Towson University (Baltimore County — a drive from DC, ~1 hr); asynchronous camp is fully online
providers.price_detail: In-person: $300 for one week half-day (9am-noon). Virtual/asynchronous camp offered at reduced cost — listed at $100 for the whole summer (June 23-Aug 22, 2026); confirm current price on the MWP page.
providers.financial_aid: None found on indexed pages
providers.hours: 9:00am-noon in person; async is self-paced
providers.sessions_2026: Grades 3-7: July 20-24; Grades 8-12: July 27-31; Async: June 23-Aug 22
providers.status_2026: Both in-person weeks are still ahead (late July) — check the site for open seats; async camp enrolls all summer

reg_2027.opens: February 1 (per Towson University's summer-camps guide, registration opens Feb. 1)
reg_2027.notes: Online first-come registration through Towson opens around Feb 1; small program, modest marketing, so it fills quietly rather than instantly. The low-cost async camp is the sleeper pick for DC families — no commute to Towson needed.

providers.fit_notes: In-person camps are a real drive from DC — mainly worth it for families toward the Baltimore side, or pick the async camp from anywhere. Half-day only, so pair with other coverage.

providers.name: Maryland Youth Ballet Summer Programs
providers.description: One of the most respected classical ballet schools on the East Coast, in downtown Silver Spring above the Ellsworth Dr shops. Little kids (roughly 3-9) get weekly story-ballet camps built around Nutcracker/Swan Lake-style tales, while trained dancers audition for the serious 5-week Academy intensive that culminates at Wolf Trap's Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods. The children's camps are genuinely beginner-friendly; the Academy track requires about 3 years of prior training.
providers.ages: 2-9 for Early Dance/Children's story-ballet weeks; roughly 10-18 for Academy intensive (levels 3-6, min. 3 years training)
providers.locations: 926 Ellsworth Dr, Silver Spring, MD 20910 (Metro-walkable, downtown Silver Spring)
providers.hours: Children's camps are partial-day; Academy intensive runs full weekdays for 5 weeks
providers.price_detail: 2026 Academy intensive: $2,100-2,750 for 5 weeks (~$420-550/wk) depending on level and MYB-student status; children's weekly camp tuition set each spring ($40 deposit); early-bird discount if registered by April 15
providers.financial_aid: Limited summer scholarships available for the Academy intensive; inquire early
providers.sessions_2026: Academy: 5-week intensive (full-program or by-the-week option); Children's: weekly story-ballet camps through summer
providers.status_2026: Season underway as of mid-July 2026; the 5-week Academy program began in June, but by-the-week children's camps for late July/August may still have openings — call the office

reg_2027.opens: February 1 at 9:00 am
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online; audition required for Academy intensive placement (new students)
reg_2027.notes: Popular children's weeks fill through spring; early-bird pricing ends April 15. New-to-MYB dancers wanting the intensive must audition — schedule that in winter/early spring.

providers.name: Matthew Harris Ornstein Summer Debate Institute (WUDL)
providers.description: The Washington Urban Debate League's annual summer institute teaches policy debate to middle and high schoolers in leveled labs — Rookie/Novice for total beginners through Varsity for returning competitors — with a deep dive on the coming season's national topic. WUDL programming is free of charge for public school students, meals included, making this the region's most accessible serious debate training. Thrives: argumentative kids of every experience level; it doubles as the on-ramp to WUDL's school-year league.
providers.ages: Middle and high school students (~11-18); leveled labs: Rookie (MS), Novice (HS), Junior Varsity, Varsity
providers.price_detail: Free for students; includes breakfast, lunch, all supplies, and a T-shirt
providers.locations: Hosted at a partner school site in DC announced each year; 2025 (11th Annual) was at DC Bilingual School near Ft. Totten Metro, Riggs Rd NE
providers.sessions_2026: 3 weeks, late July-early August 2026 (pattern of the 11th Annual in 2025, which drew 272 students); novice/JV attend ~2 weeks, varsity starts a week earlier; exact 2026 dates on the application form
providers.status_2026: Operating — the 12th Annual (2026) institute application form is live on JotForm; institute traditionally runs 3 weeks late July-early August, so apply immediately.

reg_2027.opens: spring (application form posted on WUDL's camp/how-to-apply pages; 2026's JotForm application appeared by spring)
reg_2027.mechanism: application (JotForm), effectively first-come given free admission
reg_2027.notes: Low-barrier signup relative to demand; the 12th Annual (2026) application is a live JotForm. Families wanting elite competitive intensives should look instead at Georgetown Debate Seminar (selective, residential, costly) or commercial debate camps.

providers.financial_aid: Free program (no fee to waive)
providers.fit_notes: The rare truly free academic summer program that welcomes absolute beginners. Late-summer timing conveniently fills the August dead zone after other camps end.
providers.hours: Weekday day camp (exact daily hours published with each year's application)
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed official pages (urbandebatewashingtondc.org camp + how-to-apply, 11th Annual wrap-up post) and the live 12th Annual 2026 JotForm application: org active, free with meals, leveled MS/HS labs, 3-week late-July format, 2025 venue DC Bilingual. Direct fetch blocked (403); exact 2026 dates not indexed.

providers.name: Maydale Nature Classroom Summer Camps
providers.description: The smallest and least-known of the county's five nature-center camp sites, at Maydale Conservation Park in Colesville (east county, near Silver Spring) — hands-on natural and cultural history programming with live animal exhibits, native plant gardens, trails and a nature play area. Its lower profile makes it a genuine insider tip when the famous nature centers are full.
providers.ages: Mixed by week: half-day preschool nature weeks (ages 3-5, e.g. Wee Little Nature Explorers) plus older-youth adventure weeks (ages 11-14) — verify each listing
providers.locations: Maydale Nature Classroom, 1638 Maydale Dr, Colesville, MD 20905 (Maydale Conservation Park)
providers.hours: Varies by camp: preschool ~9:30am-12pm; full-day adventure weeks ~9am-4pm (cycling camp 8am-3:30pm)
providers.extended_care: Aftercare offered for select Parks camps — check each ActiveMONTGOMERY listing
providers.price_detail: Wide range by format: ~$125 half-day preschool week (2025 rate); 2026 guide lists Outdoor Excursions (ages 11-14, July 14-18, 9am-4pm) $400 and C&O Canal Cycling Camp (ages 12-14, June 23-26, with 3 nights camping) $750
providers.financial_aid: Montgomery Parks Fee Reduction Program (need-based; application PDF on montgomeryparks.org, 2026 version posted)

reg_2027.opens: late January (2026 registration opened Tuesday, January 20, 2026 — confirmed by Montgomery Parks announcement), on ActiveMONTGOMERY
reg_2027.notes: Lower demand than Brookside/Locust Grove — a good fallback if you miss opening morning, and a good first choice for east-county families.

providers.status_2026: Season underway as of July 12, 2026; June weeks done, July weeks (e.g. Outdoor Excursions July 14-18) imminent — check ActiveMONTGOMERY availability filter.
providers.fit_notes: Quiet, small-group nature immersion; facilities are simpler than the flagship centers, which some families prefer.
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly camps June-August; confirmed 2026 examples at Maydale: C&O Canal Cycling June 23-26, Outdoor Excursions July 14-18
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via indexed copies of montgomeryparks.org (direct fetch 403-blocked): page/URL live, phone 301-962-1496, email MCP-Maydale@, Jan 20 2026 registration opening, 2026 Summer Camps guide showing Maydale's $400/$750 adventure weeks; corrected ages (not just preschool-elementary) and financial aid (Parks Fee Reduction Program, not the Dream Camp fund). Full 2026 week-by-week lineup not independently verified.

providers.name: Mayor Marion S. Barry Summer Youth Employment Program (MBSYEP)
providers.description: DC's flagship paid summer jobs program places 14-24-year-old District residents in six-week subsidized jobs across government agencies, nonprofits, and private employers — this is the default answer to 'what does a DC 15-year-old do all summer.' Teens who pick the 'Environmental Jobs' preference join the Green Zone Environmental Program (GZEP), DOEE's conservation crew that builds rain gardens, plants trees, removes invasives, and does green-career training three field days a week. Thrives: teens who want a real paycheck, work experience, and structure; it is a job, not a camp.
providers.ages: 14-24 (tracks for 14-15, 16-19, 20-24; high schoolers dominate the younger tracks)
providers.price_detail: Free to join — participants are PAID (hourly wage by age tier). GZEP participants are paid through the same program.
providers.hours: Varies by placement; program runs weekdays during the 6-week window
providers.locations: Job sites citywide in all 8 wards; GZEP field sites across DC watersheds
providers.sessions_2026: June 29 - August 7, 2026 (single 6-week session)
providers.status_2026: Closed — 2026 application ran Jan 26-Mar 6 and the program is currently in session (Jun 29-Aug 7). No mid-summer entry.

reg_2027.opens: late January (2026 cycle opened at noon on Jan 26)
reg_2027.notes: Apply online at summerjobs.dc.gov by early March (2026 deadline was Mar 6 at 11:59pm, no extensions), then complete document certification (age, DC residency, work eligibility, parental consent) — in 2026 orientation ran Apr 4-May 22 with final documents due May 26; missing certification is the most common way teens lose their spot. DC residents only. Most eligible applicants who finish certification get placed; to get GZEP specifically, select Environmental Jobs as your site preference by mid-May (2026 job-site preferences were due May 15).

providers.financial_aid: N/A — the program pays participants
providers.fit_notes: Only for DC residents — Maryland families cannot use this (see Summer RISE/TeenWorks for MoCo). Placements vary widely in quality; teens with a specific interest should research host sites and rank preferences early. Ages 14-15 get more structured cohort experiences.

providers.name: MCPL Summer Reading Challenge & Teen Summer Programs (Montgomery County Public Libraries)
providers.description: The county library system's free summer layer: the all-ages Summer Reading Challenge (June 13-Aug 21, 2026; 2026 theme 'Unearth A Story' — dinosaurs, archaeology, paleontology) with prizes via the ReadSquared app, plus teen-specific offerings — a teen writing contest for middle/high schoolers, Teen Advisory Boards, and SSL-hour volunteer roles helping run summer reading at branches like Gaithersburg. Not childcare, but the best free enrichment scaffold for summer weeks between camps.
providers.ages: all ages for the reading challenge; teen programs for middle/high schoolers (SSL volunteering typically 13/14+)
providers.locations: All 21 MCPL branches countywide + online via ReadSquared
providers.hours: Branch hours; challenge runs June 13-Aug 21, 2026
providers.price_detail: Free
providers.financial_aid: n/a (free)

reg_2027.opens: challenge kickoff typically mid-June (June 13 in 2026); teen volunteer signups post in late spring via the Montgomery County Volunteer Center
reg_2027.notes: No competition for spots on the reading challenge itself — just sign up on ReadSquared anytime. Teen SSL volunteer slots for summer reading ARE limited and claimed in May-June; apply via montgomerycountymd.galaxydigital.com.

providers.status_2026: Live now — challenge runs through Aug 21, 2026; teens can still join mid-summer and some branch volunteer roles were still posted (verified via search-indexed listings, July 2026).
providers.fit_notes: Zero-cost, zero-pressure; pairs well with any camp schedule. Teens needing SSL hours who missed camp-CIT deadlines should look here.

providers.name: MCYO Summer Strings (Strathmore)
providers.org: Maryland Classic Youth Orchestras of Strathmore
providers.description: A one-week string orchestra intensive at the Music Center at Strathmore run by MCYO, the region's oldest and largest youth orchestra (founded 1946): Junior week for rising 3rd-6th graders and Senior week for rising 6th-8th graders, with ensemble rehearsals, a composition lab, and a culminating performance in a world-class venue. It doubles as a friendly on-ramp toward MCYO's auditioned year-round orchestras.
providers.ages: Junior: rising grades 3-6 (2-3 yrs private lessons violin, 2 yrs viola/cello, 1 yr bass); Senior: rising grades 6-8 (rising 6th graders may be placed in Junior per audition video)
providers.locations: Music Center at Strathmore, 5301 Tuckerman Ln, North Bethesda MD
providers.price_detail: $775 tuition for the one-week program (current rate on Strathmore's Junior application page); application is free; non-MCYO students submit a video audition (solo of choice)
providers.hours: Weeklong day program, classes roughly 9am-3pm
providers.financial_aid: Strathmore offers limited scholarships across education programs; camp-specific aid not confirmed
providers.sessions_2026: One week, historically early-mid July (July 8-12 in 2024), classes ~9am-3pm; specific 2026 dates unverified
providers.status_2026: 2026 session likely already completed or in progress (program historically runs early-mid July); 2026 dates not search-indexed — confirm with MCYO@strathmore.org

reg_2027.opens: Spring (applications via Strathmore application pages / Acceptd portal)
reg_2027.notes: Free application; video audition required for non-MCYO musicians (none for current MCYO members); MCYO contacts accepted students with a tuition-payment link. Year-round orchestra auditions run via mcyo.getacceptd.com in late spring — the summer week is a good audition warm-up.

providers.fit_notes: For violin/viola/cello/bass kids with at least a year or two of playing who want orchestral experience in a spectacular hall. Not for total beginners or non-string players.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed Strathmore official pages (Junior/Senior Summer Strings application pages, Acceptd portal): program exists, $775 tuition, grade bands, experience prerequisites, 9am-3pm weeklong format, video-audition mechanism, MCYO@strathmore.org. Direct fetch 403-blocked; 2026 session dates could not be confirmed, so upgraded from low to medium only.

providers.name: Meadowbrook Stables Summer Riding Camps
providers.org: Meadowbrook Stables (nonprofit, on Montgomery Parks land)
providers.description: A storied 1934 stable tucked inside Rock Creek Park at the DC line in Chevy Chase, offering half-day Mini Camps (ages 6-9), Full Day Camps (10-14), and Show Camps (9-16) that mix daily riding instruction with real horsemanship — grooming, tacking, barn care. It is the closest full riding camp to most of NW DC and one of the hardest camp registrations in the area. With Potomac Horse Center's 2024 closure, close-in equestrian capacity is scarcer than ever.
providers.ages: 6-9 (Mini Camp, half day); 10-14 (Full Day); 9-16 (Show Camp)
providers.locations: 8200 Meadowbrook Ln, Chevy Chase MD (Rock Creek Park, just over the DC line from 16th St)
providers.price_detail: Full Day Camp and Show Camp $725/week; half-day Mini Camps less
providers.hours: Full day 8:30 AM - 4:00 PM; Mini Camps half day (AM or PM, e.g. 1:00-4:00 PM)
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions June-August 2026 (e.g., PM Mini Camp Session 7 July 27-31)
providers.status_2026: Mostly full — most weeks sold out shortly after winter registration; occasional late-session mini-camp spots (a July 27-31 PM Mini Camp was still listed) — email to check cancellations

reg_2027.opens: Early in the year (registration 'goes live in early' January-February)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online and famously fast — horse camps this close to DC are scarce, so returning families snap up weeks within days. Watch the site in December-January, have your session choices ranked, and register the day it opens.
