providers.fit_notes: Strong pick for kids who want variety (a different specialty each week) and for Silver Spring families wanting real aftercare. Teen Institute is skills-based rather than production-based — better for explorers than for teens chasing a mainstage credit. Now demonstrably a sells-out program: plan for winter registration.

providers.name: Russian School of Mathematics (RSM) Rockville Summer Program
providers.description: Summer math classes (not a day camp) in the rigorous ex-Soviet tradition: a few hours per week over the summer to keep skills sharp or accelerate, at the Rockville branch or online. Good for families who want structured academic continuity rather than childcare-shaped programming.
providers.ages: K-12 (placement by assessment)
providers.locations: RSM Rockville branch, Rockville MD; online classes also available via RSM Online
providers.hours: 6-week summer program with 2 classes per week — academic enrichment blocks, not full-day care
providers.price_detail: Billed per course; branch emails a tuition PDF by grade on request. Third-party 2026 estimates run roughly $41-110 per lesson ($1,476-$3,960/yr range) depending on grade and course load.
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 classes enrolling per the Rockville location and schedule pages; summer program confirmed as 6 weeks, 2 classes/week

reg_2027.opens: spring (summer schedule posted by the branch on the schedule page)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling enrollment; new students complete a placement evaluation first, so start the process a few weeks before you want classes to begin.

providers.fit_notes: Serious, homework-included math instruction; pairs well with a fun camp in the same week since it is only a few hours. Not for a kid seeking a summer off from school-style learning.
providers.sessions_2026: 6-week summer session, 2 classes per week (exact start dates on the branch schedule page)
providers.verification_note: Site blocks automated fetch; confirmed via search-indexed mathschool.com Rockville pages: branch operating, summer program format (6 weeks, 2x/week), K-12 with placement, rolling enrollment. Tuition only confirmed via third-party 2026 pricing guide — hence medium.

providers.name: Sandy Hill Camp
providers.org: Sandy Hill Camp and Retreat Center
providers.description: A traditional coed overnight camp on 643 acres overlooking the Upper Chesapeake Bay at North East, MD (~1.75 hours from DC), known for its sailing, waterskiing/wakeboarding, horseback, and adventure programs — and for offering true one-week sessions, rare among quality sleepaways. Campers who have completed grades 2-10 choose one- or two-week sessions across an eight-week season. Its real-time online availability page makes late planning unusually easy.
providers.ages: ~8-16 (completed grades 2-10)
providers.locations: North East, MD (Cecil County, Upper Chesapeake, ~1.75 hr from DC)
providers.price_detail: 1-week session $1,850 (2026); 2-week roughly double; $300/$600 deposit with application, balance due May 1. Waterski and horseback lessons cost extra.
providers.financial_aid: None advertised; ask camp
providers.hours: Residential
providers.sessions_2026: One- and two-week sessions, June 22 - Aug 15, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season runs through Aug 15; real-time availability page shows per-session openings — some August weeks typically remain bookable (check the availability link)

reg_2027.opens: fall 2026 (rolling)
reg_2027.notes: First-come with live availability displayed by grade/gender — the most transparent booking system among area sleepaways. Popular waterfront weeks go first; balance due May 1. Verified via sandyhill.com indexed pages.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal first or short sleepaway with a serious watersports program. Kids wanting elite specialty instruction (waterski, horseback) pay activity surcharges. Coed and secular.

providers.name: SCA DC Community Crews (Student Conservation Association)
providers.description: The national conservation-service organization fields paid summer crews of DC-metro high schoolers (teams of ~6 plus adult leaders) doing trail building, invasive removal, and erosion control in Rock Creek Park and other local public lands, under its Urban Green/community program. The 2026 program season spans late May to mid-August. SCA also runs national residential crews for teens who want a wilderness summer further afield.
providers.ages: 15-19 (14-year-olds may apply if they turn 15 by crew start); current high-school students or spring graduates
providers.locations: Rock Creek Park and other DC-area public lands; DC Community Program is year-round (school-year Jan-May, summer Jul-Aug); national crews nationwide
providers.price_detail: Paid/stipended for local community crews (volunteer and paid tracks); national crews are no-cost with essentials covered
providers.hours: 40 hours/week (unless noted otherwise); multi-week commitment
providers.financial_aid: Paid positions; SCA scholarships exist for some programs (e.g., Garden Club of America partnership)
providers.sessions_2026: Program season May 25 - August 14, 2026; DC community summer crews July-August (6-student teams in Rock Creek Park: trail building, invasive removal, erosion control)
providers.status_2026: 2026 program season underway (May 25 - Aug 14, 2026 per official posting); DC community summer crews run July-August, so crews are in the field as of mid-July. Applications for summer 2026 closed.

reg_2027.opens: Winter (student applications typically open around January with deadlines in Feb-Mar; 2026 crew-leader deadline was Feb 2)
reg_2027.notes: Application with resume and references — not first-come. DC-metro teens should watch the Urban Green DC page in early winter; national crews have separate earlier deadlines. Questions: scadc@thesca.org.

providers.fit_notes: For teens who want a real conservation job or a serious outdoor summer with a national brand behind it; multi-week commitment and physical work, not a drop-in program.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed against official thesca.org pages (Urban Green DC, Join the Crew, camping-crew FAQ) via search-indexed 2026 content: ages 15-19, application mechanics with references, 40 hr/week, 2026 season dates May 25-Aug 14, paid/free model, scadc@thesca.org contact.

providers.name: School of Rock Summer Camps (Silver Spring & Cleveland Park DC)
providers.description: Week-long performance-based rock camps at the Silver Spring and Cleveland Park (NW DC) franchises: Rock 101 for beginners plus themed weeks like Black Sabbath, Classic R&B, and 21st Century Indie Rock, all ending in a live show. Kids play guitar, bass, drums, keys, or sing in a band setting with the company's signature performance-first method.
providers.ages: 7-13 typical for camps (Rock 101 is for beginners ages 7-12); themed camps skew slightly older; lessons serve up to 18
providers.locations: Silver Spring: 8634 Colesville Rd; Cleveland Park: 3529 Connecticut Ave NW, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: 2026: $475/week at Silver Spring; $500/week at Cleveland Park (Rock 101) per listings; School of Rock does not publish camp prices on the marketing page - confirm at booking (Pike13 portal). Some listings show higher rates for extended-hours weeks.
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm typical (one Silver Spring R&B week listed 9am-6pm)
providers.extended_care: Not standard; some weeks run extended hours - confirm per camp
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, July-mid August: e.g., Rock 101 July 13 & Aug 3 and Black Sabbath July 13 (Silver Spring), Classic R&B July 20, 21st Century Indie Rock Aug 10; Rock 101 Aug 10 (Cleveland Park)
providers.status_2026: July and August weeks still listed/bookable as of snapshot (Rock 101 Aug 3 and Indie Rock Aug 10 pages live) - genuine late availability for 2026.

reg_2027.opens: January-February (franchise announces '2027 Summer Camps Open Now' in winter)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online enrollment per themed week. Beginner Rock 101 weeks fill first; themed camps often have space into summer. Book by spring for a specific week.

providers.fit_notes: Rock 101 is genuinely no-experience-needed; themed camps work best for kids with a little playing experience who want to gig. Not for classical/orchestral kids.

providers.name: Shout Mouse Press — Shout Squad Summer Writing Workshop
providers.description: A DC nonprofit publishing house that turns marginalized teens into published authors — its Shout Squad summer workshop has met twice weekly on summer evenings in Dupont Circle, writing poetry in conversation with social justice under professional teaching artists, with real book publication and paid public-speaking opportunities downstream. The site lists a Shout Squad Summer 2026 workshop signup. This is identity-affirming, craft-serious writing mentorship, not a generic writing camp, and it centers Black, immigrant, Muslim, Latinx, and otherwise marginalized DC youth.
providers.ages: Approx. 12+ (organization serves youth ages 12 and up; Shout Squad skews high-school)
providers.locations: Washington DC; past sessions met in Dupont Circle, NW DC — confirm 2026 venue with the organization
providers.price_detail: Programs run through nonprofit partners at no cost to youth; published authors earn royalties/speaking fees — confirm current terms directly
providers.financial_aid: n/a — free by design
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 workshop confirmed on site; approx. 6 weeks, evening sessions twice weekly per established format (exact 2026 dates not published in indexed copies)
providers.status_2026: Shout Squad Summer 2026 Workshop confirmed listed across shoutmousepress.org pages; as of July 12 the workshop is likely underway or signup recently closed — contact via the website to check.

reg_2027.opens: Spring, via shoutmousepress.org/shout-squad; recruitment also flows through partner youth organizations
reg_2027.notes: Short application/signup rather than competitive gatekeeping, but the program intentionally recruits underheard/marginalized DC youth — it is not aimed at families simply seeking a writing camp. Teens serious about publication should also submit through their school or a partner org.

providers.fit_notes: Outstanding for DC teens with stories the mainstream overlooks; not a fit for suburban families seeking general creative-writing enrichment (see Writopia et al. elsewhere in this guide). VERIFICATION FLAG: details assembled from search-indexed site copy; session dates/times for 2026 unconfirmed.
providers.hours: Evening sessions twice weekly (program's established format); confirm 2026 schedule
providers.verification_note: Site blocks automated fetch; confirmed via search-indexed shoutmousepress.org pages and the Catalogue for Philanthropy listing: org active, Shout Squad page live, 'Shout Squad Summer 2026 Workshop' referenced on multiple site pages, free nonprofit model. Exact 2026 dates/venue unconfirmed — raised from low to medium.

providers.name: Sidwell Summer
providers.description: The Quaker school's large open-enrollment summer operation, offering day camps, sports, arts, STEM, and academic 'Summer Studies' for ages 3 through grade 12 on its Wisconsin Avenue DC campus and Bethesda campus. Notable for an actual financial-aid Sponsorship Program — unusual among elite-school camps — and a program menu deep enough to build a whole summer.
providers.ages: 3-18 (Age 3 through Grade 12)
providers.locations: DC Campus: 3825 Wisconsin Ave NW, Washington DC; Bethesda Campus programs also offered
providers.price_detail: Wide range by program (half-day preschool camps to multi-week teen academics); prices in online catalog at registration
providers.hours: Varies by program; extended day options
providers.financial_aid: Sponsorship Program provides fee assistance on camp fees — do NOT register first if seeking aid; 2026 sponsorship applications ran Feb 4-Apr 8. Most awards cover one or two sessions; camper must be at least a rising kindergartner. Summer Studies aid limited to Sidwell students on school-year aid.
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly and multi-week sessions June-August on both campuses
providers.status_2026: 2026 registration opened in mid-January and remains open in-season for programs with space; many programs filled early — check catalog for remaining late-July/August openings

reg_2027.opens: Mid-January (2026 registration opened mid-January; exact 2027 date not yet posted)
reg_2027.notes: First-come, first-registered across all programs; popular camps fill within weeks. Financial-aid families should NOT register normally — the Sponsorship application is a separate window (Feb 4-Apr 8 in 2026), so watch sidwellsummer.org/sponsorship in early winter and apply there instead.

providers.fit_notes: Excellent breadth and one of the only school camps with real aid; families wanting a single simple day-camp signup may find the a-la-carte catalog overwhelming.

providers.name: Signature Theatre Summer Camps (Debut and Stage One)
providers.description: The Tony Award-winning musical-theatre powerhouse in Arlington runs two summer programs taught by working theatre professionals: Debut, a one-week non-audition workshop for ages 11-14, and Stage One, an audition-based two-week musical theatre intensive for ages 14-17 (10am-6pm) that functions as a mini professional rehearsal process. Serious musical-theatre teens in the region treat Stage One as a rite of passage; Debut is the friendly on-ramp. Both are small and fill or cast early in the year.
providers.ages: Debut: 11-14; Stage One: 14-17
providers.locations: Signature's Education Studios, 2784 S Arlington Mill Dr, Arlington, VA (main theatre in Shirlington)
providers.price_detail: Debut: $400 for one week (June 22-26, 9am-4pm). Stage One: $958 for two weeks (July 20-31, 10am-6pm) — roughly $480/wk
providers.hours: Debut 9am-4pm; Stage One 10am-6pm, Mon-Fri
providers.financial_aid: none found on public pages — ask education@sigtheatre.org
providers.sessions_2026: Debut June 22-26; Stage One July 20-31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Debut SOLD OUT for 2026 (waitlist via education@sigtheatre.org). Stage One was cast via Feb 7, 2026 auditions and is underway/complete — no summer 2026 availability.

reg_2027.opens: Audition pre-registration opens early January (Jan 5 in 2026); Stage One auditions first Saturday of February; Debut registration opens in winter and sells out
reg_2027.notes: Stage One audition = mandatory group dance call + 1-minute contemporary monologue + 32-bar musical theatre song, memorized. Debut is first-come and sells out — register the week it opens. Contact 571-527-1854.

providers.fit_notes: Stage One suits committed teen performers ready for an 8-hour professional day; Debut genuinely welcomes beginners ('reaching individuals where they are'). Verified via official sigtheatre.org pages (search-indexed).

providers.name: Silver Knights Chess Summer Camps
providers.org: Silver Knights Chess Academy (rebranded back from Magnus Chess Academy in March 2025; founders Adam and Daniel Weissbarth resumed operations)
providers.description: The dominant scholastic chess operation in the DMV — roughly 150 week-long summer camps across Montgomery County, DC, and Northern Virginia (school and partner sites in Bethesda, Chevy Chase, Rockville, Silver Spring, Potomac, Germantown), plus online camps. Having taught 100,000+ kids, they group campers strictly by skill level, so true beginners and tournament players both get appropriate instruction, mixed with recreation breaks.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-13 (K-8; most sites skew K-6, and county-partner listings show ages 5-11), all skill levels from never-played to rated tournament players
providers.locations: ~150 week-long camps: Bethesda, Chevy Chase, Rockville, Silver Spring, Potomac, Germantown (MD), DC sites, Frederick, Howard and Baltimore counties, and across NoVA (Fairfax, Arlington, Ashburn); also online
providers.price_detail: Varies by site and half/full day; verified 2026 partner-site listings run ~$325-357 for a full-day week (e.g., McLean Community Center $357/$325 resident), with half-day options less. Confirm per location on chessacademy.com.
providers.financial_aid: None found on indexed pages
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day options depending on site (typical full day ~9am-4pm; McLean sessions 7 hrs/day)
providers.status_2026: 2026 camps confirmed running across MD/DC/VA and online; individual July/August 2026 camp product pages live (e.g., Ashburn Chess Camp July 2026). Many mid/late-summer weeks typically still bookable in July, but popular close-in sites fill.

reg_2027.opens: Early January for direct-registration sites (2026 opened in early January per prior listing); county-partner sites open later on the county's own calendar (e.g., Arlington Parks & Rec opened Feb 11 for 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Straight online first-come on chessacademy.com; camps hosted through county parks departments (Arlington, Fairfax) register through the county system on separate dates. With ~150 camps there's usually somewhere with space all summer, but convenient Bethesda/Chevy Chase weeks go early — book by early spring for a specific site/week.

providers.fit_notes: The easy, everywhere option for chess: genuinely fine for a kid who's never moved a pawn, and challenging enough for club players. Kids who dislike quiet, seated activity will struggle with the format.
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long sessions June-August 2026 at ~150 sites plus online camps
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed chessacademy.com pages and 2026 partner listings (McLean CC, Bullis, Arlington Parks): org active under Silver Knights name (Chess.com covered the March 2025 transition back from Magnus), both URLs resolve correctly, ~150 camps, half/full-day formats, full-day week ~$325-357. Exact Jan 2026 opening date not re-verified, so reg_2027 phrased as pattern.

providers.name: Silver Stars Gymnastics Summer Camp
providers.description: Silver Spring's neighborhood gymnastics gym runs half-day and full-day summer camps for ages 3-14 mixing vault, bars, beam, trampoline, and tumbling with crafts, games, and ninja challenges. The killer feature for newcomer families is flexibility: you can book a full week or just the days you need, which almost no area camp allows. All skill levels welcome.
providers.locations: 2701 Pittman Dr, Silver Spring MD
providers.price_detail: Priced per day or per week, half- or full-day; mid-range weekly totals (verify current rates)
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day options; year-round day camps on school closures too
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions all summer 2026; day-by-day booking allowed
providers.status_2026: In session; per-day booking means scattered availability persists through summer, though popular weeks fill

reg_2027.opens: Late winter/early spring (schedule posted around February)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online; the gym itself advises registering early because weeks fill quickly. Join the email list by February for early-bird pricing windows.

providers.fit_notes: Great for younger kids, wiggly energy, and families needing partial-week coverage; competitive team gymnasts will want a bigger training gym like Dynamite.

providers.name: Smithsonian National Zoo (NO summer camp — discontinued after FONZ dissolution)
providers.org: Smithsonian's National Zoo and Conservation Biology Institute
providers.description: The beloved FONZ Summer Safari Day Camp that generations of DC kids attended ended when the Zoo and Friends of the National Zoo dissolved their 63-year partnership in 2021, and the Zoo's education page has since said it is not offering camps — there is no zoo summer camp to find. The Zoo itself remains free to visit (timed-entry passes) and posts occasional school-day-off 'School's Out Camp' listings, but nothing week-long in summer. Animal-loving kids should look instead at nature-center camps (e.g., Audubon Naturalist/Nature Forward, county nature centers) covered elsewhere in this guide.
providers.ages: n/a (no camp); zoo visits all ages
providers.locations: 3001 Connecticut Ave NW (Woodley Park/Cleveland Park)
providers.price_detail: Zoo entry free with timed pass; paid parking. No camp product exists to price.
providers.financial_aid: n/a
providers.sessions_2026: None
providers.status_2026: No summer camp in 2026. Check the camps page occasionally for School's Out (school-year day-off) sessions.

reg_2027.opens: n/a — no camp planned; re-check nationalzoo.si.edu/education/camps each winter
reg_2027.notes: If the Zoo ever relaunches camps it will be big local news; as of July 2026 nothing is announced. Do not trust third-party listings still advertising 'FONZ Summer Safari Day Camp' — they are stale.

providers.fit_notes: Explicit 'no camp' entry so newcomers stop hunting. FONZ dissolution verified via official Zoo press release and DCist; current camps-page language verified via search-indexed copy only (direct fetch blocked).

providers.name: Smithsonian NMNH Summer High School Internship (+ Q Crew & YES!)
providers.org: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
providers.description: A paid, competitive summer internship at the Natural History Museum for ages 15-18: interns work 2-3 days a week on real projects with museum scientists and staff, plus enrichment days with behind-the-scenes tours, workshops, and college prep (reported stipend ~$5,600 for the season). The museum also fields Q Crew, its all-teen volunteer squad, and YES! (Youth Engagement through Science), a 12-week career-immersion program for DC-region high schoolers. Thrives: science-minded teens who want the words 'Smithsonian intern' on a college application — deservedly.
providers.price_detail: Paid internship — stipend reported around $5,600 for summer 2026 (secondary source; verify)
providers.hours: Tuesday-Friday, 10am-4pm
providers.locations: National Museum of Natural History, 10th St & Constitution Ave NW
providers.sessions_2026: June 23 - August 14, 2026
providers.status_2026: Closed — 2026 applications ran Feb 16-Mar 20 with decisions by late April; internship in session.

reg_2027.opens: mid-February (2026 app launched Feb 16, closed Mar 20)
reg_2027.notes: Highly competitive with a short one-month window — set a reminder for mid-February. Q Crew teen volunteering and YES! have separate application cycles on the museum's youth-programs page; other Smithsonian museums (Air & Space, etc.) post their own high-school internships at internships.si.edu.

providers.financial_aid: N/A — paid program
providers.fit_notes: The internship is a long shot; savvy families apply to it AND line up a backup. Q Crew volunteering is the accessible on-ramp for teens who don't get the internship.

providers.name: Smithsonian Summer Camp (Smithsonian Associates)
providers.description: The region's flagship museum day camp: 90+ themed one-week camps (dinosaurs, space, art, spies, history) run out of the S. Dillon Ripley Center on the National Mall, with daily visits into Smithsonian museums and talks with real curators and experts. Best for curious kids who want a different deep-dive topic every week; it has run for over 50 years and is the camp DC families plan their winter around.
providers.ages: Entering grades K-9 (roughly 5-15), grouped by grade band
providers.locations: S. Dillon Ripley Center, 1100 Jefferson Dr SW, Washington DC (National Mall)
providers.price_detail: $425/week members, $495/week non-members for regular five-day weeks (2026); the short 4-day week of June 29-July 2 was $340/$396. Before-camp (8-9 am) and after-camp (4:30-6 pm) cost extra
providers.hours: 9:30 am-4:30 pm Mon-Fri; before-care from 8 am, aftercare to 6 pm
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly, June 22 - August 14, 2026
providers.extended_care: Yes - before-camp 8-9 am and after-camp 4:30-6 pm, purchased separately (these sell out too)
providers.financial_aid: None found on indexed pages

reg_2027.opens: Camp list posted ~December-January; registration mid-February (2026: donor lottery Feb 9-11, all members Feb 12, general public Feb 13)
reg_2027.mechanism: Tiered first-come with a donor lottery on top: Contributor-level donors ($300+) enter a lottery Feb 9-11 for the earliest registration slots, then all members register Feb 12, then the public Feb 13
reg_2027.notes: Popular weeks/topics sell out within days of public opening. Have second and third choices ready; join waitlists immediately - the office calls in waitlist order. Membership effectively buys a one-day head start; only $300+ donors get the lottery tier.

providers.status_2026: Season running June 22-Aug 14. Many camps marked sold out; the researcher's early-July snapshot showed some Aug 3-7 and Aug 10-14 sessions with availability plus active waitlists - worth checking the camp browse page for late openings (site blocks automated re-verification).
providers.fit_notes: Ideal for kids who love topics and museums; it is a classroom-and-field-trip style camp, not a sports/outdoor camp. Official site bot-blocked; details verified via search-indexed copies of official pages and Smithsonian press releases.

providers.name: Snapology STEAM & LEGO Robotics Camps (Fairfax)
providers.description: LEGO-brick-based STEAM and robotics camps in a play-first format, part of a national franchise: 3-hour themed programs (coding, robotics, science, arts) that can be paired into a full day. The nearest verified operation to DC is Snapology of Fairfax, VA — no confirmed DC or Montgomery County location, so treat this as a NoVA option only.
providers.ages: 5-12 for camps (Snapology programs overall span 2-14)
providers.locations: Snapology of Fairfax, 9524 Old Creek Dr, Fairfax VA 22032, plus partner sites in the Fairfax area; Snapology's 'Washington DC region' page is marketing only — no verified DC/MoCo branch as of July 2026
providers.hours: 3-hour half-day programs; pair an AM and PM program (with a supervised lunch hour) for a full-day week
providers.price_detail: Fairfax-specific pricing not published in indexed copies; franchise pricing varies widely by location and half- vs full-day (other franchises run roughly $250-450/wk half-day, more for full-day) — confirm at the Fairfax registration portal
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.status_2026: Fairfax camps page live and listing 2026 summer camps; per-session availability not verified

reg_2027.opens: rolling; franchise schedules typically posted late winter (no announced 2027 date)
reg_2027.notes: Franchise-run, first-come via the embed.snapology.com event portal (licensee 179); not a program that requires months-ahead planning.

providers.fit_notes: Younger LEGO-loving kids. If you are in DC/MoCo, Play-Well TEKnologies and KID Museum cover the same ground closer to home.
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long themed half-day camps through summer 2026; schedule on the Fairfax camps page/registration portal
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked at gateway; confirmed via multiple searches of snapology.com indexed copies: Fairfax camps page live for 2026, ages 5-12, 3-hour half-day format, address 9524 Old Creek Dr and phone 703-219-8556 (Yelp/official). Fairfax pricing could not be confirmed, so not high confidence.

providers.name: Sportrock Climbing Camps
providers.description: The region's best-known climbing-camp operator (a NoVA Magazine 'Best Summer Camps 2026' winner) running week-long Indoor Base Camp, Outdoor Adventure Camp (real rock trips), STEM climbing camps, and single-day options out of its Alexandria and Sterling gyms. The outdoor program is the regional standout — few area camps take kids onto actual crags with professional instruction. Included as a regionally famous NoVA option that fills the outdoor-climbing gap.
providers.ages: 6-16 depending on program (Single-Day and Indoor Base Camp 6-14; STEM Climbing Camp 8-16; Outdoor Adventure 10-16; separate Teen Outdoor Adventure)
providers.locations: Sportrock Alexandria (5308 Eisenhower Ave, Alexandria VA), Sportrock Sterling VA, and Sportrock Rio (9811 Washingtonian Blvd, Gaithersburg MD — opened as their Maryland flagship, runs the same camp lineup)
providers.price_detail: Teen Outdoor Adventure Camp confirmed at $575/wk ($540 members); other week-long camps (Indoor Base, Outdoor Adventure, STEM) price in a similar ~$450-600 range on the portal — confirm per program; single-day camps also offered
providers.hours: Camp day roughly 9am-3pm (per Single-Day Camp listing); single-day options exist
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions all summer 2026 at Alexandria, Sterling, and Rio (Gaithersburg); Rio adds STEM and Escape Room camp weeks
providers.status_2026: 2026 camps running; camps started the week of June 15 and run all summer — outdoor weeks fill first, indoor weeks usually available into summer

reg_2027.opens: Winter (registration typically opens January; no announced 2027 date)
reg_2027.notes: First-come via the Sportrock portal (per-location pages for Alexandria, Sterling, and Rio-Gaithersburg); Outdoor Adventure weeks are the scarce commodity and sell out by spring — book those in winter, treat Indoor Base Camp as the flexible fallback.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for adventurous kids ready for real outdoor climbing; DC/MoCo families should weigh the NoVA commute against Movement Rockville's closer indoor camps.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked at gateway; confirmed via searches of sportrock.com and portal.sportrock.com indexed copies: camp lineup and ages per program, 9am-3pm day, Teen Outdoor $575/540 pricing, three locations including new Rio-Gaithersburg, camps running from week of June 15, 2026. Exact per-camp weekly prices for Indoor Base/Outdoor Adventure not directly confirmed, hence medium.

providers.name: Spy Camp
providers.description: The Spy Museum's famous KidSpy week: kids build disguises, make and break codes, design gadgets, learn escape-and-evasion, hear from real former intelligence officers, and run training missions on the streets of DC. A one-of-a-kind camp that trades on the museum's genuine intelligence-community connections.
providers.locations: International Spy Museum, 700 L'Enfant Plaza SW
providers.price_detail: $625/week non-members, $545/week museum members (2026)
providers.hours: 9 am-3 pm Mon-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Two weeks only: July 13-17 and July 20-24, 2026
providers.financial_aid: None found

reg_2027.opens: Winter - check spymuseum.org around January-February
reg_2027.notes: Only two weeks with small capacity, so both sessions sell out well before summer - register the week it opens. Museum membership saves $80/week and members may hear first.

providers.status_2026: Both 2026 weeks (July 13-17, July 20-24) are SOLD OUT.
providers.fit_notes: Sweet spot is kids who love mysteries, puzzles, and running around DC; it is limited to ages 9-12 with no younger/older sessions.

providers.name: St. Albans Summer Programs
providers.description: Half- and full-day academic classes, arts, and athletic camps (football, golf, rowing, swimming, tennis) for boys and girls PreK-12 on the National Cathedral close, using St. Albans' first-rate athletic facilities. Rolling admission from January until programs fill makes it one of the more forgiving elite-school registrations.
providers.ages: 4-18 (boys and girls, grades PreK through 12)
providers.locations: St. Albans School, Mount St. Alban, Washington DC 20016 (Cathedral Heights)
providers.price_detail: Per-program pricing in the online catalog/calendar; not centrally published. Half- and full-day academic and art classes plus athletic camps (football, golf, swimming, tennis)
providers.hours: Half- and full-day options by program
providers.financial_aid: None for the general public — official FAQ states Summer Programs do not offer financial aid; limited scholarships only for enrolled St. Albans students required to take an academic course, plus select partner-nonprofit scholarships handled by those organizations
providers.sessions_2026: 40+ sessions June-August 2026, program-specific dates; searchable calendar at summer.stalbansschool.org/calendar
providers.status_2026: Operating summer 2026; rolling enrollment continues until spots are filled or programs start — worth checking the calendar for remaining sessions

reg_2027.opens: January, then rolling until full (per official FAQ: registration period typically begins in January)
reg_2027.notes: Online registration preferred; no lottery, no single frantic opening day — rolling admission until spots fill or the program starts. Sports camps with famous coaches fill first. Note: the big multi-activity camps ON the St. Albans campus (ages 3-13) are run by Headfirst with separate registration at headfirstcamps.com — confirmed Headfirst operates on St. Albans/NCS campuses in 2026.

providers.fit_notes: Strong for academics and serious sport instruction (rowing, golf) in upper NW; not a drop-off-every-week general camp — that role on this campus belongs to Headfirst.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked at gateway; confirmed via multiple searches of summer.stalbansschool.org official pages (FAQs, program policies, contact): grades PreK-12, half/full-day format, January rolling registration, no-financial-aid policy, contact 202-537-2214 / summerprograms@stalbansschool.org, and Headfirst's 2026 presence on campus. Corrected financial_aid from 'None found' to the explicit official policy.

providers.name: St. Columba's Nursery School Summer Camp
providers.org: St. Columba's Episcopal Church Nursery School
providers.description: A beloved NW DC church-preschool summer camp for 2.5-5 year-olds run by the nursery school's own year-round teachers, with daily sand and water play, creek exploration, gardening, cooking, and art on a large shady playground off Albemarle Street. Religious affiliation is nominal — programming is standard high-quality play-based preschool, and it's a fixture of the Tenleytown/AU Park parent circuit.
providers.locations: 4201 Albemarle St NW, Washington DC 20016 (Tenleytown)
providers.hours: Morning-centered preschool-day schedule
providers.price_detail: Session-based tuition; 2026 was a single 3-week session — contact school for rates
providers.financial_aid: Not advertised for summer; nursery school has aid for the school year — ask
providers.sessions_2026: One 3-week session only, June 8-26 (no camp 6/19), reduced from the usual schedule due to church construction
providers.status_2026: Season over — the single 2026 session ended June 26

reg_2027.opens: late winter (typically February-March), with priority for currently enrolled nursery-school families
reg_2027.notes: 2027 should return to a fuller schedule once 2026 construction ends. Small class sizes; NW DC church-preschool camps like this fill from the school community first, so newcomers should email in January. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: Great gentle first camp for NW DC toddlers; representative of the broader NW DC church-preschool summer scene (comparable programs at other Cleveland Park/Tenleytown parish preschools). Not a full-day working-parent solution.

providers.name: Steve & Kate's Camp (DC / Potomac)
providers.org: Steve & Kate's Camp (national chain, Bright Horizons)
providers.description: A national chain built around radical flexibility: kids self-direct all day among sewing, stop-motion animation, coding, baking, maker crafts, sports and water play, and parents buy day passes usable any day at any location with unused passes automatically refunded. The 8am-6pm day includes lunch and snacks with no extra aftercare fee. Ideal for families with unpredictable schedules or kids who hate being locked into one theme.
providers.ages: 4-12 (PreK through middle school)
providers.locations: DC-Northwest (Edmund Burke School, 4101 Connecticut Ave NW), DC-Penn Quarter (Girls Global Academy, 733 8th St NW), DC-Capitol Hill, and Potomac MD (St. Andrew's Episcopal School, 8804 Postoak Rd)
providers.price_detail: 2026: bulk rate $114/day verified when buying 15+ day passes; single day passes cost more (researcher-reported $124, not independently verified — other metro markets run $88-119). Summer Pass covers all open days (up to 39 at Potomac). Lunch, snacks, and full 8am-6pm day included; unused passes auto-refunded by Sept 11, 2026.
providers.hours: 8:00am-6:00pm, all included
providers.extended_care: Built in — the base fee covers 8am-6pm
providers.financial_aid: Financial aid program exists (see steveandkatescamp.com/fees/financial-aid)
providers.sessions_2026: Daily, roughly 9 summer weeks June-August; buy any number of day passes
providers.status_2026: Operating in DC and Potomac for 2026; day-pass model means space is generally available all summer — one of the best options still bookable for remaining July and August days

reg_2027.opens: Typically late fall (around November) for the following summer; day passes remain purchasable into summer
reg_2027.notes: No lottery, no session commitment — buy passes anytime, use any day, any open location, automatic refunds for unused days. The de-facto safety valve for families who missed January registration elsewhere.

providers.fit_notes: Best for independent, choice-loving kids and gig-schedule parents; kids who need structure and a fixed peer group may prefer a traditional session camp.

providers.name: Summer @ GDS
providers.description: Georgetown Day School's coed preK-12 summer program in Tenleytown, mixing traditional day camp with STEM, art, service-learning, and a 'Camp Explore DMV' field-trip camp that uses the whole city. Runs on CampBrain with free waitlists and a progressive, relaxed GDS culture.
providers.ages: 4-18 (PreK-12; Extended Day available for PK-8)
providers.locations: Georgetown Day School, 4200 Davenport St NW, Washington DC (Tenleytown, ~4 blocks north of Tenleytown-AU Metro)
providers.price_detail: Per-program pricing in CampBrain catalog; $100 non-refundable deposit per session at registration, installments available, balance due by early summer; unattended pickup after 4pm auto-enrolls the child in Extended Day at a $35 drop-in fee
providers.hours: Core day with afternoon/full-day options; Extended Day until 6:00pm
providers.extended_care: Yes — Extended Day until 6pm for PK-8 campers in afternoon or full-day programs; $35/day drop-in if unregistered
providers.financial_aid: Yes, limited — official Financial Aid page: aid decisions are made during registration, are not retroactive, and cannot cover the entire summer
providers.sessions_2026: Week-long camps June 22 - July 31, 2026 (e.g., weeks of 6/22, 7/6, 7/13, 7/20, 7/27), plus a two-week math review July 27 - Aug 7
providers.status_2026: Operating summer 2026; rolling registration while space remains and free waitlists for full programs — weeks run into early August, so late-July/August weeks worth checking now

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January); watch summer.gds.org — no announced 2027 date
reg_2027.notes: CampBrain first-come registration; can register until shortly before a session if not full. Waitlists are free to join; spots are offered by email with 24 hours to respond.

providers.fit_notes: Good fit for families wanting an inclusive, progressive-school vibe in upper NW; season ends in July, so plan August separately.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked at gateway; confirmed via multiple searches of summer.gds.org official pages (registration, extended day, deadlines, financial aid, programs): 2026 week dates, CampBrain mechanics, 24-hour waitlist offers, Extended Day to 6pm with $35 drop-in, contact 202-295-1091 / summer@gds.org. Corrected financial_aid — GDS publishes a Summer financial-aid page (limited, at-registration) — and corrected sessions to include late-July/early-August weeks.

providers.name: Summer Adventure Camp at Hard Bargain Farm
providers.description: A small, sweet farm-and-river camp on the Alice Ferguson Foundation's 330-acre environmental campus in Accokeek, directly across the Potomac from Mount Vernon. Days are spent with the goats and chickens, dip-netting for tadpoles, digging in the garden, and hiking to the riverbank with eagle-watching; the core day camp serves ages 4-9, with an indexed listing showing a pricier ages 10-13 option (verify directly). One of the cheapest genuine farm camps in the region.
providers.ages: 4-9 core day camp (grouped 4-6 and 7-9); an ages 10-13 option at $975/week appears in some indexed listings but could not be re-confirmed for 2026 — verify with the camp
providers.locations: Hard Bargain Farm, 2201 Bryan Point Rd, Accokeek, MD 20607 (Prince George's County, ~30-40 min south of DC; single site)
providers.price_detail: $375/week for ages 4-6 and 7-9 (confirmed in current indexed copy of the camp page); extended care $150/week ($45/day if added after registration)
providers.hours: Standard camp day; extended care available
providers.extended_care: Extended care $150/week ($45/day if added after registration)
providers.financial_aid: Stovall Outdoor Scholarship Fund historically underwrites participation for Southern Maryland kids who couldn't otherwise attend — not re-confirmed in current indexed copy; ask the camp
providers.sessions_2026: 7 weekly sessions, roughly mid-June through late July/early August 2026 (exact week dates not published in indexed copies)
providers.status_2026: Camp page live with a 7-week 2026 season; season is well underway as of mid-July — email camp@fergusonfoundation.org about any remaining weeks

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; camp page typically posts by late winter and the foundation promotes registration opening on social media — email camp@fergusonfoundation.org to get on the list
reg_2027.notes: Small program with a short 7-week season; register in late winter/early spring. The low price and small capacity mean the young-kid weeks fill first.

providers.fit_notes: Wonderful first camp for animal-loving little kids; the season ends before August and programming for ages 10+ is limited (an indexed 10-13 option exists but is unconfirmed), so treat it as a partial-summer solution. The drive from DC/MoCo is against traffic but real.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked at gateway; confirmed via multiple searches of fergusonfoundation.org indexed copies and the OSSE/Experience Prince George's listings: $375/week for ages 4-6 and 7-9, extended care $150/wk ($45/day late-add), 7-week season, address, and contact camp@fergusonfoundation.org / 301-292-5665 (phone added). The 10-13/$975 option and Stovall scholarship could not be re-confirmed for 2026, so flagged; raised from low to medium since price, ages, season length, and contacts all check out against official-source copy.

providers.name: Summer at Levine (instrument & specialty camps)
providers.description: Levine Music's menu of shorter, focused summer 'Camp Experiences' alongside Camp Levine: one-week camps like Jazz Camp (ages 10-14, July 27-31 2026, $580), Strings Camp, Rock Band Camp, Songwriting, and Music Therapy Mini Camp, plus multi-week group classes such as Intro to Guitar. These are for kids who want to actually work on an instrument or craft, in small groups with conservatory-quality faculty.
providers.ages: Varies by camp; roughly 5-18 (Jazz Camp ages 10-14; other Camp Experiences include Strings Camp, Songwriting, Rock Band Camp, Music Therapy Mini Camp), plus adult offerings (e.g., Beginning Guitar Boot Camp for Adults)
providers.locations: Primarily Levine's NW DC campus (2801 Upton St NW); some offerings at other Levine campuses (Strathmore MD, THEARC SE DC, NoVA)
providers.price_detail: Varies by program: Jazz Camp $580 for the week (confirmed in Levine's ActiveNet catalog for July 27-31, 2026); short specialty classes priced lower (e.g., Jazz Combo $219); Camp Levine full-day sessions run ~$1,599 per 3-week session
providers.hours: Varies by program
providers.extended_care: Not standard for specialty camps; check individual listings
providers.financial_aid: Levine Music offers tuition assistance across its programs (levinemusic.org tuition-and-assistance pages)
providers.sessions_2026: Various one-week camps late June-early August 2026; e.g., Jazz Camp July 27-31 at the NW DC campus; Camp Levine sessions June 22-July 10 and July 13-31
providers.status_2026: Late-July camps still listed in the ActiveNet registration system as of July 2026 (Jazz Camp July 27-31, $580, DC NW campus) - some late availability likely; verify per camp

reg_2027.opens: Late January (2026 Camp Experiences registration opened January 21, with returning campers/current Levine students able to enroll from January 14); full catalog posts on the Summer page in winter
reg_2027.notes: Rolling first-come enrollment through Levine's ActiveNet portal after the late-January opening; returning families get a one-week head start. Small class caps but these fill slower than Camp Levine; jazz and strings camps benefit from some playing experience.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who already play (or specifically want to start) an instrument and want real instruction rather than general arts camp. Jazz/strings camps expect some experience; intro classes are true-beginner.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); verified via multiple searches of levinemusic.org and its ActiveNet catalog: summer-2026 page live, Jan 21/Jan 14 2026 registration opening confirmed, Jazz Camp July 27-31 2026 ages 10-<15 at $580 confirmed in the registration index, tuition-assistance pages confirmed.

providers.name: Summer at Norwood
providers.description: Norwood School's long-running, well-regarded summer program on 38 acres off River Road in Bethesda, spanning junior day camp (3.5-5), classic day camp, and middle-school 'Academies' — with unusually adventurous electives like paddleboarding, kayaking, filmmaking, ceramics, and young-entrepreneurs. A strong all-rounder with genuine variety per week.
providers.ages: 3.5 through rising 9th grade (Junior Day Camp ages 3.5-5; Day Camp rising 1st-5th; MS Academies rising 5th-8th; Adventure Camp and older-camper options up to rising 9th)
providers.locations: Norwood School, 8821 River Road, Bethesda MD 20817
providers.price_detail: Per-program rates at summeratnorwood.org/dates-and-rates; confirmed sample: Adventure Camp $725 for 5-day week (June 22-26) and $580 for 4-day week (June 29-July 2); one-week options now offered within two-week sessions
providers.hours: Camp day approx. 9:00am-3:00pm; Before Care 8:00-9:00am and After Care 3:00-5:00pm
providers.extended_care: Yes - Before Care 8-9am, After Care 3-5pm, added during or after registration via the Camp Office; not offered the week of Aug 17-21
providers.financial_aid: Discounts and financial aid offered (per camp FAQs/discounts page); do not apply to partner-run sports camps
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly/two-week sessions June-August 2026, including a late-August week Aug 17-21
providers.status_2026: Registration open through the season where space remains; late-August week (Aug 17-21, no extended care that week) still on calendar

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January)
reg_2027.notes: Standard first-come online registration; water-sports electives (kayaking, paddleboarding) are the scarce commodity. Headfirst separately runs partner sports camps on this campus (camp discounts/aid do not apply to those).

providers.fit_notes: Sweet spot for elementary/middle kids who want to sample lots of interests in one place; Bethesda location with no big bus network means it draws mostly nearby families.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); confirmed via searches of summeratnorwood.org and norwoodschool.org: registration open, ages 3.5 to rising 9th, program lineup, Adventure Camp 2026 rates ($725/$580), extended care hours 8-9am and 3-5pm, Aug 17-21 week, discounts/financial aid, and contact info.

providers.name: Summer Fun Centers (Montgomery County Recreation)
providers.description: The county's classic, budget-friendly day camp: six weeks of themed activities, sports, games, music, drama, trips and special events at recreation and community centers across the county. It is the workhorse option for working families who need a long day (9:30am-5pm) at a fraction of private-camp prices. Kids who like variety and a big social scene thrive; it is not specialized instruction.
providers.ages: 6-12 (must be 6 and have completed kindergarten by Sept 1; entering grade 1)
providers.locations: Rec/community centers countywide, e.g. Jane E. Lawton CRC (Chevy Chase), Bauer Drive CRC (Rockville), East County CRC (Silver Spring), Longwood CRC (Brookeville); limited 'extended week' sites in mid-August
providers.hours: 9:30am-5pm Mon-Fri, June 29-Aug 14, 2026 (no camp July 3); extended-week sites Aug 10-14 run 8am-6pm
providers.extended_care: Core day is already 9:30am-5pm with no separate before/after care; the Aug 10-14 extended-week sites run full days 8am-6pm
providers.price_detail: $215/week (2026); non-county residents +$15 per participant per activity
providers.financial_aid: RecAssist: $400/person placed on your account toward Recreation programs for county residents receiving eligible public assistance OR students at MCPS Community Eligibility Provision schools; applications for each year's fund open in early December (Dec 8, 2025 for the 2026 fund), first-come until depleted, valid Jan 1-Dec 31

reg_2027.opens: mid-to-late January for camps (2026 camp registration opened Tue Jan 20 at 6:30am) — expect the third Tuesday of January 2027. Note: the county's separate 'Summer Programs' (classes) registration opens later in May; the January date is the one that matters for camps
reg_2027.notes: Register on ActiveMONTGOMERY the morning it opens; in-person help available at 2425 Reedie Dr, Wheaton (10th floor) from 6:30am on opening day. Popular sites (close-in Bethesda/Chevy Chase/Silver Spring centers) fill first, but at $215/week capacity is large — check ActiveMONTGOMERY's live 'openings available' filter for real-time seats all spring and summer. Apply for RecAssist when applications open in early December; funds run out.

providers.status_2026: Season underway (June 29-Aug 14). Many sites full, but ActiveMONTGOMERY's availability filter shows remaining seats in real time — late-July and Aug 10-14 extended-week openings are the best bet as of July 12, 2026 (live availability not individually verified).
providers.fit_notes: Best for families who want an affordable, dependable full-day general camp and don't need specialty instruction. Not for kids who want to go deep on one interest; no half-day option; must be registered by the week.

providers.name: Summer Hockey & Skating Camps at MedStar Capitals Iceplex
providers.description: The Washington Capitals' practice facility atop the Ballston mall in Arlington runs the region's marquee summer hockey camps: Slapshot's Fun Camp for littles, Mite ADM, skills camps (Deke, Score & Defensive Skills), team-play camps, and the girls-only All Caps All Her camp run with Capitals youth hockey development. Kids skate where the pros practice, and instruction ladders directly into the Caps' learn-to-play pipeline. Included as the regionally famous NoVA option for hockey families.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-14 depending on camp (Slapshot's Fun Camp ages 6-12; Mite ADM for 8U-level players; Learn to Skate camps from tot through youth; girls camp has its own age bands)
providers.locations: MedStar Capitals Iceplex, 627 N Glebe Rd, Arlington VA (Ballston)
providers.price_detail: Prices not published in indexed copies; hockey camps typically run several hundred dollars per week - verify per camp in the registration system
providers.hours: Varies by camp; Slapshot's Fun Camp runs 8:30am-4:30pm; most camps run weekday daytime blocks
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly camps June-August 2026: Slapshot's Fun Camp (June 16-20 and July 7-11), Mite ADM, Deke Score & Defensive Skills, S.P.A.C.E. Camp, All Caps All Her girls camp, Caps Team Play, plus Learn to Skate camps
providers.status_2026: 2026 summer camps on sale per the rink's registration news page; Slapshot weeks (June 16-20, July 7-11) have passed - check remaining mid/late-summer camps directly

reg_2027.opens: Spring (camps go on sale around March-April)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online registration per camp; some camps have skill/equipment prerequisites (Slapshot requires full equipment and basic hockey skating skills), and the All Caps All Her girls' camp draws region-wide with guest instruction from Olympians/PWHL/NCAA players. Hockey families can usually register into late spring.

providers.fit_notes: Best for kids already skating or eager to start hockey seriously; for cheaper, closer-in general skating camps see Cabin John, Wheaton Ice Arena, or Fort Dupont.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); confirmed via searches of medstarcapitalsiceplex.com and capsyouthhockey.com: 2026 camp lineup, Slapshot ages 6-12 and hours 8:30am-4:30pm with June 16-20/July 7-11 weeks, on-sale status, and Hockey@ email; per-camp pricing could not be confirmed, so kept medium.

providers.name: Summer in DC Portal (summer.dc.gov)
providers.org: Government of the District of Columbia
providers.description: The Mayor's one-stop summer portal aggregating everything the DC government runs for kids and teens: DPR camps, free summer meals sites, free pools and spray parks, library programs, SYEP, and events. Not a program itself but the official map of the city's free summer infrastructure - the first thing a newcomer family should read each May.
providers.ages: all ages
providers.locations: Online portal covering programs citywide
providers.price_detail: Portal is free; links to both free and fee-based programs
providers.hours: n/a
providers.financial_aid: Links to reduced-rate and free options including free summer meals for youth
providers.sessions_2026: Live for summer 2026
providers.status_2026: Live now with current camps, free meals sites, pool hours and events

reg_2027.opens: refreshed each spring (around May)
reg_2027.notes: Bookmark it and check in early February (DPR lottery), late January (SYEP application) and May (program registration). It is the fastest way to catch announcement-driven deadlines.

providers.fit_notes: A navigation entry, not a camp - included because DC's free-public summer system is spread across many agencies and this is the official index.

providers.name: Summer Playwriting Camp (Young Playwrights' Theater x Keegan)
providers.org: Young Playwrights' Theater with The Keegan Theatre
providers.description: A half-day camp (new for 2026) from DC's premier youth playwriting nonprofit, hosted at the Keegan Theatre: three weeks writing original plays with YPT teaching artists, then two weeks of acting and rehearsal, culminating in a live public performance of the kids' own scripts. The only DC camp built around kids as playwrights first.
providers.locations: The Keegan Theatre (Dupont Circle); 2026 final performance Saturday Aug 8 at Sitar Arts Center (Adams Morgan)
providers.price_detail: Tuition not published in indexed copies of the Keegan education page; YPT's school-year programs are free - ask about camp cost/aid
providers.financial_aid: Not verified for this camp; YPT is a deeply access-oriented organization - inquire at programs@yptdc.org or playrahka@keegantheatre.com
providers.hours: Half day, Mon-Fri
providers.extended_care: Possibly combinable with Keegan PLAY full-day camp (rising grades 1-3 program runs June 22-Aug 21) - ask Keegan
providers.sessions_2026: Five weeks, July 6-Aug 7 (three weeks writing original plays, two weeks acting/rehearsal), culminating performance Saturday Aug 8 at Sitar Arts Center
providers.status_2026: Season underway (camp runs July 6-Aug 7) - enrollment for 2026 effectively closed mid-session

reg_2027.opens: Spring (first offered 2026; watch YPT and Keegan PLAY-RAH-KA pages from ~March)
reg_2027.notes: Brand-new program in 2026 (Keegan bills it as new this year in partnership with YPT), so 2027 details may evolve; sign up for YPT's mailing list and watch keegantheatre.com/prk/education. Half-day format needs a plan for the rest of the day.

providers.fit_notes: Perfect for the kid who writes stories and wants to see them staged — a genuine niche no other DC camp fills. Verified only via the DC Theater Arts camp guide and YPT/Keegan program pages in search results; confirm 2027 continuation.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); confirmed via indexed copies of keegantheatre.com/prk/education and the 2026 DC Theater Arts camp guide: ages 10-14, July 6-Aug 7 half-day format, new-in-2026 YPT partnership, Aug 8 Sitar Arts Center performance; tuition not published, so medium.

providers.name: Summer RISE + TeenWorks (Montgomery County teen work programs)
providers.org: Montgomery County Public Schools / Montgomery County Recreation
providers.description: Montgomery County's two main answers to summer work for teens. Summer RISE gives MCPS rising juniors and seniors a 50-hour, 5-week career-based learning placement (in-person or virtual) with an employer in their chosen field — hospitals, labs, law offices, county agencies. TeenWorks hires high schoolers 15+ as paid seasonal Montgomery County Recreation employees (minimum wage, 200-350 hours) learning a skilled trade with on-the-job coaching.
providers.ages: Summer RISE: rising 11th-12th graders enrolled in MCPS (~16-18); TeenWorks: 15+
providers.price_detail: Both free. TeenWorks pays county minimum wage; Summer RISE is primarily unpaid career exposure (some stipend support has been offered via the MCPS Educational Foundation — verify current year).
providers.hours: Summer RISE: placements scheduled between 8am-6pm weekdays; TeenWorks: varies by host site
providers.locations: Host employers and rec facilities countywide (Silver Spring, Rockville, Wheaton, Gaithersburg, etc.)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer RISE: June 22 - July 24, 2026 (50 hours over 5 weeks)
providers.status_2026: Closed for new applicants — Summer RISE 2026 is in session; TeenWorks 2026 hiring completed in spring.

reg_2027.opens: roughly February-March for Summer RISE (verify exact date on MCPS site in winter); TeenWorks job postings appear winter/spring and require a mandatory info session
reg_2027.notes: Summer RISE: students rank three career areas plus geographic/virtual preferences; matching is not guaranteed, and MCPS enrollment is required — private-school and DC students are ineligible. TeenWorks: apply through Montgomery County government job postings; parental consent and info session required.

providers.financial_aid: N/A — free/paid programs
providers.fit_notes: Summer RISE is the single best resume-builder for MCPS 11th/12th graders but is only ~10 hrs/week — it is not full-day coverage. Newcomers should note eligibility is tied to MCPS enrollment, not just county residence.

providers.name: Summer SCORES (free soccer + arts camps)
providers.description: DC SCORES' signature free summer camps combine soccer with poetry and creative arts — the same 'soccer poets' model as its famous school-year program. Everything is provided free: balls, uniforms, coaches, journals, and writing supplies. Recent camps ran at Truesdell Education Campus (Ward 4) and Leckie Education Campus (Ward 8/SW), serving rising 2nd-6th graders.
providers.ages: Rising 2nd - rising 6th graders (roughly 7-11); 2025 camp cohort skewed rising 4th-5th
providers.price_detail: Free - t-shirt and at least one meal daily included, plus weekly field trips and pool trips
providers.locations: DC school sites that vary by year: Truesdell Education Campus (800 Ingraham St NW) and Leckie Education Campus (4201 MLK Jr Ave SW) in 2024; John Lewis Elementary School (NW DC) in 2025 - confirm current-year sites with DC SCORES
providers.hours: Weekday day camp; hours published each spring
providers.financial_aid: Entirely free - the program exists for kids at under-resourced DC schools

reg_2027.opens: Spring (typically April-May); DC SCORES directs families to summer.dcscores.org to register
reg_2027.notes: Free camps fill quickly once announced; priority audience is students at DC SCORES partner schools, and sites move between school campuses year to year. Watch dcscores.org news page and summer.dcscores.org in spring 2027.

providers.sessions_2026: Multi-week free camp expected late June-July 2026 (2024 ran June 24-July 26 at Truesdell and Leckie; 2025 ran in July at John Lewis ES); 2026 specifics unconfirmed
providers.status_2026: DC SCORES runs Summer SCORES annually (2025 camp at John Lewis ES confirmed), but indexed sources do not confirm 2026 sites/dates - contact DC SCORES directly for current-summer specifics
providers.fit_notes: Ideal for elementary kids east of Rock Creek and east of the river who love soccer or need a free active camp; the poetry component is real, not window dressing. Not for kids wanting elite soccer training.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); confirmed via indexed dcscores.org pages: program is real, free, rising 2nd-6th, includes meals/shirts/field trips, registers at summer.dcscores.org (fixed url_register from register.dcscores.org); the Truesdell/Leckie June 24-July 26 dates trace to a prior year and 2025 camp was at John Lewis ES, so 2026 site/date claims were softened.

providers.name: Summer@WES
providers.description: Washington Episcopal School's camp on Little Falls Parkway in Bethesda, where beloved WES teachers and counselors run swimming (indoor pool), theater, art, science, robotics, cooking, sports, and DC-exploration camps for rising PK4 through grade 9. A friendly mid-size program that flies under the radar compared with its bigger Bethesda neighbors.
providers.ages: 4-15 (rising PK4 through grade 9)
providers.locations: Washington Episcopal School, 5600 Little Falls Parkway, Bethesda MD 20816
providers.price_detail: 2026: standard weeks $555 (4-day closed weeks) to $690 (Week 5); CampX middle-school program $460-565/week; Discover DC program $1,025-1,130 per two-week session; $100/camper/week nonrefundable deposit; camp closed June 19 and July 3 with those weeks prorated
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm camp day
providers.extended_care: Yes - After Care 3:00-6:00pm; Before Care available before the 9:00am start
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly camps June-August 2026 across 6 weeks (closed June 19 and July 3); includes CampX for middle schoolers and Discover DC two-week sessions
providers.status_2026: Registration open for 2026 into the season; a good bet for remaining July/August weeks given lower profile

reg_2027.opens: Winter (2026 registration was open early in the year); no single frantic opening day observed
reg_2027.notes: Straightforward weekly first-come signup with $100/week deposit and full payment due ~May 1; swim-inclusive weeks (indoor pool) are the draw. Less competitive than Landon/Holton - a smart fallback for late planners.

providers.fit_notes: Warm, smaller-scale camp convenient to Westbard/Little Falls and NW DC border neighborhoods; fewer high-spectacle facilities than Bullis or Landon.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (proxy 403); confirmed via multiple searches of w-e-s.org: 2026 registration open, rising PK4-grade 9, 2026 week rates ($555-690 standard, CampX $460-565, Discover DC $1,025-1,130), June 19/July 3 closures, 9am-3pm day with 3-6pm after care, phone 301-652-7878 and camp director email; price band tightened from varies to $$$.

providers.name: SummerCulinary Teen Cooking Camp DC (Cooking Camp USA)
providers.description: An immersive teen culinary program (part of a national operator) hosted for 2026 at Union Kitchen in DC's Eckington neighborhood, where students ages 12-17 are instructed by professional chefs in commercial kitchens and prepare lunch and dinner every day. Washingtonian flagged culinary school as one of DC's hottest 2026 camp trends, and this is the most professionalized commuter option.
providers.ages: 12-17 (no experience required)
providers.locations: Washington DC commuter campus (host kitchen address not published in indexed copy; previously reported as Union Kitchen, Eckington — confirm before enrolling)
providers.price_detail: Exact 2026 tuition not published in search-indexed copy; enrollment requires a minimum $500 deposit (some pages reference $1,000) with flexible pay-as-you-go plans before the session; 10% discount for siblings, multi-session, or returning students; site states DC pricing is lower than other campuses; full refund or transfer to 2027 if cancelled before May 15, 2026
providers.hours: Full-day commuter program (daytime studio sessions; some campuses add evening food excursions)
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Multi-day/multi-week summer 2026 sessions at the DC commuter campus
providers.status_2026: Active for summer 2026 — DC campus page live and enrolling, with a May 15, 2026 refund/transfer deadline confirming 2026 sessions

reg_2027.opens: fall/winter (rolling national enrollment; 2026 families can already transfer funds to 2027 and lock in 2026 pricing)
reg_2027.notes: Rolling enrollment with a $500 minimum deposit to hold a seat; refund cutoff mid-May. Premium-priced program — compare against cheaper local options before committing.

providers.fit_notes: For teens seriously exploring cooking as a craft or career — real kitchens, chef instructors, full days on your feet. Expensive and intense; casual cooks are better served by Tiny Chefs-style enrichment camps.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via multiple searches of summerculinary.com indexed pages (2026 copy): ages 12-17, DC commuter campus, $500 min deposit, May 15 2026 refund deadline, 10% discounts, phone. Corrected deposit ($1,000 -> $500 minimum) and url_more (Washingtonian link unverified). Exact tuition and DC host-kitchen address not published in indexed copy, so price_band $$$$ is a ballpark for this premium program — medium confidence.

providers.name: Synetic Theater Summer Camps & Teen Conservatory
providers.description: The region's famous wordless-physical-theatre company teaches kids 7-13 its signature movement-based storytelling — pantomime, dance, stage movement, ensemble world-building — in ten week-long fairytale-themed sessions, plus a three-week full-day pre-professional Teen Conservatory ending in a revue of Synetic's classic Shakespeare scenes. Nothing else like it in the DMV.
providers.ages: 7-13 in two groups: Young Artists (rising 1st-5th grade) and Thespians (rising 6th-8th grade); teens for the 3-week Summer Teen Conservatory
providers.locations: Synetic Studio, 2155 Crystal Plaza Arcade T19, Arlington, VA 22202 (Crystal City)
providers.price_detail: Weekly rate not published in search-indexed copy; register via CourseStorm with monthly payment-plan option; site references a pricing calendar (buying earlier is cheaper)
providers.financial_aid: Merit- and need-based scholarships funded by a $10,000 George Preston Marshall Fund award, for both weekly camps and Teen Conservatory; apply on a rolling basis via Camps@synetictheater.org (subject line 'Financial Aid')
providers.extended_care: Camp add-ons page exists on synetictheater.org (before/after-care options not itemized in indexed copy); see Summer Camp FAQs
providers.sessions_2026: Ten week-long sessions across summer 2026, each on a single rotating fairytale theme (Session 1: Fractured Fairy Tales); 3-week Summer Teen Conservatory (Shakespeare-focused) began June 29, 2026
providers.status_2026: Running; scholarships still available as advertised June 23, 2026 — later-summer weeks likely bookable via CourseStorm

reg_2027.opens: February (2026 registration was open and promoted by Feb 26, 2026 via ARLnow/Synetic)
reg_2027.notes: Registered via CourseStorm; pricing-calendar model rewards early signup and camps can sell out. Weekly structure rarely sells out entirely; Teen Conservatory is the competitive piece. Included despite NoVA location because the physical-theatre training is regionally unique.

providers.fit_notes: The camp for physical, expressive kids — dancers, mimes, kids who act with their whole body — and for shy kids since it de-emphasizes memorizing lines. Not for kids who most want to sing or deliver monologues.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via searches of synetictheater.org, CourseStorm, and ARLnow sponsored posts (Feb 26 and Jun 23, 2026): ages/groups, 9am-3pm, Crystal City address, ten 2026 sessions, CourseStorm registration, Marshall Fund scholarships, contacts. Weekly tuition figure never surfaced in indexed copy, so medium confidence.

providers.name: TBC Racing Learn-to-Row Summer Camp (Thompson Boat Center)
providers.description: DC's premier rowing club — grown out of Thompson Boat Center at the mouth of Rock Creek on the Georgetown waterfront — runs two-week learn-to-row camps for rising 7th-9th graders (must be 12+), with two on-water rowing sessions daily and lunch at Washington Harbour. Two weeks is enough to step into novice racing in the fall, making this the standard on-ramp to high-school crew in the District. A genuinely DC experience: rowing past the monuments on the Potomac.
providers.ages: Rising 7th-9th graders; must be 12 before camp starts
providers.locations: TBC Racing Training Center, 2600 Virginia Ave NW (Watergate); on-water at Thompson Boat Center, Georgetown waterfront
providers.price_detail: Priced per two-week session (verify current rate on tbcracing.org product page)
providers.hours: Weekday daytime with two rowing sessions daily (weather dependent)
providers.financial_aid: Ask the club — competitive rowing clubs commonly offer need-based aid
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions between June 22 and July 31, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season underway; final two-week session (mid-to-late July) may have space — check the product page

reg_2027.opens: Winter/early spring (products posted alongside spring Discover Rowing)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online checkout on tbcracing.org; capacity is boat-limited so sessions do fill. Kids who finish camp can jump into TBC's novice fall programming — the intended pipeline.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for tall, athletic middle-schoolers curious about crew before high school; not for kids under 12, and it's a sport camp, not childcare — days revolve around the water.

providers.name: Teen Museum Council
providers.description: A school-year-long council of DC/MD/VA high schoolers who design teen programming for the National Portrait Gallery - early museum work experience with input on real programs, community service, art-making, and an inside view of museum operations. Application season is mid-summer, making it a natural next step to plan while arranging fall/next-summer activities.
providers.ages: High school students (grades 9-12) from DC, Maryland, and Virginia
providers.locations: National Portrait Gallery, 8th & G Sts NW (Penn Quarter)
providers.price_detail: Free to participate; whether members receive a stipend is not stated on indexed official pages (a Washington Family profile frames it as 'early work experience')
providers.hours: Meets once a week during the school year, generally 4:30-6:30pm (year-long commitment)
providers.sessions_2026: 2026-27 cohort runs weekly across the school year
providers.financial_aid: N/A - free program

reg_2027.opens: Applications each summer; 2026-27 cohort deadline is August 7, 2026
reg_2027.notes: Recruited region-wide; application form on npg.si.edu (submission-confirmation page confirms an online application). Competitive but designed for teens, not resumes. Watch npg.si.edu/learn/teens in early summer for the 2027-28 cycle.

providers.status_2026: Applications OPEN NOW for the 2026-27 cohort (due Aug 7, 2026) - one of the few museum opportunities still actionable this summer.
providers.fit_notes: Not a camp - a year-long weekly-meeting leadership program. Best for teens interested in art, history, or museum careers who can commit through the school year.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via searches of npg.si.edu indexed pages: program exists at this URL, grades 9-12 DC/MD/VA, weekly school-year meetings (4:30-6:30pm), August 7 application deadline, online application (application-submitted page indexed). Free program. Stipend question remains unresolved; all other key facts confirmed from official Smithsonian pages.

providers.name: Teen Naturalist Training (TNT) at Camp Woodend
providers.description: A three-week counselor-in-training-style program for rising 9th-11th graders at Woodend Sanctuary: teens assist in the K-8 camp program while getting their own lessons in natural history, habitat restoration, and outdoor group leadership. Participants earn 100 MCPS student service learning (SSL) hours per session. It's the natural next step for kids who aged out of Camp Woodend.
providers.ages: Entering grades 9-11
providers.locations: Woodend Sanctuary, 8940 Jones Mill Rd, Chevy Chase, MD 20815
providers.price_detail: 2026: Session 1 (6/22-7/10) $560 member/$730 nonmember; Sessions 2 (7/13-7/31) and 3 (8/3-8/21) $600/$770 — roughly $190-260/week for a 3-week session
providers.hours: Camp-day hours (approx. 8:45am-3:45pm), weekdays for a 3-week session
providers.financial_aid: Scholarship questions to the Camp Director; membership discount
providers.sessions_2026: Session 1: June 22-July 10; Session 2: July 13-31; Session 3: Aug 3-21
providers.status_2026: Placements made on a rolling basis; Session 3 (Aug 3-21) is the only remaining window — ask directly whether spots remain (unverified).

reg_2027.opens: With the camp catalog, typically December-January
reg_2027.notes: Short application plus a required interview with the camp admin team; teens are assigned on a rolling basis, so apply as soon as the form opens. Much cheaper per week than most teen programs and earns 100 SSL hours per session.

providers.fit_notes: For responsible teens who like working with younger kids and being outside; it's a training/volunteering hybrid, not a paid job, and acceptance depends on a successful interview.

providers.name: Teen Summer Programs: TeenWorks & Youth Leadership (Montgomery County Recreation)
providers.description: The county's summer track for teens too old for camp: TeenWorks hires teens 15+ as paid seasonal county employees (about $17/hour in 2026) placed on teams doing real work — tech instruction, marketing, rec-center operations — with job-readiness training and mentorship. Separate youth leadership programs build toward Junior Counselor and lifeguarding roles and can earn MCPS SSL hours, and the county publishes a dedicated Summer Teen GUIDE of low-cost teen activities each year.
providers.ages: TeenWorks: high school students at least 15 years old (paid seasonal county employment); youth leadership/SSL programs serve middle and high schoolers with program-specific age/grade requirements
providers.locations: Rec centers, school-based sites, county agencies and nonprofit host sites countywide (Recreation HQ: 3950 Ferrara Dr, Wheaton, MD)
providers.hours: Varies by placement/program
providers.price_detail: TeenWorks participants are paid seasonal county employees (Recreation Assistant I, Grade S1; $17.15/hour); leadership/teen programs are free or nominal cost
providers.financial_aid: n/a (program is free/paid)

reg_2027.opens: applications typically open in winter/spring for summer placements (exact 2027 dates not yet posted)
reg_2027.notes: TeenWorks is a real county job application, not an ActiveMONTGOMERY signup: applicants must be at least 15, attend a mandatory TeenWorks information session, have parent/guardian consent, and apply through the county employment posting (governmentjobs.com, Recreation Assistant I Grade S1) when it opens. Placements are competitive — watch the Youth Development pages and the Summer Teen GUIDE (published alongside the January camps guide).

providers.status_2026: Summer placements/programs underway; 2026 applications closed for most paid roles (verified via search-indexed county pages and governmentjobs.com posting).
providers.fit_notes: The go-to free option for teens 15+ who need something meaningful (and SSL hours or wages) rather than a camp; younger middle schoolers should look at the leadership/SSL programs instead. Requires initiative — it is a job/leadership program, not childcare.
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 seasonal employment placements plus school-year/summer youth development programs
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via searches of montgomerycountymd.gov and the governmentjobs.com TeenWorks posting: URL resolves, age 15+ requirement, $17.15/hr Recreation Assistant I Grade S1 pay, mandatory info session + parent consent + county job application mechanics. Corrected phone to 240-777-8080 (Recreation/TeenWorks contact per county pages) and pointed url_register at the county jobs portal where applications actually happen.

providers.name: Terrapin Music Camp (UMD School of Music)
providers.org: University of Maryland School of Music
providers.description: The University of Maryland's week-long band, string orchestra, and choir day camp at the Clarice Smith Performing Arts Center in College Park (just outside our core geography, but the region's flagship university music camp). Grades 6-12 get small-group lessons and sectionals from UMD and top Maryland K-12 faculty, daily large-ensemble rehearsals, electives, and a public final concert. Instrumentalists need at least one year of study; vocalists at any level are welcome.
providers.ages: Entering grades 6-12; instrumentalists need 1+ year of study, vocalists any level
providers.locations: The Clarice Smith Performing Arts Center, University of Maryland, College Park MD (roughly 25-35 min from DC/Silver Spring)
providers.price_detail: 2026: $455 if registered Feb 1-May 31; $485 June 1-15; day camp (no residential option indicated)
providers.hours: Sunday 3-6pm orientation, then Mon-Fri 8:30am-4:30pm; Friday 2pm public concert
providers.financial_aid: None found for this camp
providers.sessions_2026: Band: July 12-17; String Orchestra & Choir: July 19-24, 2026
providers.status_2026: Registration closed June 15; Band week begins July 12 (today as of snapshot), Orchestra/Choir week July 19-24.

reg_2027.opens: February 1 (early rate through May 31)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online registration with a firm mid-June cutoff and an early-bird price before June 1. Fills steadily but is larger-capacity than boutique camps - registering by spring is usually safe.

providers.fit_notes: Ideal for school band/orchestra/choir kids grades 6-12 who want a college-campus ensemble week; the choir track genuinely takes beginners. Requires a College Park commute; not for elementary kids.

providers.name: The Art League Summer Art Camp
providers.org: The Art League (Torpedo Factory Art Center)
providers.description: The venerable Alexandria art school attached to the Torpedo Factory runs summer camps at its Madison Annex, with a general art camp (a new project daily across drawing, painting, printmaking, sculpture) plus specialty weeks like Painting Camp, Printmaking & Bookbinding, and Natural Crafts & Foraging. A regionally famous fine-arts institution worth crossing the river for.
providers.ages: 5-15, in age-banded sections per camp (e.g. ages 5-7, 5-8, 8-12, 9-12)
