providers.locations: Slaters Lane Classroom Annex, 800 Slaters Lane, Alexandria VA (Old Town North) — the former Madison Annex (305 Madison St) closed and was sold for redevelopment; Art League 'Creative Campus' also includes the Torpedo Factory and Muse Studios at 1201 N Royal St
providers.price_detail: 2026 per-week tuition not published in search-indexed copy; camps sold per weekly session as half-day (AM or PM) or full-day; full-day tuition includes a supervised lunch hour (a $40/week charge applies when combining AM+PM camps yourself)
providers.hours: Weekly Mon-Fri sessions; half-day AM, half-day PM, or full-day options
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed camps through summer 2026 (painting, drawing, manga/cartooning, clay animation, ceramics, sculpture and more) at the Slaters Lane Annex
providers.status_2026: Registration opened May 11, 2026 (online only) — a late opener by DC standards, so mid-summer availability is realistic; check current listings

reg_2027.opens: mid-May (May 11 in 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Unusually late registration opening makes this a strong Plan B when everything else filled in February. Online only via theartleague.org; popular painting weeks go first.

providers.fit_notes: Serious, technique-forward art instruction in a storied institution — great for kids who really draw and paint. It's in Old Town Alexandria with mostly half-day formats, so DC/MoCo families need to weigh the commute and coverage gap.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; verified via searches of theartleague.org indexed pages: camp program live for 2026, ages 5+ (camps 5-15 in banded sections), May 11 2026 online-only registration open date, half/full-day weekly format, phone. CORRECTED location: camps moved from the Madison Annex (closed, building sold) to the Slaters Lane Classroom Annex, 800 Slaters Ln, per the Art League facilities page and ALXnow coverage. Per-week price never surfaced, so medium confidence.

providers.name: The Little Gym Super Quest Camps (Bethesda & Silver Spring)
providers.description: Half-day (3-4 hour) indoor gymnastics-and-games camps with weekly themes, small ratios, and air conditioning, for ages 3-10 with bathroom independence required. The killer feature for newcomers is flexibility: book single days, full weeks, or buy a Flex Pass and decide later — perfect for patching schedule gaps or giving a 3-year-old a first drop-off experience.
providers.ages: 3-10 (toilet-trained)
providers.locations: The Little Gym of Bethesda; The Little Gym of Silver Spring
providers.hours: 3-4 hour half-day blocks, morning-centered
providers.price_detail: Per-day and per-week pricing plus Flex Pass bundles; roughly $ to $$ equivalent for half days — confirm with each gym
providers.financial_aid: None
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed camps all summer
providers.status_2026: Open — day-by-day booking means space is usually available mid-summer, including remaining July and August dates

reg_2027.opens: rolling — camp calendar typically posted by early spring
reg_2027.mechanism: rolling first-come, day-level booking with Flex Pass option
reg_2027.notes: Never a January panic here; this is the backup camp that still has space in June. Verified from official franchise pages.

providers.fit_notes: Best as a first camp for 3-4s or as flexible fill-in weeks; too short and repetitive to be a full-summer solution for older kids.

providers.name: The Theatre Lab Summer Acting Camp & Teen Institutes
providers.org: The Theatre Lab School of the Dramatic Arts
providers.description: DC's largest independent nonprofit drama school runs nationally recognized summer programs for 550+ young people: Summer Acting Camp for rising grades 1-8 (grouped as Bravos 1st-2nd, Encores 3rd-4th, Standing Ovations 5th-6th, plus separate 7th-8th programs) and a Summer Teen Institute (ages 13-19) including a Performance Intensive and month-long Musical Theatre Institutes. Serious, warm, downtown-DC training with an unusually deep financial-aid commitment — about a third of campers receive aid.
providers.ages: Rising grades 1-8 (Acting Camp); 13-19 (Teen Institute)
providers.locations: 900 Massachusetts Ave NW, Washington DC 20001
providers.price_detail: Base tuition not verified on accessible pages; Early Drop-Off $50/week, AfterCare $150/week; early-bird discount through early February; payment plans available
providers.financial_aid: Strong: partial and full scholarships covering tuition AND before/aftercare; ~1/3 of campers aided; decisions within one week of application
providers.hours: Full camp day; before-care and aftercare extend it
providers.extended_care: Early drop-off ($50/wk) and aftercare ($150/wk) for Acting Camp
providers.sessions_2026: Acting Camp: 1- and 2-week sessions for rising grades 1-6, 1- and 4-week programs for grades 7-8, across the summer; Teen Institute (13-19): 1-, 2-, and 4-week sessions June 16-Aug 22; record enrollment year
providers.status_2026: Open with limited spots in remaining sessions ('limited spots available' per their outreach) — later Acting Camp weeks are the best bet

reg_2027.opens: December-January (early-bird pricing runs through early February)
reg_2027.notes: Register by the early-bird deadline for the discount; financial-aid applications turn around in a week, so apply as soon as registration opens. Fills to record capacity but the long mid-June-to-late-August calendar leaves late openings.

providers.fit_notes: Best value-for-craft in the District, and the most accessible financially of the big-name programs. Teens serious about acting or musical theatre get near-conservatory rigor without auditions; downtown location suits commuting families on Metro.

providers.name: The VIVA School (formerly CityDance DREAM)
providers.description: CityDance's celebrated tuition-free DREAM program spun off into The VIVA School, an independent nonprofit centering young dancers of color ages 6-18 in world-class pre-professional training plus leadership development — every accepted student trains on a full merit scholarship valued at $120,000+ through high school graduation. Summer training is part of the year-round pathway; admission is by audition only and highly selective.
providers.ages: 6-17 at audition (trains dancers 6-18)
providers.price_detail: Full merit scholarship for all accepted students — official site values the scholarship at $120,000+ from acceptance through high school graduation; no tuition charged
providers.locations: 1923 Vermont Ave NW Ste T002 (Grimke School building), Washington DC 20001; no longer based at THEARC (that was the CityDance DREAM era)
providers.hours: Year-round training schedule; not a drop-in summer camp
providers.financial_aid: Everything is free upon acceptance — the entire model is merit-scholarship based

reg_2027.opens: 2027-28 audition cycle not yet announced; 2026-27 auditions ran via pre-registration form at thevivaschool.org/audition — watch that page winter/spring
reg_2027.notes: Pre-Registration Form required before an audition invitation is emailed. Highly selective, small intake per year; female ages 8-12 seats listed as FULL for 2026-27, so boys and other age bands have better odds. Prepare for a formal audition, not a signup.

providers.sessions_2026: Year-round tuition-free training for enrolled students; 2026-27 audition cycle for ages 6-17 (female 8-12 full)
providers.status_2026: Not open to summer walk-ins; the audition cycle for the coming school year is the only entry point (2026-27 auditions held for ages 6-17)
providers.fit_notes: For serious, committed young dancers of color and families ready for a conservatory-level schedule — a life-changing free opportunity, not a casual dance camp. Casual dancers should look at CityDance's paid studio camps instead.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via search-indexed copies of thevivaschool.org home and /audition pages (2026-27 audition cycle, ages 6-17, full merit scholarship ~$120K, female 8-12 seats full) plus Yelp/DC Arts Ed Alliance listings for the Vermont Ave NW address, phone, and email. Location updated — school is at the Grimke School site near U St, not THEARC.

providers.name: The Washington Ballet Summer Programs (NW & SE campuses)
providers.description: DC's flagship professional ballet company runs summer programs at two campuses: the NW studios (Wisconsin Ave area) and the SE campus at THEARC in Ward 8, which is dramatically more affordable. Offerings run from creative-movement for ages 3-5 through fairy-tale ballet weeks (5-7), Young Dancer programs (8-12), and multi-week intensives for ages 10-18 including pointe, jazz, Horton, character, and African dance at SE. A newcomer with a ballet-curious kid can start here at any level; the SE campus is one of the best arts values in the city.
providers.ages: 3-18 (creative movement 3-5; fairy-tale/pre-ballet 5-7; Young Dancer 8-12; intensives 10-18)
providers.locations: NW campus near Wisconsin Ave NW; SE campus at THEARC, 1901 Mississippi Ave SE, Suite 202, Washington, DC 20020
providers.hours: Varies by program; SE 5-week Summer Intensive ran Mon-Fri 9:00am-3:00pm in 2026; SE ages 3-5 and 5-7 programs met Mon/Wed 4:00-7:00pm
providers.price_detail: 2026 examples: NW Young Dancer Intensive week $616; SE ages 3-5 program $240 and Pre-Ballet 5-7 $296 for four weeks; SE 5-week Summer Intensive (ages 10-18) $2,750. 5% discount for registering during the priority window (Jan 12-Feb 12 in 2026)
providers.financial_aid: Yes, but summer aid is an extension of school-year aid: TWSB awards up to $300,000/year via FACTS Grant & Aid, with school-year applications accepted May 1-June 30; families already receiving school-year aid apply for a Summer Programs Tuition Assistance Extension December-February, awarded as funding permits
providers.sessions_2026: Core window June 22-July 24, 2026 (SE ages 3-5 and Pre-Ballet 5-7 June 22-July 17; SE 5-week intensive June 22-July 24)
providers.status_2026: Programs underway and mostly ending mid-to-late July 2026; effectively closed for new 2026 enrollment

reg_2027.opens: mid-January (2026 registration opened January 11-12)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online for young-child, Young Dancer, and teen tracks; in-person audition required for NW intensives (ages 12+) and SE intensive Levels 2-6
reg_2027.notes: Register in the priority window (first month, through mid-February) for the 5% discount. SE campus (THEARC) programs are far cheaper than NW and serve ages 3-18 — a major value newcomers rarely discover. Aid seekers must plan a year out: apply for school-year aid May 1-June 30, then file the summer extension application December-February.

providers.name: The Writer's Center — Teen & Summer Writing Workshops
providers.description: Bethesda's 45-year-old literary institution runs hundreds of workshops a year, including teen creative-writing workshops and famously popular college-application-essay intensives each summer, in person on Walsh Street and online (live Zoom or asynchronous). This is a workshop, not a camp — a few hours with a professional writer, sitting alongside motivated peers.
providers.ages: Primarily 13-18 for youth offerings (teen creative writing and college-application-essay workshops for high schoolers); main catalog is adult
providers.locations: 4508 Walsh St, Bethesda, MD 20815 (downtown Bethesda) + online via Zoom
providers.price_detail: Priced per workshop, not per week; typical multi-session workshops run in the low hundreds with member discounts. 2026 teen-specific pricing not verifiable from indexed pages — check the Summer 2026 catalog at writer.org.
providers.financial_aid: Member discounts confirmed; scholarship availability not verified
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 catalog is live (Summer 2026 magazine posted on writer.org May 2026); workshops run through August and many remain bookable mid-summer

reg_2027.opens: Rolling — summer workshops post with the spring/summer catalog (Summer 2026 magazine published May 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Online first-come registration at writer.org/workshops; college-essay workshops for rising seniors are the hot ticket and fill first. No lottery. Filter the events calendar by age/genre.

providers.fit_notes: Best for serious teen writers and rising seniors needing college-essay help; too sparse a schedule to serve as childcare. Younger kids should look at Writopia or Day Eight instead.
providers.hours: Varies by workshop; short multi-session workshops rather than full-day camp weeks
providers.sessions_2026: Per-workshop sessions across summer 2026, in-person in Bethesda and on Zoom; teen creative writing and college-essay offerings appear seasonally
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed org active in 2026 via indexed writer.org pages including a Summer 2026 catalog PDF (uploaded May 2026), Bethesda venue page, and local coverage confirming teen/college-essay workshops. Specific 2026 teen workshop pricing and dates could not be confirmed, so pricing stays 'varies'; upgraded from low to medium since operation and mechanism are confirmed.

providers.name: THEARC Campus Summer Programming
providers.description: THEARC — the Town Hall Education Arts Recreation Campus on Mississippi Ave SE in Ward 8 — houses a dozen-plus nonprofits under one roof, letting east-of-the-river kids access dance, music, fine arts, tutoring, theater tech internships, urban farms, recreation, and even medical/dental care at substantially reduced or no cost. In summer that means a menu of camps and programs from resident partners (Boys & Girls Club, Washington School for Girls, arts organizations, THEARC Farm) rather than one single camp.
providers.ages: Varies by partner; collectively preK through teens (e.g., Boys & Girls Club @THEARC summer camp serves ages 6-18)
providers.price_detail: Substantially reduced or no cost by design; each of the ~14 resident partners sets its own fees (BGCGW summer camp includes breakfast and lunch)
providers.locations: THEARC, 1901 Mississippi Ave SE, Washington DC 20020 (Ward 8)
providers.hours: Varies by partner (BGCGW FBR @THEARC summer camp runs 9am-5pm)
providers.financial_aid: Reduced/no-cost access for east-of-the-river families is the campus's stated purpose; aid mechanics vary by partner organization

reg_2027.opens: Varies by partner — for 2026, BGCGW @THEARC enrollment opened April 13 (returning) / April 20 (new) and Washington Ballet SE Campus summer registration opened January 11; expect similar windows in 2027
reg_2027.notes: No single THEARC registration. Check each resident partner directly: Boys & Girls Clubs of Greater Washington FBR Club @THEARC (bgcgw.org, first-come until capacity), Washington Ballet @THEARC SE Campus (washingtonballet.org, opens mid-January), Washington School for Girls, THEARC Farm. Building Bridges events calendar covers campus-wide events.

providers.sessions_2026: BGCGW FBR @THEARC 8-week summer camp from June 25; Washington Ballet @THEARC SE summer programs; assorted partner events on the Building Bridges calendar
providers.status_2026: Campus programming ran all summer 2026: BGCGW 8-week themed camp (ages 6-18, 9am-5pm, started June 25, weekly field trips and swimming) and Washington Ballet SE Campus summer programs among others
providers.fit_notes: The single best address to know for affordable enrichment in Ward 8 — treat it as a mall of programs, not one camp. Requires a bit of legwork to match your kid to the right resident partner.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed buildingbridgesdc.org pages (campus, 14 nonprofit partners, address, contact) plus official partner pages: bgcgw.org (FBR @THEARC 2026 summer camp details, enrollment dates, hours, ages 6-18) and washingtonballet.org (SE Campus summer registration opened Jan 11, 2026). Umbrella listing — specifics intentionally per-partner.

providers.name: TIC Summer Camp (Bethesda)
providers.description: A DC-area institution running half-tech/half-sports days since the 1980s: campers pick one signature technology track — animation, filmmaking, 3D game design, Roblox, Minecraft modding, robotics, programming, or digital photography/design — for half the day and play outdoor sports the other half. The 2026 Maryland site is Sidwell Friends Lower School in Bethesda, with a long-running NoVA campus too.
providers.locations: Sidwell Friends Lower School, 5100 Edgemoor Lane, Bethesda MD (half mile from Bethesda Metro); separate Virginia campus at The Langley School, McLean
providers.price_detail: Base tuition not published — shown only inside CampBrain registration. $200 nonrefundable deposit per camper per session; multi-session discounts ($40/2, $80/3, $120/4 sessions) through May 18, 2026; balances due June 2, 2026. Historically premium-priced per two-week session.
providers.hours: Full-day, two-week sessions; half the day in a chosen technology program, half in athletics
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week sessions through summer 2026 at the Bethesda (Sidwell Friends Lower School) and McLean (Langley School) campuses
providers.status_2026: Operating summer 2026 at both campuses; 2026 registration ran through ticcamp.com/CampBrain with payment due June 2, 2026 — popular tracks fill by spring

reg_2027.opens: winter (registration live on ticcamp.com/registration via CampBrain well before summer; exact 2027 date not announced)
reg_2027.notes: Two-week commitment per session and per-track capacity means favorite tech tracks (game design, filmmaking) fill first. Long-time repeat-family camp — new families should register in winter. 2026 multi-session discount deadline was May 18 and full payment due June 2, a useful proxy for the 2027 calendar.

providers.fit_notes: The balanced pick for a kid who wants screen-creative time and running-around time in equal halves — parents like that it isn't all-day screens. Two-week sessions are a bigger commitment than weekly camps.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed ticcamp.com pages (registration, TIC-MD, TIC-VA) and sidwellsummer.org: ages/grades, Sidwell Bethesda location and address, two-week half-tech/half-athletics format, 2026 deposit/discount/payment deadlines, phone/email. Exact tuition is gated behind CampBrain so price_band stays 'varies' — medium confidence.

providers.name: Tink (Tinkering at the Cooperative School)
providers.description: The famous nature-based Takoma Park co-op preschool opens to the community each summer with 'Tink,' a five-week tinkering camp where K-5 kids build dens, whittle, use real hammers, saws, and axes, tie knots, build fires, and read maps. It's a rare genuinely risky-play, tools-in-hands program — closer to a European forest school than a typical American day camp.
providers.ages: 5-10 (must be currently enrolled in K-5th grade)
providers.locations: Takoma Park Cooperative Nursery School campus, Takoma Park MD
providers.price_detail: Per-week tuition; no co-oping required for summer — see site for current rates
providers.financial_aid: Not advertised — ask school
providers.sessions_2026: Five week-long sessions, roughly late June through July
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; small program that typically fills by spring — check for isolated openings

reg_2027.opens: January (registration for the coming summer 'launches January' per the school)
reg_2027.notes: Very small capacity and a devoted Takoma following — register in January, not spring. Verified from official site.

providers.fit_notes: For kids who want to build and break things outdoors all day; note it serves elementary ages, NOT the preschoolers the school teaches during the year. Not for kids who dislike dirt, heat, or unstructured time.

providers.name: Tiny Chefs Cooking Camps
providers.description: The DMV's biggest kids' cooking-camp operator, running weekly themed camps (Cupcake Wars, Chopped, All-American Road Trip, Breakfast for Dinner) at sites across DC, Montgomery County, and Northern Virginia — including Union Kitchen in Eckington (DC) and Stone Ridge in Bethesda for 2026. Kids cook real recipes daily in half-day or full-day formats.
providers.ages: 6-12 (official site's stated camp range; some host sites organize by grade)
providers.locations: Multiple DMV sites for 2026: Union Kitchen (Eckington, DC), Stone Ridge School (Bethesda), City of Rockville Senior Center, Bullis School (Potomac), Fairfax County school sites, plus out-of-region hosts
providers.price_detail: Host-site pricing varies; verified 2026 example at Stone Ridge: $665 per 5-day full-day week / $532 per 4-day week (Tiny Chefs half-day combined with afternoon activity block); half-day-only options cost less
providers.hours: Half-day and full-day options depending on site
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed sessions June-August 2026 across DMV host sites including Union Kitchen (DC) and Stone Ridge (Bethesda)
providers.status_2026: Registration opened Jan 5, 2026 at 10am; multi-site capacity means some mid/late-summer openings usually remain — check tinychefs.com and host-site registration pages

reg_2027.opens: early January (2026 registration opened Monday, January 5 at 10:00am; expect a similar early-January 2027 opening)
reg_2027.notes: Themed weeks (Cupcake Wars, Chopped!, Breakfast for Dinner) at convenient sites (Bethesda, DC) go fastest; the many locations mean a flexible family can usually still find a week later in spring. Register at tinychefs.com or through the host site (Stone Ridge, Bullis, Rockville register through their own systems).

providers.fit_notes: Great for elementary kids who love the kitchen — hands-on, theme-driven, and beginner-safe. It's enrichment cooking, not culinary training; teens serious about cooking should look at SummerCulinary instead.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked; confirmed via indexed tinychefs.com pages (ages 6-12, themes, DMV/2026 event listings), stoneridgesummercampus.org (2026 pricing $665/5-day, $532/4-day), Bullis summer programs page (registration opened Jan 5, 2026 at 10am), and a Feb 2026 Washingtonian piece confirming the Union Kitchen (Eckington) DC site. Price band $$$ matches verified host pricing.

providers.name: Tudor Place Time Travelers Summer Camp
providers.description: A small history camp at a National Historic Landmark mansion and 5.5-acre garden in Georgetown, where campers try real historians' tools - object study, archaeology digs, and making time capsules - to uncover how everyone from owners to enslaved workers lived at Tudor Place. Intimate scale and a genuine historic site make it feel special.
providers.locations: Tudor Place, 1644 31st St NW, Washington DC 20007 (Georgetown)
providers.price_detail: Price not published on indexed pages; snacks provided. Registration runs through Tudor Place's Blackbaud ticketing site.
providers.hours: Day camp (exact hours not verified)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 session(s) listed via washington.org and Tudor Place's Blackbaud registration; exact dates not verified
providers.financial_aid: None found

reg_2027.opens: Typically posted in spring on tudorplace.org
reg_2027.notes: Very small camp at a small institution - if interested, call in late winter and ask when registration posts rather than waiting for marketing. Registration goes through the Blackbaud ticketing link once live.

providers.status_2026: Camp offered in 2026 - listed on washington.org (Destination DC) with the theme 'travel back to the 1800s' (archaeology, object history, time capsules) and a live Blackbaud registration page; exact dates, price, and remaining availability could not be verified - call 202-965-0400
providers.fit_notes: Best for kids who like 'detective' history and small groups; narrow 9-11 age band and limited spots. Low confidence: verified only via search-indexed event listings.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (tudorplace.org, washington.org, and Blackbaud all refuse bot fetches); confirmed via indexed copies: ages 9-11, Georgetown address, camp description, snacks provided, active Blackbaud registration page, phone, and email. Price and 2026 dates remain unverified, but the 2026 program's existence and registration mechanism are confirmed - raised from low to medium.

providers.name: U.S. Chess Center Summer Chess Day Camps
providers.org: U.S. Chess Center (nonprofit; teaches chess to promote self-confidence and sportsmanship)
providers.description: The venerable DC-area chess nonprofit runs five week-long summer day camps at school sites in Silver Spring and Northern Virginia — chess instruction and play plus recreation, at notably lower prices ($230-280/week) than commercial competitors. Strong culture of sportsmanship; kids are grouped by experience from beginner to tournament level.
providers.ages: Rising grades 2-9 depending on week (Silver Spring week: rising 3-9)
providers.locations: 2026: East Silver Spring ES (Silver Spring MD, week 1); Colvin Run ES Vienna VA (weeks 2-3); Churchill Road ES McLean VA (week 4); Greenbriar West ES Fairfax VA (week 5)
providers.price_detail: $230-$280 per week depending on site; aftercare to 5pm for an added fee
providers.financial_aid: Not published on indexed pages; the org has a access-focused mission — ask admin@chessctr.org
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm, aftercare until 5:00pm for a fee
providers.sessions_2026: Jun 29-Jul 2 Silver Spring; Jul 6-10 and Jul 13-17 Vienna VA; Jul 20-24 McLean VA; Jul 27-31 Fairfax VA
providers.status_2026: Season underway; the three remaining weeks (Jul 13, Jul 20, Jul 27 — all NoVA) were open per their July posts; call to confirm space

reg_2027.opens: Late winter/early spring (2026 camps announced as open by early summer; exact opening month not published)
reg_2027.notes: Register online at register.chessctr.org; first-come, modest caps. The Silver Spring week is the only close-in MD site, so grab it early; NoVA weeks linger longer.

providers.fit_notes: Best-value chess camp in the region and welcoming to beginners; only one week is in Maryland, the rest require a NoVA drive. 9-3 day is shorter than most working parents need without aftercare.

providers.name: U.S. Senate Page Program — Summer Sessions
providers.description: The most storied civics 'summer program' in America: rising juniors and seniors (16-17, 3.0+ GPA) work on the Senate floor delivering correspondence and supporting proceedings, appointed by individual senators and paid on a real federal salary basis (annual rate around $37-38k, prorated). Summer runs as two sessions of 3-4 weeks; unlike the school year, summer pages may live with parents or relatives in the DC area, which makes the summer session unusually accessible for local families. Selection is extremely competitive and runs entirely through your home-state senators — for this guide's readers, that means Maryland's senators (e.g., Van Hollen, Alsobrooks) for MoCo teens.
providers.ages: 16-17 (rising juniors/seniors; 18-year-olds ineligible)
providers.locations: U.S. Capitol, Washington, DC; summer pages may live at home with parents/relatives in the area
providers.price_detail: Paid position — stipend based on an annual salary of roughly $37,000-38,500, prorated; health insurance required (FEHB enrollment if uncovered)
providers.financial_aid: n/a — participants are paid federal employees
providers.sessions_2026: Two summer sessions of 3-4 weeks each (June-August, set by the Senate calendar)
providers.status_2026: Closed for summer 2026 — applications were due in February (e.g., Van Hollen's office deadline was Feb 20, 2026); appointees are serving now.

reg_2027.opens: Senator-by-senator, typically December-January for summer; deadlines cluster in early-to-mid February
reg_2027.notes: Apply through BOTH home-state senators to double your odds; requires letter of interest, transcript (3.0+ GPA), recommendations, physician health assessment. MD students apply via Van Hollen and Alsobrooks; DC residents have no senator, which effectively locks them out — a painful quirk worth knowing.

providers.fit_notes: For polished, politically curious 16-17-year-olds who can handle 5am wake-ups and a dress code; it is a job, not a camp. Verified via official pageprogram.senate.gov and senators' office pages (search-indexed).

providers.name: UMD Terp Young Scholars
providers.org: University of Maryland, College Park (Extended Studies)
providers.description: A three-week, 3-college-credit summer program (July 13-31, 2026) where rising 10th-12th graders take a real UMD course — options span STEM, business, public policy and more — with commuter, residential, and online formats. One of the region's best values for earning genuine transcript credit before college, with scholarships available.
providers.ages: 13+, rising 10th grade through graduating seniors, 3.0+ unweighted GPA
providers.locations: UMD College Park campus (just outside the close-in MoCo zone, Metro-accessible via College Park); online option available
providers.hours: Full weekday program during the 3-week session; residential option available
providers.price_detail: Program fee not verified; substantially cheaper per credit than private-university pre-college. Application fee + deposit required.
providers.financial_aid: Yes — dedicated scholarships (see Program Fees and Scholarships page)
providers.status_2026: Application CLOSED for 2026; program runs July 13-31, 2026. (UMD also runs many separate youth sports/STEM camps — see today.umd.edu/2026-umd-summer-camps.)

reg_2027.opens: typically winter (applications reviewed first-come until full)
reg_2027.notes: First-come admission review — apply early in the window with transcript (3.0+ unweighted required). Course sections cap individually, so early applicants get their preferred subject.

providers.sessions_2026: July 13-31, 2026 (one 3-week session)
providers.fit_notes: The best credit-per-dollar pre-college option near DC, and the public-university vibe suits self-starters. It is a real graded college course — the grade follows the student.

providers.name: University of Maryland Youth & Sports Camps (Terp Camps)
providers.description: UMD's College Park campus (20 min from Silver Spring/Takoma) runs a huge slate of summer camps: Terp Quest general day camp for ages 6-12 ($325/week with pool swim, climbing, crafts), plus varsity-run sports camps from girls' soccer day camps ($475 full day) to elite residential camps in soccer, field hockey, gymnastics, and golf, many coached by Terps staff and student-athletes. Big-campus energy and Big Ten facilities at prices below most private DC camps.
providers.ages: 6-12 (Terp Quest); grades 1-12 for sport-specific camps
providers.locations: University of Maryland campus, College Park MD (just outside NE DC / east of Takoma Park)
providers.price_detail: Terp Quest $325/wk; girls soccer day camp $475 full / $264 half; elite overnight camps $725-825
providers.hours: Terp Quest 8:30 AM - 4:30 PM; sports camps typically 9 AM - 3 PM
providers.extended_care: Check per camp; Terp Quest's long day covers most work schedules
providers.financial_aid: none found
providers.sessions_2026: Terp Quest June 22 - July 31; sports camps June-July (e.g., girls soccer June 15-18, June 22-25, July 20-23; boys soccer elite June 27-30, July 10-13)
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; July sessions (girls soccer July 20-23, Terp Quest late-July weeks) may still have space — check each program

reg_2027.opens: January-March (each camp opens separately; Terp Quest typically by February)
reg_2027.notes: Decentralized: every sport's coaching staff and Terp Quest register separately — bookmark the UMD summer camps roundup page each January. First-come; Terp Quest weeks fill by late spring.

providers.fit_notes: Best value-for-facilities in the region and an easy reverse-commute from Silver Spring/Takoma; the decentralized registration is confusing at first, and DC-west families face Beltway traffic.

providers.name: Vacation Bible School (VBS) week camps — newcomer explainer
providers.org: Area churches (Protestant and Catholic parishes across DC/MoCo/NoVA)
providers.description: Every summer dozens of DC-area churches run Vacation Bible School: a one-week, usually morning (9am-noon) camp of songs, crafts, games, and Bible stories that is free or nearly free and typically open to non-members — the region's biggest hidden source of low-cost camp weeks for ages 3/4 through 5th grade. Verified 2026 examples: National United Methodist (NW DC, June 22-26, ages 4-5th grade, rising 6th+ as junior counselors), Bethesda UMC (ages 3-5th grade), Little Flower & St. Elizabeth Catholic parishes (Bethesda, last full week of June, 100+ kids), People's Congregational UCC (DC, Aug 3-7), and Alfred Street Baptist (Alexandria, free for the whole family).
providers.ages: Typically 4 (sometimes 3) through 5th/6th grade; rising 6th grade and up often volunteer as junior counselors
providers.locations: Individual churches across the region; aggregated lists at dcmoms.com and washingtonfamily.com each spring
providers.hours: Usually 9:00am-noon for one week (e.g., National UMC June 22-26, 2026, 9am-noon); a few churches run full-day with music camp add-ons
providers.price_detail: Free at many churches (e.g., Alfred Street Baptist, free for the whole family) to roughly $40-50 materials/T-shirt fee (e.g., Downtown Baptist 2026: $40/child by June 28, $45 after, $120 family cap)
providers.financial_aid: N/A — already free/low-cost
providers.sessions_2026: One-week sessions scattered June-August 2026, church by church
providers.status_2026: June sessions done; mid-July and August sessions still running or taking registrations (e.g., First Baptist Church of Alexandria July 13-17, 2026, half-day or full-day with music camp) — check church sites directly

reg_2027.opens: March-May, church by church
reg_2027.notes: Free spots at popular churches can fill within weeks of posting; expect explicit Christian religious content — this is worship-plus-crafts, not a secular camp with a church landlord. Junior-counselor slots (rising 6th grade+ at many churches) are an easy volunteering gig for tweens; SSL hours sometimes available for MoCo students.

providers.fit_notes: Excellent for budget gap weeks and for preschoolers (many take age 3-4); only for families comfortable with religious programming. Quality and structure vary widely by congregation.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed both aggregator lists (dcmoms.com page live in July 2026 search index; washingtonfamily.com VBS roundup) plus specific 2026 examples from church sources: Downtown Baptist ($40-45, ages 4-6th grade), National UMC (June 22-26, 9-noon, ages 4-5th grade with rising-6th+ junior counselors), First Baptist Alexandria (July 13-17). Direct fetch of dcmoms.com bot-blocked. Ages/cost/hours patterns hold; inherently church-by-church so medium confidence.

providers.name: Valley Mill Camp
providers.description: A storied family-run day camp and kayaking school on Seneca Creek in Germantown (since 1956), famous for turning out genuinely skilled whitewater kayakers alongside rock climbing, archery, pioneering, drama, and crafts. The traditional camp runs as separate boys' and girls' programs (ages 6-13) plus a coed Junior Camp for 4-5 year olds, and door-to-door bus transportation from stops across MoCo, DC, and NoVA is included in tuition. Many campers return for a decade.
providers.ages: 6-13 core single-gender programs (separate boys' and girls' camps); coed Junior Camp for preschool ages 4-5
providers.locations: 15101 Seneca Rd, Germantown, MD 20874; bus stops across Montgomery County, DC, and Northern Virginia
providers.price_detail: 2026: full season 6/22-8/21 $6,120 for 9 weeks (~$680/week, transportation included); shorter sessions available, e.g. 1st Session 6/22-7/17 $2,720; payment plans available
providers.hours: 9:00am-4:00pm; bus pick-up/drop-off extends the day at both ends
providers.extended_care: No traditional aftercare; included bus service covers transport
providers.financial_aid: None found — payment plans only
providers.sessions_2026: June 22 - August 21, in multi-week sessions (e.g. Session 1: 6/22-7/17) or full 9-week season
providers.status_2026: Season underway; mid-season openings unverified — call 301-948-0220 or email frank@valleymill.com.

reg_2027.opens: Rolling/early — enrollment for the following summer is typically open by fall-winter, with spring open houses (2026: March 22, March 29, April 26); exact opening date not published
reg_2027.notes: First-come; returning families roll over heavily and popular age groups waitlist. Newcomers should inquire in early fall, visit an open house, and note the camp prefers longer enrollments (multi-week sessions or full season) over single weeks.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for a kid who might fall in love with kayaking — progression is real and older kids run rivers. Note the traditional program is single-gender (separate boys' and girls' camps), and it's less ideal if you want week-by-week flexibility or a short commitment.

providers.name: VisArts Summer Camps & Teen Intensives
providers.org: VisArts (Visual Arts Center at Rockville Town Square)
providers.description: Rockville's community visual-arts center with professional studios for ceramics, glass, fiber, woodworking, painting, and mixed media. Young Artists (ages 5-6) is a one-week full-day program, Artistic Adventures (ages 7-12) runs as two-week full-day sessions where campers rotate through four studio classes daily, and teens 13-17 take the Teen Advanced Art Institute intensives. The strongest pure visual-arts camp in the Rockville area.
providers.ages: 5-17 (Young Artists 5-6; Artistic Adventures 7-12; Teen Advanced Art Institute 13-17; Extended Care 5-12)
providers.locations: 155 Gibbs St, Rockville Town Square, Rockville MD
providers.price_detail: Self-selected tiered pricing confirmed for 2026: $1,150 subsidized / $1,350 true-cost / $1,550 supporter per Artistic Adventures session (two-week sessions, roughly $575-775/week depending on tier); before/extended care extra for ages 5-12
providers.hours: Full-day camps for ages 5-12 (media include ceramics, painting, drawing, mixed media, mosaics, fused glass, woodworking); teen intensives are half-day blocks; exact daily hours not published in indexed copies — see FAQs
providers.extended_care: Before care and extended care available for campers ages 5-12 (separate registration/fee)
providers.financial_aid: Limited need-based scholarships for Artistic Adventures campers, plus the published pay-what-fits tier system ($1,150 subsidized rate); $1,550 supporter tier grows the scholarship fund
providers.sessions_2026: One-week Young Artists weeks and two-week Artistic Adventures sessions plus teen intensives across summer 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 registration opened Jan 1 and popular weeks fill by spring; camps routinely sell out — check site for remaining late-summer sections

reg_2027.opens: January 1 (confirmed pattern: 2026 registration opened Jan 1 — check visartscenter.org in December)
reg_2027.notes: Enrollment is capacity-limited and VisArts warns camps routinely sell out — register the week it opens for ceramics and glass weeks. Choose your pricing tier at checkout; need-based scholarship applications are limited, apply before enrolling if needed.

providers.fit_notes: Excellent for kids who want to work in real clay/glass/fiber studios with professional teaching artists, and for teens ready for focused medium-specific work. Note the two-week session structure for ages 7-12 (a commitment, but deeper work), half-day-only teen intensives, and the need for January planning.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch bot-blocked (403); confirmed via indexed copies of official visartscenter.org pages (camps landing, FAQs, Extended Care project page): 2026 tier pricing $1,150/$1,350/$1,550 exact, age bands 5-6/7-12/13-17, extended care 5-12, need-based scholarships, and Jan 1, 2026 registration opening all match official-source content.

providers.name: Washington Conservatory of Music Summer Camps
providers.description: One-week music camps for ages 4-12 inside the storybook setting of Glen Echo Park (the conservatory's home in the old park Arcade), with some programming in Kensington. Days mix instrument discovery, ensemble play, music-theory games, and end-of-week performances - a gentle, high-quality classical-leaning introduction run by a respected community conservatory.
providers.ages: 4-12 (per 2026 Montgomery County volunteer listing for camp assistants supporting children ages 4-12)
providers.locations: Glen Echo Park (7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD); some programs at Kensington-area site
providers.price_detail: Most recent verified pricing (2024): $487/week tuition + $15 Glen Echo Park registration fee, 9am-3pm weeks; 2026 rates not confirmed — conservatory's summer page still carries a 2024 title in the search index
providers.hours: 9:00am-3:00pm (per most recent published schedule, 2024)
providers.financial_aid: Conservatory offers scholarships for lessons; camp-specific aid not confirmed
providers.sessions_2026: One-week camps late June through August (2026 week-by-week schedule not verifiable via indexed copies)
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 season confirmed running via Montgomery County Volunteer Center recruiting for 2026 camp assistants (children ages 4-12 at Glen Echo Park); week-by-week availability not indexed — call 301-320-2770

reg_2027.opens: With the Glen Echo Park spring/summer catalog, typically February-March
reg_2027.notes: First-come registration through the conservatory/Glen Echo Park systems; smaller profile than the big camps so it fills slower — a good choice for planners and latecomers alike. Call 301-320-2770 to confirm current-year weeks since the website's summer page lags.

providers.fit_notes: Lovely first music camp for young kids (4-12), especially those drawn to piano/strings and a calm, artsy park setting. Older kids and teens will outgrow it; not a performance-blowout rock camp.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch bot-blocked; confirmed org active (Yelp updated Mar 2026, Glen Echo Park partner page, MoCo Volunteer Center 2026 camp-volunteer listing citing ages 4-12), phone 301-320-2770, and 2024 pricing $487+$15 at 9am-3pm from indexed official page. 2026 pricing/schedule unconfirmed — official summer page title still says 2024 in the index, so medium.

providers.name: Washington Nationals Youth Baseball Academy (BASE Program + YBA PLAY)
providers.description: The Nats' flagship youth-development center in Fort Dupont Park (Ward 7) runs a completely free 6-week summer academy blending baseball/softball with academics, meals, field trips, and monthly Nationals Park outings for 'Scholar-Athletes' from Wards 7-8 who commit year-round. The lighter-weight YBA PLAY program offers free twice-weekly instructional clinics and summer league play for ages 5-12 at four sites in Wards 5-8, with all equipment and uniforms provided. This is a community program, not a drop-in camp — families 'pay' with commitment and attendance, and it is one of the best free summer options in the city for east-of-the-river families.
providers.ages: BASE Program: elementary through 8th grade (Wards 7-8); YBA PLAY: ages 5-12 (Wards 4-8 eligible)
providers.locations: Youth Baseball Academy, 3675 Ely Pl SE, Washington DC (Fort Dupont Park, Ward 7); YBA PLAY also at sites in Wards 5-8
providers.price_detail: Free, including uniforms, equipment, and meals; requires year-round commitment (70%+ attendance) for BASE Program
providers.hours: Summer academy weekdays; YBA PLAY clinics Mon/Wed (ages 5-8) and Tue/Thu (ages 9-12)
providers.financial_aid: Program is entirely free — no fees at all
providers.sessions_2026: 6-week summer academy (June-August) for enrolled Scholar-Athletes; YBA PLAY clinics run year-round
providers.status_2026: Summer academy underway for enrolled families; BASE enrollment is year-round cohort-based (not bookable mid-summer). YBA PLAY registration via LeagueApps is periodically open on a rolling basis.

reg_2027.opens: Spring (rolling outreach; BASE cohorts enroll before the school year/summer)
reg_2027.notes: BASE Program requires an application plus a short 20-minute parent interview and a year-round commitment through 8th grade; Ward 7-8 residents only. YBA PLAY is simple free registration on LeagueApps, open to Wards 4-8, no experience needed. Contact the Academy directly — this works like a community program, not first-come camp registration. Official site verified via search-indexed copies (nats4good.org blocked direct fetch).

providers.fit_notes: For DC families east of the river wanting a serious, free, mentorship-heavy summer program — not for families outside Wards 4-8 or those wanting casual one-week baseball camp (for that, the Nationals also license paid Headfirst-run baseball camps across the region).

providers.name: Washington Sailing Marina Summer Sailing School
providers.description: Week-long youth sailing camps on the Potomac at Daingerfield Island, just south of Reagan National along the Mount Vernon Trail — a regional fixture for learning to sail, with tracks from true beginners through racers for ages 8-15, plus big-boat options for older kids. Views of the DC monuments from the water are part of the deal. Registration runs through UltraCamp.
providers.ages: 8-15 overall (verified July 2026 session was ages 10-15; beginner weeks admit younger — confirm per session on UltraCamp)
providers.locations: 1 Marina Dr, Alexandria, VA 22314 (Daingerfield Island, GW Parkway)
providers.price_detail: Verified July 13-17, 2026 session (ages 10-15) at $695; register for 3+ camps and get 10% off the 3rd and subsequent camps; beginner dinghy weeks typically run lower — check UltraCamp for exact rates
providers.hours: 9:30am-4:00pm Mon-Fri (verified 2026 session); some listings show 10am-4pm with extended-day option
providers.extended_care: Extended day available per marina listings — drop-off as early as 8:30am and pickup as late as 5:30pm Mon-Thu for an additional fee
providers.financial_aid: None found (multi-camp 10% discount only)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly Mon-Fri sessions June-August 2026 at Daingerfield Island
providers.status_2026: Mid-July 2026 sessions (e.g., July 13-17, $695) listed on UltraCamp as of this check; August weeks typically bookable — a realistic late-2026 option

reg_2027.opens: December (2026 sessions opened for registration Dec 10, 2025 on UltraCamp)
reg_2027.notes: First-come on UltraCamp; fills steadily rather than instantly, and advanced/racing weeks have prerequisites. 10% multi-camp discount from the 3rd week on. One of the safer camps to still get into in spring.

providers.fit_notes: The regionally famous learn-to-sail option (included as NoVA gap-filler); driving access only, no Metro. Kids must pass a swim requirement.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetches bot-blocked; confirmed via indexed UltraCamp session data (camp ID 1232/WSM): July 13-17, 2026 session, ages 10-15, $695, 9:30am-4pm, registration opened Dec 10, 2025, 10% 3+-camp discount, all camps at Washington Sailing Marina Alexandria. Org, URL, price band, and registration mechanics all confirmed from 2026 official-platform content; extended-day details added from marina listings.

providers.name: Wheaton Ice Arena Summer Skating Camps
providers.description: Wheaton Regional Park's rink runs a full-day summer skating camp where kids skate twice daily (lesson plus practice) with off-ice games and fitness in between — welcoming absolute beginners with rental skates provided — plus specialty figure-skating camps (freestyle, theater on ice, synchro) and hockey camps for progressing skaters. The east-county sibling to Cabin John with the same county pricing and registration system.
providers.ages: Roughly 6-14 (specialty camps grouped by skating level)
providers.locations: Wheaton Ice Arena, 11717 Orebaugh Ave, Wheaton MD (Wheaton Regional Park)
providers.price_detail: County rates; full-day camp includes two daily skates and rental skates if needed
providers.hours: Full-day camp; specialty camps vary
providers.extended_care: Check current season for extended-care options
providers.financial_aid: Marye Wells-Harley Dream Camp Scholarship via Montgomery Parks
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions June-August 2026
providers.status_2026: Mid-season; popular weeks sold out from January registration, occasional openings on ActiveMONTGOMERY

reg_2027.opens: Mid-to-late January (6:30 AM on registration day — the rink's own social posts hype the 6:30 AM opening)
reg_2027.notes: Same ActiveMONTGOMERY 6:30 AM first-come rush as all Montgomery Parks camps, with virtual queue; early-bird discount by early April. Figure-skating specialty weeks (synchro, theater on ice) are unique in the region and fill fast.

providers.fit_notes: The place for east-county kids to fall in love with skating, and the specialty camps serve budding figure skaters well; hockey-track kids may outgrow it toward the Capitals Iceplex programs.

providers.name: Wheaton Park Stables Summer Camp
providers.description: Half-day pony and horsemanship camps at the public riding stable in Wheaton Regional Park, covering riding basics, grooming, and barn care in a low-key, small-barn setting. Camps start the first full week after MCPS lets out and serve a wide age range, with group riding from age 8. It's Montgomery County's most accessible entry point to horses.
providers.ages: Riding camps from age 8 (child must turn 8 before camp starts); site markets horse activities for ages 3-18, with pony rides for the youngest outside camp
providers.locations: Wheaton Regional Park, 1101 Glenallan Ave, Silver Spring/Wheaton, MD
providers.price_detail: Half-day camps only; 2026 pricing not verifiable via indexed copies
providers.hours: Half-day sessions only (confirmed 'HALF day camps only' on official page)
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly half-day camps from mid-June 2026 (first full week after MCPS ends)
providers.status_2026: Registration is CLOSED for 2026 — fully booked (confirmed on official summer-camp page as of this check).

reg_2027.opens: Winter (exact date announced on their site/email; 2026 filled and closed well before summer)
reg_2027.notes: First-come and closes fast once full. Their own advice: if your child already rides there, email BEFORE signups open about placement. Newcomers should email WheatonParkStablesMD@gmail.com in fall to ask when registration posts.

providers.fit_notes: Half-day only, so it needs pairing with other childcare; ideal affordable-ish first horse experience for MoCo kids. Assume you must plan a season ahead.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch bot-blocked; confirmed via indexed copies of official wheatonparkstables.com summer-camp and FAQ pages: 'Registration is now CLOSED for 2026,' half-day-only format, riding minimum age 8 (must be 8 before camp), address, and contact email. Pricing still unpublished in indexed sources, so medium.

providers.name: WISummer — Washington International School Language Camps
providers.description: French and Spanish summer camps for PreK-grade 6 on WIS's storybook Tregaron campus in Cleveland Park, with separate 'immersion' groups (French, Spanish, and Chinese offered) that run entirely in the target language for kids who already speak it, alongside discovery tracks for newer learners. Grades 5-10 can do DC-area and international travel programs instead.
providers.ages: Age 4 (PreK) through grade 6 for French/Spanish/Chinese language day camps; grades 5-12 for travel, leadership, and CIT programs
providers.locations: WIS Tregaron campus, 3100 Macomb St NW, Cleveland Park, Washington DC
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates still not published on search-indexed official pages; $100/week deposit at registration, full payment due May 22. Third-party listings suggest mid-$500s/week — confirm at wiscamps.org.
providers.financial_aid: None found on official pages
providers.sessions_2026: About six weekly sessions, mid-June through late July 2026 (enroll 1-6 weeks); indexed catalog shows a holiday-shortened Session 3 of June 30-July 2, 2026
providers.status_2026: Season nearly over — camp runs only through late July and closes for August; late-July availability must be checked directly

reg_2027.opens: Winter (typically January) via CampBrain (wissummer.campbrainstaff.com)
reg_2027.notes: First-come online via CampBrain; true-immersion groups (especially Chinese and French) have limited seats and fill by spring. Season ends in late July — plan August coverage elsewhere. No 2027 dates announced yet.

providers.fit_notes: Immersion groups genuinely require existing language comfort — they move fast and stay in-language; discovery tracks suit beginners. Gorgeous, Metro-ish (Cleveland Park) location.
providers.hours: Mon-Fri, core day 8:30am-3:30pm
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); verified via multiple searches of wiscamps.org/wis.edu indexed copies: confirmed org, URL, ages (Age 4-grade 6 day camps; grades 5-12 travel/leadership), hours 8:30-3:30 with extended day to 6pm, $100 deposit, May 22 payment deadline, CampBrain portal, and a 2026 Session 3 date; weekly pricing remains unindexed, so medium confidence.

providers.name: WNO Opera Institute (ON HOLD — not running 2026)
providers.org: Washington National Opera (independent of the Kennedy Center since Jan 2026)
providers.description: Historically a celebrated three-week summer vocal intensive (held at American University) for high school singers exploring opera as a career, with strong financial-assistance commitments. Washington National Opera left the Kennedy Center in January 2026 amid institutional upheaval (covered by NPR), and the official application page now states that planning for the Institute is on hold. Newcomers should not count on this for 2027 without confirmation.
providers.ages: Grades 9-12 (rising and graduating seniors welcome historically)
providers.locations: Historically American University campus, NW DC; future location unknown post-Kennedy Center split
providers.price_detail: Historic tuition not confirmed for future years; WNO explicitly worked with low- and middle-income families on affordability
providers.financial_aid: Historically strong — applicants could request financial assistance in the application
providers.sessions_2026: None — program did not run
providers.status_2026: Not running. Application page (updated Jan 2026) says planning is on hold due to institutional changes; WNO now operates independently and is rebuilding programs.

reg_2027.opens: Unknown — program on hold
reg_2027.notes: Email info@washnatopera.org asking to be notified about career-development programs for high school students. If revived, expect a recorded/live vocal audition and winter application window as in past years.

providers.fit_notes: Entry included so newcomer families stop searching: the famous Kennedy Center teen opera program is paused. Verified via search-indexed copy of the official smapply page plus NPR reporting.

providers.name: Wolf Trap (no children's camp — Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods performances instead)
providers.org: Wolf Trap Foundation for the Performing Arts / National Park Service
providers.description: Wolf Trap, the national park for the performing arts in Vienna, VA, does not run a kids' summer camp — what it offers families is Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods: $8-12 outdoor morning performances (music, puppetry, dance, author-based musicals like Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus) Tuesday-Saturday at 10:30am from late June to mid-August, ideal for ages 4+. For older students, Wolf Trap runs competitive paid summer internships and apprenticeships ($5,000 stipend, application deadline March 1) aimed at college students and recent grads living in the DMV. Regionally famous enough that newcomers assume a camp exists; it does not.
providers.ages: Performances best for 4-10 with adults; internships college-age (18+)
providers.locations: Children's Theatre-in-the-Woods, Wolf Trap National Park, 1551 Trap Rd, Vienna, VA
providers.price_detail: Tickets $8-12 per show; 'Pick 3' saves $2/ticket on 3+ shows; group discounts for 10+. Internships pay a $5,000 summer stipend.
providers.financial_aid: n/a for performances (already low-cost)
providers.sessions_2026: Performances late June-mid August 2026, Tue-Sat 10:30am; 2026 lineup includes Jules Hoffman and Don't Let the Pigeon Drive the Bus
providers.status_2026: OPEN and bookable right now for July 2026 — individual show tickets on sale at wolftrap.org/woods; a good late-availability outing when camps are full.

reg_2027.opens: Season lineup and tickets announced in spring; internship applications open winter, due March 1
reg_2027.notes: No lottery, no sellout panic — buy show tickets days ahead. College students wanting the internship should apply well before the hard March 1 deadline; DMV residency required during the internship.

providers.fit_notes: A 'no camp, here is what exists' entry. Perfect low-cost culture mornings for preschool/elementary kids; nothing here provides day-long coverage. Verified via official wolftrap.org pages (search-indexed).

providers.name: Writopia Lab DC Summer Writing Camps
providers.org: Writopia Lab (national nonprofit, DC Metro region)
providers.description: The DMV's flagship creative-writing camp: week-long workshops capped at eight kids, led by published writers, in genres from fiction and screenwriting to Dungeons & Dragons storytelling and college essays. Kids who love making up stories — including shy kids who hate 'school writing' — tend to thrive because instructors meet each writer where they are. Half-day workshops (3 hrs) or full-day camps (morning workshop + electives) at the Tenleytown center, with a Falls Church VA site too.
providers.ages: 4-18 (Language Play ages 4-6; core age-banded workshops roughly 8-18 for elementary, middle, and high school; college-essay track for teens)
providers.locations: 4000 Albemarle St NW, Suite 308, Tenleytown, Washington DC 20016 (Metro-accessible); second site at Christ Crossman United Methodist Church, 384 N Washington St, Falls Church, VA 22040
providers.price_detail: Official published rates: one-week $850 full-day / $560 half-day; two-week sessions $1,600 full-day / $1,020 half-day. Honor-based sliding scale — pay what you can.
providers.financial_aid: Exceptional: honor-based sliding scale plus need-based scholarships; org states ~50% of students attend on partial or full scholarship and that no student has ever been turned away over cost
providers.hours: Half-day 3-hr blocks (AM or PM) or full-day (3-hr workshop plus two 1.5-hr electives, lunch/park break), Mon-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly and two-week half-day/full-day sessions through summer 2026 at Tenleytown (and Falls Church); typically runs into August — confirm specific weeks in the online catalog
providers.status_2026: Open/rolling — enrollment live for remaining summer weeks; 8-writer caps mean individual weeks close without notice

reg_2027.opens: Winter (roughly December-January) when the summer catalog posts
reg_2027.notes: Rolling online enrollment; no lottery. Each workshop caps at 8 writers, so popular genre/age combos fill by late spring — book by March for a specific week. Contact Writopia to use the sliding scale. No 2027 dates announced yet.

providers.fit_notes: Best for kids who genuinely want to write or tell stories; not a general day camp. The D&D and screenwriting weeks hook reluctant writers. Not a fit for kids who need lots of outdoor/physical time in a full day.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via searches of writopialab.org indexed pages: org active, URL correct, Tenleytown and Falls Church addresses, phone, official pricing ($850/$560 week), 8-writer caps, sliding scale/scholarship claims (50%, never turned away), Language Play ages 4-6 — key facts all official-source, 2025-2026 content.

providers.name: WTEF Summer Tennis & Learning Camp
providers.description: The charitable arm of DC tennis (connected to the city's pro tournament legacy) runs summer camps combining tennis instruction, match play, and educational enrichment at its center at 16th & Kennedy St NW, with additional DPR-partnership programming. All levels are welcome, and the mission is access: pricing is modest with generous multi-week discounts, and WTEF's core purpose is serving low-income DC youth. A standout choice for families who want tennis plus academics without country-club prices.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-17 (grouped by age/level)
providers.locations: WTEF Center for Excellence, 1600 Kennedy St NW, Washington DC; some programming at DPR sites
providers.price_detail: Modest weekly fees with multi-week discounts: 15% off 2+ weeks, 20% off 4+, 25% off 8+; contact staff for discount/aid codes
providers.hours: Weekday day camp hours (verify current schedule)
providers.financial_aid: Core mission — need-based assistance and discount codes available; contact Alex Shek or Safeeyah Iverson before registering
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions from June 17, 2026 through summer
providers.status_2026: Camp running; weekly registration via CourtReserve typically remains open for later-summer weeks

reg_2027.opens: Spring (camp pages and CourtReserve events posted by April-May)
reg_2027.notes: Create a CourtReserve account, add WTEF under 'My Organizations,' add campers under 'My Family,' then register per week under Events. Rolling first-come; rarely a sellout crunch. Ask about aid before paying full price.

providers.fit_notes: Great mission-driven, affordable tennis camp for city kids of all levels; families seeking elite training intensity should look at JTCC.

providers.name: Yellow Barn Studio Youth Painting & Drawing
providers.org: Yellow Barn Studio & Gallery (Glen Echo Park resident program)
providers.description: Glen Echo Park's celebrated painting and drawing residency, founded by legendary local art teacher Walt Bartman, known for serious instruction in watercolor, oil, acrylic, pastel, and plein air painting. Summer offerings for young people are studio classes open to beginning and intermediate students 13 and up (teens learn alongside adults), registered through the Glen Echo Park system — not weeklong kids' day camps.
providers.ages: 13-18 for teens (drawing, watercolor, pastel, colored pencil, oils & acrylics classes open to beginning/intermediate students 13+ in a mixed teen/adult setting; no younger-kid camps)
providers.locations: Yellow Barn Studio, Glen Echo Park, 7300 MacArthur Blvd, Glen Echo MD
providers.price_detail: Not a day camp: once-weekly 2.5-hr studio classes. Summer 2026 classes run $120-$180 for a 4-5 week series (e.g., Exploring Pastels $180, Mondays 7/6-8/3/2026; Still Life Simplified $120) — about $30-45 per class meeting.
providers.hours: Varies by class; once-weekly 2.5-hr sessions (e.g., Mon 10am-12:30pm, 1-3:30pm, 4-6:30pm), not camp days
providers.financial_aid: None found
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 drawing/watercolor/pastel/oil/portrait classes for students 13+, mostly 4-5 week once-weekly series in July-early August (e.g., 7/6/2026-8/3/2026)
providers.status_2026: Glen Echo Spring/Summer 2026 online registration open and ongoing; summer Yellow Barn classes (July 6-Aug 3 series) listed with per-class availability in the catalog

reg_2027.opens: Early February (with Glen Echo Park spring/summer registration; catalog posts in January)
reg_2027.notes: Registers through Glen Echo Park's ProClass/Imperisoft system. Known feeder studio for serious teen painters, but these are 13+ studio classes shared with adults, not drop-off camps. No 2027 catalog announced yet.

providers.fit_notes: The pick for a motivated teen (13+) who wants to actually get better at drawing and painting, possibly toward a portfolio. Not a day camp and not for younger children; childcare hours are not the model.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via searches of glenechopark.org and its Imperisoft catalog indexed copies: program active, URL correct, 13+ mixed teen/adult format, specific summer 2026 class dates and prices ($120-$180), registration open/ongoing, registrar phone and email.

providers.name: YMCA Anthony Bowen Summer Camp
providers.description: Day camp at the historic Anthony Bowen Y on W Street NW (the nation's first African American YMCA, founded 1853), with traditional, sports, aquatics, specialty STEM/arts, travel, and leadership camp tracks and an on-site pool. A genuine neighborhood institution serving U Street/Shaw/Columbia Heights families, walkable and Metro-accessible for car-free households.
providers.ages: Roughly 5-12 for core camp; travel and leadership/CIT tracks extend to middle schoolers/teens — grade cutoffs by track not published on indexed pages, verify with branch
providers.locations: YMCA Anthony Bowen, 1325 W St NW, Washington, DC 20009
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates not search-indexed (published in branch Day Camp Guide/EZ to Enroll); per-week deposit at registration; financial assistance available
providers.hours: Full-day (verify current-year hours with branch)
providers.financial_aid: YMCA income-based financial assistance; apply through the branch

reg_2027.opens: Typically midwinter (Jan-Feb)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via EZ to Enroll plus branch registration forms (due one week before camp starts)
reg_2027.notes: Aquatics weeks (on-site pool, certified instructors) are the draw and fill first; travel and leadership tracks give older kids a ladder. One of the more affordable full-day NW DC options after aid. No 2027 dates announced yet.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly traditional, sports, aquatics, leadership, and travel sessions, summer 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 season underway; camp pages live with Traditional, Sports, Aquatics, Leadership, and Travel tracks — remaining-week availability only visible inside EZ to Enroll
providers.fit_notes: Strong pick for central-DC families wanting swim-every-day without leaving the neighborhood; the travel/leadership tracks also cover older siblings. Verified via search-indexed copies; call to confirm rates/hours.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via searches of ymcadc.org indexed pages: URL correct and live for 2026, all five camp tracks, EZ to Enroll mechanism, forms deadline, financial assistance, phone, and camp email AnthonyBowenCamp@ymcadc.org; ages and weekly rates not indexed, so medium confidence.

providers.name: YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase / Ayrlawn Summer Day Camp
providers.description: Classic YMCA day camp run from the Ayrlawn Program Center campus near NIH, with traditional camp (games, swim, crafts, sports, STEM) plus specialty weeks and a leadership/CIT camp track for older kids. A dependable, affordable-ish default for elementary kids whose families want weekly flexibility rather than a multi-week commitment. Thrives: kids who like variety and being outside; it is not a deep-dive specialty camp.
providers.ages: 5-12 for core camp (~K-6); leadership/CIT track for middle schoolers/teens — grade cutoffs not published on indexed pages, verify with branch
providers.locations: YMCA Ayrlawn Program Center / YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase, 9401 Old Georgetown Rd, Bethesda, MD 20814
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates not published on search-indexed pages (in EZ to Enroll/branch camp guide); per-week deposit required at registration. YMCA financial assistance available (branch-administered).
providers.hours: Full-day; typical Y camp day ~9am-4pm with extended-day options (verify with branch)
providers.extended_care: Before/after care historically offered at Y day camps; confirm hours with Ayr.Camp@ymcadc.org
providers.financial_aid: YMCA DC offers income-based financial assistance; apply through the branch

reg_2027.opens: Typically midwinter (Jan-Feb); 2026 forms went live by late winter
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via YMCA 'EZ to Enroll' portal plus branch registration forms
reg_2027.notes: Week-by-week enrollment; popular mid-summer weeks fill by late spring but the Y adds capacity, so it rarely locks out as fast as boutique camps. Early enrollment recommended by the Y itself. Leadership/CIT spots are fewer than traditional-camp spots. No 2027 dates announced yet.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions across summer 2026 (typical Y calendar, mid-June through late August); traditional, aquatics, sports, dance/gymnastics, tennis, STEM, arts, leadership, and travel tracks
providers.status_2026: 2026 season underway; camp pages live with Traditional, Aquatics, Sports, Dance & Gymnastics, Tennis, STEM, Arts, Leadership, and Travel tracks — per-week availability only visible inside EZ to Enroll
providers.fit_notes: Good value-flexibility default for newcomers in Bethesda/Chevy Chase; not for kids wanting a single-focus specialty camp. ymcadc.org blocks direct fetch — details verified via search-indexed copies.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via searches of ymcadc.org indexed pages: URL correct and live for 2026, Ayrlawn/BCC address, full track list (incl. dance/gymnastics, tennis, STEM, arts), EZ to Enroll + branch-forms registration, financial assistance; ages, hours, and weekly rates not indexed, so medium confidence.

providers.name: YMCA Camp Letts
providers.description: A 100+-year-old YMCA overnight camp on a peninsula in the Rhode River near Annapolis — the closest true sleepaway camp to DC (about 45 minutes) — with sailing, kayaking, horseback riding, mountain biking, and classic waterfront camp life. Weekly Sunday-Friday sessions let families try just one week, and consecutive-week campers can stay over the weekend for $200. Its YMCA backing means real scholarship money and a less precious, more accessible culture than the private sleepaways.
providers.ages: 7-16 overnight (day camp historically 6-16 — confirm with camp office)
providers.locations: 4003 Camp Letts Rd, Edgewater MD (~45 min from DC)
providers.price_detail: 2026 rates not captured in indexed copies; last verified pricing (2022) was $855/week member, $905/week non-member overnight — expect roughly $1,000/wk now. Stay-over weekend between consecutive weeks $200.
providers.financial_aid: Scholarships available (limited funds, awarded by application, due March 1); YMCA payment assistance also available
providers.hours: Residential Sun-Fri
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly overnight sessions (Sun-Fri) throughout summer 2026, with $200 stay-over weekend option between consecutive weeks
providers.status_2026: 2026 season underway; weekly structure means late-July/August weeks sometimes have space — call 410-919-1410 to check

reg_2027.opens: Typically fall-winter (rolling); scholarship applications due March 1
reg_2027.notes: First-come by week; one-week Sun-Fri format makes it the easiest sleepaway trial run in the region. Apply for scholarships well before the March 1 deadline. No 2027 dates or rates announced yet.

providers.fit_notes: Best first sleepaway for a hesitant camper (single weeks, close enough for quick pickup) and for families needing aid at an overnight camp. Facilities are rustic-YMCA, not resort-grade.
providers.verification_note: Direct fetch blocked (403); confirmed via searches of ymcadc.org/campletts.org indexed pages plus ACA/YMCA-USA listings: camp active for 2026, URL correct, ages 7-16 overnight, Sun-Fri weekly format, $200 stay-over, March 1 scholarship deadline, phone and email; 2026 weekly rates not indexed (2022 baseline $855-905), so medium confidence.

providers.name: YMCA Camp Letts Day Camp
providers.description: The day-camp arm of Camp Letts: kids get the same 219-acre Chesapeake waterfront — boating, pool, ropes, farm/nature areas — and go home each night. Mostly serves families toward Annapolis/Edgewater, but it is a real option for East-of-the-Anacostia and Capitol Hill commuters wanting big-outdoors day camp rather than a rec-center program.
providers.ages: 6-12 (day camp; resident camp serves older kids)
providers.locations: YMCA Camp Letts, 4003 Camp Letts Rd, Edgewater, MD 21037 (~40-50 min from Capitol Hill)
providers.price_detail: Weekly day-camp rates not search-indexed and could not be confirmed (site blocks fetches); well below resident-camp pricing (2022 resident rate was ~$855-905/wk); add-ons like horseback riding extra; Y financial assistance applies
providers.hours: Full camp day roughly 9:00am-4:30pm; drop-off Mon-Fri 8:30-9:00am at Fisher Hall; pick-up 4:30-5:30pm (Fri pick-up at the Rustics)
providers.extended_care: Built-in drop-off/pick-up windows (8:30-9:00am, 4:30-5:30pm); a separate paid extended-day program was not confirmed — ask the camp office
providers.financial_aid: Scholarship funds available and awarded by application; applications due March 1 (shared deadline with resident camp)

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; historically opens midwinter alongside resident-camp registration
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online registration via campletts.org / ymcadc.org, week by week (registration-portal name unverified; paperwork portal was being updated in 2026)
reg_2027.notes: Far less competitive than the overnight program; a realistic late-planner option. Scholarship application deadline is March 1. Confirm whether any bus/transport from DC exists in a given year — historically limited.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly themed day-camp sessions all summer 2026, running alongside the resident camp
providers.status_2026: Season underway summer 2026; day camp historically has more late openings than resident weeks — call 410-919-1410 to check July/August space
providers.fit_notes: Only makes sense geographically if the Edgewater drive works for you; in exchange you get waterfront acreage no in-town day camp can match. Verified via indexed copies only.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed copies of ymcadc.org/campletts.org (direct fetch blocked): org, url, ages 6-12, drop-off/pick-up hours, address, phone, email, March 1 scholarship deadline. Weekly price and exact 2026 session dates could not be confirmed.

providers.name: YMCA Camp Tockwogh
providers.description: Storied co-ed overnight camp on 320+ waterfront acres of the upper Chesapeake's Eastern Shore (Worton, MD, ~1.75 hrs from DC), regionally famous for its sailing and waterski/wakeboard programs, plus horses, challenge course, and arts. Sessions run from 3-day mini-camps for the youngest through 1- and 2-week sessions and 4-week teen leadership programs — many DC families treat it as the step-up sailing sleepaway after Camp Letts.
providers.ages: grades K-11 across programs (mini-camp intro for youngest; traditional overnight for completed grades 2-9; LIT/CIT teen leadership programs; all-ages family camps)
providers.locations: YMCA Camp Tockwogh, 24370 Still Pond Neck Rd, Worton, MD 21678 (Kent County, Eastern Shore, on the Chesapeake Bay)
providers.price_detail: Exact 2026 session rates not search-indexed (site blocks fetches); teen LIT/CIT programs list program values of $2,050-$3,600, consistent with premium overnight-camp pricing well above $700/wk; verify current rates with camp
providers.hours: Residential; one-week sessions run Sunday-Friday, two-week sessions last 12 days; mini-camp intro sessions and family camps also offered
providers.financial_aid: Needs-based financial assistance and larger scholarships available through YMCA of Delaware — apply via camp; inquire at 410-348-6000

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced for 2027; historically fall for the following summer
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come online registration via the YMCA of Delaware portal (my.ymcade.org)
reg_2027.notes: Signature sailing/waterski weeks and teen leadership (LIT/CIT) programs fill earliest — inquire by September-October. Note the camp is run by the YMCA of Delaware despite its Maryland location.

providers.sessions_2026: Season June 2 - Aug 23, 2026: mini-camps, 1-week (Sun-Fri) and 2-week (12-day) traditional overnight sessions, watersports specialty camp, teen leadership programs, and family camps
providers.status_2026: 2026 season confirmed running June 2 - Aug 23, 2026; late-summer session availability unverified — call the camp office
providers.fit_notes: The serious-water-sports sleepaway of this sector — best for kids who specifically want to sail or waterski, and for families ready for premium sleepaway pricing. Outside our core geography but a fixture among DC families.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed ymcacamptockwogh.org pages and directories (direct fetch blocked): operator corrected to YMCA of Delaware, url live, grades K-11 / traditional grades 2-9, session structure, 2026 season window June 2-Aug 23, phone, address, LIT/CIT $2,050-$3,600 values. Exact traditional-session rates unconfirmed.

providers.name: YMCA Leadership Camps & Counselor-in-Training (Silver Spring / Bethesda-Chevy Chase)
providers.description: The Y's two-phase teen leadership track: a training week covering child development, group management, and camp skills, followed by placement weeks assisting day-camp staff with 5-12-year-olds — earning about 40 MCPS SSL hours per session (10 for training, 30 for placement). Offered at YMCA Silver Spring and YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase/Ayrlawn Program Center. Thrives: teens who want a structured, supervised first work-like experience with a well-known organization.
providers.ages: Teens, roughly 13-15 (two-phase program: a training week, then CIT placement assisting staff with 5-12 year olds; exact age/grade cutoffs not published in indexed copy — verify with branch)
providers.price_detail: Registered as a camp program with weekly fees; rates vary by branch and by community vs family membership (all Y DC camp registrants must hold a membership) — exact fees not search-indexed
providers.hours: Full camp day
providers.locations: YMCA Silver Spring (9800 Hastings Dr, Silver Spring, MD 20901); YMCA Bethesda-Chevy Chase / Ayrlawn Program Center (North Bethesda)
providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions through summer 2026 at Silver Spring and Bethesda-Chevy Chase/Ayrlawn (training week + CIT placement weeks)
providers.status_2026: 2026 season in progress at both branches; Y DC camps often retain late openings — check branch leadership-camp pages for remaining July/August weeks

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; Y DC summer camp registration historically opens midwinter (roughly January-February), members typically first
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via YMCA DC's EZ to Enroll online system; membership required before camp registration
reg_2027.notes: Two-phase structure confirmed: teens complete a training week (group leadership, songs, games, crafts, working with ages 5-12) before earning CIT status and being assigned to assist camp staff — so book the training week early in the summer.

providers.financial_aid: YMCA of Metropolitan Washington offers income-based financial assistance across programs
providers.fit_notes: Less selective and more available than boutique CIT programs — a reliable fallback if ATMTC/Barrie fill. Quality varies by branch and summer staff.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed ymcadc.org pages (direct fetch blocked): both branch leadership-camp URLs live for 2026, two-phase training-then-CIT structure, membership requirement, EZ to Enroll system. Exact ages/grades and weekly fees not published in indexed copy.

providers.name: YMCA Silver Spring Summer Camp
