providers.description: Large multi-track day camp at the Silver Spring Y: traditional camp plus specialty, sports/dance, tennis, aquatics, travel camp for older kids, and leadership/CIT tracks for teens. One of the few close-in camps where a family can place a 6-year-old, an 11-year-old, and a 14-year-old CIT at the same site.
providers.ages: Approx. 5-15: traditional/specialty/aquatics/sports camps for elementary-middle schoolers, teen leadership/CIT track (~13-15); exact per-track brackets in the branch Day Camp Guide — verify
providers.locations: YMCA Silver Spring, 9800 Hastings Dr, Silver Spring, MD 20901
providers.price_detail: Weekly rates in the branch Day Camp Guide, not search-indexed. All camp registrants must hold a Y community or family membership (each with different rates/discounts), which changes effective price
providers.hours: Full-day; extended-day options historically offered — verify in Day Camp Guide
providers.extended_care: Before/after care historically offered; confirm with branch
providers.financial_aid: YMCA income-based financial assistance available

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced; historically midwinter (Jan-Feb)
reg_2027.mechanism: first-come via YMCA DC's EZ to Enroll online system; community or family membership required before camp registration
reg_2027.notes: Aquatics weeks are among the fastest to fill. Budget for the required community/family membership on top of weekly fees. Register per-session using the branch registration forms page.

providers.sessions_2026: Weekly sessions summer 2026 across traditional, specialty (LEGO/robotics, art), aquatics, sports & dance, and leadership/CIT tracks
providers.status_2026: 2026 season underway; week-by-week enrollment continues where space remains (per-week availability not verified)
providers.fit_notes: Best Y option for Silver Spring/Takoma Park families and for teens needing a CIT ladder. Membership requirement is the gotcha newcomers miss. Verified via search-indexed copies only.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed ymcadc.org pages (direct fetch blocked): url live for 2026, address, camp tracks (traditional/specialty/aquatics/sports & dance/leadership), membership requirement, EZ to Enroll mechanism. Removed unverified 'travel camp' track; exact ages and weekly rates unconfirmed.

providers.name: Young Actors Program (Summer)
providers.description: A three-week summer conservatory for ages 10-17 at the Columbia Heights school that grew out of Studio Theatre's famed conservatory: mornings of voice, movement, and musical theatre; afternoons of ensemble acting with veteran faculty, building original ensemble pieces. Radically accessible — half of all class places are offered on full scholarship, and there is no audition.
providers.ages: 10-17 (directory listings say 10-17; one indexed page copy says 12-17 — confirm bracket by phone)
providers.locations: Studio Acting Conservatory, Columbia Heights, Washington DC (Holmead Pl NW)
providers.price_detail: Full tuition not search-indexed (site blocks fetches); $100 non-refundable registration fee included in tuition; distinctive policy: one-half of class places offered with FULL scholarship to students with demonstrated need
providers.financial_aid: Half of all class places offered on full scholarship to students with demonstrated need (scholarship spaces involve an audition) — call 202-232-0714; the strongest aid ratio of any theatre camp in the region
providers.hours: Daily for three weeks: mornings in movement, voice, and musical theatre; afternoons in a 2.5-hour acting intensive (monologues, scenes, contemporary and classical)
providers.sessions_2026: Summer 2026 term June 8 - Aug 4, Final Scenes July 31 - Aug 4; Young Actors three-week summer intensives run within this window (exact session dates on the summer-2026 schedule page); placement auditions were June 13
providers.status_2026: Summer 2026 term underway (June 8 - Aug 4); second-session space as of mid-July unverified — call 202-232-0714

reg_2027.opens: Not announced; enrollment historically opens when the summer schedule posts in spring
reg_2027.mechanism: rolling — a $100 registration fee holds a place in any class
reg_2027.notes: No audition required to enroll (placement auditions by appointment; scholarship spaces do involve an audition). The full-scholarship half of each class is the thing to move fast on — inquire as soon as the summer schedule appears.

providers.fit_notes: The hidden gem for families priced out elsewhere and for teens who want real acting technique (not a show-in-a-week). Smaller and less flashy than Imagination Stage or Theatre Lab; no culminating musical spectacle.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed studioactingconservatory.org pages (direct fetch blocked): org active, urls live, summer 2026 term June 8-Aug 4, half-of-places full-scholarship policy, $100 registration fee, phone. Tuition amount and exact intensive session dates unconfirmed; prior entry's session dates replaced with official term window.

providers.name: Young Artists of America: Summer Performing Arts Intensives
providers.org: Young Artists of America at Strathmore
providers.description: The region's premier pre-professional musical-theatre training organization (performances at Strathmore) runs summer intensives for serious young musical-theatre performers, including a two-week residential Junior Conservatory for rising 7th-9th graders living 24/7 at UMD's Clarice Smith Center while mounting a full musical with room, board, and recreation included. Orchestra-backed, mentorship-driven, and audition-caliber.
providers.ages: Junior Conservatory & Junior Academy: rising grades 7-9; Senior Conservatory & Senior Academy: high school
providers.locations: Based in Rockville, MD; performances at Strathmore (North Bethesda); 2026 Junior Conservatory in residence 24/7 at The Clarice, University of Maryland College Park, with final performance in the Kay Theatre
providers.price_detail: Junior Conservatory 2026: $3,895 for the two-week overnight residency (full room, board, recreation included); $3,795 returning-student rate through Sept 30, $3,845 early-bird through Dec 31; day intensives priced separately
providers.financial_aid: Need-based financial aid and scholarships available: best consideration by Feb 1, 2026; scholarship/aid applications due Mar 1, 2026 — see youngartistsamerica.org/scholarships or ask info@yaa.org
providers.hours: Residential 24/7 for Junior Conservatory (two weeks); day schedules for the Academy intensives
providers.sessions_2026: Two-week Junior Conservatory overnight residency at The Clarice, UMD College Park (final performance July 30, 2026), plus Junior/Senior Academy and Senior Conservatory summer intensives; register-by June 1, 2026
providers.status_2026: 2026 registration deadline was June 1 (to guarantee a spot); enrollment effectively closed by July — Junior Conservatory culminates July 30, 2026

reg_2027.opens: Per the 2026 pattern, enrollment opens the prior fall: returning-student rate through Sept 30, early-bird through Dec 31 (2027 dates not yet announced)
reg_2027.mechanism: online registration with date-tiered tuition; register by ~June 1 to guarantee a spot; financial-aid applications due ~Feb 1/Mar 1
reg_2027.notes: Apply-early, pre-professional pipeline. The overnight Junior Conservatory (rising 7th-9th) is the area's only residential musical-theatre option for middle schoolers; 2026 final performance July 30 at the Kay Theatre, The Clarice.

providers.fit_notes: For the kid who already lives for musical theatre and wants conservatory-level training with orchestras and professional venues — overkill for casual performers. Pricing and application mechanics could not be fully verified; confirm directly.
providers.verification_note: Confirmed via search-indexed youngartistsamerica.org pages (direct fetch blocked): org active, urls live, rising-grades-7-9 Junior Conservatory, $3,895 tuition with $3,795/$3,845 tiered rates, UMD Clarice residency, July 30 2026 performance, June 1 registration deadline, Feb 1/Mar 1 aid deadlines.

providers.name: Youth Teaching Assistants (YTA) at Smithsonian Summer Camp
providers.description: A structured teen volunteer corps that helps run Smithsonian Summer Camp: YTAs assist instructors in the classroom, lead games, and manage drop-off/pick-up, earning community-service (SSL) hours at a national institution. A one-week YTA Boot Camp trains younger teens (rising 9th graders / age 14+) in camper-wrangling and museum navigation, and confers priority in YTA selection the following summer.
providers.ages: High school students 15+ before the first day of camp (15 or older before June 22, 2026; spring 2026 graduating seniors welcome). YTA Boot Camp: age 14 or entering grade 9 in fall 2026
providers.locations: S. Dillon Ripley Center, 1100 Jefferson Drive SW, Washington, DC 20560 (National Mall)
providers.price_detail: YTA is an unpaid volunteer position (earns student service learning hours). YTA Boot Camp is a separately ticketed one-week program; Smithsonian weeklong camps run $425 members/$495 non-members in 2026, but the Boot Camp's exact fee was not confirmable on indexed pages
providers.hours: YTAs must commit to a full week and be available 8:15 am-5:00 pm daily (camp day runs 9:30 am-4:30 pm); YTA Boot Camp runs 9:30 am-4:30 pm
providers.sessions_2026: Week-by-week assignments across the June 22-Aug 14, 2026 camp season (no camp Friday, July 3); YTA Boot Camp week June 22-26, 2026
providers.financial_aid: N/A - volunteer role

reg_2027.opens: Not yet announced for 2027; applications historically open in winter with an early-spring deadline (e.g., a mid/late-March cutoff in past cycles)
reg_2027.notes: Online application with parent/guardian approval; must be 15+ before the first day of camp and commit to full weeks (8:15 am-5:00 pm); selected YTAs must attend an orientation. Popular with local high schoolers for SSL hours, so apply early. Completing YTA Boot Camp (for 14-year-olds/entering 9th graders) gives priority in the next summer's YTA selection. Smithsonian camp registration generally uses a member lottery in late January (2026 lottery was Jan 27), but the YTA application track is separate.

providers.status_2026: YTA applications for summer 2026 are closed (application page states applications are not currently being accepted); the 2026 YTA Boot Camp (June 22-26) is sold out.
providers.fit_notes: For teens who want real responsibility with younger kids and museum-world exposure; requires reliability for full camp-day shifts. Verified via search-indexed copies of official YTA and Boot Camp pages.
